The system achieves inference latency below 125 ms and operates with low power consumption suitable for prolonged use. The results confirm the feasibility of deploying multimodal Edge-AI perception on resource-constrained assistive devices.

title: Degradation Aware Adaptive Charging Strategies for Ultra-Fast Electric Vehicle Charging
authors: K Lakshmi Prasad Reddy, M. Vijaya Kumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Ultra-fast charging (UFC) is essential for improving the usability of electric vehicles (EVs); however, aggressive charging currents significantly accelerate battery degradation and electro-thermal stress, adversely impacting safety and lifetime. This paper presents a degradation-aware adaptive charging strategy for ultra-fast EV charging that systematically translates battery electro-thermal degradation insights into practical charging control. A physics-informed battery model incorporating state-of-charge (SoC) dynamics, electro-thermal behavior, and state-of-health <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{SoH})$</tex> degradation is employed. The proposed strategy dynamically regulates the charging current based on SoC and battery temperature, without relying on learning-based optimization. The charging framework is evaluated for a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 5 0\ V}, \mathbf{1 8 0\ A h\ E V}$</tex> battery pack under ultra-fast charging conditions with charging currents up to 350 A over a 3600 s charging horizon. Performance is compared against conventional constant-current-constant-voltage (CC-CV) and multistage charging strategies. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed degradationaware strategy significantly reduces electro-thermal stress and battery degradation. Specifically, the peak normalized battery temperature is reduced from 2691.8 (CC-CV) and 1975.6 (multistage) to 880.6, corresponding to a 67.3 % reduction relative to CC-CV charging. Moreover, cumulative degradation is reduced by more than 36 orders of magnitude, from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4.9 \times 10^{58}$</tex> under <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{C C - C V}$</tex> charging to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8. 9} \times \mathbf{1 0}^{\mathbf{2 2}}$</tex> using the proposed strategy. Although the proposed method increases charging time to 3600 s, compared to 1975 s for CC-CV charging, it establishes an effective trade-off between charging speed and battery health preservation. The results confirm that embedding degradation awareness into charging control substantially improves battery longevity and thermal safety, and provides a systematic transition toward learning-based optimal charging strategies for ultra-fast EV applications.

title: Model-Less Forecast-Proactive Intelligent Control for Inverter in Islanded Microgrid
authors: Mustafa A. Kamoona, Juan Manuel Mauricio
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Islanded inverter-dominated microgrids with high penetration of renewable energy sources face significant voltage regulation challenges under unbalanced and time-varying load conditions. Conventional voltage controllers, such as proportional-integral-derivative (PID) schemes, rely on accurate system modeling and careful parameter tuning, which limits their adaptability under uncertain operating conditions. This paper proposes a model-less forecast-proactive Reinforcement Learning (RL) voltage control framework for islanded AC microgrids supplied through a single DC source and grid-forming inverter. The proposed controller directly adjusts the inverter voltage reference using only local measurements, without requiring an explicit network model. A simple non-learning forecast feature based on the next-hour load schedule is incorporated to improve anticipatory control actions during peak demand periods. The effectiveness of the proposed approach is validated on a fourbus islanded AC microgrid with unbalanced and dynamic loads. Comparative studies against a model-based PID controller and an LSTM-based baseline demonstrate that the proposed RL controller achieves voltage regulation performance comparable to optimally tuned PID control while maintaining strict voltage limits of 0.98-1.02 pu. The results confirm that the proposed model-less framework provides a practical and robust alternative for intelligent voltage control in islanded microgrids.

title: Investigating the Impact of Dimensionality Reduction on Spectral Unmixing Accuracy Using NIST-Traceable Colour Calibration Standard
authors: Ondřej Vaculík, Mojmír Šerý, Lukáš Šilhan, Denisa Šilhanová, Pavel Zemánek
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: This study explores the impact of dimensionality reduction (DR) on spectral unmixing accuracy using a National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) traceable colour transmission calibration slide. Hyperspectral images were acquired and analysed alongside high-accuracy NIST reference spectral transmission data. The used analysis pipeline integrates DR techniques (principal component analysis, non-negative matrix factorisation, and autoencoder neural networks) with spectral unmixing algorithms (non-negative least squares and multivariate curve resolution). Reconstruction accuracy was evaluated using root mean squared error and spectral angle distance. The findings indicate that low-dimensional representations effectively yield low spectral reconstruction error. In particular, the combination of non-negative matrix factorisation with three components and multivariate curve resolution achieved the lowest reconstruction error. This work demonstrates that integrating DR with spectral unmixing enables reliable colour patch composition estimation, balancing data compression, and analytical accuracy.

title: Open-Set Domain Generalization for Hyperspectral Image Classification via Evidential Uncertainty Quantification
authors: Amirreza Khoshbakht, Erchan Aptoula
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: Open-set domain generalization for hyperspectral image classification tackles challenges from both unknown classes and cross-domain generalization without target adaptation. We propose a framework that extracts domain-agnostic features through attention-weighted frequency analysis, while capturing complementary spectral-spatial information via parallel pathways. It provides reliable open-set classification through uncertainty-aware pathway weighting. Experiments on Pavia University and Centre indicate performance levels comparable to state-of-the-art domain adaptation methods despite not requiring target domain access during training.

title: Low-Area Fused Floating-Point Four-Term Adder
authors: Thiruvenkadam Krishnan, Parthibaraj Anguraj, Barkunan SR, Saravanan Subramanian, Vivek Karthick Perumal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Floating-point (FP) logic is used in the computation field. Among different operations, FP addition has been studied more in recent years. This paper deals with an area-efficient architecture for Fused FP Four-Term (FFPF-T) addition. In the proposed design, a modified exponent processing unit and a new Significand Alignment (SA) block are used. A simple adder is applied for Mantissa Addition (MA) to perform the computation. The existing method is extended to support FFPF-T addition. The efficiency of the designs is evaluated using the Altera Quartus II simulation tool. The implementation outcome indicates that the proposed design requires less hardware area compared to the existing schemes.

title: Li Ion Life Estimation System using Machine Learning
authors: Sarathkumar R, Fathima Shirin M, Haririthanyaa S, Jayaprakash S, Premnath S
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: This paper presents an intelligent Battery Management System (BMS) designed to enhance the performance and lifespan of lithium-ion batteries through the integration of machine learning techniques. The proposed system leverages real-time sensor data to predict key battery parameters such as State of Health (SOH) and State of Charge (SOC), enabling proactive fault detection and optimized energy management. Utilizing microcontrollers and analog-to-digital converters, sensor data including temperature, voltage, and current is continuously monitored and processed. A machine learning model trained on historical battery data identifies anomalies and adjusts charging and discharging operations dynamically. Additionally, the system incorporates self-healing mechanisms that respond to early-stage faults, ensuring safety and system stability. Experimental validation demonstrates the model's accuracy and the system’s effectiveness under varied load conditions. The proposed solution supports predictive maintenance strategies, reduces operational risks, and aligns with the growing demand for efficient and sustainable battery technologies. This work contributes to the development of reliable, AI-driven BMS solutions for applications such as electric vehicles and renewable energy storage systems.

title: GN2T: A Hybrid Graph Neural and Tree-Structured Architecture for ECG-Based Arrhythmia Recognition
authors: Ashraful Goni, Rubiat Jahan Momtahina
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Electrocardiogram (ECG) arrhythmia classification remains challenging due to heterogeneous signal morphology and overlooked inter-feature dependencies in conventional models. This study introduces GN2T, a hybrid framework that combines correlation-driven graph convolution with tree-structured hierarchical learning. Graph-based adjacency construction captures physiological feature relationships, while CNN-RF hybrid nodes refine temporal patterns through gating mechanisms. Multi-head attention integrates multi-scale representations, enhancing the detection of subtle arrhythmia variations. Tested on four PhysioNet databases—MIT-BIH Arrhythmia, MIT-BIH Supraventricular, INCART 12-lead, and Sudden Cardiac Death Holter—GN2T achieved 98.48%–98.99% accuracy with precision above 98.70%. Comparative results show improvements of up to +2.38% over Random Forest and +9.19% over CNN, while maintaining cross-dataset stability under 1.48% variance. GN2T demonstrates strong potential for robust, clinically reliable arrhythmia detection across diverse populations.

title: Optimized Multiplexer-Based Carry Select Adder Implementation
authors: Parthibaraj Anguraj, Thiruvenkadam Krishnan, Mani P, Vivek Karthick Perumal, Saravanan Subramanian
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: In this work, a MUX-based Carry Select Adder (CSA) is designed to improve throughput and reduce LUT usage. The design combination of multiplexers with logic gates. The multiplexer selects the carry value as either 0 or 1. This method avoids using extra adder blocks. As a result, the hardware area is reduced without affecting the accuracy of the adder. The delay is also reduced, which improves performance. The design is tested for sizes from 8 bits to 128 bits. It is implemented on a Virtex4 FPGA. The results show that the design works well and is efficient.

title: Leveraging VGG16 and ResNet50 for Clinically Aligned Assessment in Breast Cancer Histopathology
authors: Yara Nassar, Mohanad Alayedi
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Recently, deep learning (DL) models have achieved high accuracies on the BreakHis dataset for breast cancer histopathological classification; these results are often inflated by data leakage from non-patient-level splits and idealized conditions. This study conducts a realistic comparative evaluation of VGG16 and ResNet50 transfer learning architectures for binary (e.g., benign against malignant) classification. In this paper, we introduce three clinically motivated protocols: first, strict patientlevel splitting to prevent leakage; second, cross-magnification testing (training on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$40 \times$</tex> only, testing on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$100 \times / 200 \times / 400 \times$</tex>); and third, robustness assessment under strong Gaussian noise and blur degradations. Metrics are reported at both the image and patient-level (via probability aggregation). According to simulation results, ResNet50 consistently outperforms VGG16, showing up to 15 % less accuracy degradation in cross-magnification and degradation scenarios due to its residual connections. These experiments reveal a significant performance gap relative to literature benchmarks, advocating for stricter evaluation standards. The proposed framework and splits are released for reproducible research.

title: Design and Fabrication of CSRR based Multi-Port Antenna System for 5G Smart Phone Devices
authors: Alamelu Alias Rajasree S, Kalaimathi B, Adline Jancy J, Shri Lakshmi S, Swathiga A, Vinodha N
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The performance of future wireless communication systems largely relies on the behavior of technologies like Massive MIMO in 5G and future networks. The antenna is designed on a 75 × 150 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> thick 1.5 mm FR4 substrate. An 8×8 MIMO antenna system is optimized within the frequency range of 3.1 GHz to 3.6 GHz with a resonant frequency at 3.4 GHz. Simulation and analysis is carried out employing Ansys HFSS, analyzing the most critical performance parameters including S-parameters, gain, efficiency, directivity, VSWR, Envelope Correlation Coefficient (ECC) below 0.2, Diversity Gain (DG) of 10dB. Simulation results reveal the maximum gain to be 4.57 dB, directivity of 5.23 dB, and return loss of -25 dB at resonant frequency of 3.4 GHz. The antenna has a high radiation efficiency of 87.38%, thus ensuring efficient transmission of power with low losses. The proposed design has better radiation properties, reduced mutual coupling, and superior isolation between antenna elements. These properties together render the antenna a good candidate to be integrated into future wireless communication systems.

title: AI-Enabled IOT Health Monitoring with Multi-Vital Sensing and Location-Triggered Emergency Alert Mechanism
authors: Aarav Singhal, Prasenjit Sarkar, Saumy Singh, S. Latha, P. Muthu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: In today's scenario, due to the increasing burden of chronic diseases, the aging population, and the limitations of periodic health checkups, the demand for health monitoring has increased drastically. Health monitoring in conventional healthcare systems is done through observation, which is not effective in detecting changes in the body. Hence, the development of intelligent health monitoring systems is highly required. This is the challenge addressed in the proposed Health Monitoring System, which combines wearable sensor technology for real-time health monitoring with machine learning analytics. These include data on essential physiological parameters (heart rate, body temperature, oxygen saturation, motion) which are acquired by embedded sensors, which are processed via an Explainable Boosting Machine (EBM) model. Unlike other machine learning models, the EBM model offers high predictive accuracy and interpretability that allow easy interpretation of how each physiological parameter affects health risk prediction. It classifies health conditions into predefined risk categories and presents explanations and predictions together which augments trust and clinical usability. Integration of IoT allows for real-time data transmission and remote monitoring through a connected interface as a preventive measure when abnormal conditions are detected. Coupled with sensor-based data acquisition, explainable machine learning and remote access, the proposed system can be applied for continuous health monitoring with reliable, explainable and scalable systems. This not only advances early detection and decision-making but also is a major step forward for patient-centric intelligent health systems.

title: Scalable Artificial Intelligence Models for Big Data Processing and Analysis
authors: Surya Veera Brahmaji Rao Sunnam
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Scalable artificial intelligence (AI) models have emerged as a pivotal solution for processing and analyzing large-scale datasets in a variety of domains, including healthcare, finance, and e-commerce. With the advent of big data, the need for intelligent systems capable of handling vast amounts of unstructured and structured data has increased exponentially. This paper explores the integration of scalable AI models with big data frameworks to address challenges such as data volume, velocity, and variety. Key methods for improving scalability in AI models, such as distributed learning, parallel processing, and cloud-based systems, are discussed. Furthermore, the paper highlights the role of advanced algorithms like deep learning, reinforcement learning, and ensemble methods in managing big data. A focus is placed on how these AI techniques can optimize data processing, enhance predictive analytics, and provide valuable insights for decision-making. Additionally, the paper reviews case studies demonstrating the application of scalable AI models in real-world big data environments and discusses future directions in the evolution of AI models for big data analytics.

title: Intelligent Robotic Waste Segregation using YOLOv11 for Real-Time Object Detection
authors: Doha Masrour, Yassine Salih-Alj
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: With the rapid increase in global waste generation, efficient segregation remains a critical challenge due to limited automation and reliance on manual labor. This paper presents the design and development of an intelligent waste segregation system that integrates a six-degree-of-freedom (6DOF) robotic manipulator with the YOLOv11 Nano object detection algorithm for real-time classification and sorting. The YOLOv11 Nano network is trained to identify seven waste categories: paper, plastic, metal, glass, cardboard, and textile, with optimized inference speed and detection accuracy. The robotic arm, modeled using Denavit-Hartenberg (D-H) parameters, executes inverse kinematic control for precise pick-and-place operations. By integrating the state-of-the-art YOLOv11 Nano vision-based detection model with a fully articulated 6DOF robotic manipulator, the proposed design enhances the automation, accuracy, and safety of waste segregation operations.

title: Hybrid Machine Learning Approach for Short-Term Photovoltaic Power Forecasting Using Meteorological Data
authors: Adhin Thomas, Mohamad Nassereddine
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: Photovoltaic (PV) power generation is inherently dependent on meteorological conditions, making accurate day-ahead forecasting critical for scheduling, reserve management, and grid stability. However, many existing machine learning (ML) approaches rely on limited meteorological integration or evaluate such inputs in an ad-hoc manner, resulting in sub-optimal performance for longer forecasting horizons. This paper investigates a hybrid deep learning approach for day-ahead PV forecasting that integrates multiple meteorological variables within a single production-feasible architecture. A Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) model cooperatively capture structured relationships between meteorological inputs and temporal PV dynamics. The key contribution is the systematic evaluation of individual meteorological variables irradiance, air pressure, wind speed, temperature, humidity, and cloud cover within a unified hybrid ML framework, addressing the gap where such variables are often included without explicit contribution analysis. Experimental results show that meteorological augmentation improves forecasting accuracy compared to persistence, Random Forest, XGBoost, and LSTM-only baselines, while sensitivity analysis provides actionable insight into the relative operational value of each variable, supporting informed sensor deployment in practical PV applications.

title: Decentralized Deepfake Detection: Feature-Aligned Federated Learning with Multi-Backbone Fusion
authors: R. Geetha, Jayanthi. V, G.R.Priyasri, P. Geetha, Kavitha. T
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Digital security besides media forensics now face a noteworthy difficulty in detecting fabricated faces due to the spread of deepfake technologies and facial forgery techniques. In order to identify face forgeries while protecting user privacy, this research presents a new-fangled framework named FFDL, which stands for Feature Fusion- Deep Learning utilising Federated Learning. This approach creates a privacy-aware, decentralised detection scheme by combining federated learning (FL) multi-source feature fusion. The model extracts strong visual features on client side by utilising the Inception-v3 and Xception architectures. Prior to being fed into a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) classifier, these characteristics are fused using Canonical Correlation Analysis (CCA) to boost their correlation besides discriminative capacities. By keeping all raw data inside the client environment during model training with the Federated Averaging (FedAvg) protocol, can guarantee that your data will remain private. In validate the experiments using FaceForensics++ and WildDeepfake for cross-dataset generalisation, Deepforensics-1.0 dataset was used. Outperforming both traditional centralised and non-fused models, the suggested FFDL model attained a peak accuracy of 98.99%, precision of 98.80%, recall of 98.85%, besides F1-score of 98.82%. Results from ablation studies validated the importance of each component, besides model's consistency in performance across new datasets proved its durability. In addition, the training and validation AUC-ROC scores showed that the classification capability was almost flawless. The efficacy of federated, fused-feature deep learning in attaining accuracy, scalability, and privacy is demonstrated in this paper. -supporting the identification of fake faces, a crucial step in the development of trustworthy methods of authenticating media and verifying identities in practical settings.

title: Detection of Diabetic Retinopathy using Intelligent Gradient Boosted Decision Trees
authors: M. Sharmila, K G.Sathiya, G.Uma, R Hemalatha, P.Pavithra, R.Suresh
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR), a progressive eye disease brought on by long-term diabetes, can lead to blindness or visual impairment if left undiagnosed. Due to minor retinal changes, image variability, as well as limited access to experts, timely and accurate detection is essential but challenging. Efficient screening necessitates the adoption of effective, automated, as well as easily comprehensible diagnostic techniques. Hence, this research is developed to perform the DR detection using intelligent machine learning methodology. The dataset is first gathered from the online sources called Optical Coherence Tomography Angiography (OCTA) India Dataset. This gathered data undergoes the preprocessing by the Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE) + Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) approaches. The extraction of the features from these pre-processed data is done by the Visual Geometry Group (VGG19) approach. The detection of DR is done finally by the intelligent machine learning model referred as Gradient Boosted Decision Trees (GBDT). The proposed GBDT-DR model in terms of accuracy and recall is 5.51% and 6.90% better than the considered existing methods for the DR detection model respectively.

title: Feature Aggregation and Multi-Class Detection (FAMCD) Framework for Skin Cancer Classification
authors: Ifjaz Ahmed M, Sahithy Ayyagari, Uma Maheswari M, Premaladha Jayaraman
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: An unwelcome threat to life, melanoma, occurs if it is inconspicuously missed in an early detection stage. This research article presents a smart detection system called FAMCD (Feature Aggregation and Multi-Class Detection-to better detect and classify melanomas). Steps involved in the smart detection system include pre-cleaning such as hair removal from skin images, manual annotation of the lesion (area of suspicion), and then feature extraction of both common features (shape, color, and margin of the lesion) and deep learning features based on classical rule sets such as the ABCD rule (which considers parameters such as shape and color). Additionally, special techniques are incorporated that claim a measurement of uncertainty from the model in its predictions: frequency dropout and Monte Carlo sampling. To better understand the extent of model performance, three new metrics are proposed for evaluation: TUI (Total Uncertainty Index) for quantifying uncertainty level, BPS (Boundary Preservation Score) for verifying how well the lesion edge is preserved, and SCE (Structure Consistency Error) for verifying how well the structure of the lesion is preserved. This experimental evaluation demonstrated that the approach yielded a TUI value of 1.0847, along with a BPS score of 0.4000 (30% high uncertainty) and an SCE of 0.4875 (80% retained data). The system was tested on the popular skin cancer image dataset HAM10000. In terms of classification performance, the method yielded an overall accuracy of 0.8616 and a macro-AUC-ROC score of 0.8151, highlighting strong predictive ability. However, the balanced accuracy (0.3562), macro recall (0.3562), and macro F1-score (0.3519) indicate limitations in handling class imbalance. And the Local Interpretable Model (LIME) generates intuitive visual explanations that enhance physicians understanding of the model's reasoning, thereby fostering greater trust in its decisions.

title: Smart Cradle: An IoT-Based System for Intelligent Infant Monitoring
authors: Adithiyaa D, Lakshhmi Narayanan T, Gokulakkrishna Saravanan, Parvathy A K
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of an IoT-based smart monitoring system that is fitted onto a baby's cradle and performs predefined, automated actions to help keep the baby safe and to help soothe the baby without the physical intervention of caregivers. An ESP32 microcontroller is used as the main controller to track key environmental conditions. The BME680 sensor monitors air quality and temperature and soil moisture sensor is used to detect diaper wetness. Baby crying is identified using a sound sensor. The system is triggered automatically whenever any sensors crosses its set threshold limit and as a response it performs one of the following actions, which includes switching on the fan, swinging the cradle using a servo motor, sending alerts to the caregiver, and playing lullabies to calm the baby. Also, to help caregivers monitor the baby remotely at any time, an ESP32 CAM module is used to stream live video of the cradle. The system is designed so that it can be operated through manual remote control in the Blynk platform, and even a web dashboard is provided for real-time data monitoring. Overall, this work presents a complete and practical childcare solution by combining embedded hardware with cloud-based IoT technology.

title: Real-Time GR-Based Shoulder Dislocation Risk and Strength Monitoring System Using Arduino for Rehabilitation and Preventive Care
authors: Neeththi Aadithiya B, Nivasan K, Praveen Kumar K, Nithishkumar M
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: One of the most recurrent musculoskeletal injuries is shoulder dislocation, which, if not identified quickly, can result in pain, instability, and long-term issues. Expensive imaging procedures like MRIs and X-rays are a major part of current diagnostic procedures, but they are not always practicable for ongoing monitoring or preventive care. In the presented research a non-invasive, portable shoulder dislocation monitoring device that tracks abnormal muscle activity around the shoulder joint using surface Electromyography (sEMG) sensors is thus proposed. The presented system identifies the dislocation, weariness, and strain by using RMS-based threshold detection. The analysis of the real-time recorded signals is processed via a Raspberry Pi, which is actually amplified and digitalized using an ADC. The proposed method can be readily utilized by athletes and can be utilized in physiotherapy clinics and rehabilitation centers as the system is user friendly. Overall the Proposed system will be an alternate solution for the expensive scans and will promote the earlier detection.

title: An NRBO-Enhanced CNN-BiLSTM Framework for Multivariate Photovoltaic Power Forecasting
authors: Xingbo Fu, Wenhui Luo, Yang Liu
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Control
abstract: None

title: Deterministic Embedded Machine Learning for Adaptive Fighter Aircraft HUD Stabilization
authors: Ihsen Ben Mbarek Brahmi, Sandric Bretecher, Line Hoffmeyer-Kuntz, Clément Chanvalon
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Head-Up Displays (HUDs) are mission-critical cockpit interfaces in fighter aircraft, requiring high visual stability under extreme flight dynamics including rapid rotations, structural vibrations, and aggressive maneuvering. Conventional inertial stabilization approaches rely on fixed model-based filtering techniques that lack adaptability to rapidly varying motion conditions, often resulting in residual visual jitter and increased pilot cognitive workload. This paper proposes a deterministic embedded machine learning approach for adaptive fighter aircraft HUD stabilization. The core contribution lies in integrating lightweight on-device intelligence capable of real-time inertial motion classification under strict memory and latency constraints compatible with safety-critical avionics systems. Using tri-axial gyroscope data, the system classifies four fundamental aircraft motion statesyaw, roll, pitch, and level flight-through temporal signal windows of 1536 samples (512 per axis). A comparative benchmarking study evaluates Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP), Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Machine (SVM), SEFR, and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) models trained on 150 motion sequences per class. Experimental results demonstrate that the MLP-based implementation achieves the optimal trade-off between classification accuracy and embedded resource usage, reaching a balanced accuracy of 97.50% and a quality index of 93.5, while requiring only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5.8 \text{KB} \text{RAM}, 24 \text{KB}$</tex> Flash memory, and 3.3 ms Worst Case Execution Time (WCET) on a microcontroller-class platform. The deterministic execution behavior ensures bounded latency suitable for realtime avionics applications. Compared to conventional inertial stabilization methods, the proposed adaptive strategy enables context-aware HUD rendering with improved visual robustness and operational reliability under dynamic flight conditions.

title: YOLOv8-Based Intelligent Traffic Monitoring for Multi-Class Vehicle Detection, Counting, and Speed Estimation in Smart Cities
authors: Gautami G. Shingan, Priyanka N. Jadhav, Amol M. Jagtap, Priyanka Gejage, Pradnya Mane, Aishwarya Waghmode
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Efficient real-time traffic monitoring is essential for smart cities and road safety. This paper proposes a YOLOv8-based Intelligent Traffic Monitoring System that detects, counts, and estimates the speed of various vehicle types using the GRAM-RTM M-30 dataset. The framework tackles challenges such as heavy traffic, occlusions, and poor visibility, providing timely and accurate monitoring. Experimental results show F1-scores ranging from 92% to 96% for detection. Counting accuracy exceeds 96%, and speed estimation errors are under 4%. This system outperforms YOLOv5 in both accuracy and processing speed. With an average processing speed of 35 FPS on standard GPU hardware, it is well-suited for live traffic monitoring and works well under different traffic densities, lighting conditions, and vehicle types. Future improvements will focus on better traffic analysis, improved handling of occlusions, and broader city deployment. The proposed YOLOv8-based system offers an effective and scalable solution for intelligent traffic monitoring, supporting smart city projects and improving road safety.

title: Hybrid CEO–LMO based Combined Economic–Emission Dispatch with Renewable Energy Integration
authors: K. Deepak, G. Pandu Ranga Reddy, Y. Hazarathaiah, Shaik Sameena, Devanuru Rishika, Mangali Meghana
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Combined economic-emission dispatch becomes difficult when renewable generation pushes thermal units toward their operating limits. Photovoltaic and wind power reduce net thermal demand, shrinking the feasible dispatch region and making constraint handling dominant. This work addresses that problem directly. Economic-emission dispatch is solved as a capped-simplex optimization in which power balance and generator limits are enforced by projection, not penalties. A hybrid metaheuristic combining Cosmic Evolution Optimization and Logarithmic Mean Optimization is used. Exploration and refinement operate in parallel within each iteration, and objective normalization is applied to reduce sensitivity to weight scaling. Renewable generation is treated as a fixed injection that lowers thermal demand. When high renewable output violates minimum generation feasibility, curtailment is applied only to restore feasibility. Curtailment is not optimized. The method is tested on the IEEE 30-bus system with six thermal generators. All candidate solutions remain feasible at every iteration. Compared to thermalonly dispatch, renewable integration leads to lower thermal cost and emissions and faster convergence, without forcing generators to boundary limits. The results show that explicit feasibility enforcement and normalization produce stable CEED solutions in operating regions where conventional weighted-sum methods become fragile.

title: Memory-Efficient Speculative Decoding for Large Language Model Inference on Commodity GPUs
authors: Tejas Pravinbhai Patel
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Large language model (LLM) inference remains computationally expensive due to the autoregressive nature of token generation. Speculative decoding has emerged as a promising technique to accelerate inference by using a small draft model to predict multiple tokens in parallel, which are then verified by the target model. However, existing speculative decoding approaches require loading both models simultaneously, often exceeding the memory capacity of commodity GPUs. We present a memory-efficient speculative decoding framework that enables practical deployment on resource-constrained hardware through model quantization and optimized memory management. Our approach achieves a 2.31 × speedup in token generation throughput on an NVIDIA RTX A4000 (16 GB) while maintaining output quality, using only 12.0 GB of GPU memory. We demonstrate that speculative decoding can be made practical for edge and on-premise deployment scenarios where hardware resources are limited. Experimental results on LLaMA-7B show an average acceptance rate of 76.3% and consistent latency reductions of 56.8% across diverse prompts.

title: A Production Framework for Adaptive GPU Resource Management in Deep Learning Inference
authors: Tejas Pravinbhai Patel, Rohit Nimmala, Milan Parikh
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: Deploying AI models in production requires balancing throughput, latency, and infrastructure cost. Existing inference systems often rely on static GPU allocation and fixed batching strategies, leading to underutilized resources during low traffic and degraded performance during demand spikes. This paper presents a practical framework for adaptive AI inference that dynamically adjusts batching and GPU allocation based on real-time workload patterns. We implement the system using commodity GPU instances and evaluate it with vision and language models under varying traffic conditions. Results from experiments on NVIDIA A4000 and A5000 GPUs show throughput improvements of 1.4× to 2.1× compared to baseline configurations, with latency reductions of 18–32% during steady-state operation and 25–38% better GPU utilization. An ablation study confirms that adaptive batching and elastic GPU scheduling are complementary: neither mechanism alone captures the full benefit of their joint operation. The framework is designed for straightforward deployment in existing cloud environments without requiring specialized hardware or extensive reconfiguration.

title: Smart Home Device Anomaly Detection Using AI
authors: Marina Ahmad Shyyab, Salma Hameedi Abdullah, Ayman AbuBaker
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: As the integration of Internet of Things (IoT) devices into smart homes grows, securing and ensuring the reliability of these devices is ever more critical. Changes in the behavior of a device can signal possible malfunctions, cyber threats, or misconfigurations, which can thus impair the performance of a system and the safety of users. This paper presents a new robust AI-enhanced framework designed for the anomaly detection in smart home environments. The proposed method incorporates machine learning and deep learning algorithms in the monitoring of real-time device activities to determine when they deviate from normal operational patterns. By utilizing datasets composed of smart home device logs and sensor data, the framework is trained to differentiate between benign anomalies (e.g., temporary network disconnections) and malicious occurrences (e.g., unauthorized access or malware attacks). The proposed system highlights scalability, flexibility in the usage of different device types, and energy efficiency that would be compatible with the constraints of smart home applications. Experiments show that the system can detect anomalies with a high degree of accuracy, deliver low rates of false positives, and significantly enhance the detection of threats at an early stage. This research emphasizes the importance of AI-driven smart home security, while also preparing the path for the invention of more advanced intelligent networking security solutions.

title: A Study of Data-Driven Insights into the Growth and Development of India’s Contemporary Equity Market
authors: Aishwarya Tewari, Nisha Bhandari, Mukund Ramniklal Jakhiya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: This study employs data-driven analysis to examine the evolution of India's stock market since the implementation of the 1991 liberalization policy. At the same time, exploring the role of how foreign direct investment (FDI) and institutional investment (FII) have changed India's equity landscape, how regulatory bodies such as SEBI have been affected through the evolution and adoption of digital technologies and technological innovation, specifically looking into key trends during the COVID-19 pandemic and its impact on the online and market landscape. This will be assessed through data analysis to illustrate the technical development of the Indian equity market, highlighting the sector's progress and future prospects while providing researchers, investors, and policymakers with insights into emerging patterns and business intelligence.

title: GRU-based Voltage Control for Islanded DC Microgrids
authors: Dudekula Ismail, V Chandra Sekhar
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The rapid penetration of renewable energy resources, such as photovoltaic (PV) and fuel cell systems, has significantly transformed modern power networks, leading to the development of decentralized and flexible micro-grids. Among these, DC micro-grids are gaining prominence due to their compatibility with DC-based renewable energy sources and efficient energy management. However, maintaining voltage stability in an islanded DC micro-grid poses significant challenges, particularly when the micro-grid becomes disconnected from the main grid. Islanding can occur intentionally for maintenance, fault isolation, or unintentional events such as grid faults and disturbances. During islanding, the micro-grid loses the voltage reference from the main grid, leading to potential voltage fluctuations and instability, especially under varying load and generation conditions. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an Artificial Neural Network (ANN)-based voltage controller specifically designed for islanded DC micro-grids. The ANN controller continuously learns from system dynamics and adapts its control strategy in real-time, effectively managing voltage variations despite unpredictable changes in power generation and load demands. The proposed controller enhances voltage stability by rapidly compensating for disturbances and maintaining a steady voltage profile. Comprehensive stability analysis, including Bode, Nyquist, and root locus plots, demonstrates the superior performance of the ANN-based voltage controller under various operational scenarios. Simulation results show that the ANN-based controller significantly improves voltage regulation, mitigates transient oscillations, and ensures grid stability during islanded operation. This adaptive and intelligent control strategy provides a reliable solution for maintaining voltage stability in islanded DC micro-grids, contributing to the resilient and sustainable integration of renewable energy resources.

title: Enhancing Gold Price Forecast Accuracy through Feature Engineering and Financial News Sentiment Integration
authors: Rosalinda Abou Mrad, Khalil Chtaiwy, Yaacoub Khalil, Michel Owayjan, Ali Abdallah
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: This research explores an enhanced approach to predicting gold prices by combining machine learning algorithms with real-world news sentiment. The project focuses on generating daily Buy, Sell, or Hold signals and predicting short-term gold price trends using models like LSTM, Prophet, and Linear Regression. To evaluate their performance, the models were trained on gold price data from January 2023 to March 2025 and tested on April 2025. Alongside price-based features, additional indicators such as moving averages, momentum, volatility, and trend signals were used. One of the key contributions of this work is the use of news sentiment— headlines and summaries were analyzed using GPT-4o to assess whether they had a likely positive, negative, or neutral impact on gold. These daily sentiment scores and event flags (e.g., mentions of inflation or geopolitical conflict) were merged with the price data to improve accuracy. Results show that adding sentiment features significantly reduced prediction error. The best-performing model, a linear regression with sentiment input, achieved a very low root mean squared error of $2.35 when forecasting April 2025 prices, showing strong potential for practical use in financial forecasting.

title: A Novel Approach to Detect Speech Impairment Severity Using Multi-Channel Spectrograms
authors: Manisa Manoswini, Biswajit Sahoo, Aleena Swetapadma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid model using self supervised learning and deep convolutional neural networks for the assessment of the severity level of speech impairment. Wav2vec2 embedding technique are used for the generation of pseudo-severity labels using the cosine distance between embeddings for healthy and impaired speeches. Speech signals are converted into three-channel spectrograms, which include log-mel, delta and delta-delta spectral features. The custom convolutional neural network designed for classifying the severity level in binary, three, and four-class classification problems. The experimental results demonstrate over 99 % classification accuracy.

title: Multimodal Integration of Sensors with Computer Vision to Detect and Alert Wild Animal Intrusion
authors: Jefferson David Kingston, V. Adithya, V. Srivatsav, C.V. Santhosh, P. Mercy Rajaselvi Beaulah
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: This paper introduces a novel AI-powered wildlife detection and alert system that integrates YOLOv8 for Object Detection, DeepSORT for real time tracking, and sensor based proximity detection. The proposed approach enables accurate detection and tracking of wild animals in natural environments, particularly in human wildlife conflict zones. The Detection and Tracking of these wild animals are crucial in order to maintain a safe ecosystem and ensure that the animals do not harm the villagers living close by and vice versa. Unlike Traditional methods that rely solely on camera-based detection and motion sensors, this hybrid approach reduces false positives, improves detection in areas of low visibility and estimates the distance precisely. The system provides an alert in the form of SMS to forest officials and small buzzers placed near the villages to warn the villagers in case any animal has been spotted. The outcomes reveal its possibility of real time surveillance, ecological research, and mitigation of human-wildlife conflict which provides a scalable and robust solution of wildlife monitoring.

title: Microfluidic Simulation Framework for Point-of-Care Diagnostics: Modeling Advection-Diffusion-Reaction Transport in Lab-on-Chip Systems
authors: Tashdid Ahmed Shitab, Anika Ibnat Oni, Md. Emam Hossain Emon, Razin Rayan Rahat, Anjuman Ara Mim, Prima Fariya Auishe
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Point-of-care diagnostics (POCD) rely heavily on microfluidic lab-on-chip systems, yet their design process is often hindered by trial-and-error prototyping and inconsistent reproducibility. Efficient diagnostic performance requires a clear understanding of how advection, diffusion, and biochemical reactions jointly govern analyte transport at the microscale. This work presents a lightweight numerical framework for modeling solute behavior in microfluidic channels using the advection–diffusion–reaction equation. A finite-difference solver was applied to a 5 mm × 100 μm channel under physiologically relevant flow conditions, comparing three regimes: diffusion-only, advection + diffusion, and advection + diffusion + first-order reaction. Results show that high Péclet numbers (Pe ≈ 1000) drive plug-like transport with minimal lateral mixing, while Damköhler numbers near unity (Da ≈ 1) produce significant axial decay due to reaction kinetics. Visualizations of concentration fields and breakthrough curves demonstrate how Pe and Da directly influence sensor placement, analyte preservation, and diagnostic sensitivity. The framework provides a transparent and computationally efficient tool for early-stage device design, offering practical guidance for optimizing flow control, channel geometry, and capture-surface behavior in lab-on-chip diagnostics.

title: VM Integrator Using Single VDCC
authors: Devanshu Saxena, Meghana Shrivastava
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: This paper illustrates a efficient minimal component design of voltage-mode Integrator using the active building block, namely Voltage Differencing Current Conveyor (VDCC). The proposed circuit avails only one VDCC element and a single grounded capacitor. The circuit is designed to operate with a supply voltage of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 0.9 \mathrm{V}$</tex>, a bias current of about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$30 \mu \mathrm{A}$</tex>, and an electronically controllable amplitude. The proposed integrator circuit have been verified through PSPICE simulations using CMOS VDCC implemented in 0.18m TSMC technology and also validated using MATLAB evaluations.

title: AI for Drug Repositioning in Rare Diseases
authors: Safa Daoud
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Drug repositioning, the process of identifying new therapeutic uses for already existing drugs, is a cost-effective and time-efficient alternative when compared to drug discovery through traditional means. This approach does not only serve the rare disease but also is the last resort to the drug company when either the patent of a new drug is about to expire and it can no longer be sold at a luxurious price or when it creates a competitive disadvantage in the market because of its high price. Artificial intelligence (AI) has emerged as one of the most important tools of this field. AI, through its power of data analysis, pattern recognition, and predictive modeling, provides the means for searching out a potential drug-disease relationship. The scope of this paper lies in the implementation of AI-driven methodologies via machine learning, natural language processing, and graph-based algorithms to be used in the field of drug repositioning primarily for rare diseases. We investigate a number of data sources including genomic datasets, electronic health records, and biomedical literature, in order to be able to discover hidden connections between already existing drugs and the rare diseases they target. Other problems such as data scarcity, model interpretability, and validation in rare disease scenarios are also considered. Through the combined use of AI and domain expertise, this study aims to shorten the time taken to point out promising drug candidates and ultimately contribute to the metabolic syndrome treatment pipeline by the development of innovative medications that can enhance patient outcomes in rare disease management.

title: Trans-SPAnet: A Unified Transformer-CNN Architecture with Enhanced Boundary Optimization for Polyp Segmentation
authors: Tasneea Zahra, Kalyan Kumar Halder
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Accurate segmentation of anatomical structures in medical images, such as polyps, remains a critical, yet challenging task due to the variability in appearance, low contrast, and irregular boundaries. While U-Net-based architectures demonstrate strong local feature extraction, they often lack the capability of global context modeling. On the other hand, transformer-based models capture long-range dependencies but struggle with local detail preservation, which requires a huge amount of data. To overcome these limitations, we propose a novel hybrid CNN-transformer U-Net architecture that integrates spatial pyramid convolution blocks (SPCB) and spatial pyramid pooling blocks (SPPB) for multi-scale local feature extraction, with a transformer bottleneck for global context understanding. Furthermore, we introduce a boundary-sensitive loss function to enhance performance at object boundaries, a known problem in conventional approaches. Our decoder employs refined transformer fusion modules to reconcile semantic gaps across different scales. We evaluate our model on Kvasir-SEG dataset for polyp segmentation, which performs 2-3% better than existing baseline segmentation models. The results demonstrate that our architecture performs better than existing CNN-only or transformer-only models in medical image segmentation.

title: Explainable Malaria Detection with Efficientnet and XAI: a Human-Centric Diagnostic Approach
authors: Prince Nath, Ritesh Kumar Muduli, Gourab Dandapat, Anuja Kumar Acharya
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: This work presents an Explainable Malaria Detection system that integrates an EfficientNet-based classifier with interactive visual explainability and agentic diagnostic reporting for transparent, clinician-oriented malaria screening. The deployed Streamlit application allows users to compute and visualize pixel-level saliency and SmoothGrad maps on demand, adjust explanation parameters, and download overlay images, offering a fully interactive exploration of model reasoning. In parallel, an embedded large language model (LLM) generates concise, clinician-style diagnostic summaries with a local fallback template for offline use. This combination of XAI visualization, LLM-driven narrative reporting, and a polished, user-friendly interface bridges the gap between deep learning accuracy and clinical interpretability, advancing malaria AI systems toward human-in-the-loop, trustworthy diagnostics rather than opaque automation.

title: Edge-Guided U-Net with Frequency Domain Refinement for Efficient Skin Lesion Segmentation
authors: Aryan Kumar Singh, Md Rakibul Islam Midul, Md Shohan Mia, Priyanshu Kumar Gupta, Md Kayser Ahmed Hridoy, Chowdhury Hasibur Rahaman, Rabul Hussain Laskar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Precise segmentation of skin lesions is crucial to support the diagnosis of skin cancer within computer-aided diagnosis (CAD) systems. Although deep learning-based models like U-Net along with its variants have demonstrated strong segmentation performance, they often require substantial computational and energy resources. In this study, a lightweight and computationally efficient framework for skin lesion segmentation is proposed, leveraging information across the spatial and frequency domains. The proposed model, Edge-Guided U-Net (EGU-Net), integrates edge information directly into the input and incorporates a frequency-domain refinement step based on the discrete cosine transform (DCT). Through this integration, EGU-Net enables improved delineation of lesion boundaries while suppressing high-frequency noise in the predicted masks. The model underwent training and evaluation on the ISIC 2018 dataset, resulting in a Dice score of 87.85%, recall of 90.62%, precision of 89.02%, an intersection over union (IoU) of 80.35% and an accuracy of 95.47%. These results demonstrate that EGU-Net can approximate the performance of more complex architectures while maintaining significantly lower computational complexity. Consequently, EGU-Net establishes a strong baseline for interpretable and efficient pixel-wise skin lesion segmentation under constrained computational settings.

title: Design of a Real-Time High-Gain Microstrip Patch Antenna from 2 GHz to 4 GHz for RF Transceiver Applications
authors: Naman Gupta, Durga Prasad Mishra, Harish Chandra Kumawat
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: This paper presents the design and analysis of a rectangular probe fed microstrip patch antenna operating at 2.4 Ghz within the S-band freqency range of 2-4 GHz frequency band. Its main applications is in weather radar, satellite communication links, and IoT backhaul systems. Traditional waveguide and horn antennas have many limitations to overcome them, this planar structure uses a rectangular patch on an FR-4(epoxy) substrate to achieve a balance between performance and stability. Antenna design and optimization were performed using ANSYS HFSS through parametric Analysis. Important performance parameters including return loss (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S_{11}$</tex>), gain, and radiation patterns were analyzed. The results demonstrate excellent impedance matching across the operating band and stable radiation pattern, reliability and suitability of this low-profile antenna for modern wireless communication platforms.

title: A Hybrid Machine Learning–Driven, Explainable Framework for Competition-Aware and Risk-Sensitive Athlete Nutrition
authors: Sasikanth Yangalasetty, Sai Vamsi Chennupati
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: The paper introduces a Hybrid AI Systems-based, Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) framework to competition conscious and risk conscious athlete nutrition as a part of a safety-critical Decision Support Systems architecture. The system combines the multimodal food knowledge, the modespecific context-sensitive reasoning and the hierarchical decision modeling to produce high-quality individualized dietary proposals based on competition dynamic constraints. RiskSensitive Modeling is added to implement the medical safety limits with underperforming readiness. The multimodal athlete nutrition datasets obtained through experimental assessment showed better predictive reliability with a 0.95 accuracy, F1-score of 0.93 and a lower Risk Violation rate at 3%. The framework has a low Expected Calibration Error of 0.021, or can be sure of probabilistic confidence in the recommendations. The explainability consistency stands at 0.92, which makes valid the consistent and transparent attribution of decisions. Feasibility in real-time is established and end-to-end latency is 52 ms on average. The findings support the efficiency of the integration of Hybrid AI Systems, Context-Aware Decision Systems, and Personalized Health Informatics to provide interpretable, risk-conscious, and competition-wise nutrition intelligence to high-performance athletics settings.

title: A Machine Learning-Based Disease Prediction and Healthcare Assistance System Using Feed-Forward Neural Network
authors: Debanjan Pathak, Hitesh Mohapatra, Soumya Ranjan Mishra, Subhadip Pramanik
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: In recent years, with the introduction of advanced machine learning-based diagnostic approaches in the healthcare industry, intelligent healthcare systems have become increasingly important for early disease detection and providing initial diagnostic support. This work presents a machine learning powered web application with the functionality of predicting probable diseases a person may be suffering from according to the description provided by them and further helping them connect with doctors, nearby hospitals and pharmacies. A FeedForward Neural Network (MLPClassifier) was trained using backpropagation on a dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4, 9 6 1}$</tex> patient records containing 132 symptom features and 41 disease classes. After pre-processing which included Boolean normalization, one-hot encoding, median imputation, feature scaling and duplicate record removal, the dataset was reduced to 304 unique samples. The proposed model achieved 98.36% test accuracy and macro F1-score of 0.9707, with cross-validation accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.9967 \pm 0.0066$</tex>. In addition to disease prediction the platform integrates geolocation services for locating nearby hospital and pharmacy, emergency contacts, and doctor recommendations. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the model in predicting diseases based on symptoms, making it a valuable step toward hybrid machine learning-assisted healthcare and early diagnosis support.

title: Anomaly Detection in BETH Dataset Using Bi-LSTM for Cybersecurity
authors: Iman Akour, Said Salloum, Amaal Al-Amawi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Cybersecurity threats are constantly evolving, requiring strong anomaly detection methods. Highly imbalanced datasets in cybersecurity make it hard for typical machine learning models to detect rare anomalys, resulting in low recall. We evaluate the effectiveness of a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (Bi-LSTM) model, trained on data from normal and anomalous cases, on the BETH dataset. For class imbalance, we use SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique) and class weighting. It reaches a final accuracy of 90.49%, proving that it learns in a good way normal data; but it fails the detection of anomaly data, with a 15% recall. Demonstrated that additional optimization (e.g., attention mechanism, customized hybrid models, and tuning of detection threshold) is required to enhance model recognition capability of cyber threat detection. These contributions inform on potential opportunities for improving anomaly detection in real world cybersecurity settings.

title: Implementing Secure API Gateways: A Case Study of AWS API Gateway and Spring Cloud Gateway
authors: Saket Chaudhari, Rajkumar Modake, Sharath Reddy Venna, Pratik Badri, Anusha Nerella, Piyush Ranjan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: APIs are the lifeblood of modern software systems, allowing service interaction. Securing user interaction with an API is vital, however, as its function in manipulating sensitive data and providing critical functionalities are at risk. API gateways forms a layer on which a centralized security element can be created with the following basic features: authentication, access control, request rate limiting, and request monitoring. In this context, the research involves a case study that focuses on implementing secure API gateway case, namely, AWS API Gateway and Spring Cloud Gateway. The security mechanisms are studied and compared on the basis of their capability in authenticating through OAuth, JWT, and API keys, as well as their capabilities in traffic management, threat detection, and auditing. Deployment limitations and scaling aspects, plus integrations in the cloud models, are also part of the study. From the findings, key differences emerged between AWS API Gateway-as-a-service, a fully managed cloud-native solution, and Spring Cloud Gateway, which is an open-source alternative designed for microservices. On fine-grained security access control and seamless integration with AWS services, AWS API Gateway seems to be built-in security focused. In contrast, Spring Cloud Gateway includes flexibility, customizability, and substantial control over the API security policies. Performance trade-offs, cost factors, and best practices are also discussed: develop and choose a gateway on the basis of security and business justification. This considers security in APIs not just a technical concern but a strategic business decision that demands proper planning and execution. By contrasting two of the most frequently used API gateways, we show how to increase the API security, scalability, and flexibility against evolving threats in modern cloud environments.

title: HAF-Net: Hybrid CNN–Transformer Model with Multi-Head Attention for Accurate and Explainable Pneumonia Diagnosis from Chest X-Rays
authors: Progga Parmita Roy, Shionty Ghosh, Amit Chakraborty, Md Shakib Ahamed
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Pneumonia is a widespread and serious respiratory infection which requires immediate and accurate treatment. In this study, a hybrid deep learning architecture is proposed on a base of Multi-Head Attention Pooling (MHAP) with the use of pretrained CNNs (DenseNet121, ResNet152) and a novel multitask transformer model architecture called Dual Intent and Entity Transformer (DeiT). The proposed HAF-Net model showed good diagnostic results with the test accuracy of 93.68% and the Area under the Receiver Operating Characteristic Curve (AUC-ROC) of 0.99. Attention mechanisms were used to locate clinically relevant regions to improve interpretability, and a report generator based on LSTM generated initial radiology-style summaries that can assist with the making of clinical decisions faster and more uniformly. The structured radiology-style reports enhance interpretability and enhance the possibilities of efficient clinical decision-making.

title: TalkWell: Natural Language Processing-Assisted Deep Learning Model for Real-Time Mental Health Monitoring
authors: Krishna Goswami, Krisha Raval, Arpan Gadhiya, Rajat Chaudhary, Prajakta Sadaria
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: This paper introduces a truly novel way of determining mental health problems like anxiety, feelings of sadness, and stress in chat and audio communication, on the fly in online chats. The proposed model involves natural language processing (NLP) and voice analysis to understand the content as well as emotions expressed in text, in contrast to ordinary chatbots which rely only on keyword spotting. In this research, the system model operates in two phases; initially, the model interprets textual data for psychological signs using powerful language models, including but not limited to BERT models, and machine learning algorithms such as K-Nearest Neighbours, Convolutional Neural Networks, Support Vector Machines, as well as Long Short-Term Memory networks. Secondly, Deep Learning algorithms are adopted to extract the emotional component from the speech clips. This integrated proposed model performs a real-time mental health risk score, which can alert caregivers or identify appropriate support providers. Lastly, the performance analysis of the model is added as a browser plugin or linked into current chat platforms to allow for early identification of mental health challenges, raise awareness, and provide timely support to prevent catastrophic mental health emergencies.

title: TriTree-VoteNet: An Interpretable Soft Voting Ensemble Framework for Liver Disease Diagnosis
authors: Mehazabin Hossain Lamiha, Md. Ehsanul Haque, Jeba Maliha, Md Naimul Islam, Sabrina Khan Mim, Md Asif Khan
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Liver disease is a severe health issue that in most cases grows without any signs thus, early diagnosis is important in treatment. Machine learning has demonstrated the promise in aiding clinical decision-making, including the detection of complex trends among patient data. Nevertheless, most current methods have problems with imbalance in classes, poor interpretability, and the absence of computational efficiency assessment. This paper presents the TriTree VoteNet, a softvoting ensemble framework, which is optimized with grid search to predict liver disease. The framework uses a combination of dual-stage feature selection based on One-Way ANOVA and Genetic Algorithms with a hybrid data balancing approach that incorporates GAN and SMOTE Tomek. Random Forest, Extra Trees, and XGBoost are the three best-performing individual baseline models, which are combined into the ensemble to enhance predictive performance. Cross-validation, 95% confidence intervals, Matthews Correlation Coefficient, and an inference time analysis are used to evaluate the model on a publicly available liver disease dataset, yielding a test accuracy of 99.74%, crossvalidation accuracy of 99.78%, Matthews Correlation Coefficient of 0.9935, precision of 0.9982, recall of 0.9953, and a total test inference time of 0.3279 seconds. These findings indicate that systematic feature selection, hybrid data balancing, and explainable ensemble learning can be used to improve liver disease prediction with moderate complexity and realistic feasibility.

title: Locomotive Scheduling Optimization Based on Bio-Inspired Algorithms
authors: Adarsh Ramachandran, Swapnesh S., Rekha K. S.
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: This paper presents an optimization technique for locomotive scheduling using the bio-inspired Genetic Algorithm (GA) and Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO). The locomotive scheduling problem is an important problem in railway operations management. As the cost of locomotives is high, reducing the fleet size significantly saves capital and operational costs for railways. Manual locomotive scheduling is a challenging task due to the scale of operations and the constraints involved. We have done the scheduling using both GA and PSO, and a comparative analysis is also presented. Robustness of the algorithm used for optimization is checked using Monte-Carlo analysis.

title: A Review of Data-Driven Approach to Congenital Fetal Heart Disease Detection Using Machine Learning and Deep Learning Models
authors: Seemakurthi Jaswanth, K. Kavitha
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Congenital fetal heart disease (CFHD) is one of the most prevalent congenital anomalies and a leading contributor to neonatal morbidity and mortality. Early identification during pregnancy plays a crucial role in improving perinatal outcomes and enabling timely clinical intervention. The new developments in machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) applications for detecting fetal and congenital heart diseases (CHD) enable 2D echocardiography to undergo full analysis automatically, and cardiac chambers to be segmented by experts, or ventricular volumes and ejection fraction to be accurately determined. Automated pipelines incorporating view classification as well as multi-vessel segmentation and feature extraction have played a crucial role in the detection and identification of congenital defects, including ASD, VSD, and complex heart defects, in fetal ultrasound. This research review provides a comprehensive analysis of datadriven approaches employed in prenatal screening and diagnosis of CFHD, emphasizing imaging-based assessment, cardiac structure segmentation, multi-view analysis, and physiological signal interpretation. In addition, the review discusses the impact of image quality control and standardized performance evaluation metrics on screening accuracy. Emerging trends in multi-view fetal echocardiography, video-based analysis, and integrated diagnostic pipelines show promise in reducing dependence on operator expertise and expanding access to prenatal cardiac screening, particularly in resource-limited settings. The review concludes by outlining current limitations, ethical considerations, and future directions required for the effective clinical translation of scalable and reliable prenatal heart disease detection systems.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning-Based Edge Computing System with Multi-Vital Sensing for Health Monitoring
authors: Agniva Mukherjee, Sayantam Giri, Ronald Julian, S. Kolangiammal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: The convergence of Internet of Things (IoT) and Deep Learning (DL) is transforming the contemporary healthcare system by providing a real-time health monitoring and early disease prediction. This paper introduces the design of a smart hybrid disease prediction system that integrates physiological measurements collected from IoT-based sensors and user symptom inputs for effective disease identification. The proposed system is designed and developed using a Raspberry Pi microcontroller that is interfaced with a MAX30102 SpO2 sensor for the measurement of vital physiological parameters such as heart rate of the person and the person's blood oxygen saturation levels. The model is trained using symptom datasets for disease prediction on Deep Neural Network (DNN) model, and Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model is used to examine the waveform data from sensors for the evaluation of the patient's physiological status. The hybrid system improves the accuracy of disease prediction by integrating symptom-driven classification with real-time vital analysis. The experimental results show an average prediction accuracy of about 90%, thus proving the efficacy of the system for remote health monitoring and preliminary diagnosis. The system gives disease information and preventive guidelines, thus acting as a useful and intelligent tool for next-generation smart healthcare systems.

title: Lung Cancer Data Feature Selection using Improved Grey Wolf Optimization with Learning Rate Adaptation (IGWO-LRA) in Data Mining
authors: N. Malathi, A. Prakash
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The increasing global incidence of lung cancer necessitates improved early diagnosis and predictive modeling. While high-dimensional biomedical datasets offer valuable insights, they often contain noisy, redundant, or irrelevant features that compromise model performance and interpretability. This paper presents an Improved Grey Wolf Optimization with Learning Rate Adaptation (IGWO-LRA) for feature selection in lung cancer data mining. Unlike dimensionality reduction techniques like Principal Component Analysis (PCA), our method preserves original feature interpretability while effectively selecting the most discriminative biomarkers. The proposed IGWO-LRA enhances conventional Grey Wolf Optimization through adaptive learning rate mechanisms and opposition-based learning, addressing critical challenges of premature convergence and local optima stagnation. Experimental results demonstrate superior performance in selecting optimal feature subsets that significantly improve prediction accuracy while reducing computational complexity. The framework proves particularly effective for structured, non-image lung cancer datasets, offering medical professionals transparent and actionable insights. Comparative evaluations show substantial improvements in classification metrics, underscoring IGWO-LRA's potential to advance precision medicine in oncology.

title: FuzionNet: Fuzzy-Enhanced VGG16 for Weed and Crop Classification
authors: M. Prasannakumar, P Anusha Devi, Muthupandi M, Surendran S, Vanitha L, M. Revathi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Sustainable farming depends on the classification of weeds and crops. Although CNNs are very effective in image classification, their performance is usually constrained by the availability of datasets. This is tackled by FuzionNet which integrates deep learning, fuzzy logic and superior data augmentation methods. It provides data consistency based on real-life datasets and extensive preprocessing, whereas LeafGAN increases the adaptability of new situations. FuzionNet is able to utilize uncertainty and noise in agricultural images because of the combination of expertise through fuzzy rules, which results in a strong classification. The Fuzzy-Enhanced VGG16 architecture combines both fuzzy logic and deep learning, and it performs exceptionally well with 98.45% accuracy, better than state-of-the-art and pre-trained models. The proposed approach supports sustainable weed management, enhances crop productivity, and promotes environmentally friendly agricultural practices.

title: Design and Analysis of a Reduced-Switch Dual-Input DC-DC Converter for PV-Powered Homes
authors: Lavanya A, Divya Navamani J, Kaushal Chakraborty
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: In this paper, a dual input DC-DC converter is proposed, focusing on rooftop solar-powered domestic applications. It has the capability of combining two input sources, as well as achieving an improved voltage gain when compared to the traditional DC-DC boost converter. RSDI was proposed as the dual input DC-DC converter by incorporating auxilary switch with a source cell into the traditional Luo converter configuration. The operational analysis of the proposed RSDI DC-DC converter is also explained. Unlike the traditional DC-DC converters, the proposed dual input structure has only one path of the source network, thus eliminating the chances of brownout occurring due to source switchover. To examine the performance of the proposed DC-DC converter, the efficiency analysis of the DC-DC converter is conducted. The validity of the proposed 100 W RSDI converter prototype is proved through simulation and experimental results

title: A LoRa Based IoT System for Precision Monitoring and Smart Irrigation in Rose Cultivation
authors: Pavan Kumar P, Koushic N, Manoj Krishna R, Diana Emerald Aasha S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
