abstract: A LoRa-based Smart Rose Plant Monitoring System with automatic irrigation for precision agriculture is presented in this study. Sensors check the soil's moisture, temperature, humidity, light intensity, and pH. These things can all affect how roses grow and bloom. LoRa technology sends data over 1 to 5 km with very little power, which is great for farmers in the country. A water pump turns on when the soil moisture level drops below a certain point, which saves water. A cloud platform lets you check sensor data in real time using web or mobile apps. Smart agriculture moves forward with real-time monitoring and automated control in a scalable, cost-effective way that works for both tiny gardens and big commercial farms.

title: Toward Robust AI Agents: A Closed-Loop Task Planning–Execution–Feedback Framework for Open Scenarios
authors: Ning Lyu
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Background: In open environments, AI agents often suffer from insufficient robustness and verifiability, primarily due to target drift, tool uncertainty, and data distribution variations. Consequently, their reliability and verifiability remain insufficiently realized. Methods: A closed-loop mechanism of “Planning-Execution-Feedback” (PEF) was constructed. The model was constructed as a risk-sensitive POMDP model, combining the task graph with the safety threshold. Counterfactual gain allocation (IPS/DR) and off-policy evaluation (BCa CI, Holm - Bonferroni) were used to update the policy. Temperature and Dirichlet calibration strategies were used to reduce the ECE evaluation value. Results: In the specific set of NYC-311 (2024) subsets, the PEF test results for the NYC-311 (2024) subset were ideal, with a success rate of 83.3 %, a latency of 903 milliseconds, a hallucination rate of 4.0 %, a violation rate of 7.1 %, and an ECE index of 0.047. This system outperformed the Planner+Executor by 7.0 percentage points, while maintaining the same latency performance. The Holm-corrected p-value was less than 0.0001. Even in the face of high disturbances, the success rate remained at 78 %, and the cost level remained unchanged. Conclusion: The application of closed-loop technology effectively improves the robustness of the system and achieves Pareto optimality in efficiency. Even in the face of growth in scale and tool complexity, it can maintain stable expansion. This system design is suitable for implementation in open scenarios.

title: Efficient Stock Market Forecasting Through Hybrid Neural Architecture: Integrating Parameter Transfer Learning with Multi-Modal Feature Synthesis
authors: Sayantan Mazumder, Adyan Ali, Akil Ahmed Choudhury, Saurabh Bilgaiyan, Chiranjib Parida
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: Financial markets are very volatile and financial price prediction is affected in two ways: Deep models such as LSTM and Transformer require a lot of hyper-parameter tuning and market data is not uniform and consists of different datasets such as market data, technical data and macro data, as well as sentiments. To address this challenge, we propose a hybrid approach that optimizes parameters from lightweight ANNs to larger models, thereby reducing both training and cost, and combines several feature extraction streams: CNN, which is good for detecting local patterns, LSTM, which is good for modeling sequential data, and Transformers, which are good for longterm dependencies. In the course of the research, LSTM, CNN, GRU, variants of CNN-LSTM, CNN-BiLSTM, Transformer, and several tunings were considered. The simplified Temporal Fusion Transformer (TFT) provides the best performance and the lowest RMSE at 3.2669 and is low by 80%, which shows the potential of theoretically accurate and computationally efficient models for practical market forecasting.

title: A Smart Vision-Guided Autonomous System for Safe and Efficient Coconut Scraping
authors: Rohini Dasgupta, Areen Paresh Churi, Advay Shukla, R. Jansi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: This paper discusses a robot which autonomously scrapes coconuts. The coconut scraping robot has an integrated vision system and force feedback control system to allow it to determine the size and location of the coconut and to receive feedback from the scraping process. Once the robot knows that it has scraped enough from a coconut, an adaptive spiral trajectory is ready to be programmed into the robot before starting the scraping process. The robot will use PID control through a load cell to perform real-time force regulation and maintain a constant penetration depth during scraping to prevent damage to the coconut material. The robot records vibration through an accelerometer and captures the features of the vibrations using features extracted from both the time and frequency domains. These features are analyzed using an Isolation Forest model for anomaly detection to ensure that the robot can operate safely throughout the entire coconut scraping process. Overall, the coconut scraping robot has a hierarchical structure, whereby the high-level perception and anomaly analysis is performed on a Raspberry Pi/laptop, while the low-level motion and force control is done by an Arduino microcontroller. Performance logistics demonstrate that the coconut scraping robot accurately estimated the geometric shape of the coconut, and due to the implementation of a multiple-sensor approach to determining if there was any anomaly detected during the coconut scrapping process, the coconut scraping robot is performing effective, reliable, and robust methods to monitor the coconut robot's safety.

title: Design and Performance Evaluation of a Compact Multi-Port Metasurface Antenna for Sub-Terahertz Applications
authors: Parnapalli Sanjay Kumar, Parnapalli Sanjay Kumar, Jakki Vasanthasena, Talari Sivakumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: In this study, a compact multi-port Meta Surface Antenna for sub-terahertz communication and sensing is proposed. In this design, the periodic pentagonal array of the Meta Surface Cell is introduced for the improvement of gain, directivity, and impedance bandwidth for the frequency range of 40-220 GHz. A 4×4 Multi-Port Feed Network is implemented to feed the Meta Surface for efficient radiation. The simulation of the proposed design is performed by full wave EM analysis. The proposed design illustrates a maximum realized gain of 11.82 dB at 62.73 GHz frequency and stable radiation patterns for the higher sub-terahertz frequencies such as 157.11 GHz. In S-parameter analysis, the minimum S11 = −19.3 dB at 90.6 GHz frequency having a return loss of less than −40 dB at 157 GHz. Radiation patterns demonstrate directive broadside beams with Meta surface-controlled field distribution. The proposed antenna provides a promising low- profile solution for nextgeneration sub-THz systems.

title: Digital Meme Forensics: A Multimodal Transformer Framework for Offensive Content Detection on MultiOFF
authors: Vignesh S, Tamil Selvan V P, Srinivasan R, Nikkath Bushra S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: With the rise of memes on social media, it has become harder to moderate content, especially when the combination of text and images makes something offensive. This paper presents a multimodal approach for identifying offensive memes utilizing the MultiOFF dataset. It uses DistilBERT to understand the text and DeiT (Data-efficient Image Transformer) to understand the images. It combines the two by using methods like concatenation, bilinear fusion, attention, gated and crossmodal attention. Experiments demonstrate that the integration of text and image features significantly outperforms their individual use, as evidenced by the DistilBERT+DeiT attention fusion model, which attained an accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 6. 5 1 \%}$</tex> and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 5. 9 5 \%}$</tex>. And with the rise of meme culture in social media, it is important to regulate those activities as this may sometimes cause negative effects among the audience also. These results show that it's important to know how words and pictures are related in order to find offensive content in memes.

title: Virtualization in Cloud Network Security Architecture: A Comparative Analysis
authors: Joud Alhodaithy, Nouf Mohamed, Jaidaa Hafez, Mahnoor Iftikhar, Abul Bashar
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Virtualization has become the foundation of modern cloud computing, allowing for scalable, efficient, and flexible infrastructure. However, it also brings in challenges in network security and stability. This study investigates the importance of virtualization in cloud network security architectures, giving an overview of the virtualization ecosystem and its crucial role in resource isolation and communication. This paper articulates the security implications of virtualized settings, with an emphasis on network stability issues and hypervisor vulnerabilities. To assess the practical impact of these topologies on network performance, a quantitative analysis was conducted using three major cloud service providers: Amazon Web Services (AWS), Microsoft Azure and Google Cloud Platform (GCP). For analyzing network resilience in virtualized environments, a methodology based on 500 ping tests was used, which measured critical performance metrics such as latency, jitter, and packet loss. The research draws on these findings to identify significant performance differentiators and explores the trade-offs between virtualization benefits and security threats. The paper concludes by summarizing the comparative findings and suggesting future possibilities for hardening virtualized network systems against rising threats.

title: Entropy-Driven Multi-Scale Convolutional Neural Networks for Image-based Malware Classification
authors: K Malathi, Md Afnan Faheem K, Manoj Kumar M, Hamsaananth A N
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: This study presents a novel and robust framework for malware classification by integrating grayscale image transformation with an optimized Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), addressing critical gaps in real-time threat detection. Unlike conventional methods, our approach introduces a unique hierarchical feature extraction mechanism that captures intricate malware patterns with unparalleled precision. The proposed system leverages a large and diverse malware image dataset, meticulously preprocessed using advanced augmentation and normalization techniques to enhance model generalizability.A key innovation is our custom-designed CNN architecture, which combines multi-scale convolutional layers with adaptive pooling to efficiently extract discriminative features while minimizing computational overhead. Additionally, we incorporate a dynamic class-weight balancing technique to mitigate data imbalance, significantly improving detection accuracy for underrepresented malware classes. The model’s performance is rigorously evaluated using metrics such as accuracy, recall, F1-score, and confusion matrix analysis, achieving 93% classification accuracy—a substantial improvement over existing methods.Our framework not only demonstrates superior generalization across diverse malware variants but also sets a new benchmark for real-time processing, making it a scalable solution for practical cybersecurity applications. This work represents a significant advancement in deep learning-based malware detection, offering a faster, more accurate, and adaptable system capable of combating evolving cyber threats.

title: Federated Learning for Rural Healthcare Diagnosis
authors: Martala Abiram Reddy, Shaik Mohammed Humza, Usama Abdur Rahman
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Federated learning has become a potential paradigm of privacy-preserving machine learning in a healthcare setting where sensitive patient information could not be centrally distributed. This paper introduces a patient monitoring system that is federated learning-based and applied with a distributed edge-cloud platform with the use of classical machine learning algorithms, i.e., the Support Vector Machine (SVM) and the K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN). The suggested system embraces organized electronic health documents consisting of demographic, clinical, administrative, billing, and time characteristics of patient admission and discharge. Categorical encoding, feature normalization, temporal feature extraction, and missing value manipulation are all data preprocessing methods that are executed locally at an edge node. The training of the models takes place in isolation at the edges, so that the raw patient information does not exit the source and this maintains the privacy of the data and guarantees compliance with regulations. The cloud server only receives model parameters and then does federated aggregation to create a global model that is later sent back to edge nodes to undergo continuous refining steps. SVM can be used to deal with high-dimensional structured healthcare data effectively, and KNN is a light weight adaptive solution that fits well in edge cases. Experimental findings show that the given framework is capable of scalable, privacy-conscious, and efficient patient monitoring, which is why it can be applied to real-time healthcare analytics in distributed healthcare institutions.

title: DMLO-6G: A Distributed Machine Learning Orchestration Framework for Ultra-Reliable Low-Latency QoS Prediction Across Heterogeneous Network Slices
authors: Vinod Kumar R, Velmurugan P, Sree Dharma A V
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The fast development of 6G networks requires ultra-reliable and low-latency Quality-of-Service (QoS) forecasting and various network slices. The work presents a design of a Distributed Machine Learning Orchestrator (DMLO) that optimizes learning on the edge, fog machine, and cloud layer and delays the provision of timely and accurate QoS information. DMLO integrates four novel algorithms, namely Adaptive Slice-Aware Federated Optimizer (ASFO) to dynamically aggregate models, Latency-Constrained Gradient Pruning (LCGP) to minimize communication overhead, Cross-Tier QoS Fusion Network (CT-QFN) to fuse features among multiple layers and Real-Time Slice Drift Detector (RTSDD) to observe sudden fluctuations in services. Assignments to learning tasks are made based on slice sensitivity, mobility patterns and predicted congestion to be able to update models resource-efficiently and responsively. Inter slices simulations of eMBB, URLLC and mMTC show better accuracy of QoS prediction, lower inference latency, and resource usage. The findings confirm the feasibility of distributed intelligent orchestration as a feasible remedy to improving slice-level performance and reliability on 6G networks in the future.

title: Switched-Capacitor Based Bidirectional DC–DC Converter with Enhanced Efficiency for EV and Renewable Systems
authors: G. Melviah, R. Ramaprabha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: In this work, a MBC using the S-C technology for an electric vehicle system has been provided. The proposed topology is a three-port system with the ability to support buck and boost operations. This makes it possible to handle power transfer among multiple sources as well as the load. The proposed topology attains high voltage gain with lower switch voltage stress with fewer components compared to the traditional topology. The proposed system is validated for its effectiveness as a multi-source energy manager.

title: A Robust Deep Learning Approach for Automated Cardiovascular Disease Diagnosis Using ECG Image Analysis
authors: M D. Allama Shiam Shanto, Md. Ferdous Jaman, Sadia Jannat Mitu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Cardiovascular disease (CVD) continues to be one of the leading causes of death worldwide, with approximately 20 million deaths taking place in 2015. Studies show that deaths from CVD will amount to 23.6 million annually by 2030. Early recognition is vital to improve patient survival rates, particularly in the health system where there are limited resources and insufficiency of experienced professionals. The objective of this study was to develop an automated machine learning technique which would classify paper electrograph (ECG) recordings into four electrocardiographically different classes, namely acute myocardial infarction (MI), previous MI, irregular cardiac rhythm, and normal cardiac function. Using the broad ECG Images DataSet on Kaggle with cardiac patient data (i.e., 11,148 images processed and augmented) three different convolutional neural network (CNN) models were tested: VGG16, MobileNetV2 and InceptionV3. Of those VGG16 gave the best result overall by balanced performance measures, achieving 98.71% classification accuracy and F1 score 0.9853 in respect of results. This was a marked improvement when comparing results obtained with the InceptionV3 and MobileNetV2 models especially for classes of cases less well represented within these data, such as previous myocardial infarction cases. This is encouraging for the use of systems based on CNNs allowing rapid and effective interpretation of ECGs thereby guiding rapid diagnosis and reducing the risk of fatal outcome.

title: Nano-Stream: Quantized Online 1D-CNN for Adaptive Traffic Classification and Dynamic QoS in SDN
authors: Varun Kumaran V, Sivarjun Aravind, R Mohandas, Kandavel N, Lydia Shammi W, Karthikeyan P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: The rapid development of end-to-end encryption protocols, of which TLS 1.3 is a prime representative, has dramatically reduced the efficacy of traditional traffic classification methodologies based on Deep Packet Inspection. Recent works have shown that Deep Learning (DL) models can accurately classify such encrypted traffic. However, state-of-the-art solutions rely on computationally intensive architectures-such as ResNet or LSTM, which are ill-suited for resource-constrained edge routers, these static models are liable to concept drift due to dynamic network patterns, thus leading to rapid performance degradation. This paper presents Nano-Stream, a lightweight, self-adaptive traffic classification framework for Software-Defined Networking (SDN) environments. A quantized 1D-Convolutional Neural Network (1D-CNN) is deployed, which was trained from scratch using 32-bit floating-point representations and then quantized in an 8-bit integer format, reducing inference latency by 41.5% while retaining competitive accuracy. More importantly, our framework leverages a drift-aware replay buffer that enables online finetuning, allowing it to adapt at runtime without experiencing catastrophic forgetting. Empirical evaluations show that Nano-Stream achieves an accuracy of 89.4%, with an inference latency of only 1.17 ms per flow, thereby allowing the direct enforcement of dynamic QoS rules directly on the switch.

title: Multimodal Analysis of Family Interactions and Children's Psychology
authors: Yingtong Aia, Libin Yang, Liwen Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI in Education Technology and Applications (AIETA)
abstract: Family interaction is a crucial environmental factor influencing children's psychological development. Traditional assessment methods have drawbacks such as strong subjectivity and insufficient dynamic capture. To achieve an objective and precise correlation between family interaction and children's psychological states, this paper proposes a family interaction analysis scheme based on multimodal data. Firstly, a multimodal data collection system covering vision (facial expressions, body movements), audio (voice emotions, dialogue interaction), and text (dialogue transcription) is constructed to obtain natural interaction data from 120 families and complete preprocessing and annotation; Secondly, a Transformer-based multimodal fusion model with attention mechanism is designed to efficiently extract family interaction features; Finally, a correlation model integrating the fused features with children's psychological states (such as happiness, anxiety, concentration, etc.) is established. Experimental results show that the accuracy of children's psychological state recognition by the proposed multimodal fusion model reaches 89.7%, which is 6.2% to 8.5% higher than the optimal single-modal model (83.5% for visual modality and 81.2% for audio modality), and through SHAP value analysis, five core influencing features such as the frequency of parent-child eye contact and the proportion of positive language are clarified. This research provides objective quantitative technical support for children's mental health assessment, and can assist in family parenting guidance and early intervention.

title: Drone-based GPR with YOLOv10 for Intelligent Mapping and Detection of Subsurface Pile Foundations
authors: Dhruvkumar Jayantilal Patel
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: A study was done to develop an intelligent framework for detecting and mapping the underground pile foundations by employing a drone-mounted Ground Penetrating Radar (GPR) system combined with the YOLOv10 deep learning model. This innovative method takes advantage of the drone's mobility and aerial coverage for efficiently surveying huge construction or infrastructure sites; thus, it captures high-resolution subsurface data. The GPR system gathers the real-time radar signals reflected from the objects, which are then processed and converted into visual records. The records become the input for the YOLOv10 model, which has been trained to identify and categorize the features of pile foundations with a high level of confidence. The use of YOLOv10 means that fast object detection and localization in complex radar images are possible, thus the automation and the reliability of pile foundation mapping are improved. Besides that, the proposed system allows users to avoid ground surveys, and therefore, it is less time-consuming, less expensive, and less labor-intensive. This method is a breakthrough in geotechnical monitoring, and it is also very helpful in the implementation of smart construction practices as it connects advanced sensing and deep learning technologies.

title: Smart Fabric Design and Defect Inspection System
authors: Dineshkumar Thangaraju, Asokan R, Balamurugan R, Abishiek R, Adithya P
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: In the textile industry, ensuring fabric quality is crucial to maintaining product standards and reducing waste. This project aims to develop an automated system for fabric design verification and defect detection using image processing with interactive web application. High- resolution images of cloth fabrics are captured and analyzed to ensure consistency of design and identify defects such as tears, holes, stains, mis- weaves, etc., and their count. Techniques like template matching, edge detection and convolutional neural networks (CNNs) are employed to detect and classify damage, while Structural Similarity Index (SSIM) is used for pattern comparison. The system provides a real- time detection interface using OpenCV and Stream lit, making it suitable for textile manufacturing industries. The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach significantly improves accuracy, consistency and efficiency in fabric inspection. The system enhances the quality control in textile manufacturing, reduces manual inspection errors and minimizes fabric wastage, offering an efficient solution for fabric inspection across various industries.

title: Social Media Bot Detection with Multimodal Sentiment Analysis
authors: Varun HG, Vishnu Venkatesh Vasista, Ashwin PM, Yamanuri Shrishail Nad, Swati Pratap Jagdale
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Social bots are a critical threat to the integrity of digital information, user privacy, and public safety. These automated accounts commit deceptive practices, manipulate public opinion, and pave the way for fraud. Most of the conventional detection methods are based on either account metadata or simple content analysis; nuanced, multi-faceted understanding in identifying sophisticated bots mimicking human behavior is missing. This paper presents an advanced bot detection framework that relies on multimodal sentiment analysis to overcome such limitations. The system implemented here processes the textual and visual content of social media postings in a unique way. It uses a cutting-edge, multilingual XLM-RoBERTa language model for the task of text sentiment classification, while fine-tuned ResNet-50 performs the extraction of visual sentiment. A novel mechanism fuses such signals in an intelligent way into comprehensive emotional profiles, which are analyzed together with behavioral metadata for the detection of inconsistencies typical of automated accounts. This has achieved accuracies as high as 75 percent for text and 75 percent for image sentiment analysis. Our framework facilitates the integration and correlation of emotional cues from multiple modalities, which can be more resilient and accurate against evolving bot strategies-a meaningful step beyond the conventional single-modality detection systems.

title: Smart ID Card Detection and Authentication Using Deep Learning and Computer Vision
authors: D. Vinoparkavi, V. Mythili, Dharun Raj R, Deepika Y, Nithwin V M
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: ID card verification has become operational bottleneck in banking and education sectors etc. Manual verification of ID for example, takes 2-5 minutes for each person and because of this the queues during peak hours can get very long. In these conditions the percentage of error could get as high as 15-20%. The following idea is for an automated system for the mentioned above problem using a multi-stage framework which uses YOLOv5 for detecting ID cards in real-time Tesseract OCR with a template-based method for text extraction and face recognition for identity matching of persons. This system will operate on generic computing hardware and does not require any special device. Experimental results show that the system detects with an accuracy of 96.8% extracts text with precision of 94.2% and has a speed of 25-30 frames per second. Security features developed in this system have been able to detect fraud from the printed or digital reproductions with an efficacy of 85.8%. This solution in the paper reduces the spell of verification from minutes to just a few seconds and with high accuracy without being sensitive for the type of cards and light conditions.

title: Multiscale Deformable Attention Enhanced YOLO Framework for Cervical Cancer Cell Classification
authors: Jheelam Mondal, Rajdeep Chatterjee, Mahendra Kumar Gourisaria
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: Cervical cancer is the fourth most common cancer globally and one of the most common cancers in women. Pap smear tests make it possible to find and screen cervical cancer early. The smear analysis which is done by experts, is laborious, takes a long time and is likely to make mistakes. The lack of resources and highly qualified professionals causes a low output of cervical cancer detection procedures in mainly developing and under-developed countries. Researchers are inspired to use deep learning (DL), which has recently gained popularity to diagnose cervical cancer. This research employs a hybrid convolutional neural network (CNN) and Transformer framework, wherein convolutional elements (YOLOv5 encoder and Spatial Pyramid Pooling Fast layer) facilitate fast local feature extraction, while a multiscale deformable attention neck incorporates transformerstyle global contextual modeling. This integration improves representation learning by merging local spatial accuracy with adaptable long-range dependency modeling. Our proposed model achieves a test accuracy value of 96.77% using the SIPAKMED benchmark data set.

title: Design and Analysis of a Low-Cost Smart Parcel Receiving System with Dual-Layer Authentication
authors: S.N. Kumbar, Rahul J. Teradal, Radhika Kiran Lohar, Muskan Abdul Latif Dhalayat, Soumya Gadag, Ganesh H Gadiwaddar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: E-commerce's rapid expansion has significantly raised the need for safe and dependable methods of parcel delivery, especially in the case of unattended deliveries that security breaches, losses, and denial of delivery still constitute the major risks. To mitigate these issues, a design and analysis solution are given in this document for a budget-friendly intelligent parcel receiving system with dual-layer authentication that can/have the ability to facilitate delivery safety and energize the handling of operations in residential environments. The main controller of the proposed system is an Arduino Uno that will use sensor data to decide, perform authentication, control the lock, and manage communication. The system is turned on by an IR sensor detecting the delivery person, while a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4 \times 3$</tex> keypad is used for OTP-based authentication to allow a safe parcel placement. A servo motor is used for automatic locking and unlocking, and a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$16 \times 2$</tex> LCD is there to give the system's real-time feedback to the user. The owner's authentication is done with an RC522 RFID module, and the delivery and access notifications are sent using an ESP8266 Wi-Fi module. A regulated 5 V power supply is used to keep the system operating smoothly. Experiment findings show an authentication accuracy of 98.46 %, delivery success rate of 96 %, security efficiency of 97.91 %, and average system response time of 5.45 seconds. The effectiveness of the proposed approach is confirmed by these results. The system provides a safe, economical, and scalable method for household and office parcel delivery. The next developments comprise sophisticated sensors, encrypted communication, and smart authentication methods.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning Model for Classifying Myocardial Infarction Complications Through ECG Images with XAI
authors: Progga Parmita Roy, Aishwarya Saha, Mahafujer Rahman, Khadiza Akter
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Every year, cardiovascular diseases cost the lives of around 18 million people; this number is sometimes increased by inaccurate or delayed diagnoses. Standard Electrocardiogram (ECG) interpretation is important, but it often faces challenges due to unclear procedures and human bias. In response, this work presents a daring vision: an ECG picture categorization framework that combines clinical rigor with Deep Learning (DL) and its revolutionary ability, surpassing the limitations of convention. Its core is a redesigned DenseNet201 that has been transformed via dual dropout regularization, transfer learning, and judicious augmentation. The model achieves an accuracy of (99.46%) while maintaining equilibrium across precision, recall, and F1-score after being trained on a carefully selected dataset of 928 ECG pictures from four important diagnostic classes. To prevent the overlapping between training and validating images, a 5-fold cross-validation is applied. Finally, the model is validated with an external independent dataset to prove its robustness. The use of Grad-CAM as a form of Explainable AI (XAI) method enhances interpretability in addition to numerical superiority, making the decision-making process transparent and clinically significant for practitioners.

title: Cybersecurity in Medical AI: Parkinson Voice Biomarkers Security: Anti-Adversarial Protection Against Attacks
authors: Md Muhasin Ali, Ashraful Hasan Chad, Shovan Samanta Turzo, Mohammad Soad Khan, Md. Jahidul Islam Mozumdar, Nasim Parvez
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Parkinson Disease (PD) is a progressive nervous system disease that interferes with motor control and speech, and therefore, it is necessary to detect it early but challenging because there are no conclusive lab tests. Clinical assessment is commonly used in the traditional diagnosis and this might be subjective and time consuming. The proposed research is intended to develop a reliable and correct artificial intelligence system of PD detection based on voice biomarkers. The publicly available speech features Kaggle dataset was used. In the preprocessing stage, such techniques as feature scaling and dimensionality reduction were used, six machine learning models were run: LightGBM, CatBoost, Extra Trees, Quadratic Discriminant Analysis (QDA), Gaussian Process Classifier (GPC), and Multi-layer perceptron (MLP). The findings show that GPC and MLP performed better with 95% accuracy and CatBoost was second with 94%. Adversarial attacks (FGSM and PGD) were also proposed to evaluate robustness, and a substantial decrease in performance was found. This was followed by the use of adversarial training that somewhat regained accuracy and enhanced resistance to attacks. The novelty of this work consists in collaboration between the cybersecurity perspectives and medical AI in the detection of PD so that models are not only precise but also robust. This system provides a secure, nonintrusive, deployable telemedicine application. A quick comparison to the state-of-the-art (CNN, LSTM, ensemble models) shows our system similar or better accuracy along with more adversarial robustness.

title: Efficient Sensor Data Transmission via Finite-Memory Estimation for Remote Monitoring of Photovoltaic Systems
authors: Soonwoo Lee, Hui-Myoung Oh, Jung Min Pak
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Photovoltaic (PV) power plants are distributed as small-scale units across multiple locations, and the power generated varies considerably with the season and weather. At a central base station, a distributed energy resource management system is employed to monitor data generated from the power plants; such monitoring is helpful for optimizing the power supply and responding to incidents. However, the frequent transmission of sensor data from numerous power plants to the central base station at fixed intervals leads to unnecessary network traffic and high transmission energy consumption (and hence cost). This study proposes an efficient sensor data transmission technique that can reduce network traffic and energy wastage. At each power plant, finite-memory estimation is used to predict PV power generation in real time. Data are transmitted only when the difference between the predicted and measured values exceeds a threshold. The base station employs the same predictor, and when no data are received, it substitutes the predicted value for the measured value. Experiments were conducted with this twin-predictor-based event-triggered transmission technique by varying the model, prediction algorithm, and conditions, and the results showed the effectiveness of the technique.

title: TransVort: A Temporally-Coherent Physics-Guided Neural Network for Super-Resolving and Denoising 4D Flow MRI of Cerebrospinal Fluid_supp2-3690957.docx
authors: Vitaly Rayz
publish: N/A
abstract: Objective: To enhance the diagnostic utility of 4D flow MRI in assessing cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) dynamics by super-resolving and denoising measured velocities using temporally coherent, physics-guided neural networks (PGNN). Methods: Synthetic 4D flow MRI was generated from 40 computational fluid dynamics (CFD) simulations across 10 ventricular geometries. These simulations were used to generate paired synthetic 4D flow MRI and high-resolution velocity fields used for supervised training. Here, we compare a previously developed temporally independent network (div-mDCSRN-Flow) using divergence-based regularization with two novel temporal PGNNs (tempo-mDCSRN-Flow using divergence-regularization and TransVort additionally constrained by the vorticity transport equation). Results: In application to synthetic 4D flow MRI of a double gyre flow showed the temporal PGNNs improve vorticity estimation. Similarly, both temporal methods improved estimation of vorticity and time-averaged wall shear stress (TAWSS) of synthetic 4D flow MRI in the 3rd and 4th ventricle. While using temporal PGNNs improves velocity and vorticity quantification across temporal resolutions, TransVort demonstrated additional improvement at fine temporal resolutions. Application of TransVort to in vivo 4D flow MRI of CSF flow captured vortex formation and dissipation in the 4th ventricle over the cardiac cycle. Conclusion: Leveraging the temporal information of 4D flow MRI improves reconstruction of high-resolution velocity fields. This leads to better estimation of gradient-based flow metrics such as vorticity and TAWSS, which are associated with neurodegenerative and neurovascular diseases. Significance: Augmenting the accuracy of 4D flow MRI increases its potential for adoption as a clinical tool for diagnosing and monitoring disorders of neurofluid dynamics.

title: Probing Deeper Zone of Lung using Four Electrode Techniques: An FEM Approach
authors: Mahjabin Mobarak, Habiba Begum, Md. Moniruzzaman, Abdullah Al Mahfazur Rahman, Md. Ruhul Amin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Electrical Impedance Techniques are being explored as a low-cost, non-invasive method for detecting lung disorders by measuring bioimpedance differences between healthy and diseased tissue. Air-filled lungs (inhalation) show higher impedance, while fluid-filled or collapsed lungs (exhalation) show lower impedance. Methods like Tetrapolar Impedance Measurement (TPIM) and Focused Impedance Method (FIM) enable localized lung assessment. In this study, Finite Element Method (FEM) simulations using a simplified lung model evaluated the effectiveness of various electrode configurations-implementing electrodes on one side and both sides. Simulation results revealed that the contribution of the lung to the total measured impedance (CLTI) varied significantly depending on the electrode configuration and the respiratory phase. For the both-sided electrode placement, CLTI ranged from 13–24% during inhalation to 23–45% during exhalation, indicating a strong influence of the lung’s condition on the overall impedance. In contrast, the one-sided electrode configuration yielded lower CLTI values, ranging from 7–12% during inhalation to 14–20% during exhalation, suggesting reduced sensitivity to lung-specific impedance changes when current and voltage electrodes are confined to a single side of the thorax. The transfer impedance changes of 28–37% and 10-16% for both sided and one-sided configuration respectively. These findings indicate that the both-sided setup allows deeper current penetration through the thoracic region and greater responsiveness to changes in lung aeration. The results suggest that simplified Electrical Impedance Methods with both sided electrode placement can potentially detect localized lung abnormalities.

title: Automatic Red Teaming LLM-based Agents with Model Context Protocol Tools_supp1-3691201.pdf
authors: Shouling Ji
publish: N/A
abstract: The remarkable capability of large language models (LLMs) has led to the wide application of LLM-based agents in various domains. To standardize interactions between LLM-based agents and external resources, model context protocol (MCP) tools have become the de facto standard and are now widely integrated into these agents. However, the incorporation of MCP tools introduces the risk of tool poisoning attacks, in which malicious MCP tools can steer the behavior of LLM-based agents toward unintended outcomes. Although previous studies have identified such vulnerabilities, their red teaming approaches have largely remained at the proof-of-concept stage, leaving the automatic red teaming of LLM-based agents under the MCP tool poisoning paradigm an open question. To bridge this gap, we propose AutoMalTool, an automated red teaming framework for LLM-based agents by generating malicious MCP tools. Our extensive evaluation shows that AutoMalTool effectively generates malicious MCP tools capable of manipulating the behavior of mainstream LLM-based agents while evading current detection mechanisms, thereby revealing new security risks in these agents.

title: Consumer Data Privacy-Aware Federated Orchestration for Communication, Computing, and Control in 6G Consumer Services
authors: Syed Danial Ali Shah, Ali Kashif Bashir, Daehan Kwak, Amina Salhi, Farman Ali
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: Emerging Consumer Electronics (CE) systems, such as connected vehicles, immersive AR/VR environments, cloud gaming platforms, and intelligent home robotics, are increasingly characterized by their dependence on distributed intelligence, seamless connectivity, and autonomous control. These systems often span multiple Mobile Network Operators (MNOs) and serElectronicsders, requiring coordinated management of communication, computing, and control resources at the network edge. When AI and RAN workloads share edge infrastructure, this coexistence naturally achieves integration: RAN workloads provide communication and control while AI workloads supply computing intelligence. However, as MNOs increasingly share infrastructure under the Open Radio Access Network (O-RAN) paradigm, efficient resource allocation across heterogeneous workloads and independent Service Level Agreements (SLAs) becomes critical. At the same time, preserving consumer data privacy across administrative boundaries remains a major concern. This paper proposes the Converged AI and O-RAN Architectural (CAORA) framework, a federated and SLA aware orchestration solution that integrates AI and RAN workloads with communication, computing, and control. CAORA employs local spike-aware workload forecasting using attention-based LSTMs and aggregates predictions through federated learning to avoid raw data sharing between operators. A centralized Soft Actor Critic (SAC) reinforcement learning agent then allocates edge resources fairly and efficiently while meeting SLA constraints. Experiments on real 5G traffic traces from Barcelona show that CAORA achieves about 90% task completion, reduces SLA violations to below 10%, and reaches nearly 100% resource utilization under peak load.

title: Multitree Genetic Programming with Behavioral Semantics for Large-Scale Dynamic Air Traffic Flow Management
authors: Tong Guo, Yi Mei, Mengjie Zhang, Hui Ding, Wenbo Du
publish: IEEE Transactions on Evolutionary Computation
abstract: Dynamic Air Traffic Flow Management (DATFM) is vital to modern aviation industries, requiring real-time routing and sequencing aircraft within constrained airspace to ensure safety and efficiency, particularly during unforeseen disruptions. Multitree Genetic Programming (MTGP) provides an automatic design framework to simultaneously learn routing and sequencing policies. However, the coupling interdependence between the routing and sequencing decisions in MTGP tends to produce structurally valid but semantically ineffective offspring, thereby limiting MTGP’s search ability within the heuristic space. This paper presents a novel Multitree Genetic Programming with Behavioral Semantics (MrGPBS) approach that automatically evolves effective reactive scheduling heuristics for DATFM. First, we develop a specialized multitree representation incorporating informative terminals that enable concurrent evolution of routing and sequencing decisions, coupled with a problem-tailored heuristic template that decodes these representations into executable solutions in an online manner. Second, we propose a behavioral semantics-guided evolutionary mechanism that addresses the coupling interdependence issue of existing MTGP methods. Our method preserves well-evolved tree structures by promoting recombination between behaviorally compatible individuals, thereby enabling incremental improvement within the heuristic search space. We evaluate MrGPBS on comprehensive benchmark instances derived from real-world air traffic data, encompassing diverse problem scales and dynamic operating conditions. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods.

title: D-PolTransGAN: Scattering Guided Hybrid Transformer-GAN for SAR-Optical Image Translation using Dual Polarization SAR
authors: Jagrati Talreja, Tewodros Syum Gebre, Leila Hashemi-Beni
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) provides reliable imaging in all weather and lighting conditions, but its speckled appearance makes visual interpretation difficult and limits its integration with optical-based analyses. To address this challenge, we propose a new SAR-to-optical image translation framework tailored for flood assessment. The proposed model processes SAR VV and VH polarizations through separate feature extraction streams, enabling each stem to learn its unique scattering characteristics. These features are then fused using cross-attention to capture complementary information from both polarizations. The architecture combines convolutional networks for local detail extraction with transformer modules to model global context, all within a generative adversarial learning framework. We train and evaluate the model using the DEEPFLOOD, SEN1FLOODS11 and SEN12MS datasets, which provides well-aligned Sentinel-1 (VV, VH) and Sentinel-2 image pairs. Experimental results show that the proposed method outperforms existing approaches in terms of PSNR, SSIM, and perceptual quality (LPIPS), while preserving fine spatial details critical for flood analysis. Qualitative results on real flood events further demonstrate that the generated optical-like images recover visual structures and contextual information that are difficult to interpret in raw SAR imagery. <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/JagratiTalreja01/D-PolTransGAN</uri>

title: A Conceptual Study for Prediction of the Floating behavior of Metallic Particle
authors: Seungmin Bang, Chanyeol Ryu, Bang-Wook Lee
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Metallic particle was generated inside the Gas Insulated Switchgear (GIS) due to incorrect assembly process or mechanical operation. During normal operation, the influence of the electric filed causes the metallic particle to float over the conductor, resulting in partial discharge and dielectric breakdown. It is very important to prevent insulation accidents by predicting the floating height of metallic particle. However, the shape and size of metallic particle very diverse, and the floating height varies depending on the type of voltage. Previous studies had analyzed the movement or partial discharge characteristics of metallic particle through experiments. Also, the movement of the metallic particle was performed only through simulation, and were not consistent with the experimental results. Therefore, in this paper, a conceptual study for prediction of the floating behavior of metallic particle. The floating height and lift-off voltage of the metallic particle were investigated according to the shape, size and voltage type. The floating height of metallic particle were simulated by Boundary Element Method (BEM), and the results of the BEM and the experiment were consistent. It is found that lift-off voltage and floating height of the metallic particle could be predicted without conducting experiments.

title: Unlocking Flexibility Potential through DVFS-controlled Data Center Clusters in Unit Commitment
authors: Yiping Yuan, Yufei Teng, Liqian Zhu, Yihang Cao, Zhenyuan Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: Data center clusters (DCCs) have become recognized as significant energy consumers in electrical power system oper-ations. Workload reallocation processes for multiple DCCs, im-plemented by dynamic voltage and frequency scaling (DVFS) control schemes, can provide flexibility potential capabilities and contribute to maintaining the security of power systems opera-tions through Unit Commitment (UC). To achieve this, The flexi-bility potential of workload reallocation hosted by multiple DCCs is analyzed. Building on this analysis, an interval-based work-load reallocation strategy is extended to depict flexibility poten-tial from workload response while avoiding service-time latency. Then, an innovation set of physical security constraints repre-senting DVFS-controlled DCCs is developed, and related special-ordered sets-of-type-1 (SOS1) linearized constraints are derived through the McCormick Envelopes method. Furthermore, these intractable constraints are incorporated into UC, and an en-hanced UC model, which considers the flexibility potential offered by DVFS-controlled DCCs, is developed. Several experiments are conducted on the IEEE 6 buses test systems to assess the validity and scalability of the proposed models.

title: Towards Robust Alignment for Video Dehazing with Temporal Lookup Table
authors: Haoyou Deng, Zhiqiang Li, Feng Zhang, Bin Xu, Qingbo Lu, Changxin Gao, Nong Sang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: Video dehazing aims to restore clean scenarios from a sequence of hazy frames, where frame alignment is a critical stage for leveraging temporal information. However, haze degrades contrast and obscures details, making alignment challenging. Existing methods ignore the impairment of haze on alignment and thus struggle to align frames accurately. To address this challenge, we propose an alignment network with the temporal lookup table (temporal-LUT), which effectively enhances the haze-degraded frames and provides vivid cues for precise alignment. Specifically, to tackle the color degradation of haze, we employ a learnable lookup table (LUT) to enhance hazy color. The color mapping nature of LUT favorably preserves the naturalness of enhanced outcomes. Besides, we introduce a temporal weight prediction strategy to strengthen inter-frame interaction, which ensures temporal consistency across enhanced results and thereby benefits alignment. Extensive experimental results on two widely used benchmarks and real-world scenes demonstrate the superiority of our method.

title: Corruption-Resilient Combinatorial Bandit Learning for Heterogeneous Network Systems
authors: Junkai Wang, Xutong Liu, Jinhang Zuo, Yuedong Xu, John C.S. Lui
publish: IEEE Transactions on Networking
abstract: We study online decision-making problems in network applications using the framework of contextual combinatorial multi-armed bandits (C<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>MAB). Although bandit methods provide a natural solution, two key challenges arise in practice: corruption in feedback and heterogeneity across clients. Corruption may stem from adversarial behaviors or system anomalies, leading to biased reward estimations, while heterogeneity reflects differences in clients’ environments such as computation capabilities or network conditions. To address these challenges, we formulate the C<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>MAB under corruption (C<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>MAB-C) problem and extend it to the setting with heterogeneous environmental parameters. We then propose two novel algorithms, namely CW-C<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>UCB, which leverages confidence-weighted estimations to mitigate corruption effects, and CW-C<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>CLUB, which further integrates an online clustering mechanism to adaptively group tasks based on shared structure. We derive tight theoretical regret bounds for both algorithms under various corruption models showing that our upper bounds match the established lower bounds up to logarithmic factors. Empirical results on three real-world applications, content delivery networks, client selection in federated learning, and VR video streaming, demonstrate that our proposed algorithms significantly outperform existing baselines, achieving lower regret and stronger resilience in adversarial environments.

title: Variable Selection-Based Recursive Method for Adaptive Quality-Related Fault Detection
authors: Yanyu Chen, Hao Ma, Yan Wang, Feng Ding
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: Fault detection (process monitoring) for dynamic and nonlinear industrial processes, which can be viewed as complex systems, has become increasingly important in recent years. Under complex and dynamic operating conditions, traditional multivariate statistical process monitoring (MSPM) methods struggle to effectively capture the evolving behaviors of quality indicators at the system level. In this article, a novel variable selection-based adaptive MSPM method is proposed for quality-related process monitoring. First, an improved part mutual information (PMI) method is proposed for variable selection, categorizing process variables into quality-related and quality-unrelated groups, addressing the shortcomings of the original PMI being able to measure the correlation between only two variables. Second, a novel recursive kernel principal component regression (RKPCR) incorporating the hierarchical model order-reduction strategy is proposed to effectively track quality indicators. This method screens high-quality data for model updates based on distance-based classification and linear approximation, which mitigates model degradation caused by faulty data. In addition, a recursive kernel principal component analysis (RKPCA) method similar to RKPCR is proposed to monitor quality-unrelated faults. Finally, a numerical example and two industrial processes are used to demonstrate the performance of the proposed method.

title: TransVort: A Temporally-Coherent Physics-Guided Neural Network for Super-Resolving and Denoising 4D Flow MRI of Cerebrospinal Fluid_supp3-3690957.docx
authors: Vitaly Rayz
publish: N/A
abstract: Objective: To enhance the diagnostic utility of 4D flow MRI in assessing cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) dynamics by super-resolving and denoising measured velocities using temporally coherent, physics-guided neural networks (PGNN). Methods: Synthetic 4D flow MRI was generated from 40 computational fluid dynamics (CFD) simulations across 10 ventricular geometries. These simulations were used to generate paired synthetic 4D flow MRI and high-resolution velocity fields used for supervised training. Here, we compare a previously developed temporally independent network (div-mDCSRN-Flow) using divergence-based regularization with two novel temporal PGNNs (tempo-mDCSRN-Flow using divergence-regularization and TransVort additionally constrained by the vorticity transport equation). Results: In application to synthetic 4D flow MRI of a double gyre flow showed the temporal PGNNs improve vorticity estimation. Similarly, both temporal methods improved estimation of vorticity and time-averaged wall shear stress (TAWSS) of synthetic 4D flow MRI in the 3rd and 4th ventricle. While using temporal PGNNs improves velocity and vorticity quantification across temporal resolutions, TransVort demonstrated additional improvement at fine temporal resolutions. Application of TransVort to in vivo 4D flow MRI of CSF flow captured vortex formation and dissipation in the 4th ventricle over the cardiac cycle. Conclusion: Leveraging the temporal information of 4D flow MRI improves reconstruction of high-resolution velocity fields. This leads to better estimation of gradient-based flow metrics such as vorticity and TAWSS, which are associated with neurodegenerative and neurovascular diseases. Significance: Augmenting the accuracy of 4D flow MRI increases its potential for adoption as a clinical tool for diagnosing and monitoring disorders of neurofluid dynamics.

title: Front Cover
authors: N/A

title: Segmented Trajectory Optimization for Pinch-Based Assembly of Modular Truss Components
authors: Mingchang Chen, Hao Wen, Wei Zhang, Yixing Gong
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: Automated robotic assembly is an appealing solution to on-orbit construction tasks. However, the traditional direct plug-in strategy is difficult to assemble connectors that are distant from the robot in low-stiffness module components. A forcible plug-in not only induces impacts that cause harmful vibrations but may also damage the structure. To address this issue, we propose a pinch-based assembly strategy that achieves direct assembly of distal connectors by planning robot trajectories that enter the interior of the module. Considering that general motion planners perform poorly in cluttered environments, we develop a segmented trajectory optimization algorithm that decomposes the complex global problem into several simple local problems to solve sequentially. In addition, we propose an obstacle prefiltering method and a trajectory quality evaluation metric to enhance planner performance. Finally, we validate our algorithm in simulations and real experiments, and demonstrate the pinch-based assembly strategy in a truss assembly experiment using a dual-arm robot system.

title: NOMA based Multi-Link Operation for Performance Enhancement of Wi-Fi 9 and Beyond
authors: Jomon K. Charly, Vettath Pathayapurayil Harigovindan, Lorenzo Galati-Giordano
publish: IEEE Communications Letters
abstract: Multi-link operation (MLO), firstly introduced in Wi-Fi 7, is going to be an integral part of beyond Wi-Fi 8 protocol evolution. This feature enables the seamless coordination of multiple frequency bands under a single association, however it maintains the basic contention principles on each link. In this letter, we propose non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) based MLO for enhancing the throughput performance of future Wi-Fi 9. The proposed scheme allocates the links to the MLDs based on propagation characteristics as well as incorporates NOMA multiplexing principles. We also propose a simple analytical model for the throughput analysis. The analytical and simulation results show that the proposed scheme has the potential to significantly improve the throughput performance of futureWi-Fi 9 and beyond networks by permitting multiple nodes to transmit simultaneously. Our scheme achieves a throughput improvement of 108.49% for a network with 90 users.

title: IEEE Aerospace and Electronic Systems Scholarship Program
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Aerospace and Electronic Systems Magazine

title: Efficient Voltage Boosting and Leakage Current Suppression Multi-Input Multilevel Inverter Structure for Grid-Tied PV Systems
authors: Alaaeldien Hassan, Mohamad Abou Houran, Mustafa Abu-Zaher, Ahmed Ismail M. Ali, Mokhtar Aly, Fernanda Carnielutti
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Power Electronics
abstract: Multilevel inverters (MLIs) offer efficient and effective solutions for integrating different renewable energy systems (RESs) with different outputs and separate controllers; however, the integration of multi-input MLIs in photovoltaic (PV) applications remains challenging due to increased component count, high switching stress, limited voltage boosting capability, and leakage current issues. Therefore, this paper proposes an efficient multi-input MLI structure for grid-tied photovoltaic (PV) systems that exhibits voltage boosting and leakage current suppression. The proposed structure is based on the dual-source switched capacitors (QT3L-QSC5L) 15-Level MLI topology. The proposed (QT3L-QSC5L) MLI has fewer components, produces high-quality output waveforms, and achieves high efficiency. Additionally, A sinusoidal pulse-width modulation (SPWM)–based control scheme is employed to enable independent maximum power point tracking (MPPT) for each PV source while providing inherent voltage balancing of the switched capacitors. Furthermore, the proposed structure offers an alternative solution for leakage current suppression, using an insulation resistor to attenuate the leakage current component to an acceptable level. The verification of the control method and the leakage current attenuation is performed through detailed MATLAB/SIMULINK©simulations under a realistic irradiation profile. The experimental setup based on dSPACE (DS-1103) hardware controller further validates the simulation results.

title:
Ferroelectric-Modulated Spin to Charge Conversion and THz Emission in Bilayer WTe
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Heterostructures
authors: C Kishandev, Joel Isaac Joy, Jeevan Thomas Korah, Eldho Markose, Nikhil Kumar
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: Bilayer WTe2 is investigated as an efficient spin–charge conversion interface for ultrathin spintronic terahertz (THz) emitters. First-principles calculations reveal that the out-of-plane ferroelectric polarization non-volatily dictates the symmetry of the Rashbasplit bands, enabling a binary reversal of the Edelstein tensor sign and leading to polarization-dependent spin accumulation near the Fermi level. While spintronic THz emitters have emerged as a novel route toward compact THz sources, the next challenge remains the active, low-power modulation of emission without relying on external magnetic fields. We address this by exploiting the intrinsic structural symmetries of bilayer transition metal dichalcogenides; specifically, the distorted 1T′ crystalline phase and the emergence of sliding ferroelectricity in WTe2. In this work, we present a theoretical analysis of an Al/CoFeB/WTe2/LiNbO3 heterostructure where the ferroelectric polarization acts as a low-energy gate to modulate the interfacial inverse Rashba–Edelstein effect (IREE). By implementing a propagation model based on the Transfer Matrix Method (TMM), we show that the ferroelectric polarization direction induces a sizable modulation of the THz emission amplitude along with a clear phase reversal upon polarization switching. Unlike traditional spintronic emitters that require energy-intensive magnetic field bias for modulation, our results demonstrate that sliding ferroelectric architectures enable non-volatile, reconfigurable regulation of THz emission.

title: Robo-taxi Fleet Coordination at Scale via Reinforcement Learning
authors: Luigi Tresca, Carolin Schmidt, James Harrison, Filipe Rodrigues, Gioele Zardini, Daniele Gammelli, Marco Pavone
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control of Network Systems
abstract: Fleets of robo-taxis offering on-demand transportation services, commonly known as Autonomous Mobility-on-Demand (AMoD) systems, hold significant promise for societal benefits, such as reducing pollution, energy consumption, and urban congestion. However, orchestrating these systems at scale remains a critical challenge, with existing coordination algorithms often failing to exploit the systems' full potential. This work introduces a novel decision-making framework that unites mathematical modeling with data-driven techniques. In particular, we present the AMoD coordination problem through the lens of reinforcement learning and propose a graph network-based framework that exploits the main strengths of graph representation learning, reinforcement learning, and classical operations research tools. Extensive evaluations across diverse simulation fidelities and scenarios demonstrate the flexibility of our approach, achieving superior system performance, computational efficiency, and generalizability compared to prior methods. Finally, motivated by the need to democratize research efforts in this area, we release publicly available benchmarks, datasets, and simulators for network-level coordination alongside an open-source codebase designed to provide accessible simulation platforms and establish a standardized validation process for comparing methodologies.

title: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
authors: N/A
publish: N/A

title: Enhancing Multi-Step Solar Irradiance Forecasting through TVF-EMD-AWNN: a Time-Varying Hybrid Framework for PV Power Plant
authors: Sakshi Shukla, Sumanta Pasari, Kshitij Gupta
publish: IEEE Transactions on Sustainable Computing
abstract: Accurate solar irradiance prediction is inevitable for estimating photovoltaic energy generation and meeting utility demands. This paper presents a novel TVF-EMD-AWNN hybrid framework that integrates time-varying filter empirical mode decomposition (TVF-EMD) with an adaptive wavelet neural network (AWNN) for enhanced prediction accuracy at an hourly timescale. The proposed framework encompasses a sophisticated three-fold practically relevant integration architecture. First, the adaptive filtering mechanism of TVF enables dynamic frequency time optimization while maintaining non-stationary signal characteristics through its time-varying filtering process. Then, the EMD decomposes these enhanced signals into intrinsic mode functions, exhibiting unique temporal-spectral signatures and facilitating comprehensive multi-resolution analysis. Finally, the adaptive wavelet parameters in AWNN synergistically represent these decomposed components through dynamic scale-translation optimization in the wavelet space. Additionally, the proposed TVF-EMD-AWNN model is also validated through the expanding window cross-validation approach. The theory is illustrated on a long hourly solar irradiance data (2015–2021) at four diverse climatic regions of India. Results demonstrate noteworthy accuracy improvement in terms of relative RMSE reduction in comparison to established standalone and hybrid models, such as MLP (83%), LSTM (85%), Bi-LSTM (82%), EDBi-LSTM (80%), AWNN (78%), CEEMDAN-LSTM (83%), CEEMDAN EDBi-LSTM (80%), TVF-EMD-LSTM (65%), and TVF-EMD BiLSTM (54%). Besides, the performance of TVF-EMD-AWNN remains consistent even at multi-step hourly predictions. Therefore, the proposed hybrid framework with additional benchmarking, ablation studies, and time series validation offers robustness towards practical power system operation.

title: Dynamic Shelf Life Prediction of Fruits and Vegetables During Cold Chain Transport
authors: M Sathya Sai Ramya, K Indumathi
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Reducing food waste in perishable supply chains requires accurate, real-time shelf life prediction during cold chain transportation. While existing methods rely on static models or controlled lab conditions, this study presents a hybrid AI-IoT framework that dynamically predicts shelf life under real-world transport variability. Our model combines LSTM networks for time-series sensor data (temperature, humidity, CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, ethylene) with Random Forest for static features (product type, packaging, cold chain violations), achieving superior prediction accuracy (MAE: 0.82 days, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>: 0.91) compared to prior approaches. Key findings reveal that even brief temperature violations (>4°C) reduce leafy greens' shelf life by 12–18%, while ethylene exposure accelerates spoilage in sensitive produce. The proposed system offers a scalable, cost-effective solution for logistics operators, enabling proactive quality management and waste reduction. By integrating real-time monitoring with adaptive machine learning, this research advances dynamic shelf life prediction in perishable food supply chains.

title: Design and Implementation of a Unified Financial Decision-Support System Using Predictive and Anomaly-Based Analytics
authors: Joshua S, Abdul Azim S, Vengadesan S, B. Monica Jenefer
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Accelerated expansion of digital payment infrastructures, algorithmic trading environments, and continuous market ecosystems has amplified demand for intelligent, cohesive, and adaptive financial decision-support research platforms. Conventional financial applications function as fragmented utilities, providing restricted predictive insight, limited anomaly awareness, and negligible user-level customization. A stock trend forecasting component applies statistical learning techniques over historical equity data to estimate price movement across a 90-day interval, achieving a predictive accuracy of \textbf{92.4%} during experimental evaluation, with automated notification delivery. anomaly-oriented classification models capable of recognizing irregular behavioral patterns in real time, yielding \textbf{94.1% detection accuracy} during prototype validation. A live equity observation interface incorporates publicly available financial data streams for continuous price tracking and performance ranking. Personal finance supervision includes income-expense logging, expenditure visualization, budget deviation alerts, and rule-driven allocation guidance supporting investment-oriented planning. Experimental outcomes confirm improved financial awareness, strengthened risk mitigation, and enhanced personalization under real-time operational conditions. The originality of the presented financial intelligence framework can be highlighted. It clearly describes the system design, analysis process, and design architecture of integrated modules. The most important evaluation indicators and the results of experiments are briefly stated in order to mark quantitative performance. Any type of generic and literature-style descriptions have been eliminated to enhance technical specificity. The updated abstract is dedicated to the originality and practice applicability of the suggested work. These changes bring about greater compliance with the standards of publication at conferences and originality.

title: Field-Oriented Control of Induction Motor Drive Using Indian Drive Cycle
authors: Soha Yadav, Meera Murali, Sushil Ramdasi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive simulation and performance analysis of a Field-Oriented Control (FOC) based induction motor drive intended for electric vehicle applications. The Indian Drive Cycle (IDC) is employed as the reference speed profile to emulate urban driving behavior. The induction motor drive (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7.5 \text{kW}, 1440 \text{rpm}, 400 \mathrm{V}, 50 \text{Hz}$</tex>) is simulated in Simulink. The drive is tested under a near-rated constant torque load of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$49 \mathbf{N} \cdot \mathbf{m}$</tex> to emulate demanding EV traction conditions such as vehicle launch and gradeability. MTPA and field weakening algorithm is employed to regulate proper functioning of speed and torque according to the IDC parameters as per the torque load. Simulation results demonstrate effective decoupled control of torque, accurate speed tracking, reduced torque ripple, improved efficiency and robustness under specific load.

title: Federated Learning for Distributed Energy Management in Smart Grids
authors: Saif Almheiri, Sawsan J. M. Malaka, Hussain Dawood, Mohammad A. Hamdan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: The fast expansion of smart grids has changed the way energy is managed because everything is monitored in real-time, the power is transported efficiently, and clean energy is brought into the mix. This has become a challenge to keep track of how much power is consumed and produced by the various devices that are connected to the grid. Besides, they need to guarantee that the private information remains safe. Federated Learning (FL), a decentralized machine-learning method, could be a great help by enabling all entities to work together without the necessity of changing any raw data. This paper looks at the option of using FL for managing distributed energy in smart grids, namely, predicting the energy demand, maximizing supply, and balancing load. The suggested plan uses the information created by smart meters and distributed energy resources (DERs) to build the machine learning models together, ensuring privacy at the same time and minimizing the communication amount. We value the capability of FL to incriminate different data, solve security issues, and give the grid proactive insights through predictive strategies. The results of our tests show that FL can reach the same accuracy as the centralized methods and, at the same time, lower data transfers and thus increase the consumer's privacy level. The current research shows that FL can not only revolutionize smart grid operations but also lay the groundwork for the building of scalable, secure, and efficient energy management systems.

title: LSTM-based Time Series Forecasting of Solar Irradiance and Temperature
authors: Alagar Karthick, S.Sankar Ganesh, K Moorthy, P.Bhuvaneshwari
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Optimizing the efficiency and dependability of photovoltaic (PV) systems and guaranteeing efficient grid integration of solar energy depend on accurate sun irradiance forecasting. We compare two neural network models for short-term solar irradiance prediction, Unidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (UniLSTM) and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM). Both models were trained and evaluated using historical data on solar irradiance and weather conditions. Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root Mean Square Error (RMSE), and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score were among the key performance measures used to evaluate the accuracy of the forecasts. While both models effectively capture the data's temporal dependencies, the results indicate that the BiLSTM model achieves better predictive accuracy by utilizing data from both previous and future time steps. This comparative study demonstrates that BiLSTM networks can improve solar irradiance forecasts, enabling more effective management of solar energy resources.

title: PharmaBot: A Multimodal AI System for Drug Interaction Detection
authors: Fatima Nasser, Hadi Hasan, Safaa Salman, Ammar Mohanna, Ali Chehab
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: Adverse drug–drug interactions (DDIs) constitute a persistent and preventable threat to patient safety, increasingly driven by widespread polypharmacy and the growing complexity of modern pharmacotherapy. However, current DDI checking tools largely rely on structured inputs and manual database lookups, rendering them ill-suited for real-world pharmacy workflows where medication data are often unstructured or image-based and decisions must be made rapidly under time pressure. We present PharmaBot, an end-to-end, microservices-based system for automated DDI screening from medication images or free-text input. PharmaBot integrates computer vision for medication detection, optical character recognition (OCR), large language model (LLM)-based drug extraction, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) grounded in DrugBank to identify and explain clinically relevant interactions.PharmaBot is evaluated on a template-generated, pharmacist-realistic dataset where the drug extraction service achieves near-perfect performance (F1-score 0.9968), while end-to-end interaction detection attains high accuracy (F1-score 0.9913). Medicine image detection achieves a mean Intersection over Union (IoU) of 97%, enabling reliable localization for downstream processing.These results demonstrate that integrating computer vision and knowledge-grounded LLM reasoning within a modular architecture enables accurate and practical DDI screening from unstructured real-world inputs, supporting safer and more efficient pharmacist-facing decision support.

title: A Low-Cost IoT-based CPR Feedback Device
authors: Abu Yousuf Md. Eftekhar Sadiq, Md. Jarjis Mondal, Md. Ishraq Anzum
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Cardiac arrest is quite unpredictable, occurring not only among elderly individuals but also in middle-aged people. Complications like myocardial infarction require immediate medical response, and cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) is a crucial first aid technique to maintain blood circulation during such emergencies. However, performing CPR is quite challenging for untrained individuals, and even doctors require feedback systems to ensure correct compression depth and rate. Existing CPR feedback devices are often bulky and expensive, while smartphone-based systems need CPR modules itself and complex setups.This paper presents a cost-effective, pocket-sized CPR feedback device using a simple force sensing resistor (FSR) module, enhanced with Internet of Things (IoT) capabilities. The proposed device can transmit emergency alerts with the victim’s real-time location to a designated hospital via the MQTT protocol on a NodeMCU ESP8266 or any medical professional through GSM module to any smartphone.
