An integrated OLED display provides the real-time feedback on compression count and adequacy. This compact device has been tested and compared in hardware level with real patients to validate the efficacy of the developed system. Its portability, accessibility, and ease-of-use enable timely and effective CPR delivery, where even nonprofessionals can potentially provide life-saving assistance during cardiac emergencies.

title: The Effect of Artificial Intelligence on the Investment Decision: (Case Study Based on Jordanian Investment Management Companies)
authors: Majed Qabajeh, Mohammad Alqsass, Murad Qabajeh, Riyad Darwazeh, Jassim Algasawneh, Ghada Al-Rawashdeh, Mahmoud Alghizzawi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: This research explores how AI can improve the accuracy, efficiency, and strategic depth of investment decisions. The survey oriented to finance director, portfolio manager, head of risk management department, investment analyst and investment consultant working in the investment management companies in Jordan. The sample is top 15 investment management companies in Jordan in 2022. Descriptive statistical were performed with adequate methods on the variables, while simple regression analysis was used to test the hypotheses.In this research study adoption of AI was used as independent variable, and in turn the enhancement of investment decision was used as the dependent variable. The results confirm that employing AI technologies in a investment management firms lead to enhancement of investment decision. AI's ability to process large volumes of structured and unstructured data enables investors to gain a more holistic view of potential opportunities and risks. AI are increasingly used for predictive analytics, portfolio optimization, and risk management, outperforming traditional methods in many cases by identifying patterns that human analysts might overlook. By utilizing AI-driven tools like algorithmic trading and robo-advisors, investors can make more informed and timely decisions, adjusting their portfolios in real time in response to market movements.

title: Prior-Experience Based Unified Encoder Approach for Efficient Image-Text Retrieval in Remote Sensing Applications
authors: S. Dinesh Krishnan, Rishith Reddy Bolledla, Myla Laxmi Bhargavi, Mylapalli Mahesh, Neela Vaishnavi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Remote sensing image-text retrieval (RSITR) aims to retrieve relevant textual descriptions for a given remote sensing (RS) image, as well as corresponding images for a given text query, based on the semantic content alignment. Existing approaches, including prior experience-based dualencoder frameworks, typically employ separate visual and textual encoders for image-to-text and text-to-image retrieval tasks. Although these approaches are effective, such architectures lead to increased model complexity, higher computational cost, and training redundancy due to modality-specific encoders and task-dependent pipelines. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a unified retrieval framework CLIP(Contrastive Language Image Pretraining) that simplifies the model architecture while preserving effective cross-modal representation learning. Rather than using independent encoders for each retrieval direction, the proposed approach maps both RS images and textual descriptions into a shared multimodal embedding space, enabling bidirectional retrieval within a single framework. Semantic similarity between image and text embeddings is directly computed using cosine similarity, allowing both image-to-text and text-to-image retrieval without architectural modifications. The unified design reduces computational overhead and eliminates redundant feature extraction, resulting in improved efficiency compared to prior dual-encoder methods.

title: An Optimization-Based Framework for Database-Level Fraud Detection in Real-Time Financial Systems
authors: Raghu Gollapudi
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: The growing amount of financial processing and speed requires the implementation of strong fraud detection protocols that can be utilized at the database layer with low latency. In this paper, an optimization-based system to spot frauds in real time in financial database applications is introduced, with special emphasis to early detection of anomalies before any processing is done at the application level. The proposed framework combines constrained optimization tools together with adaptive behavior modeling to continuously assess transactional patterns stored in relational and distributed databases. The framework is efficient at detecting features under high throughput by optimizing the paths of query execution and combining path scores of each query with risk scores by dynamically optimizing the feature selection and query execution paths as well as risk scoring cutoffs. Analysis of temporal dependencies, transaction consistency, and behavior of access is conducted together to differentiate between fraud and legitimate operational variations. The architecture will be system-agnostic so as to be easily integrated with available financial data infrastructures without a complete redesign. Through experimental analysis, it is proved that database-level optimization shows large improvements in detection responsiveness with low computational overhead. The suggested framework provides a scalable and resilient solution to protecting real-time financial systems against emerging fraud schemes, which is appropriate in modern digital banking and payment systems.

title: Pruning Neural Networks for Real-Time Use
authors: Abigail G Basener
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: In this paper, we investigate how pruning a neural network affects its ability to run on edge hardware, such as the NVIDIA Jetson Nano, which is commonly used in drone-based data collection systems. These devices often face strict limitations on processing power and memory, preventing realtime analysis of large hyperspectral datasets. Our goal is to reduce model size through structured pruning, enabling realtime inference without significantly compromising performance. We evaluate various pruning levels to understand the tradeoffs between model accuracy, runtime, and resource usage. The results show that moderate pruning allows for substantial reductions in resource consumption while maintaining acceptable accuracy, making real-time onboard processing more feasible.

title: Evaluating the Robutness of LLM Against Data Posioning Attacks in Cybersecurity Datasets
authors: Maninder Kaur, Sangeeta Singh, Usha Sree R, Neha Gupta, Mohit Garg, Meenakshi Munjal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have started to become a part of cybersecurity applications, e.g., threat intelligence analysis, intrusion detection, malware classification, and automated incident response. These models are also susceptible to data poisoning attacks, although they are useful, they can be deliberately poisoned by adversaries to harm the performance of the models or cause them to act maliciously. In this paper, a thorough analysis of the effectiveness of the state-of-the-art LLMs in resisting data poisoning attacks to cybersecurity datasets is provided. We study several strategies of poisoning, such as label flipping, backdoor insertion, and semantic perturbation, on representative cybersecurity datasets, such as malware reports, network traffic logs, and threat descriptions. The effects of the attacks are measured by metrics of performance degradation, misclassification, and consistency of behavior when faced with adversarial conditions. Moreover, we discuss the performance of the current defense protocols such as data sanitization, anomaly detection, robust fine-tuning and, adversarial training. The experimental findings indicate that a low-ratio poison may cause a dramatic impact on the reliability of an LLM decision, especially in situations where security matters. The results present important robustness gaps and hint at how to come up with more resilient systems based on LLM to protect against cybersecurity threats. The research work leads to the design of credible and safe AI models that can be used in a reliable manner in adversarial settings.

title: Optimized Resource Allocation in Cloud Computing Using Hybrid Machine Learning Approach
authors: Lokesh Raj C, Lokeshwaran R, Subbiah Swaminathan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Cloud computing experiences sustained setbacks on how to develop optimal allocation of the computing resources, with much of the computing resources still under exploitation. This paper suggests a hybrid machine learning model that combines XGBoost in short-lived predictions of workloads and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks in long-term predictions of trends. The system uses the Service Level Agreement (SLA) awareness in its allocation to compromise between efficiency and quality service. An engineered pipeline of multi-window time-series preprocessing can be used to extract more than fifty engineered features based on raw cloud metrics and improve predictive accuracy. Simulated workload experimental tests show more than 20 percent saved costs, 30 percent better energy efficiency, and greatly reduced SLA breaches than traditional threshold-based methods of allocation. The structure itself is modular and, therefore, can be easily deployed on a commercial cloud like AWS EC2 to serve both research and production purposes.

title: Uncertainty-Aware Intelligent Structural Health Monitoring Using FBG Sensors and Machine Learning
authors: Sayantani Bhattacharya, Femil Mirza, Omkar Patil, Suryansh Tiwari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Structural Health Monitoring (SHM) is essential for ensuring the long-term safety and reliability of civil infrastructure. Fiber Bragg grating (FBG) sensors provide high sensitivity and immunity to electromagnetic interference; however, their signals are influenced by both strain and temperature, requiring compensation for accurate damage assessment. This paper presents a physics-informed intelligent SHM framework integrating K-Means for anomaly detection, Agglomerative Clustering for multi-class damage classification, and SHAP for explainable decision-making. A MATLABgenerated dataset incorporating realistic noise and thermal drift is used for validation. The proposed approach achieves high classification accuracy and introduces a quantitative Damage Severity Index for reliable structural assessment.

title: A Cross-Modal Semantic Alignment Method and Computer-Aided Translation System for English Translation
authors: Jia He
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI in Education Technology and Applications (AIETA)
abstract: To address the surge in cross-modal demands for English translation in a globalized context, existing cross-modal semantic alignment algorithms suffer from low accuracy and poor adaptability, and computer-aided translation (CAT) systems struggle to effectively integrate image, audio and text multimodal information. This paper proposes a translation-oriented modality adaptive attention semantic alignment (TMAASA) algorithm and constructs a CAT system based on this algorithm. Multiple original constraints and computational models are designed, and the algorithm is encapsulated and integrated into the system. Simulation experiments verify its performance. Experiments show that the TMAASA algorithm achieves an alignment accuracy of 92.78%, an average alignment error of 0.036, a BLEU-4 value of 58.96, and a single-sentence response time of 0.32s, all outperforming mainstream comparison algorithms. The constructed system is functionally complete and stable, effectively improving the quality and efficiency of English translation. This research provides a new technical path for the application of crossmodal technology in English translation, possessing significant engineering application value and theoretical reference significance.

title: High-Resolution Impervious Surface Area Segmentation Network Based on Multimodal Data Fusion
authors: Xiaohu Ma, Chenzhong Gao, Mengmeng Zhang, Yunhao Gao, Wei Li
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: With accelerated urbanization, the increase in impervious surface area (ISA) leads to urban heat island effects and heightened flood risks, making accurate high-resolution ISA extraction crucial. Traditional methods face limitations, while deep learning models, particularly U-Net architectures, demonstrate superior performance. However, traditional methods relying on a single data source for ISA identification have limitations. Utilizing multimodal remote sensing data fusion can enhance the accuracy of ISA extraction. Therefore, this study proposes a multimodal data fusion and segmentation network that integrates synchronized multimodal data augmentation and a hybrid sliding window post-processing strategy to improve model generalization and robustness. The approach was validated by achieving the runner-up position in the 2025 IEEE WHISPER Multimodal Semantic Segmentation Challenge, demonstrating its effectiveness.

title: T-GCN-Lite: A Lightweight Temporal Graph Convolutional Network for Traffic Speed Forecasting
authors: Divya Lakshmi S, Roshni Rajendran, Jeena Joseph
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: Traffic speed forecasting plays a vital role in intelligent transportation systems and smart city development. However, traditional deep learning approaches such as Graph Convolutional Networks (GCNs) and Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) often incur high computational costs and suffer from over-parameterization, limiting their deployment in real-time applications. To address these challenges, this paper proposes T-GCN-lite, a lightweight hybrid architecture that integrates temporal convolutional layers with graph convolutional layers to effectively capture both temporal and spatial dependencies in traffic networks. The proposed model reduces computational complexity while maintaining high predictive accuracy. Experiments conducted on the METR-LA dataset demonstrate that T-GCN-lite achieves a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.35 ~\text{km} / \mathrm{h}$</tex> and a Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.62 ~\text{km} / \mathrm{h}$</tex>, outperforming traditional baselines with significantly lower resource requirements. These results highlight the potential of T-GCN-lite for efficient, scalable, and real-time traffic speed forecasting in intelligent transportation systems.

title: Design and simulation of inverter for Smart Grid Using DSP-Based Model Predictive Control
authors: Hakam Youness, Tabaa Mohamed
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: This paper explores the advantages of incorporating electric vehicle (EV) chargers into the smart grid, particularly highlighting how Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) technology can reduce electricity costs for smart homes. By using Model Predictive Control (MPC), our approach enhances system performance and boosts energy efficiency. The MPC predicts changes in voltage output across different inverter switching states through a discrete-time model, identifying the best switching state for upcoming intervals. This method reduces Total Harmonic Distortion (THD) and speeds up system response, leading to a more dependable and efficient setup. Our findings are supported by MATLAB/Simulink simulations under pump motor load conditions, where we saw THD decrease from 2.56% to 1.56%, an improvement of roughly 1.00%. Further tests using Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) simulations affirm the durability and practicality of the MPC approach. The paper also discusses the design needs for high-performing boost converters and inverters, highlighting the importance of digital signal processing in improving system efficiency. We used Texas Instruments’ TMS320F28379D digital signal processor, which features dual CPUs and a 16-bit resolution ADC, to develop and test specific MPC controllers and electronic circuits. This setup significantly enhances the performance of high-efficiency charger boards and offers a cost-effective solution for smart homes, optimizing energy utilization through the smart grid.

title: Quantum Fuzzy Detection of Anomaly Objects Using Hyperspectral Generative Adversarial Learning
authors: Chia-Hsiang Lin, Si-Sheng Young, Reza Langari
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: In satellite remote sensing (SRS), hyperspectral anomaly detection (HAD) aims to identify distinct spectral signatures while suppressing the background components (BCs). Due to the lack of target spectra and the real-world challenges, such as noise effect or mixed-pixel phenomenon, the definition of anomaly and background is typically ambiguous. However, existing HAD approaches solely focus on the background reconstruction using handcrafted regularization or deep learning (DL)-based frameworks, potentially ignoring the fuzziness of the problem nature. To overcome these limitations, this study proposes a quantum-empowered fuzzy generative adversarial network for HAD (Q-FuGAN) by integrating the strength of reconstruction-based detectors, fuzzy theory, and the handcrafted HAD property. In Q-FuGAN, we first fuzzify each pixel into various types of fuzzy degrees using morphological, convolutional, and full expressibility quantum fuzzification layers. Then, the customized fuzzy rule layers infer the detection from these fuzzy degree maps, namely fuzzy detector. Moreover, under a novel masking adversarial learning framework, the multiscale frequency-spatial generator effectively reconstructs the BCs, then considers the residual as the detection, i.e., residual detector. By fusing the information from fuzzy and residual detectors, the proposed Q-FuGAN achieves remarkable detection results for real-world datasets.

title: AI-Driven Recommendation Systems for GIG Workers
authors: M.Umamaheswari, S. Shanthakumari, R.Umamaheswari, S Selva Vaishali Nachiar, N. Prem Anand, E. Lakshmi Subhiksha
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Companies and organizations are established with the purpose of achieving specific goals, which can range from financial profitability to customer satisfaction, environmental sustainability, or economic development, among others. To successfully reach these goals and objectives, the human factor plays a crucial role. At the top of the checklist for the human component is the leader. The leader has ability to influence organizational members, motivating them to willingly contribute towards the fulfillment of predetermined goals and aspirations. Therefore, leadership primarily involves the capacity to inspire individuals to perform their duties consistently over time, employing motivational strategies. Employee performance assessment is a crucial aspect of an organization's development, as it contributes to both individual productivity and the overall growth of the organization. By focusing on the development of employees, their skills can be enhanced, leading to improved performance and organizational progress. This study specifically examines the assessment of employee performance, which involves analyzing, studying, and comparing their work behaviors and outcomes. To predict employee performance, various machine learning algorithms are utilized. In this research, different categories of machine learning algorithms are compared, and an Ensemble Hybrid Model is proposed, which demonstrates superior results compared to other classifiers. The performance of the selected classification algorithms is validated using the various validation technique.

title: Reinforcement Learning–Driven Optimization of Deep Learning in Cloud–Fog Systems
authors: Saket Mishra, Ketankumar Patel, Akshay Kumar Darla, Yalamanchili Ravi Krishna, Hari Babu Damma
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: The introduction of intelligent services in the domains of smart cities, healthcare analytics, autonomous systems, and industrial automation have increased a high demand to deploy deep learning efficiently on distributed computing infrastructures. Cloud-Fog computing has become a general and powerful paradigm to serve low-delay and scalable applications and data-intensive to end-end devices. The paper introduces a reinforcement learning-based optimization algorithm of deep learning workloads in cloud-fog systems. The cloud-fog environment, modeled by the methodology, is viewed as a series of decisions by a learning agent, whose dynamic decision-making was grounded on real-time states of the system to decide task placement and resource allocation. Deep Qlearning is used to process states of high dimension and allow optimization to be adaptive without rules. It is shown in the results of an experiment that the proposed approach reduces significantly end-to-end response time to approximately 135 ms as compared to 170 ms, improves resource utilization efficiency to approximately 0.86 compared to 0.77, and increases the learning reliability to approximately 0.94 compared to 0.89 and reduces operational cost to approximately 0.52 compared with the current methods. These findings validate the usefulness and strength of the proposed web at the next generation cloud-fog intelligent systems.

title: Secure Blockchain Hybrid Framework for Enhanced Security and Efficiency in Patient Record Management
authors: K.Maithili, S.Amutha
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Securing and protecting privacy of patient records are crucial challenge in block chain. In this paper, proposed Secure Hybrid Framework for Blockchain(SHFB) is confronted by integrating AES-GCM and ECC with Zero Knowledge Proofs and Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) to enhance the security efficiency. The exponential growth of healthcare data necessitates secure and efficient frameworks to handle real time processing and protection against cyber threats. This paper presents the SHFB (Confronted) model, a block chain integrated architecture optimized for high throughput, robust denial of service (DoS) resistance, and low latency performance in electronic health record (EHR) systems. Comparative analysis reveals that SHFB surpasses conventional models such as Decentralized Security, AI Privacy and Secure EHR in key metrics, making it a scalable and secure solution for modern healthcare infrastructure. Patient records were used to conduct experimental evaluation and demonstrate that SHFB significantly reached high to compare with existing models. Particularly SHFB achieves an encryption time of 0.02 seconds, security efficiency of 95%.The proposed method shows the effectiveness of hybrid method by enhancing the performance and security of block chain in management of data using healthcare.

title: Wheat Yield Prediction Using Advanced Deep Learning Algorithms
authors: Govindaraj M, V. Asha, Preetham B S, Prajwal N, Nikhitha G S
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Predicting wheat yield is important for agricultural planning, food supply, and decision making at the farm and policy levels. predicting crop yield is morely taken by the interaction, or lack there, of multiple factors of climate, crop condition, and vegetation. In this research, the authors formulated a problem of predicting wheat yield using a neural network and crop condition and meteorological data. a neural network model using a multilayer perceptron (MLP) architecture is able to find the temperature, humidity, precipitation, wind, pressure, and NDVI (Normalized Difference Vegetation Index) by finding the best-fitting hyperplane. using data preprocessing, the datasets was splited into a testing and training set to perform the evaluation. using the evaluation metrics to gain insight into the confusion matrix, accuracy, and ROC-AUC, the authors showed that the model is have abilities to differentiate the classes of wheat yield into high and low. this research shows that data-driven methodologies and deep learnings model can helping in agricultural decision making and crop yield analysis using the yield prediction models. this research illustrates the key of predictive analytics in precision agriculture.

title: Challenges in Hyperspectral Imaging for Autonomous Driving: The HSI-Drive Case
authors: Koldo Basterretxea, Jon Gutiérrez-Zaballa, Javier Echanobe
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: The use of hyperspectral imaging (HSI) in autonomous driving (AD), while promising, faces many challenges related to the specifics and requirements of thisapplication domain. On the one hand, non-controlled and variable lighting conditions, the wide depth-of-field ranges, and dynamic scenes with fastmoving objects. On the other hand, the requirements for realtime operation and the and the limited computational capabilities of embedded platforms. The combination of these factors determines both the criteria for selecting appropriate HSI technologies and the development of customized vision algorithms that leverage the spectral and spatial information obtained from the sensors. In this article, we analyse several techniques explored in the research of HSI-based vision systems with application to AD, using as an example results obtained from experiments using data from the most recent version of the HSI-Drive dataset.

title: Design of a Tunable Microstrip Combline Bandpass Filter Using Varactor Diodes for UWB Applications
authors: Raneem A. Al-Mashni, Lujain F. Al-Hunity, Yanal S. Faouri
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: This paper presents a compact, tunable microstrip combline bandpass filter (BPF) designed for ultra-wideband (UWB) applications. By loading the resonators with varactors, continuous center frequency tuning was achieved from 3.38 to 7.105 GHz, which corresponds to a 2.1:1 ratio. According to the simulation results, the design maintained an insertion loss below 3 dB and a return loss better than -10 dB. Stability in the fractional bandwidth is observed between 310 and 450 MHz across the entire range, whereas group delay fluctuations are maintained within 1.0–2.2 ns. These performance metrics make the filter a practical solution for reconfigurable RF front-end and cognitive radio systems.

title: Harmonic Index-Driven Power Quality Detection and Forecasting for Distribution Grid Monitoring: Eliminating the Need for High-Resolution Waveforms
authors: Albashir Bashir, Mohamad Nassereddine
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: Scalable power-quality (PQ) analytics that translate measurement data into actionable grid intelligence are central to modern distribution network monitoring. A persistent operational constraint is that many utilities archive only exported harmonic indices and total harmonic distortion (THD) values from installed PQ meters, foregoing continuous high-resolution waveform storage. This restricts conventional PQ analysis pipelines, which typically depend on waveform access, time-frequency decompositions, and computationally demanding feature engineering. To bridge this gap, this work introduces a telemetry-native signal processing (SP) and machine learning (ML) framework for harmonic-state detection and short-horizon forecasting directly from time-synchronized harmonic telemetry acquired at a low-voltage (LV) point of common coupling (PCC) at 4-s logging intervals. Operating exclusively on exported harmonic indices and magnitudes, the framework combines telemetry conditioning, short-window temporal descriptors, and interpretable voltage-current coupling features. Two principal functions are provided: (i) IEEE 519-aligned voltage harmonic detection classifying PCC conditions into Normal, Watch, and Exceedance states, and (ii) 60-s ahead forecasting of voltage THD and dominant harmonic orders comprising triplen (3rd, 9th, 15th) and odd non-triplen (5th, 7th, 11th) components, realized via multi-output gradient boosting with rigorous time-series validation. Evaluation on an 11-hour operational dataset, extended through controlled synthetic harmonic spike injection, benchmarks performance against persistence, logistic regression, and tree-based baselines. Results confirm reliable classification of steady and disturbed operating regimes alongside bounded forecast errors, establishing telemetry-native analytics as a viable and deployable pathway for proactive PQ monitoring where waveform storage is unavailable.

title: Graph Neural Network Framework for Interpretable EEG-Based Emotion Recognition Using Frequency-Band and Connectivity Analysis
authors: Ch Anwar Ul Hassan, Azhar Imran, Khursheed Aurangzeb, Hashim Elshafie
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: The healthcare field, human-computer interaction, and affective computing development require accurate human emotional recognition through electroencephalography (EEG) signals. EEG signals present intricate spatial temporal and spectral dependencies that both traditional machine learning and deep learning methods fail to capture resulting in restricted cross-subject generalizability and limited understanding. We introduce MER-TFC, a new EEG-based emotion recognition system which uses GraphNeuralNetworks (GNNs) with attention mechanisms to create inter-channel connectivity and temporal models from EEG data while connecting theta, alpha, beta, and gamma frequency bands. The framework is evaluated using leave-one-subject-out cross-validation (LOSOCV) on DEAP and AMIGOS.benchmark datasets. The experimental results show that MER-TFC achieves 88.2 % average accuracy with 87.7 % F1-score and 0.93 ROC-AUC and 87.9 % balanced accuracy which outperforms Transformer-CNN, Multilayer Graph Transformer, Frequency-Adaptive Dynamic Graph Transformer (FreqDGT) and Adaptive Progressive Attention GNN (APAGNN). Attention analysis reveals brain regions that play critical roles and shows how emotional states correspond to functional brain network connections. The proposed framework also maintains high performance when EEG signals experience background noise, which demonstrates its ability to function well on different subjects.

title: Artificial Intelligence Adoption in Supply Chain Management: Qualitative Approach
authors: Muneer Abbad, Faten Jaber
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) could be used in managing the supply chain in different ways. It could improve supply chain activities by utilizing the best practices in process improvement, decision making, find the best suppliers, and improve suppliers’ relationships, sustainable practices and operations. Companies are trying to use the AI techniques in supply chain management to gain its benefits. The adoption process will be viewed from information systems (IS) point of view. It means that to facilitate the use of AI in SCM then adoption of IS theories should be considered to find the main factors that could affect the adoption process. In addition, some other factors should be considered to accommodate the new features of AI in specific field (SCM). This study will focus on how to use quantitative approach to find out the factors that could affect the adoption of AI in SCM.

title: Deep Learning-Driven Smart Framework for Accurate Drought Forecasting via Satellite Imagery Analysis
authors: Balamuri Likitha, Bandi Sravani, Chinnahyata Sreenija, Chandamala Jayanthi, B. Deena Divya Nayomi
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Drought develops gradually before becoming a natural hazard which generates substantial environmental damage and social-economic impacts mainly affecting agricultural areas. The exact and on-time prediction of drought conditions remains difficult because of complex climatic patterns and insufficient ground observation data particularly in underdeveloped regions. A deep learning-based smart framework will be developed to forecast droughts by utilizing satellite-derived precipitation data according to this research. A hybrid Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) structure makes up the proposed model which detects short and long temporal patterns within precipitation sequence data. The framework adopts CHIRPS (Climate Hazards Group InfraRed Precipitation with Station data) dataset for its public availability and high-resolution long-term rainfall historical information. The data preparation process includes merging data into monthly periods while normalizing values followed by drought classification through SPI algorithms. Multiple performance metrics evaluate the model which demonstrates better results than ARIMA and Random Forest along with standalone LSTM models. The proposed CNN-LSTM model achieved an accuracy of 91.3%, precision of 89.7%, recall of 90.4%, F1-score of 90.0%, and an RMSE of 1.29, outperforming the LSTM (accuracy: 84.2%, RMSE: 1.76) and Random Forest (accuracy: 78.6%, RMSE: 2.14). Inference time remained low at 8.7 ms/sample, demonstrating potential for real-time deployment. The research develops a sound scalable drought forecasting system which applies only satellite-based precipitation measurements and runs at computational speed. The model’s high accuracy and low latency make it suitable for integration into early warning systems, particularly in data-scarce or climate-vulnerable regions.

title: Smart Irrigation Control Using Machine Learning
authors: Mohammad Bani-Doumi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Smart irrigation systems have been identified as a fundamental answer to the pressing requirement for effective water management in agriculture. This research investigates the usage of a variety of machine learning techniques for the purpose of optimizing irrigation control, aiming at a more improved water-use efficiency and simultaneously a healthy crop yield. This will be achieved by employing data from soil moisture sensors, weather forecasts, and crop-specific requirements to adjust the irrigation schedules in real-time leading to reducing the water wastage. The most sophisticated machine learning algorithms, such as decision trees like neural networks were used for predictive analysis and decision-making. The simulation results were obtained using the actual data and compared with traditional methods, which were shown to be better for saving water and crop yield quality efforts. Also, the research demonstrates the system's scalability and adaptability to various agriculture domains. This research offered the possibilities that smart irrigation systems can lead the way for a long-term change in the way farmers approach the technology thus paving the way for ownership of the durability of various practices.

title: Regulate Abusive Comments on Kid-Managed Social Media Content by Leveraging Adaptive AI and Camunda
authors: Syamprasad Guda
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Online abuse increasingly affects young content creators. Conventional moderation methods lack dynamic decision-making capabilities. This study presents a hybrid approach where adaptive AI instantly flags users and offensive comments. Once flagged, the data is fed into a Camunda BPMN workflow, which automates the moderation process by notifying the content owner via email. The post or channel owner then reviews the flagged content and approves or rejects the comment. This ensures that moderation is not solely reliant on AI but empowers content creators to have control over their content. Experimental results demonstrate improved real-time responsiveness and moderation efficiency compared to traditional approaches.

title: An Interpretable Machine Learning Framework for Early Prediction of Cervical Cancer using SHAP and RFE
authors: S.Geeitha, P.Renuka, Hema Ramachandran, R.Sowmya, K.Sudha Devi, V.Ravindra Krishna Chandar
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The primary root cause of fatalities from cancer for women worldwide is cervical cancer, especially in nations with low or medium incomes the state of utilization of regular examination is limited. This research presents a comprehensive technique for predicting cervical carcinoma biopsy outcomes using the UCI cervical cancer risk factors dataset. The dataset consists of behavioral, clinical, and demographic variables, many of the features values contain missing values and imbalanced classes. Preprocessing is done by the replacement of non-standard missing indicators, type conversions and imputation using median and mode strategies on feature types. To identify the predictive features, Recursive Feature Elimination (RFE) is apply on the dataset, selecting ten critical risk indicators such as hormonal contraceptive use, presence of sexually transmitted diseases (STDs) and screening outcomes. Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) is integrate for addressing class imbalance, enhancing the techniques sensitivity to minority class predictions. Classification algorithms Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and Support Vector Machine is trained through stratified 5-fold cross validation. Logistic Regression achieves the best overall performance with a cross-validated ROC-AUC of 0.955 and test ROC-AUC of 0.935, balanced precision (0.60), recall (0.857), and F1-score (0.706).

title: Learning Emotions Across Modalities Through Hybrid Deep Representation Fusion
authors: Vanka Shireesha, Vinay Kumar Pamula, P. Satyanarayana Murty
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: The recognition of emotion is an important element of emotion-aware intelligent systems, and unimodal methods are characterized by ambiguity and poor robustness. This paper refers to a hybrid deep learning model of multimodal emotion recognition that combines visual, audio, textual and physiological electrocardiogram (ECG) data to obtain correct and dependable emotion identification. The suggested framework uses modalityspecific deep feature extraction networks, which are utilized to retain discriminative emotional signals of every input modality. In order to fully utilise the complementary information, a hybrid fusion method is proposed, integrating feature-level and decisionlevel fusion in order to improve inter-modal representation learning. Decades of experiments on benchmark emotion datasets show that the suggested multimodal framework is always more efficient than unimodal and traditional fusion-based techniques in various categories of emotions. The findings indicate that hybrid fusion is efficient and that the addition of physiological signals will be valuable in the practical context of robust emotion recognition.

title: A CAN-Enabled Intra-Vehicle Accident Detection and Emergency Alert Framework Using Embedded Sensors
authors: M. Karthik, S. Usha, K. V. Nitheesh, S. S. Rithik, R. Vignesh Kumar, R. Harinivas
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The paper discusses a vehicle accident detection and emergency alert system built on hardware which will be used in hilly and rural environments where network coverage is not very good. The primary objective is to provide a useful alert system that would reduce the emergency response time without relying on the availability of a continuous internet connection. Traditional classic accidentwarning systems are truly based on online communication, which is a disadvantage in isolated areas and can create enormous delays in case of an emergency. In an attempt to solve this problem, we have offered a two-controller design based on the Controller Area Network (CAN) protocol. A slave controller built on an Arduino Nano monitors the movement of the vehicle with the help of an MPU6050 sensor, identifying unusual tilting, rollover, and abrupt collisions. When any of these outcomes happens, the information is transmitted on the CAN bus to a master ESP32 controller. The information is in turn processed by the master who then identifies the position of the vehicle using a GPS module and displays real-time alerts on an LCD so that the users can know at once. One of the advantages of our framework is that it works autonomously and has a higher priority in CAN communication, which means that the messages are delivered fast and reliably. Our design was tested with a hardware prototype which demonstrated consistent performance, minimal communication delay and dependable accident detection in different test conditions. Our solution is more reliable than the systems that rely on the internet in the areas of low coverage. The findings affirm that the system is an affordable, scalable, and viable method of being able to improve vehicle safety in areas where connectivity is constrained.

title: Taxonomy on Children Face Recognition System using Artificial Intelligence Techniques for Missing Children Identification System
authors: Sreevidya Badnal Shankarappa, Rajeshwari Janathakal
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Face Recognition is a technology for identifying an individual using a digital image of their face by extracting and analyzing their facial features. The design of an effective child face identification system is important for solving different social problems, such as child abuse detection, missing children identification, kinship verification, and age-invariant face identification problems. Thus, a wide-ranging study of the current face recognition system to identify children's faces was conducted in this review. As very limited progress has been made in detecting children's faces owing to their facial growth, this study also manifests various works that have been carried out to detect the face and expression of an adult person to study the possible adoption that can be incorporated into child face detection. This paper provides different methodologies used in concepts, such as Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning and Deep Learning techniques for face recognition across diverse age group datasets in existing work and the accuracy achieved for these methodologies. The survey also highlights the limitations of current methodologies in designing effective child face recognition systems, considering highly noisy and occluded information and limited child face data. This study motivates the design of an effective face recognition system, as existing studies are limited to children’s face recognition, specifically based on missing child identification, which can achieve higher accuracy with fewer misclassifications. This drives us to build an efficient child-faced dataset, as the dataset available for this is very limited. The survey here concludes with open challenges that need to be addressed for better face recognition and missing child identification system with essential dataset

title: Soft Computing-Driven Energy-efficient Algorithm Design with Combined Optimization and Machine Learning for Resource-constrained Devices
authors: Kandimalla Sasank Sai, Karumanchi Srija, Ventrapragada Chetan Sarma, Aravapalli Sri Chaitanya, Puttur Preetham Kumar, Dangeti Suma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Recent advances in the Internet of Things (IoT) and edge computing applications have stimulated interest in intelligent, resource-aware methods for predictive nature and energy-efficient solutions. This paper proposes a hybrid framework that combines soft computing techniques such as fuzzy systems, genetic algorithms, and neuro-fuzzy systems with machine learning models, including Random Forest, Support Vector Machine, Gradient Boosting, and Artificial Neural Networks, in an effort to develop energy-efficient algorithms capable of being used on resource-constrained devices. Using a synthetic dataset of CPU load, memory usage, temperature, battery level, and cell strength, the framework reduces the computational burden on resource-constrained devices using genetic feature selection (GFS) and hyperparameter optimization (HPO) while establishing reliable classification performance. A variety of evaluation metrics include accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and Kol-mogorov-Smirnov indicate that the proposed system is capable of producing high-quality predictions with lower energy usage and facilitate the sustainability of IoT deployments. Although energy optimization is the focus, the framework lays the foundation for predictive maintenance, or anomaly detection, with communication that is performed in realtime when reliability and latency are of importance. Overall, the results indicate that AI for optimization when combined with lightweight classification allows for scalable, resilient and sustainable deployments of realtime intelligent systems in edge networks.

title: Securing Multi-Cloud Environments: Best Practices for Firewall Integration and Traffic Routing
authors: Bhupendra Singh, Manoj Babu Devapathni Yugandhar, Sahil Bucha, Mukund Kulkarni, Hariprasad Challagondla, Piyush Ranjan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Transitioning to multi-cloud environments exposes new security challenges, which can only be resolved by combining robust firewall integration with smart traffic routing methods to protect crucial infrastructure and data. Because traditional security approaches are insufficient for handling changing workloads, cross-cloud interactions, and new cyberthreats, adaptive security solutions are now needed. A novel Unified Zero Trust Firewall Orchestration framework is presented in the current paper. The UZTFO framework unifies AI-based assessment of network traffic, software-defined security perimeters, and blockchain authentication to raise the level of security in networks using multiple clouds. Using live risk appraisals, the framework makes it possible to dynamically reconfigure firewall policies, providing a transparent approach to security management in diverse cloud environments.Further, a traffic routing algorithm using an artificial intelligence is put into use. The AI powered algorithm optimizes the network latency and efficiency of the data flow, tackling security threats at once and reducing them. The performance comparison against common firewall solutions identified that UZTFO results in better threat detection and better network performance while still providing a good level of security for multi-cloud configurations. The increased network performance with a reduction in false positives gives UZTFO a good case as a reliable scalable security tool for multi-cloud deployment. The fundamental objective of the project is to implement AI-aided security systems that could support existing cloud systems and guarantee both regulatory adherence and systems efficiency for organizational data protection.

title: HYPERFUSE: Spectral-Spatial Fusion for Hyperspectral Remote Sensing
authors: Sirui Wang, Iulia Calota, Ioana Voica, Yuanyuan Wang, Corina Vaduva, Andrei Anghel, Virgil Iordache, Aurora Neagoe, Florian Bodescu, Zoltan Bartalis, Mihai Datcu
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: Hyperspectral images provide rich spectral information that is crucial for Earth observation. Spectral-spatial fusion has emerged as an effective strategy to combine the complementary advantages of spectral and spatial context. Progress in this field is limited by the absence of multi-level benchmarking datasets. In this paper, we outline an ongoing research HYPERFUSE, which aims to systematically evaluate deep learning-based spectral-spatial fusion methods across multiple hyperspectral sensors and application domains. Our planned study will investigate the current algorithm landscape, including but not limited to recurrent networks, graph convolutional architectures, and transformer-based models, with a particular emphasis on their ability to support three representative applications: environmental monitoring, heavy metal detection, and hyperspectral unmixing. We also plan to assemble and harmonize datasets from both spaceborne and airborne sensors to establish a benchmark dataset for evaluation. The ultimate goal of this work is to provide a unified framework and dataset resources that can guide future research on spectral-spatial fusion in hyperspectral imaging.

title: Arabic Tā' MarbŭṭAh in Latin Transliteration for Digital Communication: A New Proposed Character
authors: Asar Abdul Karim
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Digital infrastructure relies on consistent data representation for seamless communication across platforms. In Arabic-to-Latin transliteration, the letter Tā’ Marbūṭah suffers from inconsistent representation; rendered as ‘h’, ‘t’, or ‘a’ depending on grammatical context, creating ambiguity in digital text processing, databases, and information systems. This paper proposes a unified, Unicode-compliant character; the Cyrillic letter Tshe (ћ) as a dedicated symbol for Tā’ Marbūtah. The character visually synthesizes conventional ‘h’ and ‘t’ forms, is supported by Unicode (U+045B), and preserves morphological integrity across all contexts. Through comparative analysis of existing transliteration standards (LOC, UNGEGN, ISO 233), we demonstrate how ‘h’ eliminates contextual ambiguity, enhancing data consistency in communication systems and IT infrastructure. This solution supports Arabic NLP, improves information retrieval in cloud-based databases, and contributes to digital transformation in multilingual IT environments.

title: EdgeLearnX: Explainable and Privacy-Aware Edge-AI Framework for Adaptive Multimodal Remote Learning
authors: Shutharsun K, Naveen Kumar S, Mythili Saran K, Jinsha Lawrence
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The emergence of remote digital education system, being not sudden, but rapidly imposed, revealed limitations of the traditional e-learning systems, especially in the low-access areas. Current platforms usually do not provide personal, latency-free, and privacy-conscious learning experiences. Motivated by these gaps, we present EdgeLearnX, a real-time, explainable, and privacy-aware edge-AI framework tailored for adaptive multimodal remote learning. EdgeLearnX leverages machine learning models across the network edge with federated learning, securely transforming content delivery personalization without sharing on sensitive student data with central server stores. The approach combines questions on gaze tracking, facial expressions, voice cues, and interaction patterns to infer cognitive engagement, feeding a real-time adaptive learning engine. One of the novelties of the approach is the usage of explainable AI techniques for transparent content recommendations and trust on both educators and learners. Mature video encoding techniques, 5G/MEC integration, and flexible adaptation algorithms guarantee smooth playback under dynamic varying bandwidth environments. In our three rural schools, featuring 120 students and 15 instructors, we saw an increase in engagement by 91%, a decrease in latency by 68.3%, and an increase in content relevance by 82% from traditional cloud-based learning platforms. This work shows that it is possible to deploy intelligent, interpretable, low-latency learning environments at scale, laying the foundation for equitable and effective remote education.

title: AFDR-SA: A Speed-Aware Fresh Data Retrieval Approach for Real-Time IoT Applications
authors: Konda Hari Krishna, E N Lokesh, Bandi Jhansi Lalitha, Penumadu Girish Kumar, Ammineni Bharath, Boligarla Muralikrishna
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: With the rapid growth of Internet of Things (IoT) applications, ensuring timely and fresh data retrieval has become a critical challenge, especially in dynamic and resource-constrained environments. This paper presents AFDR-SA (Adaptive Fresh Data Retrieval with Speed-Adjustable devices), an efficient framework designed to improve data freshness while minimizing computational overhead. The proposed system integrates advanced retrieval strategies including W-DRSA, H-DRSA, and EDDS, and evaluates their performance under varying data sizes, validity intervals, and mobility speeds. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the EDDS algorithm achieves 100 % data freshness accuracy across all test scenarios, outperforming existing approaches. The W-DRSA method attains an average accuracy of 78.09 %, offering a balanced trade-off between performance and complexity, while H-DRSA achieves 39.89% accuracy with significantly reduced execution time, making it suitable for real-time applications. Performance analysis shows a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 5 - 6 0 \%}$</tex> improvement in data freshness over baseline methods, with execution times as low as 0.0002 seconds for lightweight operations. The results confirm that the proposed AFDR-SA framework effectively enhances data freshness, scalability, and computational efficiency, making it highly suitable for real-time IoT and edge-based data retrieval systems.

title: Comparison of a Hybrid Deep Learning Model for Breast Cancer Detection with an SVM-based Algorithm
authors: M. Renukadevi, S. Gomathi
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Breast cancer remains a major global health concern, highlighting the critical importance of early and precise diagnosis. Detecting the condition in its initial phases is essential for prompt intervention, enhancing recovery prospects, and ultimately promoting patient health. In order to increase classification accuracy, this work presents a hybrid deep learning (HDL) strategy that blends machine learning (ML) techniques with fully connected neural networks. The developed model was assessed in comparison to a conventional SVM-based algorithm using performance metrics such as accuracy, mean squared error (MSE), and the R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score. The dataset, sourced from Kaggle, was preprocessed and normalized before training on Google Colab. The hybrid model achieved a validation accuracy of 90.24%, outperforming the Support Vector Machine (SVM)based approach, which reached 89.00%. Additionally, the hybrid model demonstrated greater stability and reduced error rates. The research demonstrates deep learning hybrid models particularly those using CNNs enhance breast cancer diagnosis accuracy in clinical practice to become useful tools for early patient detection.

title: HYPERMIM: Mutual Information Minimization for Spectral-Spatial Decoupling Masked Autoencoding in HSI Self-Supervised Learning
authors: Li Zongjun, Yin Zhendong, Li Dasen, Zhao Yanlong
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: Hyperspectral imaging (HSI) has become a pivotal remote sensing technology by virtue of its rich spectral information, yet faces significant challenges including scarce labeled samples and difficulties in modeling heterogeneously coupled spectral-spatial characteristics. Prevailing self-supervised learning (SSL) methods fail to achieve efficient feature decoupling due to their neglect of the inherent heterogeneity between spatial and spectral representations in HSI data. To address this, we propose Hy-perMIM-a novel hyperspectral masked image modeling framework grounded in mutual information minimization theory. Theoretically, we derive a variational upper bound on mutual information to drive spatial and spectral features toward statistical independence, establishing an infor-mation-theoretic foundation for decoupled HSI modeling. Architecturally, we design a spectral-spatial decoupled dual-branch masked autoencoder that employs parallel spatial and spectral masking branches, enabling simultaneous feature decoupling and reconstruction learning. At the optimization level, the Decoupled Entropy Maximization Regularization (DEMR) method overcomes cross-dimensional optimization conflicts inherent in conventional regularization via spectral gradient information entropy maximization and spatially weighted sparsity constraints. HyperMIM demonstrates exceptional classification performance and exhibits remarkable generalizability in crossscene transfer learning, with inter-scene performance gaps less than 0.2 %. The framework rapidly adapts to downstream tasks with minimal labeled samples, establishing an efficient solution for sample-constrained HSI applications.

title: Predictive Analytics for Smart City Water Quality Monitoring
authors: Farnoosh Farzaneh, Maher Ibrahim Tawdrous, Hanady Almahmood
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: In the context of the development of smart cities, ensuring high water quality presents a critical challenge, as the quality of water is directly related to factors such as public health, environmental sustainability, and economic growth. This research analyzes the procedures of predictive analytics for real-time monitoring and forecasting of water quality. By virtue of the data obtained from machine learning models and sensors from Internet of Things (IoT) devices set up throughout urban water systems, the study attempts to identify patterns and to predict possible contamination events ahead of time. The proposed technique uses certain sophisticated algorithms, including deep learning and anomaly detection, to study crucial water quality parameters such as pH, turbidity, and levels of dissolved oxygen. In a case study in which real data were used, the adequacy of the system's ability to provide actionable insights within a short period was demonstrated, which helps to take preventive actions in advance and reduces the risk of waterborne diseases. Apart from this, the research recognizes the promise of the employment of predictive analytics in the management of water quality in smart cities, while at the same time, the strength of the city water's resilience and sustainability is being fostered.

title: Optimal Operation of Smart Cities Integrated with Data Centers and Workload Dispatch
authors: Takuma Ishibashi, Akie Uehara, Masahiro Furukakoi, Ahmed Rashwan, Hiroshi Takahashi, Paras Mandal, Tomonobu Senjyu
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: To advance green transformation (GX), the transition to smart cities (SC) is being promoted. For this purpose, an energy management system (EMS) utilizing IoT devices in SCs is a promising approach. In recent years, the proliferation of artificial intelligence and digitalization has led to an increase in data centers (DC). Along the decarbonization pathway, SCs require the introduction of renewable energy and combined cooling, heating and power (CCHP), and integration with DCs is also essential. Therefore, this paper proposes a model that integrates DCs and SCs, comprehensively addressing DC workloads, power, and cooling demands. It incorporates indirect power demand response through the dispatch of delay-tolerant workloads. To validate the proposed model, numerical simulations are conducted, comparing costs and carbon emissions. Case studies compare scenarios involving the standalone operation of SC and DC, as well as their integrated operation. Simulation results indicate that integrating DC and SC reduces costs.

title: A Lightweight Transformer-Guided Model with Edge-Enhanced Preprocessing for Multi-Class Cassava Leaf Disease Detection
authors: T.Satheesh, M.S.Geetha Devasena, Ponmythili Satheesh, C.Sathishkumar, F.Ravindaran
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Accurate detection of cassava leaf diseases is important to ensure crop health and sustainable agricultural productivity in resource-limited farming environments. This research work presents a compact hybrid model CassNet which is designed for the precise classification of different cassava leaf conditions. The proposed architecture combines a lightweight convolutional module with transformer network to efficiently capture detailed and contextual patterns. A coordinate attention mechanism is incorporated at multiple levels to highlight the disease-relevant regions and an edge-focused input enhancements are used to highlight the lesion boundaries and structural disruptions. The proposed model is evaluated on a labeled cassava leaf image dataset and the results exhibits the better classification accuracy of 93.28%, with precision, recall, and F1-score values of 0.9312, 0.9274, and 0.9293 respectively. The inference time was obtained as 18.4 ms per image which is significantly better than conventional CNN and transformer models. The proposed CassNet model provides a practical and deployable solution with minimal parameter load and high reliability across variable input conditions in real-time agricultural disease monitoring.

title: Joint Security Enhancement Using Parallel AES and Intelligent Anomaly Detection
authors: Aadesh Sreenivasan, S. Ramyashree, S. Vidyashree, Parthiban I, Selvakumar J
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Joint security enhancement is essential in embedded systems that require both high-performance encryption and robust runtime protection. The paper presents the implementation of a parallel architecture of Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) with intelligent anomaly detection. AES core is coded at Register Transfer Level (RTL) via Verilog HDL and coded with parallel round processing and complex key expansion to deliver high throughput and less latency. To increase resistance to power and timing side-channel attacks the proposed design uses multi-mask XOR-based preprocessing scheme and architectural masking and balanced logic approaches, which provide limited data-dependent switching and better security with no impact on cryptographic correctness. In addition to cryptographic protection, a Finite State Machine (FSM)-based signature matcher identifies known control-flow and fault injection attacks, and a hardware-aware Long Short- Term Memory (LSTM) autoencoder identifies unknown or zero-day anomalies by analysing behavioural deviation. The experiment results demonstrate that the proposed architecture achieves low latency, low power consumption, and low LUT usage, while maintaining the correct functionality of the AES-128 encryption, and enables real-time simultaneous anomaly detection.

title: Enhancing Remote Sensing Satellite Performance for Defense Applications in India using ML, DL, and NavIC
authors: Mitul Goswami, Sainath Dey, Somnath Mahato
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Remote sensing (RS) technologies have witnessed significant advancements, evolving from early aerial photography to sophisticated satellite-based systems capable of delivering high-resolution, multispectral, and all-weather imagery. The integration of Machine Learning (ML) and Deep Learning (DL) techniques has revolutionized the interpretation of RS data, enabling enhanced accuracy, automated analysis, and decision-making across a variety of applications, including defense. This paper presents a comprehensive review of ML and DL approaches, such as Graph Neural Networks (GNNs), Spatiotemporal Modelling, and Transfer Learning with Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), and their roles in improving the performance of RS satellites in the Indian defense context. These techniques are especially valuable in overcoming the challenges posed by India’s diverse terrain, including the Himalayas, coastal regions, dense forests, and deserts. This paper also evaluates the positioning accuracy of India’s indigenous Navigation with Indian Constellation (NavIC) system across five locations: Lucknow, Kanpur, Bengaluru, Shillong, and Port Blair. The analysis includes standard deviation, 2D accuracy (2DRMS and CEP), and 3D accuracy (SEP and MRSE), revealing spatial variations in performance. Results indicate that Port Blair exhibits the highest positional consistency, while Shillong and Lucknow show greater variability, particularly in altitude accuracy. The findings underscore the potential of combining advanced AI-driven RS methods with indigenous navigation technologies like NavIC to strengthen situational awareness, surveillance, and strategic decision-making in defense operations. This integrated approach offers a scalable and adaptive framework to enhance national security in a complex and dynamic operational environment.

title: Efficient and Interpretable Skin Lesion Classification Using Light HybridViT: A Hybrid CNN-Transformer Model
authors: Foujia Afrose Tanha, Md Asif Shahriar Arpon
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Skin lesions, both malignant and benign, represent a significant global health issue, and the timely and precise diagnosis is essential to a better prognosis. Conventional clinical examination is commonly subjective, time-consuming, and limited in inadequate resource-based settings. Deep learning, including CNNs and Vision Transformers (ViTs), has demonstrated potential, but with limitations: CNNs can only detect local features, and ViTs need extensive datasets and extensive computing power. To solve these issues, we suggest Light-HybridViT, a lightweight hybrid CNN-ViT network that uses MobileNetV2 as the base to integrate CNN-based local feature extraction with ViT-based global context modeling. Light-HybridViT was tested on the FLUO-SC dataset at six different skin-lesion types, and had a test accuracy of 98.42, outperforming standalone CNN models and ViT models. The robustness was checked using 5-fold cross-validation, whereas interpretability was checked using SHAP and LIME analyses, which identified some important areas generating predictions. The model shows an effective, precise, and explainable answer to automated skin-lesion classification.

title: Optimizing Renewable Energy Sources in Smart Grids with AI
authors: Neyara Radwan, Rasha Abousamra, Hassan Abdulmouti, Sara Alameeri, Beenu Mago, Mohammad A. Hamdan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: The incorporation of renewable energy sources (RES) into smart grids is a significant source of challenges stemming from their unstable and decentralized attributes. The crucial aspect of energy-efficient and reliable current power systems is the optimization of the use and management of these sources. This manuscript undertakes the use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) techniques to optimize renewable energy within smart grids. We provide a complete framework that utilizes AI-driven techniques, such as machine learning classifiers, predictive analytics, and optimization models, to advance accurate forecasting, effective energy distribution, and energy storage via renewables. The recommended methodology covers the essential issues of load balancing, demand-supply forecasting, and time-sensitive decision-making in decentralized energy systems. Energy efficiency as well as cost-effectiveness and grid reliability were significantly enhanced in tests performed using actual datasets. This study indicates the significant impact of AI on the speedy adoption of renewable energy in smart grids that lead to sustainable and resilient future energy systems.

title: A Machine Learning–Enabled GNN-Driven Adaptive Network Routing System for Realtime Packet Analysis and Routing Optimization
authors: Senthil Prakash K, Kathirvel E, Praveen C, Priyanka S, Purusothaman A M
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: A simulated environment software network, this paper proposes an adaptive network routing system with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$G N N$</tex>-driven real-time packet analysis and routing optimization. The primary objective of this research is to construct an intelligent routing framework that addresses the deficiencies of conventional deterministic routing algorithms, using relational reasoning abilities offered by Graph Neural Networks (GNN). For the purpose of integrating efficiency, mobility, and seamless interoperability with open-source networking Software, the proposed design integrates GNNs with the live routing engine running on the Ubuntu operating system. NS-3, a network simulator that has the ability to simulate different patterns of traffic, link conditions, and topologies in a packet-level simulation model will be applied in the proposed paper to develop the system. To extract the routing behavior based on the topological structure of the network graphs and the multidimensional traffic conditions, the routing model applies a GNN that utilizes a PyTorch framework. With the help of the NS-3 trace callback, four key PKT transmissions characteristics—delay, packet size, queue occupancy, and priority—are identified first in real-time, and then passed to the GNN inference engine to determine the optimum routing decisions at the next hop. This allows the system to include a real-time visualization interface in Ubuntu that updates packet flow density, node-level metrics, routing paths, and queue variations on the fly. Findings presented in this report indicate increased path stability through use of our proposed GNN-based method; a decrease in average packet delay by fifteen to twenty percent; minimal packet loss; and improved load balancing relative to traditional routing protocols. This work shows the potential to combine deep learning and open-source tools toward future AI-optimized communication infrastructures.

title: Explainable Hand Gesture Recognition using SHAP-Enhanced ParallelCNN
authors: Rahul Kumar Chaurasiya, Aman Yadav, Mitthu Kumar, Md Tarik Anvar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Hand gesture recognition is a vital component of machine interaction by intuition, and is used in assistive systems, virtual reality, and robotics. This article presents a parallel convolutional neural network (ParallelCNN) that classifies gestures in a stationary fashion, and it is enhanced by shapely additive explanations (SHAP) to interpret the result. The suggested system performs hand image processing on two convolutional paths, which obtain refined and general spatial patterns. This design has great accuracy, as well as being computationally efficient, which is useful in real time processes. Moreover, the SHAP integration creates pixel-level attribution maps, which graphically justify what areas of the hand drive model are predicting. Tests on the LeapGestRecog dataset demonstrate that the suggested framework is better than the prior standards, achieving an accuracy of more than 99% in tests and providing clear and understandable interpretability to the user. Although SHAP is very insightful, post-hoc map generation does incur a trade off in terms of computational load which is taken into account when evaluating usability in real life. The suggested solution facilitates powerful, open gesture detection, which validates confidence and usage in interactive systems.

title: A Bibliometrix Analysis on Clean Energy Towards Shaping a Sustainable India using R Studio
authors: Manisha Moharana, Smrutirekha Mohanty, Ananya Mitra
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: The transition to clean energy is a key factor in achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), as well as combating climate change-related issues. India, with its rapidly growing economy, has distinctly increased investment in renewable energy sources and sustainability studies to meet an ever-growing energy demand while simultaneously reducing environmental implications. The paper is a bibliometrical work titled A Bibliometrix Analysis on Clean Energy Towards Shaping a Sustainable India using R Studio, which provides a systematic research investigation of trends in research, the development of themes, and patterns of collaboration within the field of clean energy. The given work includes an extensive review of the publications associated with the topic of clean energy in 2015-2025, concerning the number of 894 research articles based on 346 different sources. To have greater coverage and reliability, the data set has been compiled by combining records in both the Scopus and Web of Science databases. With the help of the Bibliometrix package of R Studio, the paper will index key performance indicators, co-authorship networks, and patterns related to the keyword co-occurrence to identify the influential contributors, emergent themes, and changing directions in research that are influencing the sustainable energy picture in India. Results have shown that the volume of publications has been growing at an annual rate of 46.43 per cent, thus reflecting a strong increase in the scholarly interest regarding the clean-energy and sustainability transitions in the Indian context. The key areas of research defined are clean energy, renewable energy, Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), strategies of mitigation of climate change, and energy policy frameworks. Prominent scholars are developing community projects that create strong transnational connections, thus making interdisciplinary fusion. Thematic mapping has become a rising technique in analysis, and the core motor themes have been on SDGs and renewable-energy technologies. The paper ends with policy implications and indicates a lack of diverse future research directions required to continue to develop the scholarly literature of clean energy in India.

title: Resistorless DVCCTA-Based Memristor Emulator for Analog/Digital/Neuromorphic Applications
authors: Prerna Rana, Alak Majumder, Vassilis Alimisis, Paul P. Sotiriadis
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: Memristor devices have gained considerable attention for applications in neuromorphic computing, adaptive learning systems, and analog signal processing. However, practical hardware realization is limited by the complexity of fabrication and device variability. This paper proposes a minimal, resistorless memristor emulator based on a Differential Voltage Current Conveyor Transconductance Amplifier (DVCCTA) and a single grounded capacitor. The proposed configuration eliminates the need for passive resistors by exploiting the inherent current-mode processing and voltage-controlled transconductance of the DVCCTA. The grounded capacitor serves as the state-variable element, enabling dynamic modulation of memductance in response to the input signal. Simulation results for TSMC 180nm on Cadence confirm that the emulator reproduces the characteristic pinched hysteresis loop in the current-voltage plane, with hysteresis area shrinking at higher frequencies, consistent with memristive behavior. Owing to its minimal component count, resistorless structure, and CMOS compatibility, the proposed design is well-suited for low-power and area-efficient implementations. The versatility of the design is demonstrated through its use in analog, digital, and neuromorphic applications.

title: Corrections to “45°/90° Broadband Phase Shifter MMICs Using Switchable Electric–Magnetic Coupling Path Architecture”
authors: Na Ji, Guangxu Shen, Dingyuan Zeng, Wenjie Feng, Wenquan Che

title: A Hybrid Flower Pollination-Slide Mould Algorithm for DG Optimal Placement
authors: Andi Nur Aulia Azhar Mangkona, Ardiaty Arief, A. Hasniaty, Muhammad Bachtiar Nappu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Determining the best locations and capacities for DG units in distribution networks remain challenging due to the non-convex characteristics of the optimization problem and the complexity of radial system topologies. To overcome these issues, this paper introduces a hybrid optimization method that combines the FPA with the SMA. The proposed approach aims to minimize power losses and improve voltage profiles by identifying the optimal locations and capacities of DG units within a radial distribution network. Its effectiveness is demonstrated using the IEEE 33-bus simulation system for cases involving single, two, and three DG units. Simulation results indicate that the hybrid FPA-SMA method consistently outperforms both the FPA and SMA algorithms individually. The hybrid technique reduces losses to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{102.79 kW}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 4. 1 1}$</tex> kVAr with a DG, 82.9 kW and 56.78 kVAr with two DGs, and achieves the minimum losses of 70.25 kW and 48.38 kVAr with three DGs. The minimum system voltage also improves from below 0.95 p.u. in the baseline to 0.9718 p.u. with three DG units. These results demonstrate that the hybrid FPA-SMA offers an effective and robust solution for DG integration, improving loss reduction, voltage stability, and overall system performance.
