title: Robust Vehicle and Pedestrian Detection in Adverse Weather Via a Weather-Aware Transformer
authors: Xingquan Cai, Jiawei Tang, Hongli Ding, Yuanxiang Zhao, Ying Li
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: Pedestrian and vehicle in adverse weather detection technology based on deep learning has attracted increasing attention. However, YOLO-based detectors degrade under low contrast and noise, causing missed small or occluded targets, while DETR-based models suffer from slow convergence and limited multi-scale sensitivity. To address these challenges, a robust weather-aware transformer for vehicle and pedestrian detection, RWT-DETR, is proposed. Firstly, a Strip-Oriented Feature Gating (SOFG) module is designed to improve the capture of strip-like and directional features of traffic targets. Secondly, a Signed Attention Decomposition (SAD) mechanism is designed to enable the encoder to model both positive and negative correlations, thereby enhancing the model's robustness against noise interference and feature blurring. Finally, an Adaptive Multi-scale Fusion with Gating (AMFG) module is proposed to achieve dynamic calibration and denoising of cross-scale features, significantly improving semantic alignment. Experimental results show that, compared with the baseline, the proposed method improves mAP@0.5 and mAP@0.5:0.95 by 2.4% and 3.0%, respectively. Meanwhile, it outperforms existing mainstream models, confirming its effectiveness under adverse weather conditions.

title: Classifier Weighted Mixture models
authors: Elouan Argouarc'h, Franc¸ois Desbouvries, Eric Barat, Eiji Kawasaki, Thomas Dautremer
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: This paper proposes an extension of standard mixture stochastic models, by replacing the constant mixture weights with functional weights defined using a classifier. Classifier Weighted Mixtures enable straightforward density evaluation, explicit sampling, and enhanced expressivity in variational estimation problems, without increasing the number of components nor the complexity of the mixture components.

title: Uncertainty Quantification for Hallucination Detection in Large Language Models: Foundations, Methodology, and Future Directions
authors: Sungmin Kang, Yavuz Faruk Bakman, Duygu Nur Yaldiz, Baturalp Buyukates, Salman Avestimehr
publish: IEEE BITS the Information Theory Magazine
abstract: The rapid advancement of large language models (LLMs) has transformed the landscape of natural language processing, enabling breakthroughs across a wide range of areas including question answering, machine translation, and text summarization. Yet, their deployment in real-world applications has raised concerns over reliability and trustworthiness, as LLMs remain prone to hallucinations that produce plausible but factually incorrect outputs. Uncertainty quantification (UQ) has emerged as a central research direction to address this issue, offering principled measures for assessing the trustworthiness of model generations. We begin by introducing the foundations of UQ, from its formal definition to the traditional distinction between epistemic and aleatoric uncertainty, and then highlight how these concepts have been adapted to the context of LLMs. Building on this, we examine the role of UQ in hallucination detection, where quantifying uncertainty provides a mechanism for identifying unreliable generations and improving reliability. We systematically categorize a wide spectrum of existing methods along multiple dimensions and present empirical results for several representative approaches. Finally, we discuss current limitations and outline promising future research directions, providing a clearer picture of the current landscape of LLM UQ for hallucination detection.

title: Deep Learning-based Volition Detection and Action Potential Extraction for Fully Automated Diagnosis of Neuromuscular Disease Using Needle Electromyography Signals
authors: Soomin Chung, Ilhan Yoo, Jinkyu Lee, Dongmin Kim, Kwangsoo Kim, Keewon Kim, Seung-Bo Lee
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics
abstract: Objective: This study aimed to develop a deep learning-based volition-detection model to automate the diagnostic process and improve neuromuscular disease classification using needle electromyography (nEMG) signals. Methods: The model was developed using 376 nEMG signals from 57 subjects and independently evaluated on an external dataset of 751 nEMG signals from 115 subjects at a single tertiary medical center. The proposed model directly processed raw nEMG signals to automatically extract volition signals (motor unit action potentials), eliminating the need for physician-dependent preprocessing. Results: The optimal segment length for volition detection was 0.060 s, and nEMGNet demonstrated the best overall performance. Model-extracted volition signals yielded higher segment-wise classification performance than raw nEMG signals, with area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUROC) values of 0.806 for myopathy, 0.819 for neuropathy, and 0.819 for normal cases, compared with 0.761, 0.728, and 0.733, respectively. At the patient-wise level, AUROC improved by 20.10% for myopathy, 23.15% for neuropathy, and 23.15% for normal cases compared with physician-detected volition data. Conclusion: The proposed volition-detection model significantly enhances neuromuscular disease classification performance while enabling a fully automated and less labor-intensive diagnostic workflow.

title: Transformer-Based Modular Motion Generation Through Shoulder–Arm Decoupling for Upper Limb Rehabilitation
authors: Muhammad Fawad Khan, Naveed Ahmad Khan, Fahad Hussain, Prashant K. Jamwal, Shahid Hussain
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biomedical Engineering
abstract: The shoulder girdle is one of the most complex components of the upper limb, and when coupled with the arm, modeling and prediction become even more challenging. To address this, we propose a modular motion-planning framework that explicitly decouples the shoulder girdle from the arm. This separation simplifies modeling, enhances interpretability, and improves adaptability for rehabilitation scenarios. By independently targeting scapular dynamics, the framework enables better generalization of motion prediction across subjects. For human-motion modeling, a Transformer-based deep learning architecture is employed to capture nonlinear dependencies between joint angles and scapular motion. The model accepts joint-specific features as input and predicts shoulder girdle configurations, which are then integrated with arm trajectories to reconstruct complete upper limb motion. In the exoskeleton-mapping stage, a machine learning framework translates predicted human motion into the configuration space of a 6- Degree of freedom (DOF) rehabilitation exoskeleton. This ensures that generated trajectories are physically realizable and clinically suitable. By isolating human-motion prediction from robot mapping, the framework remains modular, scalable, and resilient to subject-specific variability, making it ideal for personalized rehabilitation. The methodology was evaluated by comparing Transformer-based predictions with both experimental data, reinforcement learning models and long short-term memory across multiple rehabilitation tasks. Quantitative analyses included statistical measures (F1-Score, ANOVA, T-test) and kinematic error metrics (RMSE, DTW). Results demonstrated that the Transformer model achieved higher accuracy and better temporal alignment with experimental trajectories. Combining shoulder–arm separation with Transformer-based learning provides an effective and clinically relevant solution for generating human-like motion in upper limb rehabilitation.

title: Physics-Informed Neural Networks with Learning Rate Annealing and Domain-Decomposition Subnetworks for Solving 3D Maxwell’s Equations
authors: Haoran Qin, Tiancheng Zhang, Peili Ping, Huaguang Bao, Aiqaing Cheng, Zongguang Yu, Dazhi Ding
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: In this work, a novel approach based on physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) has been proposed for solving three-dimensional (3D) Maxwell's equations. The proposed method employs a modified fully-connected neural architecture integrated with transformer networks, which significantly enhances the capacity in minimizing approximation errors. A learning rate annealing strategy is adopted to dynamically adjust the penalty weights based on loss gradients, thereby balancing multiple objective terms and improving prediction accuracy. To handle multi-material domains, dedicated subnetworks are used along with enforced boundary conditions to ensure field continuity at interfaces. The accuracy and robustness of the proposed framework are validated through simulations of resonant cavities and waveguides, showing excellent agreement between predicted and analytical field solutions. The results show that the proposed approach reduces prediction errors compared to vanilla PINNs, while maintaining stable convergence behavior. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of the method for solving 3D electromagnetic problems within Cartesian coordinates. The method uses linear dielectric materials and boundaries aligned with the coordinate axes. The framework provides a foundation for future extensions to arbitrary boundaries, multi-scale geometries, and nonlinear dielectric materials, thereby broadening its applicability in practical engineering design and analysis.

title: Flux-Controllable Design and Analysis of a Permanent Magnet Vernier Motor for Torque–Speed Performance Enhancement
authors: Deyang Fan, Linhao Qiu, Xiaoyong Zhu, Zixuan Xiang, Yixuan Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: To address the performance balance between high torque at low speeds and a wide flux-weakening speed range in permanent magnet vernier motors (PMVMs), this paper proposes a flux-controllable permanent magnet vernier (FC-PMV) motor. The contribution of this motor lies in the design of the air-gap flux density amplitude based on the flux-controllability module. Through the design of the flux-controllable module, the motor's torque output and speed regulation range have achieved comprehensive improvement. By trade-off optimization design of the flux-controllable module topology, the contradiction between improving torque and expanding the speed range is partially resolved. It is observed from the electromagnetic performance analysis that the FC-VPM motor exhibits a wide speed range and a high-torque capability at low speeds, which verifies the effectiveness of the motor design.

title: Towards Optimal User Cooperative Mobility for Mobile Ad hoc Networks with User-Specific Mobility Constraints
authors: Jihyeok Yang, Tutomu Murase
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: This paper proposes a user mobility control method that explicitly considers heterogeneous user mobility constraints in ad hoc networks. In recent mobile communication systems, the shift towards higher frequencies has reduced coverage areas, making reliable communication increasingly difficult to maintain. Although ad hoc networks have attracted attention as a potential solution, simply forming them is insufficient to ensure adequate communication quality. To address this issue, several studies have proposed cooperative user mobility control methods, which improve communication performance by guiding users to geographically favorable positions to maximize throughput. However, these methods typically employ a spring-model-based heuristic, assuming that cooperative users can move to designated positions. In practice, users may have heterogeneous mobility constraints due to mobility costs, such as being unable to move or being able to move only within a limited distance. As a result, existing methods may fail to function effectively, leading to throughput degradation. To overcome this limitation, we propose a new spring model with an upper bound on movement distance and a heuristic method for finding user positions that maximize throughput while accounting for user mobility. The proposed method supports two operational modes: decentralized control, used when information on users’ mobility constraints is unavailable, and centralized control, used when such information is available. Numerical results show that the proposed method achieves greater throughput improvement over a previous method as the standard deviation of users’ mobility constraints increases, reaching about 40% under the evaluated scenarios. These results confirm that explicitly considering user mobility can further improve throughput.

title: Current Circulation Strategy for Active Thermal Control in Interleaved DC-DC Converters
authors: Abhishek Chanekar, Nachiketa Deshmukh, Abhinav Arya, Sandeep Anand
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: Reliable interleaved dc-dc converters are desirable in numerous applications, including but not limited to, electric vehicle chargers, dc micro-grids, data centers, and industrial power supplies. In these power converters, cyclic variations in the temperature of power semiconductor devices (PSDs) occur due to varying load power and/or ambient conditions. These temperature swings cause degradation and eventual failure of PSDs, which affects system reliability. To enhance the expected lifetime of PSDs in interleaved dc-dc converters, this article proposes a current circulation strategy for active thermal control. The proposed thermal controller uses sinusoidal current circulation to thoughtfully change the power loss in PSDs and reduce the overall swings in their temperature. In addition to the reduction in temperature swings, the average thermal stress of the PSDs in each parallel connected converters is also equalized, despite the presence of practical non-uniformities in the design. Note that these changes in the thermal profile of the PSDs are done without altering the load power requirement with no extra circuitry required. The selection of parameters in the proposed thermal controller and their effect on the overall system performance are also delineated. Lastly, the effectiveness of the suggested approach for changing power loss in the PSDs is validated. A 1.5 kW solar PV to battery charger is developed to validate the proposed technique, in which the expected lifetime of the PSDs is increased by up to 37.8%.

title: Plug-and-Play Machine Learning-Based Model Predictive Control
authors: Yuxiao Ji, Yao Shi, Ming Xiao, Zhe Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control of Network Systems
abstract: This work presents a plug-and-play (PnP) machine learning-based model predictive control (ML-MPC) framework for interconnected nonlinear systems. Assuming that each subsystem is input-to-state stable (ISS), the stability of the entire system is ensured via the small-gain theorem. Sufficient conditions are derived to guarantee stability when MPCs using ML models are integrated into or used to replace existing controllers. The effectiveness of the framework is demonstrated through a numerical example and a nonlinear chemical process network.

title: RayDETR: Ray-based Geometric Consistency for UAV Multi-View 3D Object Detection
authors: Mingkui Feng, Hancheng Yu, Xiaoyu Dang, Zhongchen Li
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) have become essential platforms for 3D object detection. In contrast to DETR-style 2D detectors, the Euclidean distance between 3D predictions and the ground truth does not serve as an accurate proxy for sampling error due to complex projection geometry. To overcome this limitation, we propose the perpendicular distance from a predicted point to the ray connecting the camera optical center and the ground-truth, which serves as a precise geometric measure of sampling difficulty. Although this ray distance provides an ideal sampling reference, the isotropic circularity resulting from equal ray distances constrains the approach's ability to capture object shape and orientation. We therefore introduce an anisotropy-aware elliptical mapping that reformulates the circular projection into an ellipse aligned with the object's geometry. By integrating the ellipse-corrected ray distances into both the bipartite matching cost and the regression loss, we develop RayDETR-a geometry-consistent, multi-view 3D detection framework. Experiments on the UAV3D dataset demonstrate that RayDETR achieves state-of-the-art performance without compromising inference speed.

title: AMF-Net: A Multimodal Attention-Based Network for Rice Yield Prediction
authors: A. S. Radhamani, Laghumavarapu Chanikya, Gokaraju Surya Satya Narayana Raju, Venkata Sri Hari Haranath Pelluri, Byreddy Uday Pujith Reddy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Precise prediction of rice yield is essential to world food security and climate-resilient agricultural planning; although it is rather difficult since meteorological and soil characteristics are highly non-linear and crop phenology is quite sensitive. Current Multimodal deep learning models are mainly based on static feature concatenation and do not readily adapt to the dynamic agronomic changes, which occur over the growing season and do not distinctly distinguish different sources of data. In this regard, this paper introduces a new architecture, Attentive Multimodal Fusion Network (AMF-Net), which is a dynamic form of integrating spatial Sentinel-2 multispectral images, phenology-constrained meteorological time-series, and a prior physicochemical soil characteristics. The framework uses modality specific encoders: Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for spatial-spectral features, a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) encoder with seasonal weather dynamics, and a dense encoder with soil constraints. They are incorporated through a multi-head self-attention which selectively weighs cross-modal contributions at different stages of growth. Assessed in various agro-climatic areas of Andhra Pradesh, the experimental findings indicate that AMF-Net significantly outperforms single-modality and fixed-fusion baselines. The model proposed has a Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.2024 \mathrm{t} / \text{ha}$</tex> and a Coefficient of Determination (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex>) of 0.6765, demonstrating high predictive consistency within a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 0.5 \mathbf{t} / \mathbf{h a}$</tex> tolerance band. Also, the attention mechanism offers critical interpretability to agricultural decision-making. With a very small footprint of 191,009 trainable parameters, the AMF-Net provides a very scalable, robust, and resource-efficient basis of real-time precision agriculture systems.

title: Hyperspectral Imaging System for the Broadband Characterization of Biological Tissues in the 500-5200 NM Range
authors: Matteo Savazzi, Emma Lacagnina, Alessandro Bossi, Paola Saccomandi
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: Hyperspectral imaging (HSI) is gaining interest in the medical field for diagnostics and surgical guidance. While most studies focus on the visible and near-infrared (VNIR) range, spectral characterization of biological tissues in the short-wave (SWIR) and mid-wave infrared (MWIR) remains very limited. For the first time in the literature, we present a broadband HSI system for characterizing biological tissues across the 500-5200 nm band, using three hyperspectral cameras covering the VNIR (400-1000 nm), SWIR (1000-2500 nm), and MWIR (2700-5200 nm) ranges. Preliminary results are reported for porcine fat and spleen, using two specimens per tissue and two samples per specimen. The pipeline includes image registration for consistent pixel- and ROI-level analysis across all bands. The results confirm tissue contrast in the VNIR and indicate that the SWIR and MWIR ranges provide complementary potential for tissue discrimination. This study supports the development of a spectral reference database and future HSI applications in medical diagnostics.

title: Bank Customer Churn Rate Prediction and Minimization Using Machine Learning
authors: Bollepalli Amrutha Varshini, Bolla Gayathri, V. Surya, K. Lalitha Devi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: The customer churn turnover, which is the termination of a relationship with a bank by a customer, is a major challenge to financial institutions that may result in loss of revenue and decreased long-term stability. With the rise in the competition in the banking industry, customer retention has become as important as customer acquisition. The proposed paper will suggest a machine learningbased solution to customer churn prediction and reduction. Customer data is analysed to predict the churn with several machine learning models, like Random Forest, AdaBoost, XGBoost, Logistic Regression, and Naive Bayes. Handling missing values, eliminating duplicates, and normalization of data are some of the preprocessing processes in the methodology. Also, approaches like SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique) are also used in combating imbalance in classes. The models are compared on performance measures like recall, accuracy and ROC-AUC, where AdaBoost is able to perform best in terms of recall, implying that it is very efficient in revealing at-risk customers. The system is a webbased application developed on the Flask framework that enables real-time churn prediction. This allows banks take proactive measures towards reducing churn by keeping high-risk customers.

title: Rover Based Autonomous Waste Segregation and Donation System
authors: Sanjay Ashok, Radhika P, Vytla Shreya, Sai Ruthvik
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: The current paper encompasses design and development, as well as implementation of an autonomous robot, integrating computer vision based on YOLOv8, sensor-based navigation and the IoT-enabled smart waste segregation monitoring. The system proposed uses the ESP32 microcontroller as the central processing unit connecting with the ultrasonic sensors to detect the obstacles and avoid them, a web camera to take realtime images of the waste, and servo motors to control clamps precisely. Quick and successful learning A YOLOv8 model is trained using a personal dataset of organic and inorganic waste materials with a training accuracy of 99 % and loss of 0.15 after eight epochs. TCP protocol is used to provide effective communication between the ESP32 and the vision processing unit, which is written in Python. Moreover, a dustbin fill sensor is used to keep track of the fill level of dustbins, and the data is sent to Firebase, which is utilized to provide real-time cloud-based monitoring, which can be accessed through an App Inventor interface of MIT. The system is proven workable through experimental findings concerning autonomous navigation, waste classification, and IoT integration to show a smooth end-to-end functionality. This study adds a holistic model of independent waste management, and its implications to smart city applications and sustainable living in cities are enormous.

title: Leveraging Machine Learning for the Epidemiological Study of Autism Spectrum Disorder
authors: Dinesh Krishnan P, Srinikethan A V, Savitha V, G.Elavel Visuvanathan, Harisudha Kuresan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: The problem of early diagnosis of autism spectrum disorders is a major issue in the neurodevelopment of children. In this study, a comprehensive comparative assessment of five convolutional neural network models is carried out for the risk assessment of autism spectrum disorders using facial image-based approaches under strict integrity conditions. In this study, a previously unknown issue of data leakage in the benchmark facial image dataset is identified and rectified. In this regard, 757 duplicate image filenames are removed from the benchmark facial image dataset before model development. Five models, namely, ResNet50, EfficientNet-B0, MobileNetV2, DenseNet121, and VGG16, are assessed using a benchmark dataset of 2140 images after applying the transfer learning technique and progressive unfreezing. Medical-grade evaluation parameters such as sensitivity, specificity, balanced accuracy, Matthews correlation coefficient, and area under the receiver operating characteristic curve are used to assess the performance of the five models. Among the five models, the highest performance is obtained using the ResNet50 model, which shows an accuracy of 78.10 %, an AUC of 0.864, and an MCC of 0.568. An ensemble of the ResNet50 and DenseNet121 models using a soft voting strategy shows an improvement in the AUC to 0.867, which is further enhanced to 0.878. Gradient-weighted class activation mapping was incorporated to enable visual explanations of the predictions made by the model. The results indicate that data leakage correction significantly impacts performance, emphasizing the importance of data partition in medical imaging studies.

title: Synchrochain: a Hybrid Ai Architecture for Real-Time Supply Chain Optimization
authors: P Sreejindeth, Padmavathy T
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Modern supply chains are subject to variable demand, uncertain lead times, and evolving sustainability targets. SynchroChain is presented as a hybrid intelligence layer in which data-driven models provide signal and rule-based guardrails maintain reliability when signals are weak. The system combines: (i) a Transformer that predicts customer intent with 77.3 % accuracy and computes urgency deterministically from the predicted intent, (ii) a lightweight, attention-inspired delayrisk classifier over order-level features (no explicit graph message passing) that reaches 84.13 % precision and a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$65.59 \% ~\mathrm{F} 1$</tex>-score, and (iii) a Proximal Policy Optimization agent that balances cost, service, risk, and carbon under explicit guardrails. A lightweight orchestrator coordinates these components, validates decisions against rules, and provides graceful fallbacks when confidence is low or errors occur. In a simulated CPU-only benchmark harness, the end-to-end pipeline achieves sub- 20 ms median latency, 61.1 requests/s throughput, and 99.87 % successful execution. These results indicate that combining learned signals with conservative rules provides a practical path to dependable, real-time decision support.

title: DECAF: Dual-Domain Evidential Cross-Attention Fusion Network for MRI-Based Cognitive Decline Staging
authors: Afshan Khan, Bushara A R, Bismi Mohammed Ashraf, Navya Raj, Muhammad Salah M. A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Accurate and reliable staging of cognitive decline using MRI remains a major challenge due to limited accuracy, weak interpretability, and unreliable uncertainty estimation in existing diagnostic methods. To address these limitations, this research introduces DECAF (Dual-domain Evidential CrossAttention Fusion Network), a novel deep learning framework designed for trustworthy MRI-based dementia staging. DECAF integrates spatial and frequency-domain features from MRI scans to capture complementary information for precise classification. A pre-trained EfficientNet-B0 extracts rich spatial representations, while a lightweight CNN analyzes log-magnitude 2D Fourier transforms to model frequency-domain patterns. These features are fused through a Transformer-based cross-attention mechanism, enabling adaptive focus on the most informative feature interactions. Furthermore, supervised contrastive loss enhances class separation across similar dementia stages, while Evidential Deep Learning provides calibrated uncertainty estimation, strengthening clinical trust. Experimental results demonstrate DECAF's superior performance, achieving an accuracy of 0.9888 and a Cohen's Kappa of 0.9838, surpassing CNN (0.8725,0.8163) and Transformer (0.9588,0.9406) baselines. The confusion matrix reveals zero misclassifications in “Very Mild”, “Mild”, and “Moderate Dementia” stages, confirming its strong discriminative ability. Overall, DECAF establishes a state-of-the-art, interpretable, and reliable framework for automated dementia staging, advancing diagnostic precision and clinical reliability.

title: Predictive Maintenance in Smart Homes Using ML
authors: Najlaa Abd Hamza, Wafaa M. Salih Abedi, Shatha Habeeb Jafer Al-Khalisy, Alaa Yahya Aref Ahmad, Hanady Almahmood
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Predictive maintenance has come out as a core propriety in smart home management through allowing the timely objectification and fixing of probable system failures which in turn leads to a decrease in costs and a tolerance on system reliability. This research paper analyses how machine learning (ML) techniques will revolutionize the design of predictive maintenance systems in smart homes offering an efficient tailored maintenance service. The proposed plan which runs through real-time data collection from the Internet of Things (IoT) interconnected devices, sensors, and the like reveals significant patterns or anomalies that most likely precede equipment failure. Employing key ML algorithms namely supervised learning and anomaly detection models, the proposed system provides a highly precise prediction of the maintenance needs. The framework prioritizes scalability, energy efficiency, and data privacy, which are all the unique problems that heterogeneous smart home systems present. Experimental evaluations demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed system in minimizing downtime, optimizing maintenance schedules, and improving overall system performance. This research contributes to the development of smarter, self-sustaining homes that enhance user convenience and operational efficiency through intelligent maintenance solutions.

title: X-DOA-SBOA: An Explainable Hybrid Optimized Transformer Framework for Trustworthy Brain Tumor Diagnosis from Multi-Modal MRI Scans
authors: M. Sai Prasad, P.Pramod Kumar, A. Manjula
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: This research work proposes X-DOA-SBOA, a framework for reliable brain tumour diagnostics that makes use of explainable hybrid optimised transformers. Using a multi-modal Transformer-UNet backbone, the model incorporates the Dream Optimisation Algorithm (DOA) for adjusting hyperparameters globally and the Success-Based Optimisation Algorithm (SBOA) for optimised weight refinement. Furthermore, confidence maps and Grad-CAM are used to improve clinical transparency. Experiments conducted on the BraTS2020/BraTS2021 benchmark dataset show that the proposed framework outperforms prior representations like U-Net, Attention U-Net, then TransUNet. Improved boundary accuracy to 4.03 Hausdorff Distance and Dice scores of 0.942 (WT), 0.901 (TC), along with 0.854 (ET) are achieved by the proposed technique. Along with a 98.73% success rate, the area under the curve (AUC) for tumour classification is 0.994. These findings provide more evidence that explainable learning driven by optimising can be useful in clinic for accurate cancer detection.

title: A Smart IoT-Based Robotic Surveillance System for Remote Monitoring
authors: Paarth Singh, Konnankii Saiisarran, Peethala Snehith, S. Umamaheswari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Current research on autonomous systems and mobile robots remains one of the most significant global research fields. The systems function as essential components which enable navigation during search-and-rescue missions. The applications face their biggest challenges in dynamic environments which feature sudden environmental changes that create operational difficulties for their systems. The use of computers enables fast detection of dynamic obstacles together with living things which results in reduced collision incidents and improved search efficiency. The conventional navigation systems rely on static heuristics which use offline data for their operations. The methods provide correct results, but they require extensive time, and their results demand human evaluation. The development of artificial intelligence AI together with machine learning ML creates a new method which enables automated systems to discover hidden patterns within environmental data. The study presents a complete model which enables robots to predict their movements and navigate based on current research in probabilistic robotics and feature extraction techniques. The research explores and clarifies hybrid path planning through its application of unique methods to autonomous mobile robots. The system design integrates an Arduino UNO with an ESP32 Cam-based system which operates through Dynamic Spanning Tree Coverage (D-STC) and Hybrid RRT-A* and Triangular Segmented Interpolation (TSI) algorithms. The model uses transfer learning through pre-trained models while it employs dynamic window methods to prevent collisions and optimized classifiers to identify the final entity. The proposed system achieves excellent results through its <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{9 8. 2 5 \%}$</tex> coverage efficiency and only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{3. 0 6 \%}$</tex> overlap rate. The results exceed those of standard machine learning benchmarks. The paper goes on to present an innovative research framework which expands to new interdisciplinary research domains.

title: Legal Text Generation Using Transformer Models for Contract Drafting
authors: Muayad Hassan Al-Tawalbeh, Mahmoud Mokhtar Abdelmoghies, Nawel Ben Sassi, Majd Waleed Almanasra, Shuq Hussein, Ahmed Ali
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Contracts drafting is a thorough task that requires a high level of specialization and precision. The productive possibilities of NLP’s evolution of legal experts automation have attracted people's considerable interest, especially when it comes to transformer-based models such as GPT, BERT that generate some exceptional texts. This study investigates the applicability of transformers for the creation of top-notch legal paragraphs customized for contracts. By adapting these models to the specifics of the field, we want to determine whether they can effectively generate the appropriate, decent, and lawfully concluded sentence clauses. We confront the issues of ensuring legal compliance and dealing with regulatory language as well as the one of avoiding the generation of biased or otherwise inappropriate content. The study is based on metrics like BLEU, ROUGE, and expert legal review to measure the generated text's quality. The results of our experiments showcase that transformer-based models can bring about a reduction in drafting time while preserving legal accuracy. This study is aimed at contributing to the swiftly developing AI applications in law area by shedding light on the potential and future avenues for automated text generation’s use in law practice.

title: Analysis of VOCs for Prediction of Respiratory Disease Using TabR
authors: M. Latha, Hemavathy S, Aruthra B, Adarsh Annadurai
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Respiratory diseases such as asthma, upper respiratory tract infections (URTI), and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) are challenging to diagnose at early stages due to overlapping symptoms and limitations of conventional diagnostic methods. Exhaled breath analysis by volatile organic compounds (VOCs) in breath reflect underlying respiratory conditions. This paper presents a deep learning-based framework for multi-class respiratory disease classification using breath-derived VOC features and demographic data. The proposed approach employs a Lion-optimized Tabular Representation Learning (TabR) model to effectively model structured VOC data. Model validation is performed using standard evaluation metrics and stratified five-fold cross-validation. Experimental results demonstrate reliable classification performance and improved class-wise discrimination compared to an attention-based baseline. The study highlights the potential of modern tabular deep learning models for respiratory disease screening and could be used for point-of-care applications.

title: Optimized Ensemble Learning with SMOTE for Gastric Cancer Classification
authors: Sunipun Seemanta, Mahmudul Haque Shakir, Md. Saef Ullah Miah
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Gastric cancer (GC) is a common and mortal cancer worldwide, and the classification of its subtypes can be used as independent factors in determining the prognosis and therapeutic strategies. However, due to overlapping morphological characteristics as well as class imbalance in clinical datasets, it is difficult to distinguish the histological subtypes accurately. The objective of this study is to develop an optimal ensemble learning framework that balances the data through SMOTE and applies a hybrid soft voting ensemble that is formed with Random Forest, XGBoost and Logistic Regression classifiers. This study compare the results between the baseline and ensemble models. The findings indicate a noteworthy enhancement produced by the use of class rebalancing for minority classes. The ensemble model also performed better than all other single models as indicated by an accuracy of 90%. These results demonstrate that ensemble learning integrated with class balancing approach can achieve an accurate and equitable classification of gastric cancer subtypes, contributing to guiding precise and personalized clinical decisions.

title: An LLM-Based Framework for Structured Knowledge Extraction and Summarization from Patent Documents
authors: Gopakumar G, Sreeja M U
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Intellectual property information in the competitive landscape is quite valuable for companies around the world. Patent analytics provide insights that support commercialization risk mitigation and facilitate the use of existing patent literature to solve commercially relevant technical problems. With the patent documents available in public domain such as Google patents or WIPO portals, the volume of intellectual property data available for companies is huge and the traditional manual methods followed by companies for analyzing company and competitor patents pose a challenge. There are opportunities to leverage modern day technologies such as large language models (LLMs) in the field of intellectual property and patent analytics to generate valuable insights about the company and its competitors. Generating concise summary of patents is one of the very first steps companies adopt as part of such analysis. The proposed work is performed as a first step that aims at accomplishing the patent summarization with problem-solutionapplication ontology using two large language models (LLMs) and comparing the results both qualitatively and quantitatively. The results also discusses the impact of hyperparameter tuning for patent summarization tasks.

title: Predictive Analytics for Customer Churn in Retail
authors: Tamadher Aldabbagh, Mahmoud Alghizzawi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: In the retail industry, the issue of customer churn poses a serious danger to business revenues in the short and long term. The method of predictive analytics, which is based on machine learning and data science, is a powerful tool for businesses to spot potential customer churners and create targeted retention plans. The main idea of this research is to design and apply predictive models for customer churn in retail. We look into customer data, such as purchase behaviors, demographic profiles, and engagement statistics, to create and evaluate machine learning approaches like logistic regression, decision trees, and gradient boosting. As our research shows, data preprocessing, feature engineering, and the comprehensibility of the model are all important in attaining the best prediction results. Our proposed approach is a clear example of how predictive analytics can empower retailers to address customer retention proactively, schedule marketing efforts more effectively, and improve customer satisfaction. Our analysis underscores the enormously innovative nature of data-led strategies as they can dramatically improve customer retention and, in turn, lead to business success in a tough retail market.

title: Trends and Concepts in International R&D Organization
authors: Zakariya Chabani, Soumaya Askri, Mohamed Dawood Shamout, Salima Hamouchi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: The globalization of research and development (RD) activities has become an industry focus of multinational corporations (MNCs) to foster innovation, competitiveness, and diffusion of knowledge. This research evaluates the models of global RD structures and their changes, focusing on centralization and decentralization ramifications. Centralized RD models offer efficiency, cost savings, and intellectual property protection. However, these centralized systems may be rigid toward serving different regional markets. Decentralized models improve local responsiveness and regionally driven innovation but suffer from coordination, resource, and knowledge management challenges. RD hubs and integrated networks are the dominant hybrid structures in organized science. They enable firms to control strategic decisions while allowing local innovation. Moreover, traditional silos of the past are dissolving owing to innovations in digitalization, AI, and big data, which change RD by allowing collaboration and predictive analysis on management. The analysis highlights the criticality of strong governance, cross-functional collaboration, and open innovation in optimizing global RD systems. In the increasingly interdependent, information-driven economies, the ability of firms to embed themselves into global knowledge networks will define the timeliness and quality of emerging technology adoption at a strategic level while preserving the agility needed to respond proactively to competition over sustained periods.

title: Regional Education Management Intelligent Construction: A Multi-Modal Fusion Framework Based on Graph Neural Networks
authors: Jie Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI in Education Technology and Applications (AIETA)
abstract: In the context of educational digital transformation, regional education management faces significant challenges such as data silos leading to delayed decision-making, uneven resource allocation causing educational inequities, and privacy risks in intelligent tool applications. This study proposes a multi-modal fusion framework based on graph neural networks to address these dilemmas and optimize intelligent construction. The framework employs pre-trained GraphSAGE for encoding regional education data networks, extracting node-level and graph-level features from schools' resources and student performance. BERT processes policy texts and user feedback to generate semantic embeddings. A dynamic fusion layer uses policy semantic embeddings as queries to adjust attention weights on education graph features dynamically, mitigating generalization limitations of static mechanisms. Joint representation learning integrates multihead self-attention, feed-forward networks, graph convolutions, and multi-layer perceptrons, enhanced by a Mixture of Experts (MoE) modification for handling heterogeneous data. Training utilizes mean squared error loss combined with contrastive learning to boost robustness. Experiments on the Students' Performance Dataset demonstrate superior performance, with our model achieving up to 0.95 accuracy, outperforming baselines like MLP, GCN, and GraphSAGE. Results highlight improved decision efficiency, fairness, and privacy safeguards, providing practical strategies for regional education management optimization.

title: RISCNet: A Novel Deep Learning Model for Real-Time Radiation induced sickness Classification
authors: Raja Kafi Rahmatullah, Humayra Akhter, Kawshik Das, Md Shamim Ahmed, Ramit Kumar Sadhukhan
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: Exposure to ionizing radiation poses significant health risks, leading to a wide spectrum of radiation-induced toxicities, ranging from localized effects like cataracts to systemic acute reactions such as extensive vomiting. The accurate and early classification of these symptoms are critical for effective clinical intervention and patient management. However, hybridity of data and scarcity of balanced exposure datasets presents a significant challenge for traditional diagnostic method. This research introduces the Radiation Induced Sickness Classification Network (RISCNET), an adaptation of the XDNet hybrid machine learning framework, specifically tailored to classify radiation-induced sickness based on dosimetric and clinical features. Unlike generic AI systems, RISCNet is tailored to the unique characteristics of improved accuracy and handling complex data sets originated from radiation induced sickness. The framework combines the Deep Neural Network (DNN) to extract patterns from high-dimensional numerical data, such as absorbed dose and exposure duration, while incorporating CatBoost to handle categorical clinical symptoms and demographic variables. To overcome the inherent limitations of small medical datasets, a Synthetic Data Augmentation Network (SDAN) was utilized to generate a robust training environment. The process starts with gathering input data from cesium-137 isotope around from nuclear weapons testing and from the accidents at Chernobyl and Fukushima. Based on calibration tests of multiple device designs using the same detector), including radiation levels and environmental parameters, followed by preprocessing to clean and normalize the data. The RISCNet model is then trained on a portion of the dataset and tested on 30% to evaluate its performance. The trained model provides accurate radiation-based sickness classification, including real-time data processing. Our suggested model achieved 99.79% accuracy, with same amount of precision and recall, as demonstrated by the confusion matrix for six-class radiation induced sickness classification. These results validate its effectiveness for real-time applications which also outperforms significantly baseline single-architecture models. By providing real-time, interpretable classification of toxicities like ocular damage and gastrointestinal distress, this framework offers a powerful tool for clinicians in radiotherapy. It also enhances safety, reduces costs, and minimizes human error, offering scalable solutions for radiation sickness and other domains. Future developments can include advanced unsupervised techniques, integrating multi-modal 3D radiomic imaging and real-time wearable sensor data to enhance personalized diagnostic precision and clinical explainability in radiological emergencies and multi-modal data handling for broader applicability and real-world deployment.

title: Algorithmic Decomposition of ESG Scores: A Sustainability Governance Analysis of Indian Listed Companies
authors: Sadhana S, M. Velavan, Sowmiya R, C. Thirumal Azhagan, Dhandayuthapani Paramasivam
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) scores have become widely used indicators of corporate sustainability performance. However, the internal structure and informational content of these composite scores remain insufficiently examined, particularly in emerging markets. This study investigates the construction and explanatory structure of ESG scores for Indian listed companies using firm-level ESG data obtained from CRISIL for the year 2024. A diagnostic analytical framework is applied to decompose the composite ESG score into its underlying Environmental (E), Social (S), and Governance (G) components. Using descriptive statistics, correlation analysis, ordinary least squares (OLS) regression, residual diagnostics, and clustering techniques, the study evaluates the extent to which the aggregate ESG score is explained by its components and identifies structural patterns in firm-level ESG performance. Results show that the composite ESG score is largely an aggregation of its underlying components, with Governance exhibiting the highest marginal contribution to the final ESG score. Residual analysis reveals minor deviations between predicted and observed ESG values, suggesting limited but observable rating inefficiencies. Cluster analysis further identifies distinct sustainability profiles among firms rather than a uniform ESG progression. To enhance methodological rigor, robustness checks using Principal Component Analysis (PCA), sector-interaction regression models, and bootstrap validation are proposed to examine stability of coefficients and sectoral heterogeneity. The findings indicate that while ESG scores provide a convenient summary indicator, they primarily represent structured combinations of underlying sustainability indicators rather than independent measures of sustainability performance. The study contributes to ESG analytics by providing a structural decomposition framework that improves interpretability and transparency in sustainability ratings.

title: AI Powered Smart Mobility: Enabling Vision Free Navigation for the Visually Impaired
authors: R. Prakash, V.S Nilauvarasi, R. Sona, R. Sivasakthidevi, E. Sophiya
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Many people who have impaired vision have difficulty being able to move independently because they have no access to real time awareness of their environment and no safe navigation assistance. While traditional assistive devices like walking sticks and guide dogs can give limited access to the surrounding space, they cannot give complete identification of every obstacle, every person, and every alternative route in a constantly changing environment. There is currently nothing in existence today that allows for true independent mobility. Our project proposes a solution to the existing problem, an AI-driven smart mobility system that enables an individual to navigate freely without any sight using an embedded Raspberry Pi platform. The device utilizes a video camera attached to the Raspberry Pi to continually record the surroundings of the user and employs computer vision techniques using deep learning algorithms to detect objects in the environment, recognize people and provide general assistance throughout the route. Using a training set of data, the software will determine if someone is classified as a known individual or unknown, hence providing contextsensitive audio feedback to the individual/user and thus helping them build confidence in their capacity to independently make decisions. Intelligent obstacle identification technology is also part of the device, which aids users in identifying safe alternate pathways with now increased navigational confidence. Additionally, integrated into the device are two special emergency buttons: One button will allow the user to receive a GPS-enabled location and navigation route to a pre-determined safe destination. The other button allows the user to send an emergency message to pre-arranged contacts, providing real-time location information as well. The combination of AI, GPS, and assistive feedback will help convert complex, visual data into actionable guidance, ultimately increasing safety, independence and situational awareness for visually impaired individuals. In this work, it was suggested that an AI-based smart mobility system be implemented to enable visually impaired people to navigate without vision using embedded computer vision, GPS, and emergency communication modules. The system is capable of real-time object detection, person recognition, and audiobased adaptive route guidance. Experimental analysis in both indoor and outdoor settings shows a detection rate of up to 96 %, person recognition accuracy of 95%, and emergency alert response time below 2.5 seconds. The findings affirm that the perception, navigation, and safety systems are wellintegrated to enhance situational awareness, navigation reliability, and user safety, which is why the proposed system can be used in real-life assistive mobility contexts.

title: Brain Tumor Classification in MRI Images: A Computationally Efficient Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Md Fahimul Kabir Chowdhury, Jannatul Ferdous
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Improving patient outcomes depends on the prompt and accurate diagnosis of brain tumors, but manual MRI scan analysis is still time-consuming and unreliable. Although deep learning has shown promise, many of the models that are now in use are computationally intensive and have difficulty handling the intrinsic complexity and variety of different types of brain tumors. In this work, we propose a lightweight yet high-performing Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for multiclass brain tumor classification, employing MRI images to target gliomas, meningiomas, pituitary tumors, and healthy (no tumor) instances. The model was rigorously evaluated on two publicly accessible datasets from Figshare and Kaggle. Leveraging efficient feature extraction and optimized training strategies, our CNN achieved classification accuracies of 99.03% and 99.28%, along with ROC scores of 99.88% and 99.94% on Dataset 1 and Dataset 2, respectively-all while utilizing significantly fewer parameters than popular pre-trained architectures. In contrast to cutting-edge models like DenseNet201, MobileNetV2, VGG19, Xception, InceptionV3, and ResNet50, our approach consistently demonstrated superior performance with reduced computational overhead. These findings highlight the potential of the proposed model as a practical and reliable diagnostic aid in clinical environments.

title: Automatic Soap Weighing and Shaping System
authors: N. Senthilnathan, Dhaaranesh A, Harsitha G S, Kalaiarasu R, Saran R
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The soap manufacturing industry requires precise weight and shape control to maintain product uniformity and quality. Manual weighing and shaping processes are time-consuming and susceptible to human error, making them unsuitable for large-scale production. This paper presents an automatic soap weighing and shaping system using a 10 kg load cell with HX711 amplifier for real-time weight measurement. When the measured weight exceeds the predefined <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 5 ~ g}$</tex> limit, a motor-driven trimming mechanism automatically removes excess material. The system is controlled by an Arduino Uno and includes a brush-assisted waste removal unit, with real-time status displayed on a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$16 \times 2$</tex> LCD. Experimental results demonstrate consistent trimming accuracy within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 2 ~\mathrm{g}$</tex> and a reduction in processing time compared to manual operation. The proposed system enhances efficiency, reduces labor dependency, and offers potential for future integration with conveyor systems, wireless data monitoring, and AI-enabled shape inspection.

title: Inverse Diagnostics for Energy Efficiency: Optimizing Heat Pump Placement in Greenhouses via Gradient-Flux Mapping
authors: Loubna Khaled, Rabih Dib, Bakri Abdulhay, Nazih Moubayed
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: The energy footprint of controlled environment agriculture is related to the power demands of microclimate control systems, particularly heat pumps, which are often oversized and inefficiently deployed due to a lack of precise diagnostic tools. This paper discusses the critical challenge of reducing electrical and fuel consumption by introducing a simple, low-computational-demand inverse method for optimizing the placement of system components. The methodology evaluates available multi-point experimental data on temperature and relative humidity stratification in a 332 m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, 3m high, thermal-polyethylene-covered Quonset greenhouse in Lebanon. The simple gradient inverse method is employed to compute the inferred heat flux, the moisture flux, and the density gradient, qualitatively describing the air movement. The results show the accumulation of heat and moisture at the top-far end of the studied structure, and the inferred air movement from low (bottom inlet) to high (top outlet) density areas. This convergence locates the dominant source of thermal stratification and buoyant flow, which is the primary driver of microclimate non-uniformity and energy waste. The derived technical decision is to strategically place the heat pump and vent openings to actively break the buoyant flow, rather than simply treating the average air volume. Also, the use of a variable perforation duct system for homogeneity at the ground (heating) and plant (cooling) levels for optimum uniformity. This work provides a practical, data-driven framework for power system engineers to optimize the deployment of thermal management equipment in agricultural and industrial settings.

title: AEFormer: A Hybrid Conv1D-Transformer Model for Concrete Damage Classification via Acoustic Emission Signals
authors: Aman Kumar Gaur, Nipun Bansal, Yashasvi Bansal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Acoustic Emission (AE) signals are an attractive, non-destructive testing technique for detecting damage in concrete structures; however, computational resource limitations limit the potential of applying deep learning-based methods for instant structural health monitoring (SHM) across all types of micro-structural health monitoring systems, with both high levels of accuracy and efficiency. The paper proposed AEFormer, a hybrid lightweight network comprising a 1D convolutional layer for local feature extraction, followed by a Transformer encoder that learns global patterns across the sequence of AE signals. With fewer than 28,000 trainable parameters, AEFormer achieved 99.82 % test accuracy, 0.0052 test loss, and per-class F1 scores >0.998, and significantly outperformed state-of-theart lightweight CNN and Tiny ANN baseline models. Therefore, these results indicate that AEFormer is a viable candidate for balancing accuracy and efficiency in real-time SHM, particularly on embedded platforms.

title: Load Flow Analysis of an Islanded Microgrid Using Modified Newton-Raphson Method
authors: Supraja Kethari, Srihitha Maddiboyina, Nandini Nadiminti, Archana Kanakadurga, Chandini Moode, Shaik Rafi Kiran
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The rapid growth of distributed energy resources and renewable generation has led to increased interest in microgrids as a reliable and flexible solution for modern power systems. When operating in islanded mode, microgrids are isolated from the main utility grid and must independently regulate voltage and frequency using local distributed generators (DGs). In such conditions, the absence of a slack bus and the presence of frequency variations invalidate the assumptions of conventional load flow techniques. This paper presents a modified Newton-Raphson method for islanded microgrids with droop-controlled DGs. In this method, the system frequency is treated as a state variable, and included voltage and frequency-dependent load models represent real-time operating conditions. A six-bus islanded microgrid is modelled and simulated in MATLAB to evaluate the effectiveness of the proposed method. The performance of the modified Newton-Raphson method is compared with a modified Gauss-Seidel method under similar system conditions. Simulation results show improved convergence characteristics, accurate voltage magnitudes and angles, and frequency estimation, demonstrating that the proposed method is suitable for islanded microgrid practical load flow analysis.

title: Intelligent Human–Computer Interaction for Navigation Control Through Vision-Based Hand Gesture Recognition
authors: Venkata Sai Aditya Kondru, Shankar Balla, Dhavalkumar Thakar, Dheeraj Velaga, Sri Lekha Bandla
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: This paper describes the importance of intelligent human-computer interaction in facilitating natural communication between human beings and the computer especially in navigation control as used in robotic and virtual worlds. The possibility of hand gestures being recognized visually has been a research topic of interest because of its non-contact feature, intuitive and non-hardware issue. It offers a systematic approach to the study, which combines adaptive hand segmentation, deep convolutional feature extractions, and temporal feature aggregations to predict dynamic hand gestures. Confidence-based filtering and attention-guided classification mechanism are used to boost discriminative learning and stable navigation choices respectively. Gesture-to-command mapping also employs temporal smoothing in order to allow continuous control. The performance evaluation proves to be better behavioral in comparison to four prevailing methods. The proposed system has the specificity of 0.95, Kappa coefficient of 0.90, Intersection over Union of 0.82 as well as Navigation Stability Index of 0.89 and achieves improvements of 4-12 percent as compared to deep static and handcrafted-feature methods. These findings suggest that the proposed framework is strong and sound in providing gesture-based navigation and can be applicable in the real-world intelligent interaction.

title: A Prisma-Based Hyperspectral Dataset for Agriculture
authors: Matei Debu, Kamal Marandskiy, Serban Oprisescu, Mihai Ivanovici
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: In this article we introduce a hyperspectral image dataset derived from PRISMA hyperspectral data, entitled HyDACI6. The dataset contains six cropped and filtered hyperspectral images acquired in 2024 over the area of interest to the North of Brasov City in Romania. The images were cropped to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$700 \times 700$</tex> pixels and the spectral bands corresponding to the atmosphere blocking were filtered out, resulting in 187 spectral bands out of the 239 original ones. The dataset contains the mask with the labels of the agricultural crops of the National Institute of Research and Development for Potato and Sugar Beet in Brasov. The HyDACI6 dataset is freely available as open data on Zenodo. We illustrate the usefulness of the proposed dataset with results on an agricultural crop identification application in agriculture.

title: Stock Price Prediction: ANN or RNN - An Analytical Study
authors: Partha Sarathi Paul, Rajendrani Mukherjee
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: Prediction of stock prices is a complex task for financial researchers and computer scientists. The intricate and ever changing dynamics of the market creates this complexity. Artificial Neural Networks (ANN) and Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN) are popularly used for learning and forecasting from historical data of stocks. This paper compares the structure and data processing abilities of these two models for forecasting stock prices of the following day. It is found in the study that while Artificial Neural Network can effectively capture patterns in relatively stable markets, Recurrent Neural Networks can demonstrate superior performance in capturing sequential dependencies in volatile periods due to their inherent memory capability. The results obtained show Recurrent Neural Network is a more robust framework for time-series prediction dynamic financial context. This paper provides insight into the comparative superiority of Recurrent Neural Network model for stock forecasting and contributes to more informed selection of model in financial machine learning applications.

title: Design and Evaluation of a Hybrid Quantum Model for Intrusion Detection Systems
authors: Sweetha S, Manikandan A, Vijayalakshmi M, Harini Mai V G
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: The paper presents a hybrid structure for an Intrusion Detection System (IDS) designed to enhance cybersecurity by using advanced technologies like Quantum Support Vector Machines (QSVM) and Quantum Neural Networks (QNN). With the rising number of cyberattacks, having effective detection solutions is essential. The proposed model uses quantum neural architecture and quantum kernel methods to identify and classify complex patterns in network traffic. The large-scale CIC DDoS (256,890 records) and UNSW-NB15 (191,694 records) data sets perform better on experimental evaluation. The hybrid quantum-classical system has a maximum accuracy of 99 per cent with the Noisy Intermediate-Scale Quantum (NISQ) environment, which is better than the traditional models. These findings support the high potential of considering quantum computing to enhance intrusion detection and enhance cyber resilience.

title: Blight Disease Detection and Severity Estimation in Crop Leaves Using Deep Learning with Grad-CAM
authors: Karishma P, Akash Velan A, Vishal Ganapathy S, Malathi M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Overall, crop diseases cause sizeable yield losses all over the world and are a significant menace to agriculture productivity and food security. Standard illness identification techniques are based on manual inspection, which is inherently subjective, time-consuming, and insufficiently early warning. Although several deep learning methods guarantee a high level of plant disease classification accuracy, most of the current models and setups are black-box methods and do not provide severity assessments, limiting the potential of such precision agriculture in the fields. This article proposes an interpretable deep learning method, which integrates classification and severity assessment for crop leaf blight infection inspection. A light-weight ResNet11 model is used to categorize three types of crop diseases: late blight, early blight, and healthy and an infection rate module that obtained an infected pixel ratio is implemented, followed by an introduced Grad-CAM to visualize infection areas heating maps to improve the interpretation. This proposed approach is evaluated on the PlantVillage dataset and can provide not only classification and severity level but also quantitative explanations, and interpretable output with an accuracy of 99.02

title: Comparative Analysis of Quantum and Multi-Model Deep Learning Approaches for Skin Lesion Classification
authors: Sireesha Moturi, Chinnapareddy Anjali Reddy, Dogiparthi Dhanusha, Shaik Chikli Asifa, Chandra Mouli Kathi, Sasidhar Bola
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: In medical image analysis, skin lesion classification is a crucial problem since early detection can greatly lower health risks. The possibility of using quantum machine learning to replace traditional deep learning models has been studied recently.This study is positioned as a comparative benchmarking analysis to empirically evaluate the practical performance of quantum-based learning models against established deep learning architectures under identical experimental conditions.In this work, a renowned research technique was used to first create a Quantum Neural Network (QNN) and a Quantum Support Vector Classifier (QSVC). Many conventional deep learning models were then employed for comparison because of simulator limitations and poor performance. To provide a fair experimental setting, EFAM-Net, MobileNetV3, ResNet18, and EfficientNet-B0 were trained and assessed using the same dataset. Classical deep learning models performed better than quantum-based models, which had a modest classification accuracy of 62-65 %. ResNet18 and EFAM-Net had validation accuracy of 83.62 % and 79.89 %, respectively, while EfficientNet-B0 had the greatest validation accuracy of 90.21 %, closely followed by MobileNetV3 with 90.15 %. These findings suggest that conventional deep learning models are now more appropriate for real-world medical image categorisation since they provide more accuracy and training stability than quantum-based methods.

title: Analysis of Lossless Coding Techniques for Uncalibrated Spectral Data From James Webb Space Telescope
authors: Xavier Fernández-Mellado, Pau Quintas-Torra, Joan Bartrina-Rapesta, Jordi Portell, Armando J. Pinho, Joan Serra-Sagristà
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: This work evaluates the performance of various lossless compression algorithms on data acquired by the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST). The dataset comprises spectral images from all four of its instruments (NIRCam, NIRISS, NIRSpec, and MIRI), where spectral information is represented spatially because of prism-based dispersion, and includes multiple bands generated through the up-the-ramp readout. We compare compressors that only exploit spatial redundancy -such as JPEG, FPACK variants, LZMA, and Zstandard- and methods that employ spatial and/or spectral redundancy -such as CCSDS 123.0-B-2 and FAPEC (CILLIC and DWT). Experimental results indicate that lossless techniques using spectral redundancy significantly reduce data volume and bits per sample, outperforming purely spatial approaches. In particular, FAPEC-DWT achieves the overall highest compression efficiency, confirming the advantage of incorporating spectral information. These findings support the integration of spectral-aware techniques in future space missions, to maximize data preservation under strict bandwidth constraints.

title: Self-Adaptive HOA Driven Deep Optimization Framework for Robust Infant Pose Estimation
authors: Kalaimathi R, S. Latha, Samiappan Dhanalakshmi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Accurate infant pose estimation is crucial for neonatal monitoring because it supports early detection of developmental and neurological issues in clinical practice. In this study, we present a Motion-Enhanced HRNet that utilizes a Self-Adaptive Hippopotamus Optimization Algorithm (SA-HOA) for selecting informative feature channels in infant pose estimation. The framework starts by enhancing depth images with contrast-limited adaptive histogram equalization (CLAHE) combined with gradient-based motion cues to emphasize subtle limb movements. A HRNet backbone extracts multi-level features, and SA-HOA is then employed to identify the most relevant channels while reducing redundancy. Optimized features pertaining to infant joint keypoint prediction are fed into the HRNet regressor to extract the HRNet regressor outputs. Our technique exhibits significant improvements in MSE(13.39), MAE(1.12), RMSE(3.60), PCK(99.5), and KLE(1.71) as it outperforms baseline approaches on the BabyPose dataset. Improvement can be attributed to the optimized feature motion-aware preprocessing, relevance optimization, and pose estimation metaheuristic search, which results in the framework's enhanced computational efficiency. This work illustrates the growing importance of optimization driven deep learning over pose estimation computational efficacy in clinically accurate infant monitoring systems.

title: A Whale Optimization Assisted Deep Metaheuristic Framework for IoT-Enabled Pipeline Leakage Detection and Failure Prediction
authors: K. Arulmani, V N R Sai Krishna Kari, B. Arun Prasath, D. Mohana Priya, Narayanam R S Lakshmi Prasanthi, Sathiya Priya T
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Leakage in oil, gas, and water pipelines poses severe environmental, financial, and operational challenges, especially when traditional monitoring systems fail to detect early or small leaks due to noisy sensor data and limited modeling intelligence. To address these limitations, this study introduces a Whale Optimization Assisted Deep Metaheuristic Learning (WOA-DML) framework designed for smart, IoT-enabled pipeline leakage detection and predictive failure analytics. The proposed system integrates heterogeneous pressure, acoustic, vibration, and flow-rate sensors with a hybrid CNN-BiLSTM model to effectively capture spatial-temporal signal patterns related to pipeline anomalies. The Whale Optimization Algorithm is employed to optimize hyperparameters and feature-selection weights, enabling faster convergence, improved anomaly sensitivity, and stable learning under uncertain operating conditions. Experimental evaluation using benchmark datasets demonstrates that the WOA-DML framework achieves a superior leakage detection accuracy of 98.46 %, with corresponding precision, recall, and F 1 -score values of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$97.92 \%, 98.63 \%$</tex>, and 98.27 %, respectively. Comparative analysis shows statistically significant improvements (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}<0.05$</tex>) over conventional CNN, LSTM, Hybrid CNN-LSTM, PSO-DL, and GA-DL models. The framework further reduces training time by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$23-31 \%$</tex> and converges within 17 iterations, highlighting its computational efficiency. Robustness testing under noise levels up to 30 % confirms that the model maintains accuracy above 94 %, demonstrating high resilience to sensor fluctuations and dynamic pipeline conditions. Overall, the statistical findings validate WOA-DML as a scalable, energy-efficient, and highly reliable solution for next-generation smart pipeline monitoring and proactive maintenance systems.

title: Hyperparameter Optimization in Sentiment Analysis Using Reinforcement Learning
authors: N.K. Priyadharsini, R. Kavitha, T. Navin Raaj, P. Kavibharathi, M. Aadith, Gokul Kannan R
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Hyperparameter optimization has been a vital part of enhancing the performance and generality of Deep Learning models. The existing optimization techniques like grid search and random search are computationally expensive and are incapable of dynamic changes based on feedback from models during training practice. A Hyperparameter Optimization Framework based on Deep Q-Learning that optimizes hyperparameters in a dynamic manner in real-time conditions in stand-alone applications related to sentimental analysis in texts have been designed as a solution for the existing systems issues. The work combines a Reinforcement Learning agent paired with a designed Sentiment Environment (SentimentEnv) that takes validation feedback from models iteratively during training sessions so that hyperparameters are adjusted accordingly in an optimal way. The baseline model used in this experiment is a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) Network based on a pre-trained IMDB movie review dataset that has been effectively used in understanding text segment dependencies in a contextual manner in previous studies related to Natural Language Processing applications. Experimental observations clearly state that this DQN optimization strategy takes fewer training iterations, has a better validation accuracy rate and a stable output relative to existing optimization strategies in this context. The system has been effectively able to conceptualize a synthesis between static optimization algorithms and dynamic adaptations by machines in a way that can be scalable in a completely automated fashion in DL models. The current work lays a credible groundwork in extending hyperparameter optimization in applications related to Reinforcement Learning in computer visions applications, healthcare-related data analyses and bi-temporal predictive analyses in increasing proportions in due course.

title: Autonomous Swarm UAVs for Human Detection in Flood Environments using MATLAB Simulation
authors: Shyed Abdul Khadar Jailani H, Arulmozhi K, Anusindhiya K, Santhi S, Sathish Kumar K
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Flood disasters create immense challenges for search and rescue efforts due to their unpredictable nature and difficult-to-access locations. This study develops an autonomous swarm drone system optimized for locating and identifying stranded individuals in flood-affected areas. By leveraging swarm intelligence and advanced path optimization techniques, the system enhances navigation, search efficiency, and coverage using thermal and acoustic sensor-based simulations. The research focuses on refining swarm path planning through Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) and Ant Colony Optimization (ACO).
