A MATLAB - based simulation framework is designed to model real-time swarm behavior, integrating environmental complexities such as water currents, obstacles and human movement patterns. Optimization strategies improve route planning, obstacle avoidance, and adaptive search coverage, ensuring minimal energy consumption while maximizing search effectiveness. Performance is assessed based on detection accuracy, response speed, coordination reliability, and computational efficiency. Results demonstrate that PSO and ACO-based swarm control significantly enhance autonomous navigation, search precision, and adaptability compared to conventional search strategies. Optimized swarm paths minimize redundant searches, reduce overall mission time, and improve real-time decision-making in complex flood environments. This work presents a scalable and efficient simulation-driven solution for search and rescue operations, contributing to intelligent UAV coordination frameworks. Future enhancements will explore multi-objective optimization, reinforcement learning techniques, and real-time adaptive swarm path control to further refine disaster response efficiency.

title: Routing in Mobile Ad Hoc Networks using Hybrid DSR- OLSR Routing
authors: P. Vidhya Devi, B. L. Shivakumar
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The difficulty of accomplishing effective routing in dynamic, self-organizing environments is faced by ad hoc networks that are mobile, or MANETs. This study presents a new method Hybrid DSR-OLSR to improve routing in MANETs, shows a chart that compares the security of the suggested hybrid performance. DSR excels in on-demand routing, while OLSR offers proactive routing. By integrating both protocols, our proposed hybrid solution aims to strike a balance between proactive and reactive strategies. This hybrid approach leverages the strengths of both DSR and OLSR to improve the network's adaptability and overall performance in MANETs, ensuring more reliable and efficient transmission of data. Results from simulations show how effective this innovative routing solution.

title: Multi-Excitation Hyperspectral Imaging Toward Improved Lichen Identification
authors: Narek Chilingaryan, Arsen Gasparyan, Narine Sarvazyan
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: Hyperspectral imaging (HSI) has significantly advanced the identification and characterization of biological specimens by utilizing high-resolution data across multiple spectral bands. In the study of lichens, which are symbiotic entities formed by fungi and photosynthetic partners, our novel approach leverages the potential of HSI through a technique referred to as multi-excitation HSI (ME-HSI). This method differentiates itself by employing multiple ultraviolet wavelengths for illumination, capturing data in the visible range, and subsequently integrating these hyperspectral cubes. Our research demonstrates that this innovative technique provides superior effectiveness in identifying lichen species. By detailing unique spectral signatures, ME-HSI facilitates species differentiation, enhancing the accuracy of ecological monitoring and conservation efforts through non-invasive methods.

title: Leaf Water Thickness Estimation from Spectral Reflectance
authors: Ketaki Vinay Jambhali, Bikram Koirala, Paul Scheunders
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: Leaf Water Thickness, often described as equivalent water thickness, is an important trait in plant physiology and can be estimated non-destructively using the leaf's spectral reflectance. Leaf water thickness is defined as the depth of the water layer if all the water contained in a plant leaf were uniformly distributed over a given area. In this study, we propose a supervised machine learning approach based on polynomial regression, combined with our method, NRAL, to estimate leaf water thickness. The results are compared against those obtained using the PROSPECT model, a well-established physical model for simulating leaf optical properties. This methodology is evaluated on the LOPEX dataset, along with three additional hyperspectral datasets. In all cases, the proposed approach achieves a lower root mean squared error (RMSE) compared to the PROSPECT model. These findings highlight the potential of hybrid machine learning methods to outperform traditional models in accurately estimating leaf biophysical parameters.

title: Multi-Modal Emotion Recognition on Physiological Signals Using Self-Supervised Deep Learning Models
authors: Mayank Sharma, Divyanshu Desai, Geetha Sri, Kumaran P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: The Affective computing is the interdisciplinary field which bridges computer science, psychology, and cognitive science and aims to endow machines with the ability to recognize, interpret, and simulate human emotions. While the facial emotion recognition analysis and speech emotion recognition have achieved commercial success, they remain fundamentally limited by their reliance on external, voluntary displays of emotion which can be controlled and modulated accordingly to the situations. The Physiological signals offer an absolute, continuous, and involuntary window into the emotional state of the subject, as these are very difficult to manipulate or mask. However, the development of efficient physiological emotion recognition systems is hindered by three critical challenges: the scarcity of large-scale labeled datasets (the “small data” problem), high inter-subject variability, and the complexity of fusing heterogeneous data streams. This paper proposes an efficient, end-to-end Multi-Modal Emotion Recognition System (MMERS) that addresses these challenges through a novel combination of Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) and Attention-based Deep Fusion. We introduce a Contrastive Predictive Coding (CPC) framework to pretrain the EEG encoders on the unlabeled data, which enables the model to learn generalized temporal features before supervised fine-tuning. In addition to CPC, we also developed a hybrid deep neural architecture. In the above model we integrated the lightweight 1D-Convolutional Neural Network (CNNs) with Bidirectional Recurrent units (Bi-GRUs), in addition to that we also integrated autonomic signal processing with a multi-head attention mechanism for decision-level fusion. The above architecture was validated using the following standard datasets DEAP, SEED-IV and AMIGOS. The above architecture improves feature effectiveness against the varying subjects, where the attention mechanism effectively weighs the contribution of different modalities.

title: Analysis of Malnutrition Prediction in Children using ML Techniques
authors: A.Amuthavalli, K.Nandhini
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Malnutrition remains one of the main reasons why young children die and get sick, significantly impacting their growth and development. To avoid long-term health effects, early detection of malnutrition is essential, and advanced technologies such as deep learning (DL) and machine learning (ML) provide promising solutions in predicting malnutrition risk. This research investigates the possibilities of ML and DL models in accurately predicting malnutrition in young children by analyzing a wide array of demographic, nutritional, and health-related factors. By employing advanced data analytics techniques, the research seeks to identify key risk factors and develop predictive models It can be used in environments with low resources. The study applies a range of Convolutional and recurrent neural networks are examples of deep learning designs; support vector machines, random forests, and decision trees, and makes use of several datasets, including medical history and dietary intake records. The efficacy of these models is assessed based on model robustness, forecast accuracy, and potential for real-world application. The findings indicate that these models offer significant promise for early diagnosis of malnutrition, enabling targeted interventions that can improve health outcomes for vulnerable populations.

title: AI-Driven Portfolio Management Strategies
authors: Rasha Abousamra, Moustafa Kamal Moussa, Gamal Khalifa, Rahul Sharma, Kahled Masoud, Shatha Mustafa Hussain, Thair Kaddumi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Portfolio management is an essential part of financial planning and investment planning. That said, asset allocation optimization for maximum returns with minimum risks is the ultimate goal here. The introduction of AI into the industry has brought about a revolutionary shift in the industry that offers powerful and advanced tools such as predictive analytics, risk assessment, and decision-making capabilities. This research reflects on the processes of AI-based portfolio management strategy creation and implementation through the employment of, among others, the most notable machine learning models, and deep learning systems. Moreover, also natural language processing techniques. These instruments are successfully applied in the examination of huge unstructured financial data, as well as responding promptly to market trends. The paper thus examines the capacity of AI models of portfolio optimization by back-testing their results against conventional techniques. It also highlights the difficulties of financial applications such as algorithmic bias, data quality, and model interpretability. The study by means of case studies and simulations points out how AI can change the scenario of portfolio management and hence do away with the traditional way of data interpretation and interpretation by the investors. Thus, the IVF power of AI is presented by means of this research paper where the usage of AI will help the investors in making decisions that are driven by data that will eventually lead to better financial results.

title: Implementing a Real-Time Intrusion Detection System for Quantum-Secure Email Using AI-Driven QKD
authors: V. Karthick, Shiiv R S, Yuven Senthilkumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: While conventional cryptographic methods become increasingly vulnerable due to the rapid rise of quantum computing towards practical fault tolerant systems; this paper describes Enhanced BB84-4: a new Quantum Key Distribution protocol developed specifically for secure email communications. The Enhanced BB84-4 method includes features such as a Hyper-Entropic 7-Gate State Preparation method and an AI driven Intelligent Basis Selection method that provides security against efforts to attack these types of systems in the future. In addition to only using operations from two bases under the conventional method, Enhanced BB84-4 uses many different quantum gates for operation to create more uncertainty and enhance the security of information transmitted. To improve the efficiency of key generation, two synchronised Deep Reinforcement Learning agents are used by both communicating parties to predict and select the measurement basis; thus improving the performance of the entire transmission. The authors have prototyped a complete Enhanced BB84-4 system consisting of quantum simulation using IBM Qiskit; a back-end built in Python using Django; and a front-end email interface developed in React.js. In addition, the prototype features an intrusion detection module called “Red Alert;” which provides live visualisation of the Quantum Bit Error Rate to identify propagation of errors in real-time. The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed system has a significantly increased Key Generation Rate and reduced Keys' Error Rate; thus, providing increased reliability and robustness of the proposed Enhanced BB84-4 system than will be demonstrated by future research on currently available Quantum Key Distribution protocols.

title: Explainable Machine Learning Framework for Multi-Stage Chronic Kidney Disease Classification
authors: Abdul Haque, Nadeem Akhtar, Tameem Ahmad, Mohd Aslam
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) is a progressive condition that requires accurate staging for effective clinical management and timely intervention. In this work, we propose an explainable machine learning framework for multi-class CKD stage classification using structured clinical data. Experiments were conducted on a large CKD dataset comprising approximately 4,000 patient records with demographic, biochemical, and lifestyle attributes. Six feature configurations were systematically evaluated: (1) GFR only, (2) serum creatinine only, (3) GFR combined with creatinine, (4) top five SHAP-ranked features including creatinine, (5) top four SHAP-ranked features excluding creatinine, and (6) all available features. Multiple machine learning models—including XGBoost, Random Forest, Logistic Regression, Support Vector Machine, and k-Nearest Neighbors—were implemented, followed by a soft-voting ensemble model. Model performance was assessed using accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and class-specific sensitivity and specificity. Experimental results demonstrate that feature sets containing GFR achieved near-perfect classification performance (accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\geq 0.96$</tex>), while creatinine alone resulted in poor accuracy (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 0.19$</tex>). SHAP analysis consistently identified GFR as the most influential feature. The findings highlight that GFR is the dominant determinant of CKD stage classification and confirm the suitability of XGBoost-based explainable models for reliable and transparent clinical decision support.

title: Exploring the Effect of Leveraging Advances Technologies to Enhance Credit Scoring Process: (Case Study Based on Jordanian Commercial Banks)
authors: Abd Al-Salam Al-Hamad, Mohammad Alqsass, Abeer Al-Khoury, Ibrahim Ezmigna Azman Hashim, Jassim Algasawneh, Ghada Al-Rawashdeh, Mahmoud Alghizzawi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: The integration of AI and ML into the credit scoring process has enhanced the accuracy, speed, and efficiency of evaluating borrowers’ creditworthiness. This research explores the role of AI and ML in credit scoring within Jordanian commercial banks, focusing on the benefits these technologies offer to both financial institutions and their customers. The study highlights the disruptive potential of AI and ML in the evolution of credit scoring landscape, improving operational efficiency, and contributing to the overall stability of the banking sector. The sample, consisting of 13 commercial banks operating in Jordan, during year 2021. Appropriate descriptive statistical methods were employed to analyze the variables, and the hypotheses were tested using simple regression analysis. In this study, adoption of AI and ML was treated as an independent variable, with the credit scoring process as the dependent variable. The results demonstrate that credit scoring process has positively impacted by incorporation AI and ML. Ultimately, the findings demonstrate that AI and ML not only optimize the credit scoring process but also play a critical role in promoting financial accessibility and inclusion in Jordan’s banking system.

title: Stock Price Prediction using a Hybrid of TimeXer and FT-Transformer Models
authors: Denitta Daison, Blessy Paul P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Stock price prediction is a critical aspect of financial forecasting, providing valuable insights for traders and investors. TimeXer model is widely used Transformer based model for stock price prediction, particularly effective in modeling temporal dependencies through sparse attention mechanisms. However, despite its strengths, the TimeXer model has limitations, such as reduced capacity to capture nuanced feature interactions and dependency on temporal patterns alone. On the other hand, Conversely, the FT-Transformer is a newer architecture that uses feature-level attention to emphasize the important predictors. This model has shown promising accuracy in stock price prediction, it primarily focuses on feature importance and may fail to leverage long term sequential dependencies. This model has shown promising accuracy in stock price prediction, yet it primarily focuses on feature importance and may underutilize long term sequential dependencies FT-Transformer demonstrated accuracy, achieving an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> score of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 3}$</tex>. However, FT-Transformer is less effective in modeling sequential dependencies and temporal patterns when used alone. This limitation motivated the development of a hybrid model that integrates the temporal sequence modeling capability of TimeXer with the feature interaction learning strength of FT-Transformer. In the hybrid framework, TimeXer learns how historical time points influence future stock prices, while FT-Transformer determines which features contribute most to prediction accuracy. Experimental results on AAPL, AMZN, GOOG historical stock data from Yahoo Finance from January 1, 2000 to March 13, 2025 showed that TimeXer alone achieved an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 5}$</tex>, while the hybrid model improved performance to an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> of 0.99, making it the most accurate approach in this study.

title: EEG Signal Analysis for Motor Imagery Tasks Using Deep Learning Techniques
authors: AADHITYA S. V, S. Ishrath Sulthana, Karthikeyan. D, Sutharsan V, R. Lakshmi, Annamalai. G
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: This work examines the creation using a dryelectrode EEG setup developed for motor imagery signal classification, utilizing an array of deep learning techniques to control a wheelchair using brain-computer interface. While wet EEG devices are the industry standard for acquiring neural signals, they can pose discomfort and are not practical for longterm use. This study looks at the prospects of using dry EEG technology in which user comfort and practicality are critical aspects- the identified EEG signals corresponding to 4 motor imagery actions. As dry EEG signals can be weak, and prone to noise, the project investigated the development of discriminative features to improve classification accuracy for real-time wheelchair control. The primary classification method uses a CNN-based model and PCA is used to perform dimensionality and optimization of the feature extraction process. The framework demonstrated its ability to function as an effective BCI system because the model achieved 96.73 % accuracy with raw EEG signals and 98.86% accuracy with PCA-preprocessed signals.

title: Optimizing Fertilizer Use in Smart Farming Using AI
authors: Ahmad Azzazi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: In modern agriculture, effective fertilizer management is essential for achieving sustainable crop yields, minimizing environmental impact, and decreasing the costs incurred by farmers. This research project examines the role of artificial intelligence (AI) in the accurate application of fertilizers through smart agricultural methods. AI systems by combining signals from sensors that analyze soil health, appliance forecasters, types of crops, and product history, will offer detailed advice on what and how much fertilizer should be applied at what point. The study articulated the participatory advantage of various machine learning methods such as the neural network, decision-surface-coupled adaptive predictive method, and the evolution of a resource efficiency assessment tool called simple calorimetry accompanied by optimization strategies, to build a decision aid that would mitigate the indicated environmental damages. By employing actual datasets from field trials, the improvements realized in resource productivity as well as product quality were considerable and at the same time nitrogen excess disposal and other forms of environmental harm were reduced. The present paper outlines the overall guiding framework for AI-based practices in fertilizer management, in addition to the challenges and opportunities in implementing such systems at a large scale in precision agriculture. The findings underline the feasibility of using AI in the agricultural technology ecosystem as both economic and ecological objectives are advancing.

title: Brain Tumor Classification Using DenseNet121 and Vision Transformer
authors: G Komal Venkat Ganesh, G Ramya Sruthi, K Raja Sekhar, D Manoj Sudeep, Yepuganti Karuna, Saladi Saritha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Brain tumor classification through magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is a crucial task in the diagnosis and treatment of brain tumors. However, the classification of brain tumors through MRI is a tedious task. To improve the efficiency of the classification task, we propose a hybrid deep learning model based on DenseNet-121 and the Vision Transformer model for brain tumor classification. The proposed model is able to learn features through the fusion of convolutional and transformer models. The model is evaluated on the publicly available Brain Tumor MRI Dataset, which contains four classes of brain tumors: glioma, meningioma, pituitary tumor, and normal brain images. The experimental results show that the proposed model performs better than the baseline models in terms of classification accuracy on the same conditions. The classification accuracy of the proposed model is 99.01 Percent. The originality and contribution of the proposed model are the parallel fusion of the CNN and transformer models for brain tumor classification.

title: AI Fitness Tracker with Posture Detection and Health Insights Chatbot
authors: Kevin J, Krishna Kaushik. N, Darshan S, Padmajothi V
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: The paper presents a smart model for tracking fitness and wellness that uses real-time pose recognition and predictive health analysis. This system relies on a dynamic posture correction algorithm, which continuously extracts skeletal landmarks, unlike traditional fitness apps that follow fixed workout routines. Additionally, the model includes a predictive machine learning approach that assesses the risk of cardiovascular disease based on users' physiological data. To make the system more interactive, a conversational agent is integrated, powered by Natural Language Understanding. The system's effectiveness, as shown through real-time tracking performance, accuracy in posture classification, and reliability in predictions, is proven to be scalable and optimized for edge computing, making it a solution for preventive healthcare and personalized physical training.

title: AI for Predictive Maintenance in Industrial Systems
authors: Zainab Al Ghurabli, Ahmed Ali Otoom
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Predictive maintenance or PdM has taken the lead in the control and productivity of industrial systems. Adaptations of artificial intelligence, particularly the machine learning (ML) and neural networks, which enabled real-time predictive analytics based on sensor data, have revolutionizing traditional methods of conducting maintenance. This paper studies the power of AI in predictive maintenance of industrial systems with a focus on its forecasting abilities of equipment failures, creation of optimal maintenance schedules, and downtime minimization. The paper expresses various AI methods such as supervised learning, unsupervised learning, and reinforcement learning while explaining the strengths and difficulties of these approaches in practical industrial contexts. Moreover, we verify the Internet of Things (IoT) sensors, and the significance of big data analytics in the extraction of actionable intelligence, will be studied. This paper evaluates the advantages of AI-driven predictive maintenance such as reduction in cost, an increase in operational efficiency, and enhancement of safety. In conclusion, the paper points out the primary challenges of implementation and suggests potential expansions for research, emphasizing the incorporation of federated learning into privacy-preserving predictive maintenance in decentralized industrial systems.

title: HyGEO-MEC: A Transformer-GNN and Dueling DQN Framework for Energy-Aware Task Offloading in Edge Computing
authors: Bharani H, T.Manoranjitham
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Efficient energy utilization and real-time responsiveness are critical for the sustainable and scalable deployment of Multi-access Edge Computing (MEC) systems. To address these challenges, this study proposes HyGEO-MEC, a novel deep learning–driven framework designed to minimize power consumption in dynamic MEC environments while satisfying latency constraints. The framework integrates three key components: a Transformer-enhanced Graph Neural Network (TE-GNN) for accurate spatio-temporal workload prediction, a BiLSTM-based module for energy profiling, and a Dueling Deep Q-Network (DQN) controller for intelligent task offloading and server activation decisions. Additionally, an energy-constrained task scheduler is implemented to ensure stable resource allocation under fluctuating network demands. Performance evaluation on real-world datasets, including the GCT and Alibaba cluster traces, demonstrates that HyGEO-MEC consistently outperforms baseline methods—ESR, ESP, GAA, and GAT—across multiple metrics. Notably, it achieves an average task delay of 0.18 seconds, a violation rate of 9%, and a high decision efficiency of 0.42 milliseconds. It also records the lowest workload prediction error (MSE = 0.033) and achieves an η(t) match rate of 84.27% with ESR, indicating strong alignment with near-optimal decisions. These results validate HyGEO-MEC as a robust, scalable, and intelligent energy optimization solution for next-generation MEC systems. Its ability to adapt seamlessly to varying workloads and network dynamics makes it well-suited for real-time mobile edge applications.

title: A Generative AI–Enhanced Framework for Smart Sustainable Electrical Networks
authors: Mohamed Shaaban, Abdulrahman Al-Shanoon, Mohamad Nassereddine
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: High penetration of solar and wind generation introduces significant uncertainty that challenges conventional deterministic power system operation. This paper proposes a unified five-layer Generative-AI-Enhanced Machine Learning framework for managing smart electrical networks under renewable variability. The framework integrates deterministic forecasting, probabilistic scenario generation, physics-informed AC power flow propagation, and voltage feasibility restoration within a single end-to-end workflow. Short-term renewable prediction is performed using an LSTM model for solar power and an XGBoost model for wind generation. A conditional neural scenario generator then expands limited historical data into realistic future trajectories, enabling systematic assessment of uncertainty and rare operating conditions. Monte Carlo scenarios are propagated through a differentiable AC power flow model to identify potential voltage risks, while a physics-aware optimization layer restores feasibility using minimal reactive power support. Case studies based on UAE climate and load data demonstrate that deterministic forecasts alone underestimate network risk, whereas scenario-based analysis reveals up to 18% undervoltage violations before control. After applying the proposed restoration layer, violations are reduced to near-zero levels with reactive adjustments on the order of 0.0001 per unit. The results confirm that the framework enhances risk awareness, improves voltage security, and provides a practical AI-enabled pathway for resilient and interpretable smart grid operation.

title: Smart Vision for Safer Roads: Accident Detection using Image Intelligence
authors: G Raja Vikram, A. Harika, P.Rishi Priya, B. Kiran Reddy, A. Ravi Teja
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Traffic accidents are often inaccurately reported and are one of the main causes of fatalities and injuries at a global level. Traditional methods of accident management often rely on eyewitness reports or manual notifications, which can be limited by human delays and inaccuracies. The rapid development of artificial intelligence provides new opportunities for intelligent traffic and safety systems. New prospects have been created for the creation of intelligent traffic control and safety systems. This research proposes an IoT-based accident detection framework that uses deep learning approach for real-time identification of accidents. The system proposed employs a roadside camera to take traffic images continuously, which are processed by a deep learning model trained to identify accidents with high accuracy. When an accident is detected, a Raspberry Pi equipped with a GSM module immediately sends emergency messages to concerned authorities to facilitate quick response. The system was tested and trained in a wide range of accident situations to achieve high robustness across different traffic and environmental conditions. A working prototype, which utilized miniature vehicles in a controlled setup, achieved high reliability and performance. However, lighting inconsistency, weather conditions, and traffic patterns present challenges for the large-scale implementation. Potential improvements include the incorporation of GPS technology to facilitate accurate localization of accidents and the employment of cloud-based systems to facilitate real-time access to information. Overall, the system proposed presents an innovative, scalable, and cost-effective solution to road safety improvement and emergency response time reduction.

title: Reptile Meta Learning Based Beaconing and C2 Behaviour Detection in Network
authors: Graeson Joshua Elijah, Amrith Saras, Renu Mary Daniel, Diya Jothish
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: While covert Command-and-Control (C2)-communication is currently one of the toughest challenges in network security. Stealthy low-volume methods based on encryptions, which highly mimic benign traffic, are used in attacks. This article describes a full, to end, framework which mimics, records, interprets and categorizes C2 behavior by utilizing meta-learning. With a controlled virtual environment, a realistic reverse -shell attack was also performed, with a delivery made by the use of phishing in the transmission of a Meterpreter payload. The resultant packet stream level network traffic was converted into feature packets of flow at the feature vectors (flow temporal features and payload entropy features) and flow directionality and burstiness along with indicators of compromise (IoCs). It used these flow-level features to train a Reptile meta-learning model, thus, allowing it to quickly adapt to a variety of C2 patterns (or to a pattern that it has never seen before). The experimental analysis reveals that the accuracy (99.29-1) is high (99.29-1), its precision and F1-score are higher than the models which use conventional machine-learning algorithms significantly. In this way, the given system offers strong and scalable approach to the identification of the C2 activity in automatic mode using meta-learned behavioral features of network traffic.

title: Explainable Monkeypox Classification Using Lightweight Attention-Based Separable CNN and Real-Time Web Application
authors: Md. Ismiel Hossen Abir, Abir Mahmud Shahariar, Sanjana Islam Kasfia
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Monkeypox is a viral disease that causes skin lesions similar to other conditions, such as chickenpox. This similarity makes it difficult to diagnose accurately, especially in areas with limited medical resources. Traditional diagnostic methods are reliable but often slow and require a healthcare professional. In this study, we propose a lightweight attention-based separable Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model for binary classification of monkeypox skin lesions. The proposed architecture used depth-wise separable convolutions, channel attention mechanisms, and feature extraction blocks to improve performance. We compare our model’s performance against three transfer learning models, such as VGG16, DenseNet121, and MobileNetV2. The proposed model achieved 87.15% test accuracy, which is comparatively better performing according to the three transfer learning models. We further enhance interpretability using explainable AI techniques, including LIME (Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations) and Saliency Maps to highlight critical regions influencing classification decisions. Additionally, we also develop a real-time web application for user-friendly interaction, enabling instant predictions with confidence scores. The study highlights the potential of lightweight and interpretable deep learning models with a real-time web application.

title: SecureNet: A Next-Generation AI-Powered Cyber Defense System for Dynamic Attack Prevention
authors: T. Sasikala, J. Joshua Daniel Raj, P.Vishnu Raja, S.D.Prabu Ragavendiran, C L Vinaya, Harshitha V
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: As the Metaverse is rapidly evolving, the security framework of the system will need to evolve to protect users against more cyberattacks. The growing rise in the size of Large Language Models (LLMs) has amplified risks of email phishing attacks, ransomware hits, data breaches, and state-sponsored attacks that have become more advanced and require smarter and adaptive responses. To meet this requirement, different secure services are provided by the use of AI-based, web-based cybersecurity ecosystem, called SecureNet that combines threat-monitoring, anomaly-detection, geolocation of attack that allows tracking the attack origin, quantum inspired encryption, and deceptionbased defense mechanisms. It is based on cyberpunk-themed dashboards, the system is built on Python-Flask server code that uses dynamic HTML, CSS, and JavaScript elements to represent real-time cyber-attack conditions. The fundamental modules that incorporate the live scoring of threats, IP geolocation tracking, trap files identification, emergency lockdown measures, and MirrorNet-based simulation of threats that are effective to test the incorporation of modern cyber defense systems. It has an architecture based on a modular clientserver model that allows continuous updates, simulated API-based interaction of data and responsive UI interaction. System testing to validate the stable operation of all modules, visualizing threats, mapping of geolocation accurately, and simulated lockdown performance, and smooth running of quantum key distribution logic are all confirmed. Combining the tricks of deception and AI-powered analytics, as well as accessible and intuitive visualization, SecureNet offers a scalable and easy-to-learn environment to learn about cyber-attack behavior and defensive response flows. This project exemplifies the potential to design lightweight technologies into extensive, impactful, and real-time cybersecurity simulation that may be applied to develop academic learning, conduct prototyping, and, subsequently, expand to fully autonomous cyber defense infrastructures.

title: Portable Water Risk Estimator
authors: Ferents Koni Jiavana K, Aamir Ayaaz A R, Dhayana V, Mohammed Shaheen K S, Bhavesshna K M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: The need for rapid, user-friendly water quality assessment tools is increasingly important for active individuals and urban consumers. This paper presents the design and validation of the PWRE Development Board, a modular embedded prototype developed for a next-generation smart system capable of real-time risk estimation with hardware provisions for future UV disinfection. Built around the Raspberry Pi Pico (RP2040), the board integrates TDS, turbidity, and temperature sensors with a compact user interface comprising an SSD1306 OLED display, status LEDs, tactile buttons, and haptic feedback. Flexible power options and JST-based modular connectors enable easy sensor upgrades and field deployment. The system supports diagnostic self-tests for each functional block, ensuring robust, reliable operation. Validation results show that the platform streamlines both component evaluation and firmware development, forming a versatile base for portable water safety products and future IoT enhancements.

title: Cross-Domain AI Fusion: Integrating Smart Grid Environmental Signals with Diabetes Risk Prediction
authors: Hasan Ahamed Alif, Md. Jisan Mashrafi, Md. Wahiduzzaman, Abtahi Noor, Sebagat Selim, Md. Asif Shahriar
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: This work presents a novel cross-domain artificial intelligence fusion architecture that enhances the prediction of diabetes risks by integrating clinical health data with environmental telemetry data from smart grid infrastructure. In metropolitan areas where climate change and energy instability have significantly impacted the environment, the study acknowledges the increasing effects of environmental stressors on chronic illnesses. An Environmental Stress Score (ESS), a composite statistic, is suggested by the study. Temperature, humidity, power factor, and voltage changes are coupled with standard clinical indicators like blood pressure and blood sugar levels to calculate this score. We extracted 10,000 environmental and clinical records from publicly accessible archives. The following machine learning algorithms were tested: Random Forest, XGBoost, Multilayer Perceptron, and Logistic Regression. The XGBoost model achieved the best accuracy of 0.98 on AUC and an F1-score of 0.94. Even more so than blood pressure and glucose levels, the interpretability research of SHAP demonstrated that ESS is the most potent predictor. Clinical risk modeling may and should incorporate ecological signals, as this fact indicates. The cross-domain solution serves as the basis for future climate-aware real-time health monitoring systems.

title: DL-HOL: Deep Learning Enhanced Hybrid Ordinal Learning for Healthcare Applications with Interval Labels
authors: Padma Chigulla, Punyala Ramadevi, Annamraju Thanuja, Pallapati Solmon, N. Srihitha Gunapriya, N.Ravi Kumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Predicting disease progression is a crucial task in healthcare, especially when the outcomes lie on an ordinal scale, such as stages of disease severity or rates of cognitive decline. However, many real-world clinical datasets suffer from label uncertainty, where only interval-based information is available rather than precise class assignments. This paper presents DL-HOL, a novel deep learning-based extension of the Hybrid Ordinal Learner framework, capable of integrating both precise and interval-labeled data for robust ordinal prediction. Unlike conventional approaches that rely heavily on manual feature engineering, DL-HOL directly processes raw neuroimaging scans alongside demographic and clinical variables, extracting highlevel representations automatically. Through extensive experiments on the Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (ADNI) dataset, DL-HOL demonstrated clear improvements in overall accuracy, mean absolute error reduction, and class-wise stability compared to several existing ordinal learning baselines, including kernel-based HOL, PRIL, and VILMA. The results suggest DL-HOL provides a practical, scalable solution for individualized prediction of disease progression rates, with direct applications in early intervention strategies.

title: Decentralized nonconvex optimization with linear convergence and quantization enabled privacy preservation
authors: Xiao-Yu Yue, Xiao-Kang Liu, Yan-Wu Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control of Network Systems
abstract: Nonconvexity, communication compression, and privacy preservation are important and challenging problems in decentralized optimization. Existing decentralized nonconvex algorithms usually treat privacy and communication separately, such as introducing quantizers for communication compression and differential privacy for privacy preservation. However, they do not consider the role of quantization for privacy preservation, which causes a waste of privacy budget. To solve this problem, we propose a Quantization-based Privacy-preserving Alternating Direction Method of Multiplier (QP-ADMM), which ensures strict differential privacy and communication compression both by quantization. We show that QP-ADMM can obtain first-order stationary points at the sublinear convergence rate <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {O}(1/T)$</tex-math></inline-formula> in expectation even in the nonconvex case, where <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$T$</tex-math></inline-formula> is the number of iterations, and obtain the optimal value at the linear convergence rate under the additional Polyak-Lojasiewicz condition. Comparative simulations show the superiority of the proposed algorithm over the existing ones.

title: Normalized Maximum Likelihood Code-Length on Riemannian Manifold Data Spaces
authors: Kota Fukuzawa, Atsushi Suzuki, Kenji Yamanishi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Theory
abstract: In recent years, with the large-scale expansion of graph data, there has been an increased focus on Riemannian manifold data spaces other than Euclidean space. In particular, the development of hyperbolic spaces has been remarkable, and they have high expressive power for graph data with hierarchical structures. Normalized Maximum Likelihood (NML) is employed in regret minimization and model selection. However, existing formulations of NML have been developed primarily in Euclidean spaces and are inherently dependent on the choice of coordinate systems, making it non-trivial to extend NML to Riemannian manifolds. In this study, we define a new NML that reflects the geometric structure of Riemannian manifolds, called the Riemannian manifold NML (Rm-NML). This Rm-NML is invariant under coordinate transformations and coincides with the conventional NML under the natural parameterization in Euclidean space. We extend existing computational techniques for NML to the setting of Riemannian manifolds. Furthermore, we derive a method to simplify the computation of Rm-NML on Riemannian symmetric spaces, which encompass data spaces of growing interest such as hyperbolic spaces. To illustrate the practical application of our proposed method, we explicitly computed the Rm-NML for normal distributions on hyperbolic spaces.

title: First Demonstration of Airborne Quad-Pol SAR with Quasi-Orthogonal FM Coded Waveforms for Ambiguity Suppression
authors: Guodong Jin, Tao Zhou, Yunkai Deng, Daiyin Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: The range and azimuth ambiguity suppression issues are a technical challenge for the synthetic aperture radar (SAR) systems. The cross-polarized channels of quadrature-polarimetric (quad-pol) SARs are subject to more serious range/azimuth ambiguities compared with the single polarization SAR system since their ambiguities stem from the co-polarized echoes, and the echo signal received in cross-polarized channels is significantly weaker than that in co-polarized channels. To this end, advanced quasi-orthogonal frequency modulation(FM) codes are developed in this paper, and compared with the other quasi orthogonal waveforms (e.g., up-down chirp waveforms), they own two important characteristics: 1) low auto-correlation sidelobes, which can ensure a well range imaging performance; 2) low integral cross-correlation energy (ICCE), which can ensure the total ambiguity energy in a SAR image is effectively suppressed; 3) parameterized coding structure, which is beneficial for the generation of this FM coded waveform in real-time on the resource-constrained spaceborne SAR system. Moreover, well designed transmission schemes exploiting the proposed wave forms are presented and further applied in a real airborne quad pol SAR system with a bandwidth of 500 MHz and a carrier frequency of 35 GHz for the first time. The imaging results of the cross-polarized channel demonstrate that, compared with using the up-down chirp waveforms, the proposed method suppresses range ambiguity energy by over 4.0 dB for traditional quad-pol SAR and azimuth ambiguity energy by over 4.0 dB for hybrid quad-pol SAR. Besides, the proposed methods do not increase system and processing complexity; therefore, the improvement of 4.0 dB ambiguity suppression rate is very attractive for the SAR system, especially for the high-resolution and wide-swath (HRWS) spaceborne SAR systems.

title: VaN3Twin: the Multi-Technology V2X Digital Twin with Ray Tracing in the Loop
authors: Roberto Pegurri, Diego Gasco, Francesco Linsalata, Marco Rapelli, Eugenio Moro, Francesco Raviglione, Claudio Casetti
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: This paper presents VaN3Twin—the first open source, full-stack Network Digital Twin (NDT) framework for simulating the coexistence of multiple Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) communication technologies with accurate physical-layer modeling via ray tracing. VaN3Twin extends the ms-van3t simulator by integrating Sionna Ray Tracer (RT) in the loop, enabling high-fidelity representation of wireless propagation, including diverse Line of Sight (LoS) conditions with a focus on LoS blockage due to other vehicles’ meshes, Doppler effect, and site-dependent effects—e.g., scattering and diffraction. Unlike conventional simulation tools, the proposed framework supports realistic coexistence analysis across Dedicated Short Range Communication (DSRC) and Cellular-V2X (C-V2X) technologies operating over a shared spectrum. A dedicated interference tracking module captures cross-technology interference at the time-frequency resource block level and enhances Signal to Interference plus Noise Ratio (SINR) estimation by eliminating artifacts such as the bimodal behavior induced by separate LoS/NLoS propagation models. Compared to field measurements, VaN3Twin reduces application-layer disagreement by 50% in rural and over 70% in urban environments with respect to current state of the art simulation tools, demonstrating its value for scalable and accurate digital twin–based V2X coexistence simulation.

title: On the Deterministic Regime of 1-Persistent Age-Threshold Slotted ALOHA in Heterogeneous Networks
authors: Plínio S. Dester, Richard Demo Souza, Paulo Cardieri
publish: IEEE Communications Letters
abstract: This letter investigates the 1-persistent age-threshold slotted ALOHA (TSA) protocol in heterogeneous networks and reveals a connection with an open problem in Number Theory. We show that the protocol converges almost surely to a deterministic, TDMA-like stationary regime if, and only if, the age-thresholds form a harmonic sequence. In this regime, the throughput and age of information metrics admit simple closed-form expressions that are independent of the retransmission probability. Simulation results evaluate the robustness of the protocol to perturbations and show the performance degradation when using a non-harmonic sequence of age-thresholds.

title: An Almost Global Attitude Controller for Rigid Bodies Subject to Unknown Disturbances and Sensor Bias
authors: César Cruz-Hernández, Javier Pliego-Jiménez, César Cruz-Hernández
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: This paper studies the attitude trajectory tracking problem of rigid bodies subject to unknown constant disturbances and angular velocity measurements corrupted by constant bias. We employ the exponential coordinates of rotation and the angular velocity to describe the state of the rigid body. Exploiting the structural properties of the attitude kinematics and dynamics of the rigid body, we propose a bias observer that compensates for biased measurements and a nonlinear control law with integral action that achieves attitude tracking for almost all initial conditions and compensates for constant disturbances. We design the control law based on a Barrier Lyapunov function to circumvent the exponential coordinates' singularity. Finally, numerical simulations and experimental tests illustrate the efficacy of our control law and bias observer.

title: Analysis and Mitigation of Pose Estimation Uncertainty on SE(3) for Magnetic Localization
authors: Pingyu Xiang, Hongye Zhang, Yue Wang, Rong Xiong, Haojian Lu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Robotics
abstract: Magnetic localization, owing to its immunity to line-of-sight occlusion and non-contact nature, is considered a promising technology for medical applications. While it is intuitive that localization performance degrades as the target moves farther from the sensor array, uncertainty analysis has long been overlooked, which is essential for quantifying localization quality. In this work, we present a pose estimation and uncertainty analysis framework on SE(3) for magnetic source localization using sensor arrays, which enables concise formulation of the problem and quantitative assessment of the results. The volume of the uncertainty ellipsoid is used to characterize localization confidence, while the surface shape in Cartesian space is used for visualization. This also provides insight into the effective workspace of the magnetic localization system prior to deployment. Guided by this analysis, we designed a movable magnetic sensor array to expand the limited sensing volume and mitigate localization uncertainty, thereby enhancing overall localization performance. Simulations and pose tracking experiments validate the effectiveness of this framework. By dynamically moving the sensor array to minimize the volume of the uncertainty ellipsoid, localization errors are reduced compared with static and projection-based strategies by 67.04%, 16.51% in position and 43.87%, 20.73% in orientation, respectively. Furthermore, phantom experiments on distal locking screw alignment and magnetic capsule endoscope tracking demonstrate the system's capability in improving localization accuracy (reducing alignment errors by 71.88%) and expanding the effective workspace by several folds.

title: Human-Interpretable Time Series Feature Extraction Using Model-Based Clustering
authors: João Eduardo Hoffmann, Asad Waqar Malik, Max Mauro Dias Santos, João Francisco Justo
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Feature extraction is an essential task for Deep Neural Networks (DNNs), as high-dimensional data are often available and filtering can highlight relevant patterns for the reconstruction or forecasting of a target. Current forecasting strategies, which use multivariate time series data, become a major challenge as the distance to relevant compressed representations increases with the difficult task of selecting optimal samples. To overcome these high-dimensional feature extraction problems, graph-based clustering can be applied, which allows the dynamic definition of parameters to achieve human-interpretable clustered environments, improving the quality of patterns captured in feature spaces when compared to autoencoders (AE), which may present over-specialized compression processes. Here, we present a human-interpretable feature space representation using the Toeplitz Inverse Covariance-Based Clustering (TICC) method, as an optimal and dynamic model-based clustering strategy for multivariate time series data. In addition, an experimental comparative evaluation, consisting of model accuracy and several parameters, is presented to comparatively highlight the improvements achieved with such a human-interpretable feature extraction process.

title: AI-Driven Framework for Zero-Day Vulnerability Detection and Prevention in Application and Cloud Environments
authors: Abdul Jaleel D, Krishna Sai J, Manikandan R, Dinesh K, Shankar M
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Zero-day vulnerabilities are difficult to identify in modern software systems because exploit triggers are often novel, semantics-aware, and spread across both application logic and cloud execution contexts. This paper proposes an AI-driven autonomous discovery framework that prioritizes zero-day detection while preserving high quality forensic evidence at the moment a fault is surfaced. The framework integrates a Deep Q-Network (DQN) agent to guide semantic input exploration with an instrumented execution harness that supports hybrid monitoring and crash-resilient evidence logging. The design enables consistent analysis across local applications and cloud-hosted services, allowing vulnerability signals to be captured from heterogeneous runtime behaviors. In the implementation, the autonomous discovery engine executed 268,435,456 cycles and revealed 13 distinct logic- and memory-related failure modes, including high-severity deserialization driven faults and deep semantic gate violations.

title: A Novel Multimodal Biometric Framework for Identifying Missing People in Hajj and Umrah Using Iris and Ear Recognition
authors: Jawad Hasan Alkhateeb
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Because of their great robustness and dependability, both iris and ear recognition have become essential biometric identification techniques. A reliable identification system is essential in the modern world for crowd control, security, and individual verification. Because the human iris is unique and complicated, iris identification is one of the most successful biometric modalities. Iris-based identification is very accurate because studies have shown notable variances between an individual's right and left eyes. In a similar vein, ear recognition offers an additional biometric modality that improves the accuracy of the system. During the Hajj and Umara, iris and ear recognition can be employed. With millions of pilgrims performing the Hajj and Umrah annually, accurately identifying lost or missing individuals is a significant challenge. This research presents a hybrid biometric system that blends edge-cloud computing with facial, voice, iris, and ear recognition for fast and accurate authentication. The framework combines deep Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) with traditional machine learning techniques to improve recognition accuracy. The solution, which is built as a mobile application and connects to the Ministry of Hajj's vast pilgrim database, enables real-time identification and matching. Experimental results show that the proposed framework is faster and more accurate than existing methods, providing a practical and effective means to manage large pilgrimage crowds.

title: Optimized Incentive Framework for Two-Tiered Social Mobile Crowdsourcing
authors: C. Shyamala Kumari, C. Naga Sai Manikanta, Birukulla Nithin
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: An optimized two-tiered mobile crowdsourcing framework that leverages social networks for effective and scalable task distribution is presented in this paper. The system tackles problems like low user participation, inaccurate data, and high operational overhead by implementing a structured incentive mechanism and a decentralized agent-participant model. Access control and hash-based authentication strengthen security. Compared to conventional models, real-time simulations and performance evaluation show enhanced scalability, task throughput, accuracy, and fraud detection.

title: Evaluation of Flaxseed Extract-Based Hydrogel for Antibacterial Activity
authors: Nafisa Nawal, Sadia Islam Shuchi, Prapti Thapa, Maruf Hasan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Wound healing is a critical challenge globally, especially in preventing bacterial infections. This study develops hydrogels incorporated with flaxseed extract for antibacterial wound healing. Hydrogel samples were prepared by chemically crosslinking polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) and gelatin, followed by soaking in pure and diluted flaxseed extracts, labelled HG-100, HG-50, HG-25, and HG-12.5. The samples were evaluated for swelling behaviour, moisture retention, water vapour transmission rate, scanning electron microscopy (SEM), Fourier transform infrared spectroscopy (FTIR), tensile strength, and antibacterial properties. The extract-loaded hydrogels’ wound-healing ability aligns with previously reported studies. Statistical analysis revealed that varying concentrations affected the swelling behaviour, moisture retention, water vapour transmission, and antibacterial properties. No distinct pores were visible in the SEM images, which is likely due to limitations in sample preparation. All hydrogels displayed strong mechanical properties, confirming their elasticity and flexibility. The successful integration of the bioactive compound lignan inside the hydrogel was validated by FTIR. Compared to other hydrogels, the HG-100 formulation showed the most antibacterial behaviour against Staphylococcus aureus, likely due to its higher concentration of bioactive compounds. To summarize, the hydrogels provide an in vitro basis for wound dressing applications; however, additional in vivo evaluations, cytocompatibility assessments, and drug release studies are necessary to establish their biological significance.

title: Frequency-Channel Attention and Prototype Calibration with Uncertainty-Aware Inference for Brain MRI Classification
authors: Toha Gazi Muhammed
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Accurate classification of brain tumours is fundamental in medical imaging, as it supports diagnosis and informs treatment decisions. This study presents an advanced multi-modal deep learning framework for brain tumour classification using MRI data, integrating sophisticated preprocessing, Frequency-Channel Attention, Prototype Calibration, and Uncertainty-Aware Inference modules. The dataset was prepared through augmentation, cropping, resizing, and systematic division into training, validation, and testing subsets. EfficientNet-B0 served as the backbone network, enabling efficient and discriminative feature extraction. The model achieved outstanding performance, with an average accuracy of 97.19%, a recall of 97.04%, an F1-score of 97.05%, and a corrected AUC of 0.96, confirming strong discriminative capability. Visual analyses, including confusion matrices, further validate the framework’s robustness. Uncertainty quantification enhances prediction reliability, ensuring clinical trustworthiness. This method exceeds the performance of existing techniques across multiple tumour categories, including glioma, meningioma, pituitary, and non-tumour, offering a scalable and explainable AI framework. Future studies will investigate multi-modal fusion and real-time optimisation to strengthen clinical applicability.

title: Traffic Sign Image Classification Using Deep Learning
authors: Answin Mariya, Sushitha Susan Joseph
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Recognizing traffic signs plays a crucial role in road safety and autonomous driving technology. Quick and precise recognition of traffic signs allows both human drivers and autonomous systems to respond correctly, reducing accidents and enhancing traffic flow. This study keeps computational requirements reasonable by using a three-stage deep learning approach that achieves high classification accuracy without heavy resource requirements. During the first stage, a baseline Convolutional Neural Network architecture was implemented on a selected portion of German Traffic Sign Recognition Benchmark dataset, to evaluate its first stage performance on classification as a baseline for comparison. The next stage focuses on improving the MobileNetV2 architecture by applying Region of Interest (ROI) cropping, resizing inputs to 75 × 75 pixels, and the application of various of data augmentation methods. The optimized model outperformed the baseline CNN considerably by achieving the accuracy of 97.17% on GTSRB test set. In the third stage, transfer learning was applied by retraining the German Traffic Sign Recognition Benchmark trained MobileNetV2 model using 85 classes from the Indian traffic sign image dataset with 1,288 images from different sources. The dataset is small, but the trained model is able to get 60.25% test accuracy. The proposed framework offers a trade-off between accuracy, model size and deployment feasibility, allowing practical implementation in real-time intelligent transportation especially in resource-limited situations.

title: Modeling and Simulation of a Smart Flyback DC Power Converter for Irrigation Automation
authors: Ahlam Moussa, Ilham Nait Him, Ronja Wimmer, Salma Sebbar, Moulay El Hassan El Azhari
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: This manuscript presents a design, modeling, and mixed-signal simulation framework for an intelligent solar-powered DC-DC flyback converter tailored for smart drip irrigation automation in Moroccan agriculture. The converter operates in Continuous Conduction Mode (CCM) under stringent ripple constraints, with an output current ripple below 20 % and output voltage ripple below 2 %. A microcontroller-based PWM control paradigm dynamically modulates duty cycle in response to stochastic solar irradiation and load variations, while integrated hardware-level protection mechanisms confer enhanced system resilience against electrical faults. Multi-domain co-simulation employing PSIM for power stage validation, Proteus for mixed-signal system integration, and MATLAB/Simulink for comprehensive performance characterization confirms correct operation under diverse operational scenarios. The topology achieves regulated 15 V output from an 18-24 V photovoltaic input with 92.7 % peak efficiency and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$100 \mu ~\mathrm{s}$</tex> fault response time. This investigation contributes to sustainable agricultural cyber-physical systems by providing an efficient, analytically validated power conversion solution for precision irrigation in water-scarce regions.

title: REALM: A Robust Evidence-Based Audio Logic Matrix for Forensic Deepfake Detection
authors: Hessa Albaqami, Lusan Alahmadi, Sawsan AbdulRahman
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: This paper addresses the vulnerability of audio evidence integrity in legal and financial contexts caused by contemporary text-to-speech technologies. We propose REALM (Robust Evidence-Based Audio Logic Matrix), a framework using a 180-dimensional feature representation and a novel Dual-Stacker Meta-Learner designed for robust detection. While initial ensemble models (Random Forest, SVM, and 1D-CNN) collapsed to a 52% baseline accuracy on external benchmarks due to systematic probability compression, our five-stage transductive supervised domain adaptation pipeline improved performance to 99% accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(AUC=0.9976)$</tex> under rigorous Leave-One-Out cross-validation on the labeled target domain. This approach kept misclassifications to a minimum, integrated with an HMAC-SHA256 watermarking module for tamper-evident reporting.

title: ECG-Based Cardiac Abnormality Identification with Customized Hybrid Lightweight Architecture
authors: Md. Abid Hasan Rafi, Semanto Sharma, Md Morsalin Islam, Nahid Islam, Pankaj Bhowmik
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases are the leading cause of global mortality, highlighting an urgent need for rapid and accurate diagnostic tools. Although electrocardiography (ECG) serves as a primary non-invasive cardiac assessment method, manual interpretation remains time-consuming and subjective. This study presents an automated deep-learning framework for classifying cardiac conditions from ECG images. We introduce a custom lightweight hybrid architecture that integrates a pre-trained MobileNetV2 backbone with a dedicated convolutional branch to capture both general and precise morphological features. Our pipeline incorporates two public datasets covering Myocardial Infarction (MI), History of MI, Abnormal Heartbeat, and Normal conditions, enhanced through advanced contrast adjustment and tailored augmentation. A two-phase training strategy with weighted loss effectively addresses class imbalance and data constraints. The model achieves exceptional performance, with near-perfect results (Accuracy: 0.9973, AUC: 0.9999) on a four-class dataset and strong generalization (Accuracy: 0.9510) on a smaller three-class dataset. Integrated Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) provides transparent visual explanations, confirming clinically relevant feature detection in ST-segments and QRS complexes. This work bridges high-performance automation with clinical trust, delivering a reliable and interpretable tool suitable for integration into diagnostic systems and point-of-care applications.

title: Fluid Antenna Aided Beam Split Mitigation in Wideband Terahertz Communications
authors: Parthiban. I, H. H. Sidhwa
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Terahertz (THz) communication provides ultra-wide bandwidth and promote applications like augmented reality and virtual surgery. The THz communication system provides wider bandwidth at the expense of extensive path loss, restricted dispersion and hardware limitations. For proper alignment and focusing of THz signal to a far distance and to improve the signal strength, deployment of massive MIMO or Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces in THz communication system becomes the viable solutions. Beam split is a phenomenon that arises due to improper alignment of the transmitted beams across subcarriers. In this paper to accomplish robust beam alignment, a receiver architecture based on Fluid Antenna Systems is proposed. Unlike conventional fixed-position antennas that suffer from gain loss when the beam drifts, the proposed system dynamically adjusts the physical position of the antenna elements to track the frequency-dependent beam direction. Further to reduce the implementation complexity, a low-complex Particle Swarm Optimization algorithm is proposed and compared with Grid Search and Steepest Descent Algorithms. The performance metrics of the proposed system is compared with the conventional fixed antenna systems.

title: The Role of Implementation Machine Learning to Enhancing the Prediction of Share Price: (Case Study Based on Jordanian Commercial Banks)
authors: Majed Qabajeh, Mohammad Alqsass, Jassim Algasawneh, Ibrahim Ezmigna, Mahmoud Alghizzawi, Murad Qabajeh, Ghada Al-Rawashdeh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: This paper aims to investigate the effectiveness of a machine learning (ML) methods in enhancing the prediction of commercial Jordanian banks share prices. This leads to larger datasets being processed, the ability to detect complex patterns and trends, and therefore, adaptability to market shifts quickly and making much more accurate and timely predictions compared to traditional data analysis methods. A sample of 18 commercial banks was utilized in the study from the commercial banks operating in Jordan in 2022. Data were analyzed using descriptive statistics and simple regression analysis was performed to assess the hypotheses; Machine learning adoption was the independent variable, and share price prediction was the dependent variable. The results illustrate that the incorporation of ML has led to a positive effect in predicting share price. The results indicate that, by introducing machine learning to stock price prediction models, the accuracy and efficiency of these predictions can be greatly improved reducing the risk and increasing confidence in the market, in particular for emerging economies such as Jordan. Additionally, the insights gained from this study can potentially contribute to designing successful financial forecasting models that can be applied to other developing countries across the region.

title: Automated Classification of Brain Tumor Based on MRI Images Using Deep Learning
authors: Somaia Sarmin Shouma, Md. Rubel Miah, Shadhin Ahmed, Md. Ariful Islam, Ragib Raiyan, Md. Saymon Ahammad
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: This study proposes a deep learning-based approach to classifying brain tumors using MRI images to enhance accuracy and stability for different kinds of tumors. Three new datasets from Mendeley Data are used: D1 (PMRAM: Bangladeshi Brain Cancer – MRI Dataset), D2 (Brain Tumor MRI Dataset with four types: Meningioma, Pituitary, Glioma, and No Tumor), and D3 (Brain Tumor Data). For training and testing purposes, D1 and D2 were combined to form a dataset of 13,664 MRI images (Meningioma: 3209, Glioma: 4173, Pituitary: 3450, No Tumor: 2832). D3 was used as an external validation set of 7023 MRI images (Meningioma: 1645, Glioma: 1621, Pituitary: 1757, No Tumor: 2000). A few of the state-of-the-art architectures—VGG19, InceptionV3, Xception, MobileNetV2, ResNet50V2, and DenseNet201—were compared against the proposed hybrid model, BrainNet. BrainNet achieved the highest model accuracy of 99% on the combined dataset and provided exceptionally well performance on the external validation set with an overall accuracy of 98.18%. It recorded the least error values among all models with MSE 0.0184, RMSE 0.1358, and MAE 0.0128, while other models achieved VGG19: 93.12%, InceptionV3: 97.16%, Xception: 97.41%, MobileNetV2: 97.87%, ResNet50V2: 98%, and DenseNet201: 98.22%. These results confirm the efficacy and accuracy of BrainNet in computerized brain tumor diagnosis and its feasibility in the clinical environment.

title: An Efficient Ensemble Learning Approach for Real-Time Anomaly-based Intrusion Detection in IoT Environments
authors: Harikrushna Gantayat, T Sri Lakshmi, Murali D, Avinash Amaranayani, B Swarajya Lakshmi, Prakash K
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) has also brought a notable threat to security because of the heterogeneity of devices, high-scale data transfer, and minimal in-built security. The proposed research paper is aimed at a machine learning-based anomaly detection system of security threat mitigations in real-time in IoT networks using secondary benchmark datasets. It was organized as a quantitative experimental method, and the data were pre-processed, feature extracted and selected, train the model and evaluate the performance using standard metrics. Various machine learning classifiers were discussed, and an ensemble model based on voting proved better detection performance with the increased accuracy, high reliability and decreased rates of false positives in comparison to single ones. The selection of the features also maximized the computational efficiency through a shorter detection latency, and the framework was therefore applicable in the real-time. The findings affirm the hypothesis that enhancing the resilience of infrastructures against IoT hacking is possible by using ensemble learning with optimized feature selection methods that can detect and cure security threats in a timely manner and accurately.

title: An IoT-Based Integration for Monitoring Real-Time Health Vitals and ECG Validation
authors: Mohd Azhan Umar Kamil, Abdul Razim, Ahsan Waseem, Zenab Elgamal, Mohammed Abdul Qadeer, M. M. Sufyan Beg, John Minton
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Internet of Things (IoT) describes a network where physical things are connected over the internet and have the capability of interacting with each other. This is done by embedding physical objects with sophisticated hardware including sensors, microprocessors and Wi- fi chips and then using a web server to exchange information with these modules. This gives the impression that the physical objects have become ’smart’ and can make various decisions without the need for manual intervention. Today, IoT has become an extremely popular and in-demand field in Electronics & IT, with applications ranging from day-to- day consumer electronics to highly advanced, reliable and secure military systems. Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) is a subset of IoT involving patient-centered medical applications. Such a system enables medical gadgets and equipment’s to make decisions, communicate information and automate repetitive tasks. Consequently, this can not only ease the workload on healthcare workers but also regulate the functioning of a healthcare center. IoMT finds its use in applications like remote health monitoring and disease prevention and control.

title: Optimizing Logistics in Supply Chains Using AI
authors: Haitham M. Alzoubi, Mohamed Ahmed Elbadawi, Samer Hamadneh, Thayer Mhd Nadim Marouf, Naseem Abidi, Sufian Nathmi Al Sheyab, Omar Kanaan, Ammar Mohammad Al-Ramadan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: The global supply chain management complexity is growing, and in this regard, it warrants developing innovative solutions for logistics operations optimization. The focus of this study is the use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) for logistics optimization in supply chains, through the analysis of relevant areas like route optimization, demand forecasting, inventory management, and the reduction of costs. This paper will show how advanced AI technologies, such as machine learning, predictive analytics, and deep learning, can help make better use of resources and help get the right information timely and the right decision made. AI case studies along with simulations illustrate the real benefits such as reduced transport costs, shorter delivery times, and better resource allocations. The research, in addition, deals with hurdles restricting the wide adoption of AI solutions in the area of logistics such as the integration of data, scalability issues, and ethical questions on AI use. This paper makes available a structured approach to the implementation of AI-based optimization of logistics, which supports the sustainable advancement of resilient supply chain systems.

title: ER-SE Hybrid Model for Automated Kidney Abnormality Detection in CT Images
authors: Yesrif Hasan Emon, Shyla Afroge, Mahmudun Nabi Saikat, Sumaiya Rahman, Shatadal Sikder, Spandan Basu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Kidney abnormalities have become a significant health problem that requires early and proper detection to improve diagnosis and treatment. This paper suggests a deep learning model, the ER-SE Hybrid Model (EfficientNetB0-ResNet50 with a multi-head Squeeze-and-Excitation module ) to classify kidney anomalies-kidney stones, cysts, tumors, and normal conditions, using a dataset of 12,446 CT images. The model is a synthesis of the EfficientNetB0 and ResNet50 focusing on the strengths of feature extraction, enhanched by an SE attention mechanism with multihead(4) functionality to concentrate on the most informative features of the image. It has a state-of-the-art performance of 99.92 percent accuracy and great precision, recall, and F1-scores when optimized by Adam, outperforming 99.49 percent F1-score of the base IR-CNN model. In order to optimize our solution, we have trained individual models: ResNet50 and EfficientNetB0 had the highest accuracy at 99.9199 percent, VGG16 had lower accuracy at 74.05 percent, and AlexNet had the lowest accuracy at 50.2044 percent. These findings inspired the development of the ER-SE Hybrid architecture which offers a clinically viable solution towards early and precise diagnoses of kidney abnormalities.

title: VARENet: Variance-Aware Residual Enhancement Network for Microscopic Fungal Classification
authors: Mahamodul Hasan Mahadi, Souhardo Rahman, Md. Nasif Safwan, Md. Iftekharul Mobin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Superficial fungal infections are widespread and require reliable diagnosis for effective treatment, yet conventional microscopic examination is time-consuming, subjective, and prone to interobserver variation.
