Deep learning approaches have shown promise for fungal image analysis, but existing attention mechanisms often fail to capture channel variability, limiting their ability to discriminate subtle morphological patterns. To address this gap, we propose VARENet, a variance-aware residual enhancement attention module integrated with the EfficientNetV2S backbone for microscopic fungal classification. VARENet uses variance-based channel weighting and residual fusion to strengthen feature representation and improve stability. Experiments on a large collection of labeled microscopic images demonstrated that the proposed model achieved an overall accuracy of 92.98%, outperforming the baseline and conventional attention mechanisms. Grad-CAM++ visualizations indicate that VARENet focuses on diagnostically significant fungal structures, highlighting its interpretability. This study provides a reliable deep learning framework for fungal image classification with potential clinical utility.

title: Spectral–Spatial Physics-Constrained Deep Neural Networks for Underwater Image Restoration and Semantic Perception
authors: Malathi V, Bharath K, Virubash Neppolian, Srijan P, Divyalakshmi P, Manimegalai C T
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Capturing usable images beneath the water surface is harder than it looks. Wavelength-selective absorption drains red light within a few meters; suspended particles spread luminous energy into a grey-white veil of forward scattering; and shipboard or diver-mounted lights produce gradients of saturation that change from frame to frame. Individually, these effects challenge existing restoration algorithms. Taken together, they break most of them. Equally troublesome is the tendency of the research community to treat restoration and semantic perception as separate problems, a division that costs accuracy in both objectives. We address this gap by introducing the Spectral–Spatial Physics-Constrained Deep Neural Network (SS-PCDNN), a single trainable system that encodes the Jaffe-McGlamery formation model as differentiable operations within the forward pass. Doing so lets the network infer scene-specific wavelength attenuation coefficients and ambient background radiance from the image itself, with no external depth sensor or water-quality probe. Our architecture centers on a dual-branch Spectral-Spatial Fusion Block (SSFB). One branch corrects color using a Spectral Channel Attention (SCA) module; the latter suppresses spatial haze via a Dilated Residual Dense Network. A content-adaptive Cross-Modal Attention Gate (CMAG) evaluates local image statistics and blends the two branches dynamically, depending on whether color distortion or haze dominates locally. We evaluated SS-PCDNN on three well-established benchmarks—UIEB, UIEBC, and RUIE-and found it reached a UIQM of 3.79 and UCIQE of 0.678 on UIEB, placing it ahead of all compared methods. Joint training with a segmentation head yielded 74.0% mean IoU on the SUIM eight-class benchmark, a result that neither pure restoration nor pure perception baselines can match simultaneously.

title: Synergistic Optimization of Solar Photovoltaic Generation and Energy Storage Deployment for Smart Grid Performance Enhancement
authors: Duy C. Huynh, Viet D. Q. Nguyen, Loc D. Ho, Phat T. Nguyen, Corina Barbalata, Matthew W. Dunnigan
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: The increasing integration of renewable energy sources in smart grids requires effective planning strategies to reduce power losses and maintain voltage stability. This paper proposes a synergistic optimization framework for coordinated deployment of solar photovoltaic (PV) generation and battery energy storage systems (BESS) to enhance smart grid performance. The optimization problem is formulated to minimize total active power losses under operational and voltage constraints and is solved using an advanced artificial bee colony (ABC) algorithm with chaotic initialization. Numerical simulations on the IEEE 33-bus smart grid demonstrate that coordinated solar PV-BESS integration significantly outperforms solar PV-only deployment, achieving a maximum loss reduction of 65.84% while satisfying all voltage limits. Comparative results with GA, PSO, and the conventional ABC algorithm confirm the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed approach for smart grid planning applications.

title: Arduino Fire Fighting Robot with Alert System using GSM Module
authors: Ranjith Kumar R, Pavithra N, Sanchal Joy, Tanisha K
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: In this project we explore how to design an intelligent robotic system that can automatically detect and extinguish fire in hazardous environments called The Arduino Fire Fighting Robot with Alert System. Description: This robotic system integrates an Arduino interface and GSM module for remote monitoring/control. It has fire sensors to detect if a building is on flames and it can move through an environment full of obstacles in order to attend the necessities. Through the GSM module, we provide real-time communication to process alarms or control requests and monitoring of firefighting operation status at a distance using (local) User Interface. Whenever fire is detected, GSM Module sends alerts to predefined mobile numbers along with desired actions.

title: Forecasting Shipping Delays Using Machine Learning
authors: Haitham M. Alzoubi, Naseem Abidi, Ayman Mansour, Fadi Ammouri
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Shipping operation management must be done effectively to decrease delays and, as a result, make the supply chain run smoothly. Due to the increase in the complexity of global logistics networks, it has become a priority for the stakeholders to subscribe to accurate shipping delay forecasting. The current research debates the use of machine learning techniques for predicting shipping delays on the basis of the analysis of various datasets including weather conditions, port congestion, traffic information, and historical shipment records. Various machine learning algorithms such as gradient boosting, neural networks, and support vector machines are assessed by this study in order to find their predictive power and computational efficiency. By employing feature engineering, it was possible to identify the key factors responsible for delays; the data preprocessing methods help to guarantee the robustness of the model. The findings show that ML-based predictive models possess the ability to detect early signs of delays and therefore allow management to take timely decisions. This article highlights the implications for increasing supply chain stability, lowering operational costs, and boosting customer satisfaction via the use of data-driven approaches.

title: Parkinson’s Disease Prediction using Time-Aware Transformer Networks on Longitudinal EHR Data
authors: J. Jamuna, K. Kasturi
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Parkinson’s Disease (PD) is a disorder that is progressive and neurodegenerative, and is often not diagnosed until considerable overt motor signs are present, so there is little value in beginning early interventions. The study presents a new machine learning framework that uses longitudinal Electronic Health Record (EHR) data to predict Parkinson’s Disease earlier and in an interpretable manner. Specifically, a built a Time-Aware Transformer (TAT) model that accounts for specific temporal dependencies and irregularities in the sequences of patient visits, as well as provides clinical concept embeddings with constructs extracted from medical ontologies. To make the most of the data available, we included a self-supervised pretraining step that allows the model to learn important patterns using a large virtual unlabeled EHR dataset. The model was trained to predict the outcome of PD within a 12-month time period, with different baseline models, which were logistic regression, random forests, GRUs, and Med-BERT, evaluated the performance of the original model in various ways. In the end the TAT model scored 0.89 AUROC and 0.58 AUPRC against the baselines in accuracy, recall, and F1. The TAT model could highlight important features such as early tremor, early diagnosis, and dopamine prescriptions that predicted PD. Overall, our results suggest that there is a capability for time-aware deep learning models to improve the early detection of Parkinson's Disease, which may assist clinicians in making appropriate timely decisions and has the potential to guide the creation of AI-based neurodegenerative disease monitoring in future.

title: Adaptive Consensus Selection Using Random Forest Classifier for Blockchain-Enabled Resilient Traffic Networks
authors: T. Vairam, Vishal K, Kavin T, Sengathirsoorian E. T.
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS) are also becoming dependent on broadcasting, sharing of data among vehicles, roadside units and among traffic management authorities. Such environments are subjected to tamper-resistant data integrity using blockchain technology; but a choice of suitable consensus mechanism continues to be a challenge depending on the density of network traffic, network conditions and latency needs. The paper suggests a Intelligent Consensus Selection Mechanism (ICSM) to use ML-based in traffic blockchain networks by concentrating on two radically different consensus protocols: Proof of Work (PoW) and HotStuff. PoW is secure and decentralized when the workload is low, and HotStuff allows high finality and scalability when the traffic load is high. On the fly, a trained machine learning model is used to decide on the best consensus protocol at the time, as it is informed by real time traffic and network on demand data (node density, transaction rate, and latency tolerance), etc. The experimental findings indicate that the offered strategy is much better in terms of throughput, confirmation delay and network resiliency than the usage of the static consensus deployment. The conclusions show that smart switching of consensus is a viable solution to the next-generation traffic systems done by blockchain.

title: Eye Gaze Tracking System for Behavioural Analysis in Autism Support
authors: Kavitha R, Uma Barathi U, Thuriya M, Santhosh Raj V, Thamizhselvan I
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is a neurodevelopmental disorder characterized by impairments in social interaction and communicational abilities. Eye gaze atypical behaviour is one of the first behavioural features of autism. In this paper, a webcam based, non-invasive, eye gaze tracking system to support behavioural analysis during the autism support initiative is presented. The system suggested uses the computer vision methods to identify facial marks, monitor the eye movements and gaze patterns using Python and OpenCV. The duration of fixation, the distribution of gaze, and visual attention patterns are examined and presented as eye-gaze characteristics that can be used to assess the behaviour. Typical and atypical gaze behaviors are visualized using scatter plots and heatmaps. The suggested method is a facilitating tool to support early behaviour analysis of autism in a cost-efficient and accessible fashion without having to use specialized equipment in eye tracking.

title: Estimation of Mortar Brick Composition from Spectral Reflectance
authors: Zakaria Bnoulkacem, Bikram Koirala, Philippe Soubrier, Xueqing Hu, Michiel Vlaminck, Hiep Luong, Nathan Van Den Bossche, Paul Scheunders
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: Characterization of mortar samples from spectral reflectance is essential for both the conservation of historical buildings and modern construction practices. We propose an approach to estimate the composition of mortar by spectral unmixing. Changes in the spectral reflectance of raw powder mixtures after chemical reactions with water complicate the problem. The challenge becomes even more pronounced when the endmember spectra of the cured pure components are unavailable for unmixing. The proposed approach mitigates spectral variability by projecting the dataset onto the unit hypersphere on which the endmembers are estimated using a minimum volume optimization technique. The endmembers are then manually assigned to the pure components and the original composition of the mortar samples is determined by minimizing the reconstruction error between the measured and reconstructed spectra. The methodology was validated using spectral reflectance data from 93 different mortar samples. Experimental results demonstrate the potential of the proposed approach.

title: A Multi-Layered Banking Security Framework Integrating AI, Biometric MFA, Hybrid Encryption, and Blockchain Auditability
authors: Jagadeesh Kola, Sayee Phaneendhar Pasupuleti, Sree Harsha Palli, Sai Phaneendra Manikantesh Kodete, M Jeevana Sujitha, Raghu Dhumpati
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: This study can respond to threats that digital banks are currently experiencing due to the nature of the quickly changing cybersecurity environment by suggesting a new level-based framework of security that unifies high-level user verification, encryption, artificial intelligence, and blockchain audit. Although the security models developed tend to work as isolated entities without flexibility, the proposed system will relocate a new architecture that includes five major components, like biometric-enhanced multi-factor authentication (MFA), hybrid AES-RSA encryption, AI-fraud detection, blockchain-based audit logging, and a layer of compliance awareness. Innovation of this work is in the compound architecture design, such that each of the layers supports and complements the other in strengthening the vulnerabilities within the user access, data transmission, transaction validation, and regulatory compliance. The experiments were carried out on the synthetic bank setting with real-world datasets, like IEEE-CIS Fraud Detection and the UNB CIC Financial Logs. Random Forest-based module of fraud detection and a success rate of 98.5% with good spoof resistance were achieved in a biometric MFA system and a high F1-score of 93.3 percent. Hybrid cryptographic systems apply performance and security, and the audit layer blockchain is energized with a Raft-consensus protocol, safeguarding a 100 percent abuse detection rate and tiny delay. Model transparency was also enhanced by means of SHAP values to describe AI predictions. The framework has not just the technical strength but also provides operational scale and regulator preparedness, and thus is a progressive way of digital banking that is secure and intelligent.

title: A Novel Hybrid Stacked Ensemble Model with XAI for Robust and Interpretable HIV Infection Prediction
authors: Khaled Mahmud Sujon, Aminul Islam Miraz, Tahmedur Rahman, Md. Tahmid Farabe Shehun, Adnan Shafi, Md Muzahidul Islam, Md Monirul Islam Molla
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Accurate and early prediction of HIV infection remains a critical public health priority, particularly in resource-limited settings where timely intervention can substantially reduce disease burden. However, existing machine learning approaches often exhibit limited interpretability and suboptimal sensitivity when applied to imbalanced clinical data, restricting their diagnostic applicability. In this work, we propose a hybrid stacked ensemble framework that integrates five heterogeneous base classifiers—Logistic Regression (LR), Random Forest (RF), Decision Tree (DT), XGBoost (XGB), and Artificial Neural Network (ANN)—with LR serving as a meta-learner. Evaluated on a publicly available clinical dataset under 10-fold cross-validation, the proposed model achieved an AUC of 0.711 and recall of 0.748, outperforming all individual classifiers. The ensemble’s higher recall underscores its effectiveness in minimizing false negatives—a critical factor in early HIV detection—while maintaining balanced precision and generalizability. To ensure interpretability, SHAP-based analysis was applied, revealing that LR and RF contributed most significantly to final predictions and that key features such as CD4 count and viral load aligned with clinical evidence. Overall, the proposed framework provides a lightweight, explainable, and clinically aligned approach for AI-assisted HIV risk screening in data-limited healthcare environments.

title: Cardiovascular Distress Prediction System using Multimodal Signal Integration
authors: Shilpa Chaudhari, Aditya JK, Pratyush Pai, Mangesh Nesarikar, Nikhil Sebastian
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Early diagnosis cardiovascular disease (CVD) remains a pressing clinical need. Inspired by the diagnostic limitations of isolated bio-signals and traditional Electrocardiogram (ECG), -based assessments, the proposed model integrates multiple physiological signals to identify stress-induced deviations counted for early indicators of cardiovascular distress. This work focuses on analyzing physiological signals: ECG, Photoplethysmogram (PPG), and Laser Doppler Flowmetry (LDF), which were captured under controlled rest and stress conditions. The system preprocesses these signals using filtering and segmentation algorithms, extracts key features such as heart rate variability, pulse transit time, and vascular resistance, and performs differential analysis to highlight stress-induced deviations. A machine learning model, trained on curated feature sets, is then used to classify cardiovascular risk status. Through the integration of ECG, PPG, and LDF signals, the model achieved high classification accuracy, rapid inference times, and strong consistency across synthetic and real datasets. These outcomes validate the effectiveness of multimodal physiological signal fusion for early-stage cardiovascular risk assessment.

title: Architectural Variations of YOLO for Skin Cancer Detection: Backbone–Head Flip Analysis
authors: Midhun P Mathew, Athira Pradeep, Albin John Wilson, Adarsh S, Amal Sabu, Aswajith Sajeev
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Skin cancer, particularly malignant melanoma, remains one of the most life-threatening yet preventable cancers worldwide, where early and accurate detection is essential for improving patient survival rates. This work investigates the application of the YOLOv11 real-time object detection framework for multi-class skin lesion detection using dermoscopic images from the HAM10000 dataset. Five clinically significant lesion categories—AKIEC, BCC, BKL, MEL, and NV—were selected to evaluate detection performance in a practical medical imaging setting. In Phase I, a baseline YOLOv11 model was developed with comprehensive preprocessing and augmentation strategies, and its performance was evaluated using standard object detection metrics including mAP, precision, recall, F1-score, and inference time. In Phase II, selected architectural variations were explored by replacing the default backbone with MobileNetV3 and the detection head with an SSD-style head to analyze the trade-offs between computational efficiency and detection accuracy. Experimental results demonstrate that the baseline YOLOv11 configuration provides strong multi-class detection performance, while the lightweight architectural variants offer insights into deployment-oriented efficiency trade-offs. This study highlights the adaptability of modern object detection frameworks for medical image analysis and provides a practical foundation for efficient AI-assisted skin lesion detection systems.

title: Dynamic Energy-Saving Design for Double-Faced Active RIS-Assisted Communications With Imperfect CSI
authors: Yang Cao, Wenchi Cheng, Jingqing Wang, Wei Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: Although the emerging reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) paves a new way for next-generation wireless communications, it suffers from inherent flaws, i.e., double-fading attenuation effects and half-space coverage limitations. The state-of-the-art double-face active (DFA)-RIS architecture is proposed for significantly amplifying and transmitting incident signals in full-space. Despite the efficacy of DFA-RIS in mitigating the aforementioned flaws, its potential drawback is that the complex active hardware also incurs intolerable energy consumption. To overcome this drawback, in this paper we propose a novel dynamic energy-saving design for the DFA-RIS, called the sub-array based DFA-RIS architecture. This architecture divides the DFA-RIS into multiple sub-arrays, where the signal amplification function in each sub-array can be activated/deactivated dynamically and flexibly. Utilizing the above architecture, we develop the joint optimization scheme based on transmit beamforming, DFA-RIS configuration, and reflection amplifier (RA) operating pattern to maximize the energy efficiency (EE) of the DFA-RIS assisted multiuser multiple-input-single-output (MISO) system considering the imperfect channel state information (CSI) case. Then, the constrained stochastic majorization-minimization (CSMM) based AO algorithm address non-convex problems. Simulation results verified that our proposed sub-array based DFA-RIS architecture can benefit the EE of the system more than other RIS architectures.

title: Hyperspectral Monitoring of Perchlorate-Contaminated Wheat
authors: P. Chatelain, G. Delmaire, H. Demailly, L. Gutierrez, G. Roussel, G. Decocq
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: During the First World War, the use of artillery spread chemical elements in the soil. Perchlorate, in particular, can have harmful effects on the environment and human health so it needs to be detected. As a consequence, effects of perchlorate absorption on crops may be explored by inspecting In-lab wheat leaves with 2 hyperspectral cameras. Therefore, state-of-the-art indicators, interpretable by biologists, are investigated in this article to identify the most discriminant wavelengths. The results show that the ARI2 indicator is able to detect high-perchlorated wheat in different experimental contexts. The temporal monitoring indicates that the effect begins to be visible 30 days after sowing while the spatial investigation enables to propose leave sub-areas where the effect is visible.

title: A Comparative Study of DenseNet121 and Random Forest for Fundus Image Classification of Eye Disorders
authors: Mushtaq Mahyoob Saleh, Walla Mustafa, Albraa Ali
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: The global burden of preventable vision loss necessitates the development of accurate and accessible diagnostic tools for retinal diseases. This paper presents a comprehensive comparative analysis of a CNN model and a Random Forest model for multi-class retinal disease classification using fundus images. Using a balanced dataset of 4,000 fundus images (1,000 each of Normal, Myopia, Glaucoma, and Diabetic Retinopathy), our CNN model achieved an overall accuracy of 99.33%, which significantly outperformed the Random Forest model, which achieved an overall accuracy of 88.32%. Furthermore, the proposed approach outperforms existing approaches reported in the literature. The CNN model demonstrated high classification performance across all classes: Diabetic Retinopathy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=100 {\%}$</tex>, Glaucoma <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=98.67 {\%}$</tex>, Myopia <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$= 100 {\%}$</tex>, Normal <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=98.67 {\%}$</tex> with only four misclassifications. In contrast, the Random Forest Model had 94 misclassifications, with noticeable weakness in Glaucoma detection which was F1score <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=80 {\%}$</tex>. Our findings establish that deep learning achieves higher diagnostic accuracy in comparison to machine learning for multi-class retinal disease classification.

title: Automated Radiance-Level Data Quality Assessment for Hyperspectral Imaging
authors: Simon A. Trim, Daniel Schläpfer
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: To accommodate growing demand for streamlined processing of uncrewed aerial system (UAS) based hyperspectral imaging (HSI) data, an integrated raw-to-reflectance processor has been developed within the m4mining project. HSI workflows typically emphasize data calibration, geocoding, and atmospheric correction, yet data quality issues are rarely addressed using a standardized approach, and community-wide adoption of quality indicators is lagging. To address this, we present an automated Data Quality Assessment (DQA) toolbox. The DQA operates on calibrated radiance cubes and provides a standardized suite of quality metrics, including bad pixel detection, shadow masking, and band-by-band signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) estimation using a spectral differencing approach. Results are exported as a multi-layer ENVI file and summarized in a comprehensive PDF report with visualizations and recommendations. Validated with HySpex Mjolnir VS620 data from mining surveys, the toolbox enables rapid evaluation of HSI quality, supporting informed decisions before atmospheric correction and product derivation.

title: Osteovision: An AI-Enabled Deep Learning and Diagnostic Analytics System for Osteoporosis Detection
authors: S. Arun Inigo, Tamilselvi Rajendran, Parisa Beham Mohammed
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Osteoporosis is a persistent skeletal disorder marked by diminished bone mineral density (BMD) and deterioration of bone microarchitecture, resulting in heightened fracture risk and related morbidity. Dual-Energy X-ray Absorptiometry (DEXA) is the recognized reference standard for evaluating osteoporosis. However, its limited accessibility, high operational costs, and dependence on specialized equipment constrain largescale population screening, particularly in low-resource clinical environments. Optimal bone delineation and reduced imaging variability across modalities are achieved through a standardized image preparation pipeline that includes noise reduction, contrast enhancement, and illumination normalization. To provide a comprehensive skeletal evaluation, the model is trained and tested on a curated dataset of X-ray and DEXA images from various anatomical regions, including the spine, hip, and knee. A web-based clinical interface built with Next.js leverages the trained model, enabling rapid image processing and immediate diagnostic reporting. The experimental results show that the proposed framework performs well across multiple imaging modalities, achieving an overall classification accuracy of 90%. This shows that it could be a useful tool for early osteoporosis screening because it is effective, easy to use, and supported by clinical evidence.

title: Optimized Random Forest Regression for Predictive Modeling of Environmental Correlation Metrics in Typhoon Data
authors: Epifelward Niño O. Amora, Jose C. Agoylo, Jorton A. Tagud, Jimson A. Olaybar, Patrick D. Cerna
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: This study presents an optimized Random Forest Regression model to predict environmental correlation scores in typhoon data within the Philippine Area of Responsibility (PAR), aiding climate impact assessments and disaster preparedness. Using typhoon records from 2000 to 2023, key features—latitude, longitude, and wind speed—were selected for their predictive relevance. Model performance was enhanced by restricting tree depth (max depth = 7) to balance accuracy and generalization, with 5-fold cross-validation ensuring consistency. The optimized Random Forest model achieved high predictive accuracy, with an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.95 on test data, effectively capturing complex environmental interactions in PAR-specific typhoon datasets. This approach underscores the value of targeted feature selection and parameter tuning in environmental predictive modeling, providing a scalable solution for future studies focused on typhoon-related risks within the PAR.

title: Detecting and Predicting Wildfire Risks in Changing Climates Using AI
authors: Mohammad Alshinwan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Wildfires are a growing problem for ecosystems, human lives, and the economy, and are made worse by climate change. Traditional wildfire risk assessment methods are unable to fully consider the complicated interconnections between natural environmental variables, weather patterns, and human factors, especially in a changing climate. In this study, the application of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in wildfire risk detection and prediction in dynamic and diverse landscapes is investigated. This research intends to set up a context that can predict the occurrence of fire risk accurately, with the help of AI techniques which through the multicasting of data with various texts of origin, like satellite photos, meteorological data, and fire records, local fires, as well as other models trained in the occurrence of fires, the models will be created. The proposed method consists of a combination of the expert-driven selection of the features and the analysis of the time series in order to determine the principal factors of fire risk such as vegetation moisture, temperature variation, and wind speed, and the model created. In addition, this study will focus on the way through which deep learning could be used to detect and identify patterns in spatio-temporal data and to forecast fire spread and intensity. The findings show that AI-based systems have the potential to enable better early warning systems together with the efficient utilization of resources and would be capable of limiting the harmful effects of fires amid the transformation of the environment. This scientific exploration has computed intelligent information systems of disaster management as its contribution, recognizing AI as the most integral factor in complicated environmental issues.

title: Low-Cost, Open Personal Health Wearable for Sports Performance and Rehabilitation
authors: Narasimha Sai Yamanoor, Srihari Yamanoor
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: Participation and success in sports used to be an exclusive domain, with only a few nations of certain wealth and resource levels able to succeed and dominate international events, including quadrennial events such as Olympics, World Cups in various sports and others with interim frequencies. As economies develop, sports enthusiasts have emerged and found growing success in various sports endeavors. A fundamental requirement of competitiveness in the world of sports is the ability to measure and understand one’s health metrics to benchmark and set goals for improvement and readying oneself for participation and ultimately success. Citizens of developing nations continue to be stymied by the expense of measuring and managing metrics, as well as locating appropriate healthcare consultants. The current work presents an effort at tackling metrics at a minimal expense to the user. The configuration presented will have a variety of sensors useful for the determination of health including IMU, Temperature Sensor, Heart Rate & Pulse Oximetry Sensor, and Blood Glucose Sensor. The design is open and therefore can be appropriately modified or expand sensory metrics as desired. A costed BOM for the prototype is presented to guide the understanding and development of such open products on a scale. The open design also provides opportunities for success in regional entrepreneurship and manufacturing initiatives such as Make in India and may be adopted elsewhere worldwide.

title: An Intelligent Machine Learning Framework for Beamforming Management in 6G-IoT Networks
authors: M. Venkata Subbarao, P. Aasritha Ramani, Y. Veera Sai Sindhu, V. Sravya Lakshmi Tulasi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Beamforming optimization in 6G-enabled Internet of Things (IoT) networks remains challenging because of rapid environmental variations and device mobility. These factors often lead to beam misalignment and increased latency, and inefficient resource utilization. Conventional beamforming techniques based on exhaustive beam training, classical signal processing, or deep reinforcement learning often incur high computational complexity, limiting their suitability for real-time IoT scenarios. This paper presents a comprehensive comparative analysis of machine learning (ML) techniques for intelligent beamforming optimization using a computer vision assisted 6G IoT dataset that integrates network parameters, environmental factors, device characteristics, vision-derived features, and beamforming performance metrics. To improve prediction accuracy and reduce computational overhead, Chi-square (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${\chi}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex>), Analysis of Variance (ANOVA), and ReliefF feature selection algorithms are employed to generate optimized feature subsets by capturing statistical relevance, discriminative power, and neighborhood-based feature importance. These subsets are used to train and evaluate multiple classifiers, including Decision Trees(DT), Support Vector Machines (SVM), k-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Neural Networks, and Ensemble models. Experimental results demonstrate that tree-based and ensemble classifiers, particularly Bagged Trees, consistently achieve high accuracy up to 99.5% on test data and 99.6% during validation even with reduced feature sets. The results confirm that featureoptimized ML frameworks enable accurate, low-complexity, and energy efficient beamforming decisions, making the proposed approach well suited for real-time and edge enabled 6G IoT beam management applications.

title: An Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation Framework for Dynamic Career Path Optimization
authors: Chunduru Anilkumar, Kanithi Gunasagar, Akasapu Gayatri, Sai Ganesh Padhy, Susarapu Kavya, Sathishkumar V E
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: The accelerated change in technical positions has shown the constrainment of stagnant manually populated career advice, such as obsolete advice, imaginary resources, and insufficient personalization. The present paper gives an introduction of CareerFlow AI, a Gemini-3 based Agentic Retrieval- Augmented Generation (RAG) system providing verified and market-aligned career roadmaps with interactive skill verification. The architecture coordinates two-stream retrieval by integrating Google Search Grounding, due to which real-time verification is provided, and a Qdrant vector database, which has <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2. 3 M}$</tex> embedding of semantic matching. In an experiment of 150 learners in four experimental conditions, CareerFlow AI had hallucination rate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 2 \%}(\mathbf{3} / \mathbf{1, 5 0 0}$</tex> recommendation), which is lower than 11.8% of a usual RAG baseline, and 9.2% of an ungrounded LLM. It cut end-to-end response time by 40 percent as compared to online platform baselines, and the roadmap generation was under 30 seconds. The system achieved 88% accuracy in prediction of time-tocompetency, which is defined through scenario-based Readiness Lab tests, and a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$68 \% 7$</tex>-day return rate, which is 2.2 times higher than the work with a static guidance. Ablation experiments also measure the role of search grounding, dual-stream retrieval and interactive validation to the reduction of hallucinations, latency and engagement.

title: Hysteresis-Driven Thermal Behavior and Geometry Effects of Multi-Tube PCM Tanks: A 2D Enthalpy Model with Tank-Scale Correlations
authors: Noureddine Itani, Nazih Moubayed, Chantal Maatouk, Rabih Dib
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: This paper presents AHMT-2D, a two-dimensional axisymmetric enthalpy-based finite-difference model for paraffin-based latent heat thermal energy storage in a multi-tube cylindrical tank. Direction-dependent logistic hysteresis functions distinguish melting and solidification and capture asymmetric phase-change behavior. The model resolves coupled heat transfer among the heat-transfer fluid, tube wall, and PCM while accounting for geometry-dependent PCM mass scaling and volume-weighted energy evaluation. Results show that the multi-tube configuration enhances thermal response, achieving complete melting in about 10.2 h and solidification in about 10.5 h, with an approximately 7 °C shift between melting and solidification midpoints. Reduced-order correlations are proposed for tank-averaged liquid fraction, enthalpy, effective heat capacity, and melting-solidification time asymmetry. The framework provides a compact basis for system-level modeling of PCM storage in solar-assisted absorption cooling and related renewable thermal applications.

title: Assessment of Knee Joint Movements Using Inertial Sensors and Ensemble Machine Learning Models
authors: Adinath R, Madhavan Bharanidivya, Samiappan Dhanalakshmi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: This research introduces a real-time system developed for monitoring knee rehabilitation activities utilizing embedded artificial intelligence.This paper presents a, real-time system designed for monitoring knee rehabilitation exercises using embedded artificial intelligence. The proposed project uses two external LSM6DSOX inertial measurement units (IMUs) connected to an Arduino Nano 33 BLE microcontroller to record knee movement data. By deploying TensorFlow Lite for Microcontrollers (TinyML), a random forest model is trained on sensor data collected by the two LSM6DSOX sensors which is placed just above the knee and for femur bone and just below the knee for tibia bone. The collected data is then used to train a random forest, XGboost and SVM machine learning model to identify the accuracy of the knee movements which will be rated. The results of the experiments shows that the random forest model being the most accurate with an accuracy of 86.86 followed by XGboost and SVM with an accuracy rate of 86.72 and 74.15 respectively. This study confirms that the TinyMl based wearable devices are reliable enough and cheaper for real time knee rehabilitation monitoring

title: Performance Analysis of FO-PI Controller for Inner Current Control Loop in a SPV-Grid Connected System Under Sudden Change in Loading Condition
authors: Bhabasis Mohapatra, Renu Sharma, Ritesh Dash, Binod Kumar Sahu
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: The intelligence of solar photography system into the power grid has several operational challenges particularly in managing the sudden changes in loading conditions. Effective inner current control loop is a critical for ensuring great stability and thereby maintaining power quality as per the standard reference values. The classical pi and the resonant controller struggles to provide optimal performance under dynamic conditions due to their limited tuning flexibility. In this research an attempt has been made with the fractional order PI controller as an advanced control strategy for the inner current control loop to mitigate oscillations and improve system robustness. A comparative analysis of performance between the fractional order PI controller and proportional resonant controller along with dead beat controller is conducted to evaluate their effectiveness in stabilizing the DQ Current components under sudden load changes. The simulation result carried out under MATLAB Environment has demonstrated that the fractional order of PI controller has significantly reduces the oscillation ensuring better current tracking and reduced overshoot. The direct and quadrature trajectory plots clearly shows that the fractional pi controller ability to smooth out the oscillatory behavior is a major contributor in improving the reliability of photovoltaic grid interconnection.

title: Predicting Electromagnetic Emissions from Cable Configurations Using Deep Neural Networks
authors: Zaynab Hamyeh, Moncef Kadi, Mahmoud Mehdi, Ali Alaeddine
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: Radiated electromagnetic emission from cable harnesses poses a major challenge in the electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) design of modern electronic systems, particularly in automotive and hybrid electric vehicle applications. This paper considers a representative cable configuration used in hybrid vehicles to connect the battery to DC/DC converters, for which radiated electric field levels constitute a critical EMC concern. A hybrid data-driven framework based on deep neural networks (DNNs), combining spectrum-based and pointwise prediction models, is proposed for the efficient estimation of frequency-domain radiated electric fields. The framework is applied to predict the total, horizontal, and vertical components of the radiated electric field. Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves high accuracy while reducing computation time compared to full-wave electromagnetic simulations.

title: Advanced Light Management Studies Ofsilver Capped Silica Nanoparticle for Thin C-Si Solar Cell Application
authors: Veda Samhitha Lakshmipurapu, Kavya Nair, A. Sandeep Kumar Reddy, Arijit Bardhan Roy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: As the next-generation photovoltaic devices, thin and flexible crystalline silicon(c-Si) solar cells have generated a lot of attention because of their lightweight, bendable design and potential for incorporation into wearable and portable applications. Nevertheless, their smaller thickness restricts optical absorption, which results in a reduction in efficiency. In order to overcome this difficulty, sophisticated light management techniques are necessary. This study explores the use of Silver caped silica <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\mathbf{A g}-\mathbf{S i O}_{\mathbf{2}}\right)$</tex> nanoparticles as a back mirror means of improving light absorption and trapping in thin c-Si solar cells. While <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{A g - S i O}{}_{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> nanoparticles scattering and plasmonic- like behavior help to increase optical path lengths within the active layer, their significant absorption coefficient and tunable bandgap enable better photon harvesting over a wide spectrum range. Almost 7% reduction of reflectance is shown in the proposed structure using FEM (Finite element Method) analysis tool. The work describes design strategies, material integration methods, and optical simulations that show how Silver caped silica nanoparticles can maximize photocurrent generation, reduce reflection losses, and enhance light coupling. The suggested tactics show a possible technique to achieve sophisticated nanostructured light management schemes in c-Si solar cells that are mechanically flexible, lightweight, and efficient.

title: IT-Enabled Smart Workplaces and Employee Wellbeing: Pathways to Socially Responsible Management
authors: Prerana Baber, Mini Agrawal, Abhilasha Dixit, Ruturaj Baber, Ujjwal Kumar Pathak
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: The study has uniquely contributed to the literature by offering a configurational perspective on employee wellbeing instead of linear models and analyzed configurational conditions that lead to a high level of employee wellbeing. This study investigated the combination of three variables i.e., smart workplace, job autonomy and organizational support, to predict employee wellbeing. fsQCA technique (Fuzzy-set qualitative comparative analysis) has been used to explore the different combinations research. Responses from 339 employees employed in an IT-enabled manufacturing environment have been collected. Findings revealed smart workplace management as a strong predictor of employee wellbeing. Whereas a combination of smart workplace and job autonomy or organizational support predicts equal employee wellbeing. A combination of all three variables predicts the highest level of employee wellbeing. The results highlight the importance of a systems thinking approach in the design of work environments where flexible configurations tailored to organizational context and employee needs can foster engagement and satisfaction.

title: AI-Driven Lung Cancer Detection Using Based on RF Optimization
authors: Nusrat Jahan, Al-Amain, Mirza Nadim Saad, Istiack Amin, Shifat Ahmed
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Lung cancer remains the most prevalent cause of cancer-related mortality, indicating the need for sophisticated diagnostic methods. This paper presents an improved machine learning system that combines SMOTE-driven class balance optimization, RandomizedSearchCV-based hyperparameter tuning, and hybrid feature selection (ANOVA F-test and chi-squared). With improved settings, Random Forest outperformed the other eight classifiers in an evaluation using clinical survey data (accuracy: 95.16%, F1-score: 97.25%). The classification effectiveness of SVM and Naïve Bayes was competitive (F1-scores: 95.24% and 96.34%). A dual-phase feature selection technique, MinMax scaling, and careful category encoding all contribute to the pipeline's resilience, underscoring the revolutionary potential of ensemble learning in clinical predictive modeling. These results help the development of AI-powered precision oncology decision support systems.

title: Edge-Optimized Intelligent License Plate Recognition and Access Management for Smart Campus Parking Systems
authors: Princy Paulin P, Cynthia J
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: This paper presents a lightweight, edge-based Automatic License Plate Recognition (ALPR) framework designed for smart campus parking access management. The proposed system integrates YOLOv8 for real-time license plate detection and EasyOCR for robust alphanumeric character recognition under varying illumination and viewing conditions. The extracted plate number is authenticated against a locally deployed SQLite database to enable automated entry authorization. Unauthorized vehicles are redirected to a visitor registration interface for controlled access. The model was trained on a custom dataset of 600 annotated campus vehicle images (70%) training, 20% validation, 10% testing). Experimental evaluation demonstrates a detection accuracy of 93%, with precision of 0.94, recall of 0.92 and an average inference latency of 28 ms per frame on CPU, confirming real-time edge feasibility without GPU acceleration. Compared with recent ALPR systems reporting 89-92% accuracy, the proposed framework achieves improved performance while maintaining low computational complexity. The system provides a scalable, privacy-aware, and cost-effective solution for intelligent campus access control.

title: Refining Brain Tumour Segmentation in MRI: A Synergistic Approach with Modified FCM and JAYA Algorithm
authors: Minal Kalpesh Deore, S Sakkaravarthi, Lynsha Helena Pratheeba HP, R.Indumathi, Kottaimalai Ramaraj, M Thilagaraj
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: In the cerebral cortex, gliomas are a particularly common primary tumor. In the field of clinical imaging, segmentation of brain tumors and identification are extremely difficult. In the healthcare sector, magnetic resonance image (MRI) delineation is essential and significant since it can aid in prognosis and diagnosis, general expansion forecasts, tumor intensity measurements, and individual plan of action. Since cerebral tumors can vary widely in terms of their constructions, forms, frequency, location, and visual appeal—such as extent, contrast, and visual variation—segmenting them can be challenging. The ability to analyze medical imagery is essential for helping people recognize various illnesses. One important aspect that relies on the doctor's competence and understanding is the categorizing of tumors in the brain. Employing visual instruments, physicians or other specialists manually partition the brain by defining tumors on clinical pictures. Although this method is labor-intensive and sluggish, it might be precise. Moreover, manually segmenting images is difficult to measure due to the growing need for clinical imaging and the shortage of skilled physicians. To assist doctors, a smart algorithm for identifying and categorizing brain lesions is necessary. An intriguing avenue for cerebral tumor study is autonomous dissection of medical images that could made possible by new advances in Deep Learning (DL) for tasks like image classification. To improve segmentation of tumors from MR cerebral scans, the suggested method combines the JAYA optimization algorithm with Modified Fuzzy C-Means (MFCM) clustering. The computerized approach that was built helps the professionals identify, define, and detect abnormalities in clinical imaging earlier.

title: Design and Optimization of Frequency Selective Surface (FSS) for Vehicular Communication
authors: Janagam Tejaswini, G.S. Parthybhan, K.L. Yasaswini, Bashyam Sugumaran
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: In this paper, the design, optimization and integration of a Frequency Selective Surface (FSS), with a planar antenna for vehicular communications operating in the 5.9 GHz ISM band for V2X applications is presented. The initial step included the development and optimization of a compact planar antenna that provides better than −10 dB return loss at the resonant frequency of 5.9 GHz with overall dimensions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$22.4 \times 26.6\ \text{mm}^{2}$</tex>. Subsequently, the design of the FSS was revised through several geometric modifications in order to improve its capability to filter signals. The design was also optimized so that the FSS operates at the same frequency (5.9 GHz) as the planar antenna with a transmission coefficient of less than −20 dB, while utilizing a unit cell size of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$12.28 \times 12.28\ \text{mm}^{2}$</tex>. In the methodology, the resonant characteristics of both the antenna and the FSS are adjusted independently, and then coupled together to create an optimum performance for the combined system. The independent operation of the antenna and FSS at two different resonant frequencies provides the desired filtering and radiation characteristics. The planar antenna was combined with a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 \times 3$</tex> array of FSS size of 39.6 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\times 39.6\ \text{mm}^{2}$</tex> and an optimized separation distance of 20 mm from an antenna was used in the final configuration. In addition to filtering the 5.9 GHz frequency band, the FSS also acts as a reflector to reduce the amount of back radiation from the antenna and enhance the performance of the antenna. The gain of the antenna was also enhanced from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 6 6\ d B i}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4. 2 5\ d B i}$</tex> by the addition of the FSS. The developed integrated structure demonstrates its suitability for V2X vehicular communication systems operating in the C-band.

title: A GAN-Based Vision Transformer Model for Explainable Plant Disease Identification
authors: B. Sathiyaprasad, V. Vigneshwaran, P. Sanjay
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Early identification of plant diseases is essential to avoid yield losses and support sustainable agriculture. Deep learning has shown remarkable results in identifying plant diseases, but the issue of little intra-class variation and great interclass variance of visually similar disease categories remains difficult. We show a new deep structure for potato and tomato leaf disease detection and classification. We demonstrate in the current study that training generative adversarial networks (GANs) can improve database images to high quality samples, but this does not affect feature characteristics that convolutional neural networks can exploit. To explain the image feature in training and classification, we provide a new deep architecture depending on the Deep Vision Transformer (DeepViT) model for training and classification and the XAI model based on Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) and SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP). Under lab-controlled imaging circumstances, the model trained and tested on the PlantVillage dataset of nine tomato and potato diseases classes. With macro ROC-AUC of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.995,92.11 \%$</tex> test accuracy, great class-wise balance as shown by macro precision of 89.70% and recall of 91.30%, we reach 92.11% test accuracy. The visual explanations show the XAI emphasizing the leaf lesion regions generating disease rather than background noise. More clarity and comprehension.

title: Crop Yield and Crop Stress Progression Prediction in Smart Vertical Farming Systems using a Novel STFNet -Transformer Hybrid Deep Learning Model
authors: Shoma R S, Loganathan D, Karthik P
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Vertical farming (VF) has become a viable, long-term solution to traditional agriculture, proving to be much more viable in urban and climatically challenging places. Nevertheless, it requires the capability of ensuring a regular production of high yield crops that are produced in a highly controlled environment. Plant stress may be induced by minor variations in environmental conditions (light, humidity, CO 2, and nutrients) resulting in a considerable loss of yield. Although current VF systems are furnished with multimodal IoT sensor suites, extant monitoring and forecasting techniques are frequently informed by manual examination or rudimentary models that are not suited to understanding the intricate, multidimensional and temporal interaction between plants and environment. Furthermore, existing Deep learning (DL) models used in open-field farming cannot be easily transferred to the context of architecture and geometry of VF infrastructures. The paper fulfills this gap by proposing a novel STFNet-Transformer Hybrid Deep Learning Framework which is capable of utilizing the data of heterogeneous IoT sensors, adds spatio-temporal learning to represent stress development, and properly forecasts outcomes of the yield under a variety of stress conditions. The results illustrate better prediction quality and stability in different VF conditions, which allows real-time decision-making to precision agriculture. This dual-task architecture is an important milestone towards achieving resilient, autonomous, and sustainable food production systems activities geared toward Industry 5.0 goals.

title: Power Efficient Multiplier for AI based Application
authors: A.Rosi, N.Nalini, Samyuktha Penta, Ali Elrashidi, B.Manideep, G. Karthikeyan
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The investigations made on multipliers in this research has ended up in a novel multiplier design combining Vedic multiplication (8x8,16x16 and 64x64) with the Sklenksy adder to address challenges in modern digital systems, such as high-power consumption, long latency, and large area. The Vedic multiplier reduces partial products through parallel computation, speeding up the multiplication process, while the Sklenksy adder optimizes carry propagation, enhancing overall speed. The design also integrates power suppression techniques to minimize spurious power dissipation, ensuring energy efficiency in high-speed operations. The proposed multiplier is evaluated based on key performance metrics: speed, power dissipation, and area. Comparisons with traditional multiplier designs show significant improvements in both speed, area and power efficiency. Efficiency improves by combining the Vedic multiplication, which eliminates partial products by way of parallelism, with the Sklenksy adder, which reduces the number of stages of carry propagation. Moreover, power suppression methods cut down on spurious switching activity causing a decrease in energy consumption. This optimized multiplier design is ideal for efficient, low-power applications in embedded systems, mobile devices and communication technologies, where balancing fast computation and minimal energy consumption is critical.

title: NeoGuard: AI-Powered Monitoring System for Preterm Infants in NICU
authors: N Ramya Rani, J.Dhanasekar, Kalaivannan S, Ashok kumarV, Bupesh G, Sathiyavasakan K
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The Smart Neonatal Guardian presents an advanced neonatal monitoring system aimed at optimizing care in Neonatal Intensive Care Units (NICUs) through the integration of artificial intelligence (AI) and real-time physiological data analysis. At its core, the system utilizes the ESP32 microcontroller to acquire and process continuous input from essential biosensors that monitor heart rate, body temperature, and oxygen saturation levels. Specifically designed to support the unique needs of preterm infants—who are particularly vulnerable to medical complications—the system ensures uninterrupted surveillance to detect early signs of distress. Sensor data is recorded in Excel format and subsequently analyzed using machine learning (ML) techniques, which enable the recognition of clinical patterns and the early diagnosis of critical conditions such as neonatal sepsis and respiratory distress syndrome (RDS). The system empowers healthcare professionals by offering predictive insights and facilitating prompt, informed interventions. Furthermore, Internet of Things (IoT) capabilities extend monitoring functions beyond the clinical setting, allowing for remote supervision of neonatal health. By seamlessly combining AI, IoT, and predictive analytics, the Smart Neonatal Guardian sets a new benchmark in neonatal healthcare, enhancing both the responsiveness and accuracy of NICU practices while contributing to improved survival rates and long-term outcomes for premature infants.

title: DRIFT-GAN: Distribution-Preserving Adversarial Malware Generation for Dynamic Windows Malware Detector
authors: Avantika Gaur, Mohit Bhatt, Preeti Mishra, Vinod P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: The modern malware detectors are prone to adversarial attacks. Existing literatures shows that Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) based techniques are commonly used for adversarial malware generation across multiple domains. By prioritizing binary-centric generation, these models often distort the feature distributions essential for realistic multiclass malware. Furthermore, their efficacy is mostly tested against static detectors. To address these limitations, this paper proposes DRIFT-GAN, a distribution preserving adversarial framework targeting Windows based dynamic malware detectors. A Conditional Variational Autoencoder (CVAE) based generator which is incorporated with Gaussian-driven feature selection to craft multiclass evasive samples to preserve class distributions while evading detection. These evasive samples undergo iterative evaluation against a black-box detector, guided by a discriminator to verify their adversarial effectiveness. Experimental analysis demonstrate that DRIFT-GAN achieves a 53.75 % evasion rate across targeted malware families, contributing to a reduction in F1-score from 97.57 % to 53.25 %. Overall, the proposed framework provides an effective solution for both evaluating and strengthening the robustness of malware detection systems against adversarial threats.

title: Carbon-Aware Dynamic Workload Scheduling for Large Language Model Training with Real-Time Energy Optimization
authors: Judy Simon, Nellore Kapileswar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The intense development of large language models has significantly increased the amount of energy and carbon footprint of cloud-based training. This work presents a framework known as Carbon-Aware Dynamic Workload Scheduling (CADWS) that enables the reduction of the environmental footprint of the large-scale model training process through the coordination of the computational workloads with the times of reduced carbon intensity. The framework brings in live energy tracking, short-horizon carbon-intensity prediction and workload orchestration adaptation to dynamically control training run-time in maintaining the underlying model architectures. Empirical assessment in realistic conditions in a cloud environment proves that the proposed approach can reach an area under a curve of the receiver operating characteristic (ROC) of 0.93, which is 22 % points higher than a carbon-agnostic baseline. Furthermore, CADWS achieves the decrease of the overall carbon emissions up to 40 % and reduction of the total energy expenditure up to about 16 % and planning precision is reduced to about 97 %. These results indicate predictive and carbon-aware scheduling is a viable and feasible strategy towards attaining sustainable large language model training in modern cloud systems.
