title: Optimizing Heart Stroke Prediction Using Ensemble Learning: A Comparative Analysis
authors: Anchal Kumawat, Biswaranjan Acharya, Smita Nirkhi, Subhashree Choudhury
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: Heart diseases remain a significant global health concern, underscoring the need for accurate predictive models to enable early diagnosis and intervention. This study presents an ensemble learning framework based on the Random Forest (RF) algorithm to predict the risk of heart stroke using a sensitive medical dataset characterized by inherent outliers. The proposed model demonstrates superior performance compared to conventional machine learning techniques, achieving an accuracy of 99.25%. Comprehensive evaluation metrics further validate the robustness and reliability of the RF-based approach in handling complex clinical data. The findings highlight the potential of ensemble learning methods to enhance stroke risk prediction, thereby supporting early clinical intervention and improving patient outcomes. This research offers valuable insights for informed clinical decision-making and effective cardiovascular disease management.

title: AI-Based Wearable Posture and Muscle Fatigue Monitor
authors: Rajasekar P, Nagul J, Naveen Kumar V, Neeladhasan M
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The major cause of musculoskeletal disorders is the improper body posture and extended muscle strain especially in people who do desk jobs. Traditional wearable devices can generally sense posture or muscle motion separately and do not normally provide feedback in an effective timely fashion. The proposed paper describes an Artificial Intelligence (AI)-based wearable posture correction system with an integrated muscle fatigue detection algorithm used as a continuous monitoring and real-time intervention. The proposed system is a hybrid of posture sensors based on MPU6050 MEMS inertial measurement unit (IMU) and muscle sensors based on electromyography (EMG) sensor. A Random Forest machine learning classifier is used to process multimodal sensor data to detect improper posture, muscle fatigue conditions. When unfavorable posture is detected, an embedded vibration motor will give immediate haptic feedback, causing correction to be possible immediately, without the latency caused by the cloud. The wearable gadget has a small rechargeable Li-ion battery and is designed in manner that it utilizes low power consumption to enable all- day use. Experimental testing done on actual users shows a posture and fatigue classification accuracy of over 95 percent with an average alert response time of under one second. The proposed system will provide a small, privacy-conscious, and efficient system to correct the posture and prevent injuries.

title: Intelligent Medical Report Analysis System: A Serverless Cloud Architecture for Automated Healthcare Diagnostics
authors: Adithiyaa D, Lakshhmi Narayanan T, Gokulakkrishna Saravanan, Deepa Nivethika S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: The explosion of digital medical records has created an urgent need for tools that do not just store data, but actually understand it. This paper presents a serverless ecosystem that is built on AWS and combined with Google's Gemini AI that is designed to automate the analysis of medical reports. We have structured the framework of the system to include features like secure uploads through presigned URL's, structured metadata handling in DynamoDB, text extraction from PDF files using PyPDF2, AI-driven assessment via the Gemini API, and timely notifications delivered through Amazon SNS. Performance evaluation shows an overall processing time of roughly 85 seconds, with Lambda 1 taking an average time of 0.5 seconds, Lambda 2 about 1 second, and the AI-enabled Lambda 3 taking 83 seconds. The tests that were performed on 10 reports confirms that Gemini reliably detected all clinically relevant deviations and produced clear, meaningful summaries, validating the system's accuracy, and clinical usefulness.

title: Digital Business Transformation Using Microsoft Dynamics 365: A Comprehensive Approach
authors: Chandan Kotha, Akhilesh Kalaru, Bindu Kotha, Tejaswi Rangineni
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Adoption of digital business transformation is critical for organizations wanting competitiveness in the fast-changing, technology-driven world. Microsoft Dynamics 365 is gradually being regarded as a key platform for facilitating digital transformation in organizations as a suite of ERP and CRM applications. This research explores Dynamics 365’s role in digital transformation, namely its capabilities to automate workflows, use data for decisions, interact with customers, and allow flexible use of the cloud. Also, we review the key barriers and forthcoming changes in leveraging Dynamics 365 to facilitate business transformation. The results highlight the need to integrate a comprehensive digital transformation strategy and platforms such as Dynamics 365 represent a key component.

title: Adaptive Control–Instrumentation Co-Design Framework for Real-Time IoT-Based Process Control
authors: Hifsah Nasir, Wajahat Riaz, Alaaeddine Ramadan, Maqbool Khan
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: The process control systems based on industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) are typically built on fixed sampling of sensors and fixed control strategies, which may result in inefficient use of the resources and slow reactions to the changing settings. In this paper, the co-design of adaptive control instrumentation to work over a real-time process control based on IoT is suggested. This framework incorporates the logic of decision making into the instrumentation layer whereby the sensors automatically can adapt sampling rates to the control error and process dynamics. An adaptive sensor control is calculated in a closed-loop feedback controller to perform control actions. The framework is tested with simulation based on the time-series of the industrial sensors. Findings of experiments prove enhanced control precision, diminished data transfer, reduced energy use, and quick reaction in contrast to the traditional fixed-sampling methods.

title: Innovation through Technology Integration: Transforming Strategic HR Planning in the Hotel Industry
authors: Hamza Elrehail, Shehadeh Mofleh Algharaibeh, Belal Shneikat, Iyad I. Alghoul
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: This study investigates the integration of technology into Strategic Human Resource Planning (SHRP) and its impact on innovation within the hotel industry in the United Arab Emirates (UAE). The research collected data from 209 employees who work in different hotels throughout the United Arab Emirates hospitality sector through a quantitative method. This research evaluated the connections which exist between SHRP and technology integration and organizational efficiency. SEM analysis tested four research assumptions by assessing technology as a mediator that strengthens SHRP results. The study confirmed that SHRP together with technology integration have a substantial impact on organizational efficiency yet technology functions as a partial intermediary force connecting SHRP and efficiency outcomes. This research introduces novelty by studying the UAE hospitality sector which fast-embraces digital transformation while examining technology as a mediating factor in HR strategic processes. The study fills the gap in SHRP-tech integration research while showing the way digital tools maximize workforce planning and operational performance. The research provides concrete recommendations for hotel managers and HR practitioners who want to unite their human capital strategies with modern technology.

title: Semantic-Guided Diffusion for Robust Multi-Object Tracking with Temporal Enhancement
authors: Yuhao Li, Haotian Zheng, Jinqiu Sun, Yu Zhu, Yanning Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Diffusion-based motion prediction methods have demonstrated strong capabilities in modeling nonlinear motion for multi-object tracking (MOT). However, in complex scenarios involving target interactions or occlusions, these methods still suffer from frequent identity switches and inaccurate trajectory predictions. This is primarily due to insufficient joint modeling of appearance and motion cues, as well as limited adaptability to diverse motion patterns. To address these challenges, we propose a semantic-guided diffusion-based method, termed SGDMOT, which jointly models target motion dynamics and identity consistency. Specifically, we leverage historical trajectories to query image-level global features, incorporating appearance and contextual information. These are fused with motion information via an attention mechanism, guiding the diffusion process to generate semantically consistent trajectories. Furthermore, we introduce a learnable multi-scale temporal modulation module that dynamically adjusts the encoding of diffusion time steps based on historical motion states. This enhances the model’s ability to adapt to motion variations across different temporal granularities, improving temporal modeling during the generation phase. Extensive experiments on the DanceTrack, MOT17, and MOT20 benchmarks demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach. Notably, on the DanceTrack test set, SGDMOT achieves an absolute gain of 2.3% in Higher Order Tracking Accuracy (HOTA) compared to a baseline diffusion model relying solely on motion features. Our code and pretrained models will be publicly released.

title: Ultra-Wide Voltage Range Bidirectional EV Charger with Topology Morphing Control
authors: Sachin Chauhan, Hans Anniste, Andrei Blinov, Andrii Chub, Dmitri Vinnikov
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: Various fast-charging standards, such as megawatt charging systems (MCS) and CHAdeMO 3.0, have specified charging voltages above 1kV. Also, the growing number of battery pack voltages for fast-charging solutions has driven demand for ultra-wide-voltage-range converters. To meet requirements determined by charging trends, an active-clamped dual-current-fed (ACDCF) series resonant topology has been proposed and designed for wide-voltage-range EV charging/discharging operations. This solution features a combination of an active-clamped current-fed configuration and topology morphing control (TMC), enabling an output voltage range of 150V to 1000V, while keeping the voltage stress within limits and allowing the selection of relatively low-voltage switches with low on-state resistance. The TMC also offers a more cost-effective alternative to the reconfigurable converter that uses multiple expensive breakers to achieve wide voltage ranges while maintaining soft-switching over the entire operating range of the proposed topology. The operational study, converter design, and control are briefly outlined, along with the experimental validation of a 3.3kW proof-of-concept prototype. Moreover, the performance of the proposed topology has been tested for 400V and 800V battery systems, yielding promising time/energy-weighted average efficiencies (T/EWAE) of around 95.7% and 96.5%, respectively. Although this design is primarily intended for EV charging, it can also serve as a DC interface for a different range of energy sources and storage technologies.

title: Zero-Shot Real-Time Pedestrian Re-Identification Services Via Edge-to-Edge Cooperation
authors: Guo Li, Jiandian Zeng, Zihao Peng, Ke Chen, Tian Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Services Computing
abstract: Real-time person Re-Identification (ReID) services in video surveillance remain challenging due to real-time requirements and limited generalization of supervised models. Traditional supervised methods typically require extensive labeled training data and are confined to closed identities, struggling to generalize to unseen individuals or new environments. To overcome these limitations, recent work leverages large Vision-Language Models (VLMs) for zero-shot ReID, utilizing their rich visual-textual representations to identify pedestrians without prior training. However, directly deploying such VLMs in the cloud inherently introduces high latency, further hindering real-time performance. To tackle the above challenges, we propose VL-ZSReID, a novel edge-collaborative inference framework that deploys VLMs on resource-constrained edge devices to enable zero-shot ReID in complex multi-camera campus surveillance environments. The framework partitions the ReID task into a Tracking Service and a VLM Service, which are coordinated by a Cache-assisted Heuristic Edge Scheduling Strategy (CHESS) to reduce latency and improve throughput across heterogeneous edge devices. To enable practical deployment of VLMs on resource-constrained edge devices, VL-ZSReID adopts several system-level design choices. It incorporates a Tracking ID Discriminator (TID) as an intelligent caching module, an Attribute Caption Generation (ACG) mechanism that produces standardized feature descriptions, and a Temporal-Camera Feature Generator (TCFG) that leverages spatiotemporal context to improve accuracy. Implemented and evaluated in a real campus deployment, VL-ZSReID achieves 19.98%–195.47% higher throughput and 38.00%–82.27% lower latency than baseline methods, while improving Rank-1 and mAP by 2.44%–44.68% and 4.11%–60.89%, respectively.

title: Reliability Investigation and Efficiency Analysis for a Family of High-Gain DC-DC Converters
authors: Vemparala Seshagiri Rao, Mahmoud F. Elmorshedy, M. S. Bhaskar, Dhafer Almakhles, S. Kumaravel
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Most conventional high-gain dc–dc converter topologies prioritize voltage gain enhancement. However, for practical applications such as photovoltaic and fuel-cell systems, improving converter efficiency rather than increasing voltage gain beyond a certain threshold is of paramount importance. This paper presents a comprehensive analysis of five newly developed high-gain dc–dc converters (HGDC) architectures. The introduced configurations substantially reduce current stress on both passive and active components, particularly those positioned near the input side, thereby improving overall efficiency. A detailed comparative study against published HGDCs is conducted. The results demonstrate that reduced current stress directly contributes to higher efficiency in the introduced Topologies-1 and -4, outperforming both the remaining introduced converters and conventional solutions. Furthermore, a small-signal model for Topologie-1 is developed, and the necessary transfer functions and frequency response are obtained with a Bode plot, and a PI controller is designed. Experimental validation for Topologie-1 and Topologie-4 demonstrates successful step-up of 48 V to 650 V at 0.5 kW and 50 kHz switching frequency. Finally, conducted the component-level reliability investigation to assess the failure rate of components.

title: Recurrence Network Approach for Severity Classification of Parkinson’s Disease Using Multimodal Data
authors: K Deepa Raj, G Jyothish Lal, E. A. Gopalakrishnan, Juan Rafael Orozco Arroyave
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Parkinson’s disease (PD) a multi-factorial progressive neurodegenerative disorder characterized by the loss of dopamine neurons in the substantia nigra, leading to motor and non-motor impairments. Although there is a significant advancement in the clinical assessment, its underlying mechanisms remain only partially understood, while demanding the integration of computational, signal processing, and systems-level approaches for deeper insights. Recent years have witnessed a paradigm shift in PDs research, pioneering clinical evaluation to data-driven frameworks that unleash the efficacy of network science, and multimodal sensing technologies. This study proposes to model neurodynamics in PD from a complex systems theory in the context of Recurrence Network (RN) - a transformation of a time-series data to a complex network. We classified the severity stages in PD patients using RN features extracted from the network corresponding to speech and gait signals. As the motor impairments observed in these biomarkers manifests intermittently rather than uniformly, we followed a frame-based approach in the classification framework. Precisely, we evaluated the frame-based analysis under three threshold conditions: 40%, 50%, and 60% correctly classified frames. Experimental results show that (under the strict threshold condition of 60%) the proposed multimodal approach achieves approximately a 37% and 18% improvement in accuracy compared to that of the speech-only and gait-only modalities, respectively. Besides, we investigated the qualitative and quantitative characterization of the RN topology and measures, respectively, across different PD severity stages.

title: TransVort: A Temporally-Coherent Physics-Guided Neural Network for Super-Resolving and Denoising 4D Flow MRI of Cerebrospinal Fluid_supp5-3690957.docx
authors: Vitaly Rayz
publish: N/A
abstract: Objective: To enhance the diagnostic utility of 4D flow MRI in assessing cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) dynamics by super-resolving and denoising measured velocities using temporally coherent, physics-guided neural networks (PGNN). Methods: Synthetic 4D flow MRI was generated from 40 computational fluid dynamics (CFD) simulations across 10 ventricular geometries. These simulations were used to generate paired synthetic 4D flow MRI and high-resolution velocity fields used for supervised training. Here, we compare a previously developed temporally independent network (div-mDCSRN-Flow) using divergence-based regularization with two novel temporal PGNNs (tempo-mDCSRN-Flow using divergence-regularization and TransVort additionally constrained by the vorticity transport equation). Results: In application to synthetic 4D flow MRI of a double gyre flow showed the temporal PGNNs improve vorticity estimation. Similarly, both temporal methods improved estimation of vorticity and time-averaged wall shear stress (TAWSS) of synthetic 4D flow MRI in the 3rd and 4th ventricle. While using temporal PGNNs improves velocity and vorticity quantification across temporal resolutions, TransVort demonstrated additional improvement at fine temporal resolutions. Application of TransVort to in vivo 4D flow MRI of CSF flow captured vortex formation and dissipation in the 4th ventricle over the cardiac cycle. Conclusion: Leveraging the temporal information of 4D flow MRI improves reconstruction of high-resolution velocity fields. This leads to better estimation of gradient-based flow metrics such as vorticity and TAWSS, which are associated with neurodegenerative and neurovascular diseases. Significance: Augmenting the accuracy of 4D flow MRI increases its potential for adoption as a clinical tool for diagnosing and monitoring disorders of neurofluid dynamics.

title: Advanced Preprocessing-Driven Inception-V3 for Diabetic Retinopathy Grading
authors: Mohammad Atikur Rahman, S A Sabbirul Mohosin Naim, Md Mehedi Hasan, Rashedur Rahman, Yasmin Akter Bipasha
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is a leading cause of blindness worldwide, particularly in low-resource settings. Preventing irreversible vision loss requires accurate and early diagnosis. In this study, we propose a deep learning–based automated DR detection system using the Inception-V3 model, enhanced through advanced image preprocessing techniques. Two scenarios are examined: Case 1, which applies Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE) and Enhanced Super-Resolution Generative Adversarial Networks (ESRGAN) for image enhancement, followed by data augmentation; and Case 2, which uses the original fundus images with augmentation only. Both cases are evaluated using the APTOS 2019 dataset. Case 1 achieves 98.91% accuracy, significantly outperforming Case 2 (72.33%) and exceeding the performance of several state-of-the-art models. The integration of CLAHE and ESRGAN not only improves visual quality but also boosts classification accuracy. This approach shows promise for scalable and reliable DR screening, especially in underserved regions.

title: Increasing Collaboration Efficiency for a Large Number of SMEs with Blockchain Sharding
authors: Inayatulloh, Harco Leslie Hendric Spits Warnars, Maybin Muyeba, Holly Deviarti, Gunawan Wang, Rudy Rudy
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: SMEs are part of the nation's economy that makes a major contribution to economic growth and employment absorption. SMEs have many limitations that hinder the progress of their businesses, so they need assistance from the government and other parties. The government has tried to provide various types of assistance for SMEs to develop their SME businesses. However, the low transparency of information about the use of government assistance for SMEs impacts the potential for misuse of this assistance. Blockchain technology is a solution for the monitoring system for SME funding assistance. With blockchain technology, the government and other related parties can monitor the collaboration system and the use of aid funds for SMEs in accordance with applicable provisions. However, the concept of peer-to-peer validation on the blockchain is not effectively applied to a large number of SMEs spread across several distant regions. The solution to increase the efficiency of implementing blockchain technology to support funding assistance for SMEs is the use of blockchain sharding. Thus, the purpose of this research is to apply blockchain sharding to increase the efficiency of the collaboration system and funding assistance for SMEs. The research uses qualitative methods through literature reviews and simulations with Hyperledger fabrics. The test results with Hyperledger Fabric show that every transaction related to SME collaboration must be validated by all nodes on the blockchain network to produce transparent and valid information.

title: Function-Preserving Adversarial Example Generation for Windows PE Malware
authors: Fanqi Meng, Huimin Li, Jingdong Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: The growing threat of adversarial attacks against machine learning-based Windows Portable Executable (PE) malware detectors necessitates robust defensive strategies. This paper proposes a semantic-preserving adversarial example generation framework specifically designed for Windows PE files. The study addresses the critical challenges of maintaining malicious functionality and ensuring format compliance, limitations often overlooked by existing adversarial generation methods. Through a systematic analysis of the inherent weaknesses in four major attack categories, including gradient-based perturbation, generative adversarial network (GAN) based feature manipulation, heuristic search, and reinforcement learning-based sequential modification, we innovatively integrate functional preservation verification and format-compliance constraints into the model design. This integration provides a novel pathway for constructing adversarial examples that are stealthy, potent, and portable across platforms. Finally, the paper discusses promising future directions, such as leveraging large language models for adversarial reasoning and developing multimodal defense mechanisms, thereby offering a systematic perspective for building more resilient malware defense systems.

title: Applied Deep Learning for Automated Gastrointestinal Disease Detection: Model Implementation and Assessment
authors: Sourav Das Polok, Salil Batra, Gouri Shankar Chakraborty, Makul Mahajan
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Gastrointestinal (GI) diseases encompass a broad spectrum of conditions that demand accurate and timely detection to facilitate effective treatment. Relying solely on symptom-based diagnosis often leads to delays, highlighting the need for automated and intelligent diagnostic tools. With the integration of artificial intelligence (AI) in healthcare, various deep learning models have been developed for disease identification using endoscopic images. This study evaluates and compares the performance of six deep learning architectures— EfficientNetV2S, MobileNetV3Large, ResNet50V2, VGG19, ViT-B16, and InceptionResNetV2—on the Kvasir v2 dataset. After preprocessing and applying transfer learning techniques, InceptionResNetV2 emerged as the most effective model, achieving a test accuracy of 95% along with high precision, recall, and F1 score. These results affirm the potential of InceptionResNetV2 in delivering accurate and reliable GI disease classification, offering substantial support for clinical decision-making and advancing AI-driven diagnostic systems.

title: Real-Time Detection of Alternaria Lesion Spots on Apple Leaves Using Yolov11
authors: Mansoor Ahmad Kirmani, Sartaj Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Alternaria leaf disease poses a significant threat to apple orchards, making early lesion detection critical to prevent yield losses and enabling prompt disease management interventions. The proposed research develops a DL-based YOLOv11 framework to detect and localize Alternaria lesion spots on apple leaves. The self-acquired ALD dataset, collected from apple orchard environments and annotated with lesion-level bounding boxes, was subsequently leveraged to fine-tune the proposed model within the Ultralytics training framework. The proposed model demonstrates strong generalization performance, reaching 0.93 precision, 0.92 recall, 0.93 mAP@0.50, and 0.78 mAP@0.50:0.95, indicating effective lesion-spot detection and accurate localization in real-world field imagery. With an inference latency of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$12.185 ~\text{ms} /$</tex> image on a Tesla T4 GPU, the model is well-suited for real-time deployment in agricultural settings. Visualization-based assessment confirms reliable localization of multiple lesions within a single image, supporting the framework's suitability for practical orchard monitoring.

title: Predictive Analysis of Drug Toxicity
authors: Samar Thiab
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: The issue of drug toxicity continues to pose a major problem in the drug discovery as well as development processes, leading to a series of unfavorable reactions that in turn bring barriers to the safety and effectiveness of pharmaceutical treatments. In this research, the application of predictive analytics and machine learning approaches as tools for a more precise and reliable examination of drug toxicity has been particularly scrutinized. Through the analysis of various types of information sources such as biochemical, molecular, and clinical trial information, we have utilized the latest algorithms such as ensemble learning, deep learning, combined with features selection techniques which are intended for the identification of the main factors of toxicity. The examination of the results included the disciplines of molecular modeling, pharmacogenomics, and bioinformatics through the application of a complete framework for the toxicity screening in the first steps of drug discovery. The findings show marked improvements in predictive accuracy and a decrease in both false-positive and false-negative rates when compared to traditional approaches. The present study emphasizes the power of AI-based predictive analytics to shorten the time of drug development, reduce risks, and ensure patient safety.

title: Deep Learning Driven — Side Channel Cryptoanalysis with Adaptive Trace Profiling and Cross - Platform Generalization
authors: Aron Devan, Akash S, S. Balapriya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: A variety of physical side-channels (e.g. power consumption and electromagnetic emissions) allow embedded cryptographic devices to leak sensitive information that can be used to recover secret keys, even when using strong algorithms such as AES-128. Recent developments in deep learning—based side-channel analysis techniques have improved the accuracy of attacks, however there is still the need for a large number of profiling traces and for such attacks to be resilient against noise/misalignment of traces, and for there to be limited transferability of methods across different hardware platforms. In this paper we propose a unified deep learning-based framework for side-channel cryptanalysis, which is designed to alleviate these limitations by integrating together reinforcement learning based adaptive profiling of traces, an attention-enhanced CNN-LSTM architecture to model leakage with robustness, and adversarial domain adaptation to achieve cross-platform generalization. Unlike prior works, which each focused on separate aspects of profiling efficiency, leakage modelling, or transferability, we combine all three within a single attack pipeline. The adaptive profiling mechanism retains only those traces which provide highinformation based upon both the confidence of the model and the signal-to-noise characteristics of the readings to greatly reduce the number of unnecessary (redundant) measurements. The crossplatform robustness is achieved through transfer learning, as well as by adversarially aligning the feature distributions from both devices. The results of our experiments, using ASCAD, CHES'17 and a number of additional ARM/AVR datasets, indicate that the proposed framework provides fast AES-128 key recovery with order of magnitude less traces while maintaining comparable performance across heterogeneous platforms.

title: Prominent Weed Detection Using Image Processing
authors: Nitin Manam, Jeevitha Gaikwad, K Bhanu Teja, Sangeetha M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Agriculture plays a crucial role in the production of food all over the world. But weeds are becoming a major issue for farmers by reducing the yield of the crop by competing for the essential resources like nutrients, water, sunlight, and space. Conventional weed control techniques, such as uniform herbicide application and manual weeding, are time consuming, labour-intensive, and often result in excessive use of chemicals. To address this issue, a system for weed detection using image processing techniques is proposed. Python and OpenCV are used to process images taken from agricultural fields in order to identify vegetation regions based on color features. The system uses size-based analysis and contour detection to distinguish weeds from crop plants. The proposed system supports precision agriculture practices. The images used for experimental purposes were related to crops such as maize, paddy, baby corn, and vegetable crops, which showed promising accuracy for detecting the presence of weeds.

title: Building Smart Cities: How Technology is Shaping the Future of Urban Development: (UAE Context)
authors: Soumaya Askri, Mohamed Dawood Shamout, Zakariya Chabani, Salima Hamouchi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: As urban centers worldwide expand at an unprecedented rate, the demand for sustainable, efficient, and expertly managed urban spaces has become critical. In the UAE, where rapid urbanization drives significant change, the smart city concept emerges as a strategic solution to meet citizens’ evolving needs. Advanced information and communication technologies not only enhance resource management and urban governance but also necessitate robust management frameworks—encompassing strategic planning, project management, stakeholder coordination, and risk management—to ensure seamless integration and scalability of these innovations. This article explores a range of transformative technologies—including deep learning (DL), machine learning (ML), Internet of Things (IoT), mobile computing, big data, blockchain, 6G, Wi-Fi 7, Industry 5.0, connected cars, robotics, HVAC technology, and cybersecurity—and delves into the management challenges that accompany their deployment in urban environments. By examining the complexities of integrating these technologies within a single ecosystem, the study highlights the importance of effective operations management, data governance, and collaborative leadership in overcoming issues related to infrastructure robustness, data privacy, and security. Furthermore, the review identifies key future dimensions of smart city development with a focus on the UAE’s ambitious goals for smart living. It underscores that proactive urban planning, enhanced energy designs, and advanced medical technologies must be supported by strategic management practices to achieve sustainable and resilient urban spaces. Ultimately, the success of smart cities hinges on the collaboration between public institutions, private enterprises, and citizens, all guided by innovative management solutions that balance growth with environmental sustainability.

title: Adaptive Weighted Ensemble with Dynamic Feature Selection for Enhanced Solar Power Generation Forecasting: A Novel Hybrid Machine Learning Framework
authors: S. Gomathi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: The problem of solar power generation forecasting is one of the most significant issues of renewable energy management because the models of prediction have to be correct to ensure higher efficiency of grid stability, energy storage and the work of the distribution. Traditional machine learning methods are generally unable to recognize the nonlinear relationships akin to those between meteorological factors and solar power production especially when atmospheric conditions vary. In this paper, we suggest Adaptive Weighted Ensemble (AWE) framework, which incorporates dynamic feature selection based on mutual information and intelligent weighted ensemble prediction using various learners in the background. Extensive comparisons of solar power generation data with actual data prove that it performs better (98.53% accuracy (RMSE: 832.08, MAE: 386.84, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>: 0.6501)) than traditional algorithms such as Random Forest (RMSE: 871.28, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>: 0.6163), Gradient Boosting (RMSE: 855.44, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>: 0.6301), Support Vector Machine (RMSE: 957 The model has good convergence, generalization and equal error distribution hence suited very well in operational solar power forecasting systems. Our contribution would be an enhancement of computational renewable energy analytics through an accurate, interpretable, and reliable framework of solar generation forecast.

title: GSM based Vehicle Theft Detection with Real Time Alerts and Engine Immobilization using Arduino
authors: Bathri Narayanan S M, Thangaraj J, Madhavan R, Lakshmi Narayanan L N
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: This project proposes a low-cost, reliable vehicle-theft detection and response system built around an Arduino microcontroller, GSM communication, and engine immobilization hardware. The system continuously monitors vehicle status using a combination of sensors (shock/vibration sensor. On detection of suspicious activity like unauthorized opening, while the authorized key is absent, the Arduino immediately triggers two parallel actions sends real-time alerts (SMS and optional automated call) via a GSM module to predefined owner phone numbers containing the event type and, when available, GPS coordinates; and activates an engineimmobilization mechanism (solid-state switch or relay controlling starter/ignition circuit) to prevent vehicle operation. The proposed system was experimentally evaluated under multiple real-time scenarios to assess its effectiveness. Performance assessment shows a detection accuracy of 96.2%, an average alert response time of 3.4 seconds, and a false alarm rate of 3.1%. The results demonstrate that the system provides reliable real-time alerts and effective engine immobilization, making it suitable for practical vehicle theft prevention applications.

title: Smart Anti – Snoring Pillow with IoT- based Health Monitoring and Sleep Apnea Risk Analysis
authors: M Ponni Bala, Pooja V, Sakthi Murugan P, Srimathi S, Vimal Prasath T
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Snoring is a common sleep disorder that negatively impacts sleep quality and can also be one of the potential symptoms of serious diseases, including Obstructive Sleep Apnea, or OSA. All traditional anti-snoring devices merely dampen noise and cannot track the critical health metrics. This work proposes a Smart Anti-Snoring Pillow with IoT-based health monitoring to identify snoring, together with important parameters such as sleep position, heart rate, and oxygen saturation (SpO2). Sensor data will be analyzed through a microcontroller and further sent in real time to a mobile application for analysis. The system categorizes sleep apnea risk and initiates subtle vibrations for repositioning the user to avoid airway blockage. This monitor provides discrete, continuous, all-night monitoring to enhance the quality of sleep and enable early identification of health hazards. Modeled as an affordable and easy-to-operate alternative, it has potential for widespread application at home, offering an improvement in health with accessibility to early medical intervention.

title: TinyML-Based AQI Forecasting Using CNN with Butterworth Low-Pass Filter for Edge Devices
authors: Ch Madhu Bhushan, Alekhya Lankavalasa, Hanchete Lahari Bai, Firoj Gazi, Md Muzakkir Hussain, Mohammad Abdussami
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: Air pollution, particularly <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{PM}_{2.5}$</tex>, is a major concern for public health in urban areas. In this paper, we propose a TinyML pipeline for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{PM}_{2.5}$</tex> forecasting that integrates a lightweight 1D-CNN with zero-phase Butterworth low-pass denoising to suppress sensor noise without phase distortion. Using hourly CPCB data from Perungudi (Chennai, 2024), the denoised CNN achieves strong accuracy on the normalized scale (MSE 0.000061, MAE 0.00389, RMSE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.0078, R^{2}=0.9908$</tex>). The model is converted to TensorFlow Lite and deployed on an ARM Cortex-M4 with a 42 KB footprint, 11.4 ms inference latency, and 4,344 B RAM; measured power is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2,501 \mu \mathrm{W}$</tex>. Relative to an unfiltered CNN and Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) / Feed-Forward Neural Network (FFNN) baselines, the denoising front end yields substantial error reductions while also lowering latency and memory. These results indicate that principled signal preprocessing paired with compact CNNs enables accurate, low-power, on-device <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P M}_{2.5}$</tex> monitoring suitable for smart-city deployments.

title: Supply Chain Disruption Prediction Using Machine Learning
authors: Cheng Ling Tan, Haitham M. Alzoubi, Ramakrishna Yanamandra, Samer Barakat, Fanar Shwedeh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: The growth in the size and intricacy of worldwide supply chains has made them more vulnerable. The effect of unpredictable events such as natural disasters, geopolitical tensions, and market fluctuations has increased. In this research, we focus on the use of machine learning (ML) tools as a means of forecasting supply chain disruptions, thus enabling early identification of risks and better decision-making. Collection data that typically include a historical record of supply chain performance, external environmental factors, and economic indicators together with other information, were combined for the purposes of the study to obtain accurate predictive models that will be able to identify potential disruptions. The predictive capability of various ML methods such as random forests, gradient boosting, and neural networks is compared. The research goes further to examine some engineering concepts and the incorporation of real-time data streams in the model to enhance its stability. The objective of the study is to provide practical ideas for managers of the supply chain to implement risk-reducing measures, work better, and become structurally stronger. The results shed light on the ability of machine learning to guide the development of highly secure and flexible supply chains.

title: Impact of a Photovoltaic System on High-Andean Residential Electricity Demand Through Pareto Analysis
authors: Elmer Arellanos-Tafur
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: This research presents a quantitative assessment of photovoltaic generation system impacts on residential electricity consumption within the Cotos Rural Community, Capachica Peninsula, situated at 3,860 meters above sea level in Puno, Peru. Electrical consumption patterns from 22 households were analyzed across two temporal periods: pre-installation and post-installation of a photovoltaic facility with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$313,357 \text{kWh}$</tex> annual capacity, deployed across <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$245 \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex> with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$35 \mathrm{m} \times 7 \mathrm{m}$</tex> configuration, 7-degree tilt angle, and 40 -degree azimuthal orientation. The methodology incorporated Pareto principlebased stratification to determine proportional distribution of energy demand among dwellings. Results reveal aggregate consumption reduction from 641.35 kW to 344.47 kW, representing a 46.29 % decrease in total demand. Average household consumption decreased from 30.54 kW to 15.65 kW, equivalent to 48.76 % reduction. Pareto analysis identified that 27.3 % of households concentrated 46.8 % of total pre-intervention consumption, providing partial validation of the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$80 / 20$</tex> rule. Findings demonstrate photovoltaic system effectiveness in high-altitude Andean communities and provide quantitative evidence for rural electrification project planning through renewable energy sources in geographically similar contexts.

title: INQUISITOR: An LLM-Driven Semantic SIEM Model for Automated Threat Intelligence and Log Reasoning
authors: Pankaj Kumar, Khushi Rathore, Kartik Chauhan, Hemanshu Verma, Lalit Mehra
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Security Operations Centres (SOCs) make use of the SIEM systems to identify attacks and process logs, but the outdated systems rely on hard-coded rules and interpretations. The next article is the introduction to open-source semantic SIEM software called INQUISITOR which is a mix of Large Language Models, retrieval-augmented generation, automatic indicator extraction and dynamic rule generation. The presence of the threat intelligence and contextual reasoning in the system allows improving the accuracy of the log interpretation and reduces the work of the analysts. The experimental outcomes prove to be more efficient and have superior detection performance to the traditional SIEM methods.

title: Cybersecurity Risk Assessment Using AI-Based Predictive Models
authors: Al Rafy, Mohammad Shahadat Hossain, Mostafizur Rahman, Mostafijur Rahman, Nisher Ahmed, Mashfiquer Rahman
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Over the years, traditional cyber risk approaches have become of less or no value to most dynamic organizations as a result of increased complexity and frequency of cyber threats. This paper develops Artificial Intelligence (AI)-based predictive models for automated cyber risk assessment systems and their detection, prediction, and mitigation capabilities across various network environments. Through supervised and unsupervised machine learning, the study incorporates model precision evaluation, threat intelligence, and risk quantification automation. Model effectiveness was validated through comparative performance testing on datasets. AI-driven models significantly improve detection rates while decreasing false positives and automating responses to dynamic threat scenarios. The paper also addresses engineering challenges in the deployment of such systems in enterprise security infrastructures.

title: Customer Behavior Analysis on Purchase Intention with the Mediating Role of Personalized Recommendations
authors: Ghassan Issa Ibrahim Alomari, Muhammad Alshurideh, Khaleel Ibrahim Al-Daoud, Beenish Shameem, Basma Waleed Kashmoola, Lama Alsmadi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: A quantitative analysis examines how individualized suggestion influence customer actions and shopping preferences in Jordanian retail operations. The increasing adoption of personalized marketing approaches has not resolved the unclear relationship between customer behavior and purchase intention when using personalized recommendations in Jordanian retail environments. Data from 288 retail industry respondents underwent analysis through SmartPLS in order to evaluate the proposed hypotheses. The research indicates that personalized recommendations generate positive effects on purchase intention and serve as a major decision-making bridge between customer actions and purchasing choices. The study demonstrates why personalization has become fundamental for marketers who want to influence consumer choices. This research presents substantial commercial value to Jordanian retail stores which demonstrates that dedicated recommendation engines boost consumer participation and boost purchase likelihood thus leading to greater sales performance. Retailers should use these insights to develop marketing strategies that match their recommendations to specific customer preferences and behaviors which results in better business outcomes.

title: An Assistive Glove for Multilingual Braille Communication using Vibrotactile Feedback
authors: Kavitha M, Nithya Shree E, Sathya T N, Sujith Kanna S, Sundar S
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Disabilities like blindness make lives very difficult. People with blindness often struggle to perform tasks like reading, which affects their education and everyday lives, and other assistive technologies lack either privacy or are not cost-effective. This issue is addressed by using a multilingual glove-based device that assists the visually impaired. This integrates a microcontroller, vibration motors, Bluetooth communication, power module, translation APIs, and braille encodings. This device accepts input in various languages and translates using translation APIs. The given input can be a text or speech, which is translated into English. This is because the Braille encodings are available and recognized in English, and then the text is converted into Braille patterns, which are conveyed through the vibrational motors via Bluetooth. The vibrational motors are embedded at the fingertips and the palm of the glove, which ensures that the user can feel the vibrations accurately. The microcontroller is used to activate the vibrational motors. This system has an application interface that can be used on mobile devices, and it ensures that the application is user-friendly and it is accessible to the visually impaired. The advantage of this device is that it is cost-friendly and it has privacy, which also makes it accessible. It has various applications like digital reading for the blind, multilingual accessibility, and an assistive learning tool.

title: Smart CT-based Pneumonia Diagnosis System using Deep Efficient Intelligent Vision Network Model
authors: N.Kavitha, B.Anand
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Pneumonia remains the most fatal of all the respiratory illnesses globally, with etiologic pathogens causing significant morbidity and mortality, especially in children and the elderly. Proper identification and proper classification of pneumonia through CT scanning are necessary in order to improve better clinical outcome and reduce healthcare system burdens. In addition, traditional deep learning models are typically plagued by redundant features, which degrades their classification performance and interpretability. In order to overcome these limitations, this paper advocate a new and comprehensive scheme that involves Cheetah-based Feature Selection and a Deep Efficient Intelligent Vision Pneumonia Network (DeiVi-PneumoNet) that is specially designed to offer accurate and effective pneumonia classification from CT images. Cheetah-inspired optimization approach imitates the speedy pursuit and accuracy of cheetahs in order to rapidly pick up on most informative features such that dimensionality reduction and better model performance are achieved. DeiVi-PneumoNet is a highly efficient deep neural model in terms of lightweight convolutional layers, attention, and residual learning for the purpose of making low-cost computational feature learning as well as classification possible. It is also backed by visual interpretability through Grad-CAM to highlight pneumonia-infected areas. The originality of this work rests in the distinctive blend of bio-inspired optimization and effective deep vision architecture to derive a stable, interpretable, and useful solution to the case of pneumonia diagnosis in realistic clinical settings.

title: Design and Implementation of a Voice-Enabled Maternal Care Chatbot using RAG Architecture
authors: V.V.A.S. Lakshmi, K. Yaswanth Guptha, D. Venkata Sai Akhil, K. Naga Lakshmi
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: This paper presents the implementation of a voice-enabled chatbot with the purpose of supporting pregnant women by providing reliable information related to maternal health. The system uses a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) approach, where a local knowledge base is combined with a language model to deliver accurate and context-aware responses. To make the solution more accessible in rural or low-resource settings, we built this entirely using free and open-source tools. Voice features are added using open-source models like Whisper for speech-to-text. The data is also collected through verified sources such as WHO and other health portals to maintain authenticity and accuracy. We used PDFs from these sources for RAG and stored them in vector databases for efficient document retrieval, as this system aims to bridge the information gap.The core contribution of this paper is the development of a fully offline, voice-enabled maternal care chatbot using RAG architecture, demonstrating that reliable healthcare information systems can be built entirely with open-source tools, ensuring accessibility in low-resource settings

title: Modern Healthcare: 3D Digital Imaging for Precision Diagnosis and Treatment
authors: J. Kavitha, L. Sharmila, K.Nesa Agnes Bellinta, A. Surya, S. Sowmyayani, K. K. Gaayathri
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The development of modern science and technology are more and more reflect the intersection and penetration of multiple disciplines. A new three-dimensional digital image modeling system integrating medical science, biomechanics, mechanics, materials science, computer graphics, mathematical analysis, automatic control and other multidisciplinary fields is currently in rapid development. This has led to great changes in the methods and concepts of modern medical diagnosis and treatment. In recent years, with the development of CT, MRI and other image diagnostic instruments, the application of computer virtual reality technology in medicine has been rapidly developed. Computers use this image information to model three-dimensional data images, providing surgeons with objective, accurate, intuitive, and scientific methods for surgical simulation, surgical navigation, surgical positioning, and surgical planning .At the same time, it also provides objective and accurate reference value for imaging diagnosis and interventional treatment. The medical imaging diagnostic technology is a noninvasive method to obtain the internal tissue image of a certain part of the human body. It provides an important reference for clinical disease diagnosis and treatment. The new technology of digital imaging diagnosis has promoted the rapid development of medicine in the new era. For example, optical coherence tomography can be used for efficient diagnosis of ophthalmological optical pathway disease science, and living tissue can be measured without invasion. The dermatologist can use the measurement of the body's microwave signal and body surface infrared technology to create an image. This not only allows a detailed observation of subcutaneous capillary blood flow, but also allows a proper evaluation of sympathetic nerve activity.

title: Attention-Driven Lightweight Deep Learning for Enhanced Leukemia Detection
authors: R. Manish Varma, M. Divya, S. Asokkumar, S. Prema
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Leukaemia detection involves testing of cancerous blood cells to diagnose and classify the type of leukemia and this is critical in early treatment and improvement of patients. Conventionally, it consists of manual inspection of the images of blood smears using a manual microscope, feature detection and classification through traditional machine learning methods. Nevertheless, they tend to be labor intensive, prone to errors and computationally inefficient, which restrict their accuracy and usefulness. In order to resolve such difficulties, this paper suggests a minimal and effective deep learning model with residual attention module to accurately detect and classify leukemia. The framework combines an Adaptive Color Space Enhancement Network (ACSEN) to preprocess blood smear images and equalize staining differences and boost the discriminative color features, a Lightweight Residual Attention Feature Selector (LRAFS) to extract the most informative features and suppress irrelevant information, and an Efficient Leukemia Detection Network (EffiLeuNet) to do precise multi-class classification. The experimental findings on Blood Cells Cancer (ALL) proposed system show that the proposed framework has high accuracy, precision, recall, and F 1 -score and it consumes less computational complexity, which is highly effective in clinical applications.

title: Comparative Analysis of CNN Architectures for Eye Disease Classification
authors: Abinaya V N, Jagadish N, Thiruselvan M, Kamalraj Subramaniam
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Early detection of eye diseases is critical in avoiding vision loss that would have been avoided, but manual analysis of fundus images is tedious and requires the experience of specialists. The paper is a comparative evaluation of two effective convolutional neural network architectures of MobileNet and EfficientNet in automated multi-classification of normal, diabetic retinopathy (DR), cataract (CR) and glaucoma (GR) images. An open access fundus image dataset was pre-processed through resizing, normalization and augmentation to increase the robustness of the model. Both architectures were rocked and tested on the basis of standard performance measures. MobileNet had a total accuracy of 89.06% and EfficientNet had a greater accuracy of 90.71% with better precision, recall and F1-score. EfficientNet was also particularly strong in case of DR and CR detection, and MobileNet was the best performer in case of the Normal class. EfficientNet is found to have a more trusted diagnostic power to classify diseases completely and MobileNet is also acceptable under low-resource or real-time conditions. The presented comparative analysis has presented helpful information in terms of the selection of suitable CNN models to be employed in automated retinal disease screening systems.

title: Universal Sound and Language Understanding: A Deep Learning and NLP Framework for Multilingual Music Analysis
authors: Sunil Gunin, Mahua Pal
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: In today's globalized digital world, music is being produced, shared, and consumed across diverse languages and cultures-especially on platforms like YouTube. Yet, most music analysis systems still focus on monolingual content, primarily in English, and struggle to scale across linguistic boundaries. At the same time, advances in deep learning have given us powerful tools: convolutional neural networks (CNN) for audio analysis, and transformer-based NLP models for understanding text. However, these are often used in isolation and rarely integrated into a unified framework for multilingual music understanding. This research work addresses that gap. We present a hybrid AI pipeline that combines AudioSet-pretrained CNNs for audio event detection with domain-adapted NLP tools for handling multilingual lyrics and metadata. By focusing on Hindi and English YouTube music videos, we demonstrate how this integration enables robust audio-text analysis, including transcription, translation, summarization, and keyword extraction-paving the way for better global music recommendation, discovery, and archival. We utilize the AudioSet-pretrained Cnn14 model for automatic audio event detection and integrate NLP techniques for summarization and keyword extraction from associated textual data.

title: Designing Secure and Scalable API Management Frameworks for Cloud Environments
authors: Karthik Chakravarthy Cheekuri
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: As cloud-based applications proliferate, there is an increased adoption of APIs for seamless integration, scalability and functionality areas. The scalability, performance and security nature of the existing API management frameworks have not yet met the expectations of organizations for sustainable use at extreme loads or in multi cloud environments to minimize the risks of attacks (including inefficient resource utilization and compliance to the regulatory framework). This study proposes a Secure and Scalable API Management Framework (SS-API-MF), which considers Context-Aware Role-Based Access Control (CA-RBAC), predictive auto-scaling, edge computing and federated learning-based anomaly detection to simultaneously optimize security, throughput and privacy aspects in the organization. The SS-API-MF was tested against market-leading API Management solutions including Kong, Apigee, AWS API Gateway, Azure API Management and IBM API Connect in simulated, real-time and high-traffic multi-cloud environments. In total, quantitative data of SS-API-MF latency of 52 ms, throughput of 30,500 requests/sec, attack detection accuracy of 98 %, policy compliance of 100 %, with lower resource utilization of 42 % CPU; 51 % RAM, compared to the others referenced. Ultimately, SS-API-MF demonstrated it could provide safe and efficient open ecosystem API Management capabilities that could protect privacy in real-time, catering to enterprise architectures deployed globally, yet very much facilitative of performance, compliance and legislative directives/issues surrounding security. The framework provides a basis for additional work in the areas of AI-driven, adaptive cloud API Management practices to combat the new threats to security, while making advances in compliance and performance issues.

title: Blockchain and Digital Marketing: Transforming Customer Acquisition in Fintech
authors: Mohammad Khalaf Daoud, Mohmmad Husam Odeh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: This study explores the revolutionary impact that is brought about by blockchain technology in conjunction with digital marketing strategies on fintech sector customer acquisition. Using key variables, that is, SDP, DAFP, PCE, and LRP as drivers of customer acquisition drivers (CAF), the study applies a stringent analytical strategy with SmartPLS in a bid to explore hypothesized relationships. Empirical results portray statistically significant effects on all pathways that have been probed: SDP (β = 0.350, T = 6.481, p < 0.001), DAFP (β = 0.292, T = 7.326, p < 0.001), PCE (β = 0.361, T = 5.752, p < 0.001), and LRP (β = 0.341, T = 5.682, p < 0.001) all have a direct impact on customer acquisition outcomes. The results validate the central position played by innovative digital strategies propelled by blockchain in maximizing the success rate in customer engagement as well as acquisition strategies in the dynamic fintech context. The study makes a two-way contribution towards academic research as well as industrial practices in terms of empirical validation on advanced technologies' incorporation in digital marketing, with a resultant insightful guidebook on enhancing customer acquisition strategies in a manner that is practicable in terms of application.

title: Touchless Interaction : AI Driven Virtual Mouse and Air Writing
authors: V P Leena, P Anusha, S Neethu, B Neha, P P Prathyush, K P Sayandh
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: By introducing a touchless interface, the Virtual Mouse and Air Writing system helps consumers to engage with computers solely via hand motions. The system records live video from a webcam to track finger positions, utilizing computer vision and Media Pipe for hand landmark detection. Smooth navigation without physical contact is made possible by the arrangement of certain Mouse movement functions such as scrolling, moving, and left and right clicks. Furthermore, the air writing function captures the movement of the fingertip to create characters or symbols in midair, which are subsequently shown on the screen. By doing away with the need for traditional input devices, this innovation enhances accessibility and hygiene, particularly in public kiosks, healthcare settings, and smart classrooms. A more intuitive and engaging user experience is produced by combining gesture control and air writing, which advances This project makes it possible for users to interact with their devices without making physical contact by integrating cutting-edge machine learning and sensor technologies. It was created with Python, OpenCV, Media Pipe, and TensorFlow. It functions effectively in real time and provides a scalable and reliable framework for a variety of uses, including educational technology, virtual environments, and accessibility tools. Free human-computer interaction is provided by the suggested project.

title: Fault Tolerant Transformer Based Hybrid Multilevel Inverter for Uninterruptable Power Supply
authors: Kannabiran Arumugam, R. Jayapragash, Sudha V
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: A high reliable Transformer based Multilevel inverter enhances the efficiency and life time of the system. Recent research studies focus on the development of new fault-tolerant multilevel inverter (FTMLI) topologies. However, these topologies unable to maintain rated voltage and power with a minimum switch count during switch fault conditions by preserving its rated voltage and power. This work presents a new transformer based multilevel inverter (TBMLI) to generate 27 level output voltage with minimum switch count. The proposed configuration can handle open circuit(oc)/short circuit (sc) faults, by preserving the rated voltage and power during faulty conditions. Level shifted pulse width modulation(LSPWM) is used to generate the switching signals. A redundant leg is used to tolerate the fault occurred in any one of the switch or legs in TBMLI. The proposed configuration provides uninterrupted operation during faulty conditions which increases the reliability of the circuit and reliability analysis is presented in this work. The proposed circuit is simulated in MATLAB/Simulink and the simulation results are validated with hardware results.

title: Machine Learning for Identifying Drug Resistance Patterns
authors: Raghad Mohammed Hadi, Najlaa Abd Hamza, Wafaa M.Salih Abedi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: The swift rise of drug-resistant pathogens is a major global health threat that necessitates new methods for the effective tracking and combating of these pathogens. The prevailing paradigms of tracking drug resistance often require great labor, time, and an inability to scale the studies effectively. The transformative power of ML has such a potential that it complexly figures out the resistant mechanisms of drugs using the massive data available. The research describes how novel ML technologies and bioinformatics can be used for the implementation of a novel solution that is capable of describing, predicting, and interpreting resistance patterns within a variety of different pathogens. Using genomic, phenotypic, and clinical datasets, we utilize machine learning algorithms, both supervised and unsupervised, to separate resistant and sensitive populations of pathogens with the predicted mutations. Particular attention is given to providing information to the decision-making process in a way that it becomes clear how the technology is being utilized by the personnel. The preliminary results are encouraging, giving evidence that the ML methods are more efficient in the prediction of resistance than the traditional approaches to drug screening. This paper emphasizes the importance of using ML-based methodologies in the strengthening of surveillance, regulation of treatment protocols, and improvement of the production of next-generation therapeutics.

title: Dynamic Power Optimization in Smart Grid PV Systems using Deep Reinforced Sparrow Learning Model
authors: Kamalkumar T, S.Gomathi
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Smart grid PV systems form the core of embedding renewable energy into contemporary electric grids in a cleaner and efficient way to fulfill increasing energy requirements. The efficiency of power converters, which have to maximize the conversion of energy and ensure stable performance under changing environmental conditions, pretty much dictates the efficiency of such systems. Yet conventional control techniques are unable to provide high efficiency, flexibility, and accuracy in real-time control, especially for dynamic load cases. In this paper, a new Deep Reinforced Sparrow Learning Controller (Deep RSLC) is proposed to enhance the performance of power converters for smart grid PV systems. The Deep RSLC employs a new sparrow searching algorithm for neural network parameter estimation such that the controller can produce highly precise control signals for switching devices in the converter. The novel application of deep reinforcement learning and sparrow searching algorithm enables the system to dynamically learn from and respond to changes in operating conditions, which results in increased efficiency, stability, and performance of the system. Experimental findings reveal that the proposed controller is better in performance and responsiveness than conventional approaches and is hence an efficient solution for smart grid environments-based energy conversion optimization. The current paper makes an addition to the emerging research of intelligent control methods for power converters and the application of advanced optimization algorithms in renewable energy systems.

title: Temporal Correlation Between Heart Valve Acoustic Events and ECG Signal Variations Across Varying Junctional Potentials
authors: Abu Talha Haque Miah, Miss Al Ive, Fatema Tuj Zohora, Hafsa Haque
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Electrocardiogram (ECG) is a clinical tool that records the heart’s electrical activity using surface electrodes to detect abnormalities. Phonocardiogram PCG captures valve-related heart sounds beyond what is audible by stethoscope. Simulations of over 1000 ECG-PCG cycles were compared with data from 10–15 healthy subjects. ECG features (P-wave, QRS, and T-wave) and PCG heart sounds (S₁ and S₂) were all modeled, and there were multiple options for filtering the signals. Frequency shifts of PCG were analyzed to identify frequency latency shifts using FFT, STFT, and wavelet transformations. However, prior ECG–PCG studies have overlooked how small variations in junctional potential (0.5–3 mV, ±10–50 ms) influence time–frequency coupling. This study bridges that gap through a simulated and validated model linking electrical conduction variability to valve sound dynamics. The relationship between junctional potentials (0.5– 3 mV, ±10–50 ms delay) and changes in frequency was analyzed through cross-correlation, regression, and mixed-effects modeling that according to the law of ECG. The simulation concerned the ECG-PCG signals synchronized with physiological frequency peaks (40-80 Hz) and varied junctional potentials (0.5-3 mV, ±10-50 ms) generated distinct acoustic responses, thereby supporting the predictive model comparing the electrical conduction dynamics to characteristics of heart sounds. This study shows how activity at the heart's junctions affects both ECG readings and heart valve sounds. It turns out there's a way to measure how electrical signals relate to the sounds the heart makes, both in normal and abnormal physiological mechanisms. The results suggest we could create easier, noninvasive methods to catch small heart problems. Later studies might even use AI and non-wearable health devices (advanced ECG and PCG) to diagnose better and help keep an eye on the heart in real time.

title:
Optimization of SiO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    and HfO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    based BIOFET by Varying the Channel Length in Order to Improve Impedance Characteristics
authors: S. Gomathi, D. Sungeetha
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: This study investigates the impact of channel length variations on the impedance characteristics of SiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>-based and HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>-based bio sensitive field-effect transistors (BIOFETs). The objective is to analyze how channel length influences the overall impedance and performance of these materials in BIOFET applications. SiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> and HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> were utilized as dielectric materials, with channel lengths ranging from 845 nm to 905 nm for SiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>-based BIOFETs and from 193 nm to 253nm for HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>-based BIOFETs. Simulations were conducted while maintaining a constant gate oxide thickness and substrate properties to evaluate impedance variations. The results indicate that HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>-based BIOFETs exhibit lower impedance due to their higher dielectric constant, leading to improved charge transport and reduced resistance. Conversely, SiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>-based BIOFETs demonstrated consistently higher impedance, making them less efficient for high-speed sensing applications. These findings highlight the importance of selecting suitable dielectric materials to optimize BIOFET performance, with HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> emerging as a superior choice for minimizing impedance in biosensing technologies.

title: TACA-ProtoNet for Task-Adaptive Cross Attention in Few-Shot Learning
authors: Rohith Ganesh Kanchi, Azhagesh S, S D Madhumitha, Berin Shalu S, Vergin Raja Sarobin M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Few-shot learning (FSL) aims to recognize novel categories with only a handful of labeled examples by transferring knowledge from base classes. Prototypical Networks provide a simple and effective approach by computing class prototypes as mean embeddings and classifying queries via nearest-neighbor matching. However, static prototype representations fail to capture task-specific discriminative cues, limiting generalization to novel classes. This paper presents TACA-ProtoNet (Task-Adaptive Cross Attention Prototypical Network), a framework that refines query representations by attending to class prototypes before distance computation. The central component is a multi-head Cross-Attention Refinement (CAR) module that enables queries to selectively aggregate prototype information, producing task-adaptive embeddings that emphasize class-discriminative features. A ResNet-12 backbone is used under standard few-shot protocols, evaluated on both the fine-grained CUB-200-2011 and general-domain miniImageNet benchmarks. Results show that TACA-ProtoNet achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{8 2. 4 7} \% \pm \text{0. 5 8} \%$</tex> accuracy on CUB-200 5-way 5-shot and 67.85% on miniImageNet 5-way 1-shot classification, outperforming the vanilla ProtoNet by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+4.23 \%$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+5.46 \%$</tex> respectively across 1 -shot and 5 -shot regimes. Ablation results further confirm the advantage of cross-attention refinement over self-attention and no-attention baselines.

title: Optimal and Economic Designing of Chain Sampling Plan Under Logistic Exponential Lifetime Model
authors: M. Sankara Narayanan, P. Jeyadurga, S. Balamurali
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: This article delves into the development of a conditional sampling procedure, specifically, a chain sampling plan, with the aim of product’s median life assurance which follows logistic exponential distribution through truncated life test. Further, this study aims to establish a dependable method to examine product’s quality by comparing the ratio of the true median lifetime to the specified median lifetime. A non-linear optimization problem is formulated aimed at finding the most appropriate plan parameters, including sample size (n) and the number of preceding lots (i) needed for decision, considering by different producer’s as well as consumer’s risk levels. In addition, an example is included to demonstrate how the suggested plan can be implemented in practice, followed by a comparison with an existing sampling plan that highlights the advantages of the proposed plan through average sample number and operating characteristic curve. The study further explores economic of the suggested plan and compares it with single sampling plan.

title: Dimension Decomposition Transformer for Enhanced Underwater Image Restoration
authors: Thallapally Harika, Tirumalasetti Supraja, Kankanala Srinivas
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Underwater images often suffer from low contrast, color distortion and degradation in detail due to light absorption, scattering and the complex underwater environment. Existing deep learning-based methods mainly rely on convolutional neural networks (CNN), which have limited capabilities in multiscale feature extraction and global context modeling. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a dimensionality decomposition transformer-based (DDT) network for underwater image enhancement (UIE). The framework integrates dimension decomposition locally enhanced transformer (DLT) and self-attention locally enhanced transformer (SLT) layers to effectively capture both high- and low-resolution features. A local enhancement block (LEB) is used to restore fine details and textures. The network is trained in a supervised manner using paired underwater and reference images and optimized with a combination of L1 loss, perceptual loss, structural similarity index measure (SSIM) loss and total variation loss. A discriminator (D) guides the generator (G) through adversarial learning to enhance the perceptual quality.
