Experimental evaluation on benchmark underwater datasets shows that the proposed method outperforms existing state-of-the-art approaches in both quantitative metrics and visual quality, producing perceptually accurate and visually appealing images suitable for underwater perception and visual engineering applications.

title: Acoustic Bird Species Classification Using Modified CNN and Attention Bi-LSTM
authors: Uma Maheswari Pandyan, R. Sundarajapandian, Mohamed Mansoor Roomi Sindha, S. Gandhimathi alias Usha, Zaakir Ahmed M, Manish Rahul P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Classification of bird species plays an important role in biodiversity monitoring, habitat health, etc. Earlier studies depend heavily on experts, making the process time-consuming and laborious. The proposed work aims to provide the classification system that uses bird acoustic information with modified convolutional neural network (CNN) and attention Bi-directional Long Short Term (Bi-LSTM) framework. Due to the shortage of local bird species acoustics, novel dataset with 20 different species was created. The proposed acoustic dataset comprises of 3,365 samples. The classification pipeline consists of a feature extraction phase, modified convolutional neural network, attention infused Bi-directional Long-Short Term framework. The Salient acoustic features were captured using Mel-frequency Cepstral Coefficients(MFCC) which were based on mel spectrograms from the input sound signal. Then the modified CNN extract the initial features from the MFCC and then Bi-directional layers of the long-short term memory requirements based on a recurrence network to learn all temporal dependencies. Experiment results show that the trained model can accurately classify bird species with the accuracy of 83.4% using the sample of the bird species acoustic details, and it is enabled by feature representations based on the MFCC. The experimental analysis also highlighted the potential of bioacoustics informatics of using a modified attention based deep-learning model to support ecological surveillance and avian conservation devices.

title: Explainable Ensemble Learning and Hybrid Feature Selection for Robust Low Birth Weight Prediction
authors: Inamul Haque Inam, Ashfaqul Islam, Abdul Kadar Muhammad Masum, Khandaker Mohammad Mohi Uddin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Low Birth Weight (LBW) is a major public health issue characterized by a birth weight of less than 2500 grams. This study proposes a machine learning-based prediction of LBW using the Bangladesh Demographic and Health Survey (BDHS) 2017– 2018 dataset, which contains 1863 maternal-infant records with 18 predictor features. Our approach addresses the critical gap between predictive power and clinical trust in this domain by introducing a methodologically robust framework that integrates an entire data preprocessing pipeline through a strict validation protocol to circumvent data leakage. Using a hybrid feature selection technique, a subset of 12 out of the 18 predictors was selected. We trained and evaluated 14 individual machine learning models, followed by the development of advanced voting ensembles using stratified k-fold cross-validation (CV). Our results show that a soft-voting ensemble of ExtraTrees and CatBoost establishes a new, robust performance benchmark, achieving a high score with an accuracy of 94.54% and a Receiver Operating Characteristic-Area Under the Curve (ROC-AUC) of 94.05%. Such high accuracy comes with a full model transparency through SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) analysis. These results deliver a complete framework that successfully unifies high predictive accuracy with the methodological rigor and interpretability required for a trustworthy model in early LBW risk assessment.

title: Vocational Education's Causal Effect on Income: A Method Based on Double Machine Learning
authors: Hong Ma
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI in Education Technology and Applications (AIETA)
abstract: This paper employs a double machine learning (DML) framework to estimate the causal effects of vocational education participation on individual income levels in the presence of high-dimensional confounders. By integrating Multi-Layer Perceptrons (MLPs) for flexible nuisance parameter estimation, we address non-linear selection biases that plague traditional linear models. We provide rigorous empirical validation demonstrating that MLPs significantly outperform logistic and Lasso baselines in capturing complex data dependencies, justifying their necessity for unbiased inference. The analysis reveals a robust positive average treatment effect (ATE) of approximately $10,870 on annual income. Crucially, moving beyond aggregate metrics, our novel counterfactual profile analysis uncovers profound distributional impacts: while absolute gains are widespread, vocational training delivers massive relative income boosts-exceeding 45%-specifically for low-skilled, disadvantaged individuals. These findings reposition vocational education not just as a skill-building program, but as a powerful engine for economic equity. The methodology's robustness is confirmed through extensive sensitivity checks and alternative estimators.

title: STRIDE-SA: Streaming, Transfer-Invariant, and Distilled Sentiment Analysis for Real-Time Social Media
authors: Akash Balaji, Bobby George, Medida Venkata Sai Vinay
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: To offer STRIDE-SA, a single framework for looking at how people feel on social media that works with streaming, transfer-invariant, and distilled data. The method fills in three gaps that have been present for a while: (i) cross-platform robustness, (ii) trade-offs between accuracy and efficiency, and (iii) real-time scalability. STRIDE-SA contains a RoBERTa encoder, lightweight adapters, and a lexicon-gated fusion that only adds sentiment priors when they make sense in the context. It also contains a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BLSTM) head to pick up on small changes over time, adversarial + MMD alignment for domain invariance, and a stack that is ready to be used with knowledge distillation, confidence-based early exits, and online calibration with drift monitoring. Sentiment140 is the major benchmark for the teacher, and they receive strong results offline (Macro-F1 0.892, AUPRC 0.951). At P95 latency 38 ms, which is substantially faster than RoBERTa-base (95 ms), the student with early departures preserves most of the accuracy (Macro-F1 0.878, AUPRC 0.940). Sequential mitigations, such as class-prior reweighting, temperature scaling, and adapter fine-tuning, raise Macro-F1 from 0.762 to 0.835 and lower shift indicators (PSI 0.32 to 0.12) with very little extra labor. Ablations demonstrate that lexical gating, distillation, early departures, and adapters are all beneficial. STRIDE-SA is a full, mathematically sound system that is correct, quick, and ready for use in the real world. It shows how to do sentiment analysis in real time outside of Twitter.

title: 3D Polyp Morphology Estimation from 2D Endoscopy using Deep Segmentation and MiDaS-Based Monocular Depth Reconstruction
authors: Palanivel Rajan S, Harshavarthan KS, Khader Sheriff I, Loganaath S
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: Accurate 3D characterization of colorectal polyps, capturing their height, diameter, and fine depth variations, plays an essential role in dysplasia risk assessment and clinical decision-making. However, most clinical endoscopy systems provide only 2D RGB images, making true morphology difficult to interpret. In this work, we describe a simple framework that recovers an approximate 3D geometry from a single endoscopy frame without extra hardware. The pipeline combines U-Net based polyp segmentation, MiDaS DPT-Hybrid for dense monocular depth estimation, and pinhole-camera back-projection to generate a detailed 3D point cloud. Unlike stereo or SL systems requiring specialized hardware, our method enables shape recovery using standard monocular endoscopy. We introduce a convex-hull-based normalization step that maps MiDaS relative depth values to an approximate metric scale, enabling computation of key morphological descriptors, including polyp height, centroid depth, diameter, height-diameter ratio, and estimated camera-to-polyp distance. Evaluation of ten Kvasir-SEG samples demonstrates stable geometric trends, with a mean height of 7.50 mm (range: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2.96 - 14.29 mm}$</tex>) and a centroid distance of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{11.16 mm}$</tex>, confirming that relative monocular depth can be reliably transformed into interpretable anatomical measurements. Since the method relies solely on regular RGB endoscopy and does not require additional hardware, it can fit naturally into current clinical practice. In this sense, it offers a realistic step toward real-time computer-assisted assessment.

title: Transformer-Inspired Adaptive Federated Learning for Privacy-Preserving Anomaly Detection in Decentralized Underwater IoT Networks
authors: Shobica K, M.Dharani Devi, R. Mohanram, Mr.S.Siva Kumar, Syed Ismail Abdul Lathif, R. Tamijetchelvy
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The proliferation of Underwater Internet of Things (UIoT) has enabled intelligent monitoring and autonomous control of marine environments. However, centralized anomaly detection frameworks pose critical challenges including privacy leakage, communication overhead, and vulnerability to node failures—particularly in dynamic and bandwidth-constrained underwater ecosystems. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a novel Transformer-Inspired Adaptive Federated Learning (T-AFL) framework designed for decentralized, privacy-preserving anomaly detection in UIoT networks. The approach integrates local Transformer-based encoders at individual underwater sensor nodes to capture both spatial correlations and long-range temporal dependencies from multivariate sensor data. These local models are trained collaboratively using a communication-efficient federated learning paradigm, ensuring that raw sensor data never leaves the device. To further improve adaptability and performance across heterogeneous deployments, an attention-guided adaptive aggregation mechanism is introduced at the central server, prioritizing model updates based on data quality, anomaly distribution, and node reliability. Experimental evaluations on a synthetically augmented underwater anomaly dataset demonstrate the proposed T-AFL framework’s superior accuracy, resilience, and communication efficiency compared to traditional federated LSTM and CNN-based models. The proposed architecture offers a scalable and secure solution for real-time, energyefficient anomaly detection in future autonomous underwater surveillance systems.

title: Modeling Consumer Adoption of Energy-Efficient Appliances Using Artificial Neural Networks and Clustering: Insights for Sustainable Energy Systems
authors: Sowmiya R, M. Velavan
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Energy-efficient appliances play a vital role in demand-side energy management, yet their adoption remains limited despite energy labeling and awareness initiatives. This study proposes a machine learning-based framework to analyze consumer adoption behavior using Artificial Neural Networks (ANN) and K-means clustering. The ANN model captures complex nonlinear relationships among behavioral, economic, and social factors to predict adoption likelihood. In addition, clustering analysis segments consumers into distinct behavioral groups. The results identify three segments—highly engaged adopters, resistant consumers, and transitional users—revealing significant heterogeneity in adoption patterns. The study provides valuable insights for designing targeted policies and strategies to promote sustainable energy consumption.

title: $\mu$-HCD: Annotation-Free and Scalable Long Short-Term Memory Hyperspectral Calibration for Change Detection
authors: Chia-Hsiang Lin, Ching-Yun Liang, Po-Wei Tang
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: Hyperspectral change detection (HCD), aiming at detecting changes from the bitemporal images (i.e., X and Y), plays a critical role in land cover monitoring and disaster resilience. Recently, numerous semi-supervised HCD methods have been proposed to address the challenges brought by the limited labeled data. However, these approaches remain impractical in extreme scenarios (e.g., those with newly acquired or large-scale remote sensing images), where obtaining even a small amount of labeled data could be difficult or even infeasible. To overcome these challenges, we propose a fully unsupervised HCD framework by introducing an automatic training set formation technique, which iteratively and restrictively identifies unchanged samples, from which we learn the calibration mapping from X to Y among the unchanged area. Specifically, our algorithm incorporates a long short-term memory (LSTM)-based module serving as a spectral translator to mitigate the acquisition condition variability via the above calibration, thereby reformulating HCD into an easily solvable spectrum comparison problem. Our novel and elegant Memory-Iterative-Unsupervised HCD (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu$</tex>-HCD) algorithm, yielding state-of-the-art HCD performances, highlights its superiority for scalable and annotation-free land cover change detection.

title: Glauco Vision: Early Detection System of Glaucoma using Vision Transformer
authors: Shamili P, Arivazhagan M, Boaz Aaron Anand
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Glaucoma is a type of eye disease that slowly damages the optic nerve and is one of the main causes of blindness that can't be reversed. Detecting it early is hard because the changes in the eye's structure are not very obvious, and relying on human doctors for diagnosis has its limits. This paper introduces Glauco Vision, a tool that automatically detects glaucoma using a type of artificial intelligence called Vision Transformer, or ViT. Unlike traditional neural networks that focus only on small parts of an image, this new model uses a special way of looking at the whole image to understand the bigger picture. A web-based tool built with Flask makes it easy to use in clinics. The system performs very well, with an accuracy of 96.40%, and it also has high precision, recall, and F1-score values, which are 95.44%. These results show that Vision Transformer models are a great choice for quickly and effectively screening for glaucoma, especially in places where there are not enough healthcare resources.

title: Homeyx: A Low-Cost Distributed Physical AI Framework for Multimodal Assistive Robotics with Safety-Constrained Medical Advisory
authors: Elamaran E, Aznan Ashik TP, Rishikesh Reddy Ada
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: There is a growing elderly population living alone or who are left momentarily unsupervised when their caretaker or family members are out working. There is a demand for innovative assistive technologies that enable independent living while maintaining quality of life. This paper presents Homeyx, a distributed Physical AI-based multimodal assistive robot designed for elderly home environments. A Raspberry Pi 5 is combined with a cognitive layer using a locally installed LLaMA 3 language model, deterministic motion control using ESP32, vision-based perception, and the Sarvam AI platform for multilingual conversation. A rule-based, safety-constrained medical advisory module with Indic language support is also incorporated. The system operates fully on-device for essential tasks, removing reliance on cloud dependency for sensitive medical interactions and ensuring user data privacy. Groq-accelerated Whisper is employed for low-latency speech recognition. Experimental results demonstrate 95 % vision accuracy, sub-second end-to-end response times (avg. 825 ms), over 91 % multilingual recognition accuracy across English, Hindi, and Malayalam, and 92.6 % advisory precision, at a total hardware cost of approximately Rs. 18,000.

title: SLIPT-Based Hybrid VLC/RF Cooperative Communication via Harvest-Store-Use Relays
authors: Chadi Abou-Rjeily, Nour Rajeh, Lourdes Gerges
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: This paper addresses relay selection in hybrid Visible Light Communication (VLC)/Radio Frequency (RF) dual-hop systems. In the first hop, a cluster of randomly distributed relays receives a VLC signal transmitted by a light source. The relays decode the information message while simultaneously harvesting energy from the optical signal using Simultaneous Lightwave Information and Power Transfer (SLIPT). The harvested energy is stored in buffers at the relays and later used to forward the message to a destination node via RF in the second hop. Within this framework, we propose and analyze three relay selection protocols. The first protocol selects the relay with the maximum stored energy. The second protocol minimizes energy consumption by choosing the relay that can deliver the message to the destination with the least transmission power. The third protocol jointly accounts for both accumulated and consumed energies. To further improve system performance, a Relay-to-Relay Energy Harvesting (RREH) mechanism was adopted, allowing non-selected relays to store extra energy from the RF signal of the selected relay. Simulation results demonstrate significant improvements in outage probability compared to benchmark schemes without energy storage at the relays.

title: Robust Dual-Site Cancer Screening via Multi-Scale Vision Transformer and Rapid Recognition Pipeline
authors: Istiak Kabir, Fahim Ahamed, Kamrun Nahar Bristy, Md Imranul Hoque Bhuiyan, Mostafizur Rahman Shakil, Erin Jahan Meem, Md. Nwoshad Alam Chowdhury, Shafiur Rahman
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Early detection of skin and oral cancers is essential for improving survival rates and reducing treatment costs, yet conventional diagnostic methods remain resource-intensive and limited in accessibility. This study presents a transformer-based deep learning framework for automated skin and oral cancer detection using models including Multi-ViT, MA-Transformer V2, MobileUNETR, TinyViT, and Xception. Evaluated on the PAD-UFES-20 and Oral Cancer datasets, the proposed multi-ViT achieved high performance, with 99.12% accuracy on the PAD-UFES-20 dataset and 99.37% on the Oral Cancer dataset, while maintaining high F1-scores and AUC-PR values across both datasets. A unified pipeline incorporating targeted preprocessing, model training, and lightweight deployment was developed, enabling real-time screening through a web-based application. This allows users to upload lesion images and receive instant predictions, supporting early intervention without requiring specialized equipment. The proposed system contributes to practical, scalable cancer screening, aiding timely diagnosis and improving healthcare accessibility in diverse clinical and community settings.

title:
ORION-V
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    X: A Standards-Aligned Multi-Plane Architecture for Resilient NTN-Enhanced Vehicular Communications
authors: Bhagwan Das, Nawaz Ali, Gianluca Aloi, Pasquale Pace, Magnus Johnsson, Ali Hassan Sodhro
publish: IEEE Communications Standards Magazine
abstract: Autonomous vehicles are moving beyond controlled environments into large-scale deployments where GNSS degradation, congestion, and infrastructure disruption challenge predictable V2X operation. While 3GPP Release 18 and Release 19 provide key capabilities for NR-V2X and NTN, they do not define how these should be operationally combined for safety-critical use. This article introduces ORION-V2X, a standards-aligned multi-plane framework that composes existing 3GPP capabilities without adding new interfaces. It separates time-critical local safety from edge coordination and wide-area resilience, anchors coordination at RSU micro-edge with fixed deadlines, and uses positioning integrity as a system-wide control input. NTN is integrated strictly as a resilience and coordination layer, not a real-time safety channel. Evaluation under GNSS denial, sidelink congestion, and coverage loss shows contract-level performance with bounded latency, deadline adherence, and stable operational modes. ORION-V2X provides a practical, deployment-oriented reference for industry to realise predictable and resilient NTN-enhanced V2X systems aligned with Release 18/19 evolution.

title: CAFE-Net: Context-Aware Feature Enhancement for Reliable Multi-Scale Detection of Martian Craters
authors: Zhuohang Li, Xinyao Wang, Zhaozhao Zhang, Tongtong Shen, Wen Zheng
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Reliable recognition of Martian impact craters is critical for spacecraft landing site selection and future surface exploration. However, this task remains challenging due to the pronounced multi-scale characteristics of craters and interference from crater-like structures such as Martian domes. Notably, the ejecta textures surrounding craters provide important discriminative cues for distinguishing craters from domes. To address these challenges, a Context-Aware and Feature Enhanced Net (CAFE-Net) is proposed for accurate Martian crater detection in complex environments. CAFE-Net incorporates three key components: a Multi-Scale Context Aware (MSCA) module to capture local texture information, a Feature Enhancement Unit (FEU) to alleviate the loss of small-scale features, and a channel self-learning fusion strategy to strengthen feature representation via channel-wise interactions. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed method achieves improvements of 3.94% in precision and 3.13% in recall, with an F1-score of 86.41%, outperforming existing methods while maintaining efficient inference. However, the proposed method is currently limited in handling nighttime imagery and severely weathered craters. Future work will explore multi-source data fusion to improve robustness and support applications such as vision-based landing site selection for Mars missions.

title: Efficient Edge Caching in Data Centers Using Latent Dirichlet Allocation and Hybrid Matrix Factorization with SVD++ and ALS
authors: Mbarek Marwan, Hind Ait Temghart, Safae Hmaidi, Mohamed Lazaar
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Intelligent edge caching has emerged as a promising solution for supporting next-generation data centers by improving content delivery. However, the increasing complexity of deep neural network architectures often results in significant computational overhead and increased service latency. To overcome these limitations, this study introduces a lightweight hybrid matrix factorization framework that combines Singular Value Decomposition (SVD++) and Alternating Least Squares (ALS) based on the Bates–Granger (BG) weighting scheme. Furthermore, Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) is employed for topic-based content clustering, enabling a better trade-off between recommendation accuracy and computational efficiency. The simulation results, statistically validated using the Friedman test followed by the Nemenyi post-hoc analysis, demonstrate that the proposed model consistently outperforms competing approaches. Specifically, the hybrid model achieves accuracy improvements of 20–30% on the dense Amazon Reviews dataset and 4–10% on the sparse Book-Crossing dataset compared with both classical and neural baseline models. In addition, clustering improves computational efficiency by reducing training time by 10–42% compared with models such as Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks, Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), and Graph Neural Networks (GNNs). Overall, the results indicate that the proposed proactive caching technique achieves a better balance between accuracy and efficiency, while cross-validation confirms its robustness and strong generalization capability across different data partitions.

title: Finite-Element Framework for Electrostatics and Optical Simulations in IGZO TFTs—Part I: Modeling
authors: Pietro Rinaudo, Adrian Chasin, Ben Kaczer, Ying Zhao, Harold Dekkers, Subhali Subhechha, Attilio Belmonte, Valeri Afanas'ev, Ingrid De Wolf, Jacopo Franco
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: We present a finite-element-based 1-D formalism to solve the Poisson equation for a thin-film transistor with a floating body. We apply our framework to InGaZnO (IGZO)-based devices, taking into account its subgap density of states (DoS), revealing its impact on device electrostatics, which is fundamental for predicting device performance and reliability. Our simple 1-D approach yields identical results as the more computationally intensive 2-D TCAD implementations, and it enables the convenient implementation of physics-based IGZO-specific models. Finally, we apply the new framework to quantify the IGZO deep subgap DoS measured by light-assisted <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$I$</tex-math> </inline-formula>–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$V$</tex-math> </inline-formula> spectroscopy.

title: mmOVD$^+$: Detecting Fully Occluded Vehicles With Millimeter-Wave Signals
authors: Chenming He, Chengzhen Meng, Xiaoran Fan, Dequan Wang, Lu Zhang, Yubo Yan, Yanyong Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: Reliable detection of nearby vehicles is critical for autonomous driving. Problems thus arise when a vehicle is occluded and becomes undetectable, which may lead to accidents. In this paper, we develop mmOVD+, a millimeter-wave radar-based system that enables fully occluded vehicle detection by leveraging ground-reflected signals that propagate beneath the blocking vehicle's chassis. To eliminate ghost points caused by frequent multi-path reflections, we devise a set of neighborhood behavior-based point features and integrate them into GhostNet, a customized deep network that enables accurate point classification through combined feature learning. In addition, we propose a cumulative clustering algorithm to effectively aggregate the unstable ground-reflected radar points over consecutive frames to derive the bounding boxes of the vehicles. We have evaluated mmOVD+ in both controlled and real-world environments. In an underground garage and two campus roads, we conducted controlled experiments in 70 scenes with 9 vehicles, including a minibus, a motorcycle, and an electric moped. Our system accurately detects occluded vehicles, with a 92.7% F1 score for occluded vehicle detection and a 100% success rate for occlusion event detection. More importantly, we drove 324 km on crowded roads at a speed up to 70 km/h and show we could achieve a 93.8% occlusion detection success rate and a 2.4% false alarm rate with only 10% of the training data.

title: Element-Level Access Control on Encrypted SQL Tables Using CP-ABE: Optimized on Reusable Sub-policy
authors: Juhao Hu, Jing Wu, Chengnian Long
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: With the rapid growth of cloud computing, securely outsourcing sensitive data has become crucial for protecting user privacy. SQL, renowned for its expressiveness and flexibility, remains the predominant query language for big data analytics. However, existing access control mechanisms for encrypted SQL databases primarily operate at coarse granularity levels, such as databases, tables, or columns, failing to meet nuanced user access requirements at the individual data-element level. To address this challenge, we propose EGuardSQL, a novel architecture that enables fine-grained, element-level access control over encrypted SQL tables. Our approach leverages Ciphertext-Policy Attribute-Based Encryption (CP-ABE) to define hybrid access policies that integrate both row-level and column-level permissions, facilitating precise and flexible control. To mitigate the inherent computational overhead associated with CP-ABE, particularly when dealing with complex policy structures, we introduce an optimized reusable sub-policy mechanism that effectively reduces redundant cryptographic computations during encryption and decryption processes. We formally prove the selective IND-CPA security of the EGuardSQL scheme under the decisional $q$-parallel Bilinear Diffie-Hellman Exponent (BDHE) assumption and demonstrate its resistance to collusion attacks. Comprehensive performance evaluations illustrate that EGuardSQL achieves significant improvements in computational and communication efficiency compared to conventional CP-ABE approaches, while maintaining low storage overhead and robust security guarantees.

title: Extrapolation-based practical observers
authors: Laura Menini, Corrado Possieri, Antonio Tornambè
publish: IEEE Control Systems Letters
abstract: A novel practical observer that mitigates the classical trade-off between transient peaking and steady-state estimation error is presented. The proposed extrapolation-based observer consists of multiple practical observers, whose gains are suitably chosen, and fuses their outputs using the Richardson extrapolation formula. The effectiveness of this observer is analytically studied and is validated using a chaotic system from the literature.

title: Physics-Informed Voltage Data Imputation for Robust Machine Learning-Based Battery State of Charge Estimation
authors: Showrov Rahman, Kaushallya Adhikari
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Industrial Electronics Society
abstract: Machine learning (ML) estimators used for battery state of charge (SOC) prediction are vulnerable to missing measurement data and therefore require data imputation techniques to replace unavailable observations. However, most existing data imputation methods for SOC estimation rely primarily on statistical approaches rather than incorporating battery physics and dynamic behavior. Consequently, the estimation accuracy of such estimators degrades significantly during long periods of consecutive missing data. This study addresses this research gap by proposing a physics-informed (PI) data imputation method that reconstructs missing voltage measurements using a battery equivalent circuit model. The proposed PI data imputation technique is integrated with a long short-term memory (LSTM) architecture to develop the ML estimator. To evaluate the estimation performance, the developed model is compared with two other LSTM estimators that use conventional voltage imputation methods: last observation carried forward (LOCF) and extrapolation. The estimation errors of the three estimators are evaluated using experimental data obtained from a photovoltaic battery energy storage system (PV-BESS) equipped with lithium iron phosphate (LFP) batteries. Furthermore, the robustness of the estimators is analyzed by quantifying the confidence intervals using Bayesian approximation and Monte Carlo forward passes, providing a comprehensive assessment of estimation performance. The estimators are evaluated under different missing data ratios of 25%, 50%, and 75% in order to systematically compare their performance. The results demonstrate that the proposed PI-based estimator consistently achieves superior performance across all scenarios, and the performance gap between the PI-based approach and conventional imputation methods (LOCF and extrapolation) increases as the duration of missing data becomes longer.

title: An Ultra-Compact Doherty Power Amplifier Based on Reciprocal Three-Coil-Coupled-Network
authors: Lei Zhang, Jianhua Zhu, Qian Zhang, Xiaoxian Liu, Zhangming Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems II: Express Briefs
abstract: This brief presents an ultra-compact Doherty PA based on the three-coil-coupling-network (TCCN). The TCCN supports load modulation while satisfying impedance matching, and imposes minimal constraints on the output parasitic capacitance of the transistors. Moreover, a detailed parametric analysis is conducted, clarifying the relationship between the traditional 90° equivalent lumped matching network and the specific parameters of the TCCN. A Ka-band Doherty PA prototype is fabricated in 65nm CMOS process to validate the effectiveness of this method, with a total core area of 0.074 mm². At 27GHz, the proposed DPA achieves a peak power output of 21.9 dBm, a peak power-added efficiency of 27.8%, a power-added efficiency at 6dB power back-off of 18.76%, with a maximum power density of 2.09 W/mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>.

title: Dual-Server Public Key Authenticated Searchable Encryption with Constant Trapdoor against Inside Keyword Guessing Attacks
authors: Wenjie Yang, Shujie Lin, Futai Zhang, Anjia Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Public key authenticated encryption with keyword search (PAEKS) is an advanced asymmetric searchable encryption technique that provides strong resistance against inside keyword guessing attacks (KGAs). However, most PAEKS schemes are limited to one-to-one search, resulting in a linear increase in the number of trapdoors as the number of senders grows. Some solutions have been introduced, but they are still deficient in security or privacy. In this paper, we propose a novel dual-server public key authenticated searchable encryption scheme with constant trapdoor. The proposed scheme achieves robust security against inside KGAs even if a malicious server is allowed to collude with certain data senders. Meanwhile, in contrast to existing schemes, it prevents the leakage of keyword equivalence among unretrieved ciphertexts. Moreover, the data receiver in our system only needs to create one trapdoor per keyword to search for their ciphertexts produced by different data senders, which is not achievable in most prior works. The experimental results demonstrate that our proposed construction maintains comparable performance to existing solutions, especially in multi-sender scenarios.

title: Suppression of Inverter-Induced Non-Circulating Bearing Currents Using Slot Wedges with Integrated Grounded Electrodes
authors: Konstantin Vostrov, Ilya Petrov, Pia Lindh, Juha Pyrhönen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: Bearing currents pose a significant threat in electric machines, as the phenomenon can lead to increased wear of bearings, followed by excessive friction, overheating, vibration, and premature machine failure. This issue becomes particularly critical in machines supplied by modern frequency converters, where elevated DC-link voltages, high-frequency switching, and steep voltage gradients significantly increase the risk of creating bearing current. This paper presents a practical and effective method for suppressing non-circulating (Electric Discharge Machining-type) bearing currents by integrating grounded electrodes into stator slot wedges. Referring to prior simulation-based studies, the proposed approach combines electrostatic shielding and optimized winding placement within a compact, manufacturable, and structurally robust solution. In this extended work, the concept is experimentally validated using an 11 kW induction motor prototype equipped with specially designed slot wedges containing embedded grounded electrodes. Finite element modeling is conducted to estimate parasitic capacitances and bearing voltage ratio (BVR), and the results are compared against experimental measurements. The findings confirm a substantial reduction in BVR and demonstrate strong agreement between simulated and measured values, reinforcing the practical viability and effectiveness of the proposed technique for mitigating bearing currents in frequency-converter-fed machines.

title: Normal/DME Classification in Retinal OCT Images with EfficientNetV2 Models and Ensembel-Features
authors: Mathiyazhagan Narayanan, V. Rajinikanth
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Diabetic Macular Edema (DME) is an important reason for vision impairment in diabetic patients and requires timely detection through retinal imaging. Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT) imaging is extensively considered for the diagnosis of DME. Manual analysis of the DME is time consuming process; hence this research aims to develop a deep-learning (DL) technique for classifying OCT database into normal/DME. This work employed EfficientNetV2 (EN-V2) model based scheme to achieve a better outcome and the developed framework consist stages like; (i) OCT data collection from the image repository, and CLAHE based enhancement, (ii) feature extraction form the OCT images using EN-V2 models, (iii) SoftMax based classification and best model identification, (iv) generation of the Ensemble-Deep-features (EDF) using best EN-V2 features, and (iv) classification with 3-fold cross validation. This research considered 1400 images per class and the detection is executed using individual-feature and EDF. The result substantiate that the EDF along with K-Nearest Neighbour (KNN) provided an accuracy of >96%.

title: Thermal-Aware Signal Integrity and Fault-Propagation in Distributed Electrification Architectures for Intelligent Vehicles: A System-Level Safety and Reliability Analysis
authors: Abdul Salam Abdul Karim
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: In this paper, the authors explore the interrelationship of thermal behavior, signal integrity, and functional safety in smart electric vehicle distributed electrification architectures. It investigates the role of skew and jitter caused by temperature variations and EMI drift on communication reliability of Battery Management Systems (BMS) and inverter networks based on CAN FD and daisy-chained SPI connections through secondary analysis of simulation and technical data. Thermal modeling indicates that increasing board temperature alters impedance and signal edge timing. The assessment of functional safety according to ISO 26262 Part 11 indicates that this kind of degradation increases the rate of hardware failures and expands the region of the diagnostic in safety-critical subsystems. Centralized, modular, and wireless BMS architectures were compared using a trade-off analysis between fault isolation, EMI resilience and control of latency. This work presents a system-level engineering analysis based on secondary simulation modeling and cross-reference of published FMEDA and reliability data; it does not claim recalculated quantitative safety metrics, but focuses on identifying thermal-EMI-signal-integrity interaction trends and their architectural safety implications. The combination of FMEDA data allowed confirming the relevance of early-stage modeling of the EMI-thermal interactions as a key to meeting the ASIL compliance. The paper arrives at the conclusion that shielding optimization, redundancy-based communication design, and predictive thermal management are useful in improving reliability, reducing the number of cascading faults, and maintaining deterministic operation. These results develop a thermal conscious design taking into account EMI suppression, signal resilience, and safety verification of next-generation distributed vehicle electrification systems. These findings support early architecture selection and interface partitioning for safety-critical EV networks.

title: National Roaming Enablement and Security Enhancement: A Nationwide LTE Deployment Framework
authors: Muhammad Rashid Anwar
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Deploying Long-Term Evolution (LTE) networks in geographically dispersed areas introduces engineering challenges, particularly for seamless national roaming and strict regulatory compliance. This paper provides a framework for national roaming across over 200 LTE sites, integrating layered security and lawful interception compliance. The framework uses Diameter-based signaling firewalls, inline network firewalls, and Virtual Routing and Forwarding (VRF) to isolate roaming traffic. A custom signaling and user-plane mirroring approach meets regulatory standards without increasing lawful interception (LAI) licensing costs. The solution incorporates Diameter Edge Agent (DEA) and Home Subscriber Server (HSS) based geographic verification to validate roaming subscribers. Results confirm regulatory compliance, roaming enablement at all sites, minimized fraud and spam, and reduced capital expenditure due to license optimization. The framework serves as a practical model for mobile network operators handling national roaming requirements focused on security, compliance, and cost-effectiveness.

title: Enhancing Smart Grid Reliability: An AI-Based Automated Threat Detection System
authors: Akila K, Saran Kumar AP, Sarathi T, SriRam S
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The modernization of electrical infrastructure into IoT-enabled Smart Grids has introduced critical vulnerabilities, creating a massive attack surface for sophisticated cyber threats. Traditional security measures, such as static rule-based firewalls, are becoming increasingly insufficient against dynamic exploits and lack the adaptability required for real-time industrial environments. While Artificial Intelligence (AI) offers predictive capabilities, traditional ‘Black Box’ models fail to provide the interpretability necessary for security analysts to trust and act upon alerts. In this paper, we propose an intelligent, automated Intrusion Detection System (IDS) featuring a novel Hybrid Machine Learning approach and Explainable AI (XAI) integration. We conduct an extensive comparative study of multiple algorithms—Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Machine (SVM), XGBoost, and a proposed Hybrid RF-BPNN—evaluated on the NSL-KDD dataset. Results demonstrate that the proposed Hybrid RF-BPNN achieves superior detection accuracy by leveraging the feature extraction capabilities of RF with the non-linear learning power of Neural Networks. Furthermore, the system integrates SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) to generate real-time waterfall plots, identifying specific features that trigger alerts. This study confirms that combining Hybrid Ensembling with XAI improves detection metrics and significantly enhances operational trust. The proposed framework, deployed as a modular web application, offers a proactive, transparent, and scalable solution for securing critical IoT-digitalized energy infrastructure.

title: Deep Autoencoder Features with Metaheuristic Optimized ML Models for Brain Tumor Detection
authors: T. Shailaja, M. Shalini, A.Srinivasa Reddy
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: This research work proposes a hybrid intelligent framework that combines deep autoencoder-based feature representation with metaheuristic-optimized machine learning classifiers to achieve precise brain tumor detection. A deep autoencoder is used to learn compact and distinct latent representations from MRI scans. Then, a metaheuristic optimization algorithm is used to adjust the hyperparameters of machine learning classifiers to make them work better at classifying. The suggested framework does a good job of combining deep representation learning with optimized decision modelling. Experimental assessment on benchmark brain MRI datasets reveals enhanced performance regarding accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, and F1-score in comparison to traditional deep learning and machine learning methodologies.

title: An Adaptive Hybrid Learning Framework for Cyber Threat Identification and Automated Response in Industrial IoT Networks
authors: P Sai Prasad, K Naga Tulasi, N Shyam Prasad, M Keerthi, K Phani Sekhar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The growth of Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) systems has seen an increase in the risk of cyber intrusions, which is a result of large-scale connectivity, a diverse array of devices, and continuous data exchange. Many traditional intrusion detection methods have had trouble keeping up with the evolution of attack behaviors and also do not perform well in real time. To that end, this paper presents a deep learning-based intrusion detection framework and is evaluated on the NSL-KDD benchmark set. The proposed model employs a convolutional neural network (CNN) for feature extraction, followed by a bidirectional long short-term memory (BiLSTM) network for out-of-sequence pattern analysis. An attention element is incorporated into the design, which puts focus on important features, thus improving classification. The preprocessing stage includes data cleaning, categorical feature encoding, normalization, and attack grouping to establish a consistent learning environment.

title: Securing Blockchain from Double-Spending: A Study of Attacks and Preventive Techniques
authors: Rintu Augustine, Rintu Augustine, A. Krishnaveni
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Blockchain operates as an innovative technology employing a distributed and decentralized digital ledger to ensure the secure and transparent registration of transactions across diverse participants. This process entails organizing transactions into interconnected blocks using cryptographic algorithms, resulting in an immutable and easily comprehensible history of all transactions. This, in turn, fosters heightened levels of confidence and accountability by establishing an unwavering and visible record of transactions. The technology is indeed making its way into various fields and industries, where it is being embraced and used in a wide range of applications. Finance, supply chain management, healthcare, government, intellectual property, real estate, and education are among the notable sectors where blockchain is having a significant impact. The introduction of digital currencies such as Bitcoin initially drew attention to blockchain technology. This series of events resulted in the emergence of blockchain applications in the financial sector, which stand out as among the most promising and consequential applications. Financial market efficiency has been significantly improved by reducing counterparty risk, increasing transparency, and implementing real-time settlement mechanisms. This transformation gained traction as more people moved away from traditional banking systems and into the realm of blockchain. While blockchain technology has many benefits, it also has a number of risks and vulnerabilities. To reach its full potential in the financial technology sector, it must effectively address these challenges and potential security threats. This paper mainly focuses on (1) the basics of blockchain, (2) popular attacks identified in blockchain, and (3) a comparison of various defensive mechanisms proposed by various authors against double-spending attacks in blockchain

title: A Synergistic Machine Learning-Metaheuristic Approach for Smart Grid Load Dispatch
authors: Chodagam Srinivas, Amrutha Bs, N Hema Prakash, A V Pavan Kumar, BSH. Shayeez Ahamed, S. Padma
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: This paper introduces a synergistic approach to addressing the Economic Load Dispatch (ELD) challenge in thermal power systems by seamlessly combining Ridge Regression Learning (RRL) with the African Vulture Optimization Algorithm (AVOA). The ELD task involves distributing generation loads among multiple power units to minimize operational costs while adhering to system and operational constraints. However, the inherently nonlinear and non-convex nature of generator cost characteristics, compounded by practical considerations such as valve-point effects and prohibited operating zones, often limits the effectiveness of traditional optimization techniques. To navigate these complexities, this study leverages RRL to derive robust generator cost models from historical or simulated operational data, with L2 regularization mitigating issues related to multicollinearity and overfitting. These predictive cost formulations are then embedded within the AVOA framework to achieve optimal dispatch decisions. The efficacy of the proposed methodology is demonstrated through comprehensive studies on the IEEE 30-bus system under diverse loading conditions. Comparative analyses highlight that integrating RRL with AVOA not only accelerates convergence but also ensures more stable and cost-efficient dispatch outcomes relative to conventional methods. This hybrid strategy underscores the promise of data-driven, intelligent solutions for advancing the efficiency and reliability of modern smart grid operations.

title: Intelligent MPPT-Driven Modelling of SOFC Systems with DC–DC Boost Conversion
authors: Priya M.S., Saravanan S.
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Solid Oxide Fuel Cells (SOFCs) deliver clean energy conversion and high efficiency, but require a smart controller to perform at the maximum power point (MPP) under dynamic load and thermal conditions. This work presents an Artificial Neural System (ANN) based MPPT algorithm for a 3 kW SOFC with a boost converter. Unlike conventional methods like Perturb and Observe (P&O), the proposed work predicts the optimal duty cycle using inputs such as stack voltage and current. Trained on data from a validated MATLAB/Simulink SOFC model, the ANN-MPPT attains rapid convergence in less than 0.2 seconds with no steady-state oscillations. Results show stable output power of 2940 W, a steady current of 10.5 A, a regulated voltage of 280 V, and an efficiency of 95.4%, confirming superior accuracy, stability, and efficiency. This work establishes the ANN-MPPT as a reliable, adaptable solution for high-performance SOFC power systems.

title: Wearable IoT Device for Health Monitoring and Emergency Notification
authors: Geetha S K, Diana Julie D, Monika S, Madhumita T S, Pavithra D
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Wearable health monitoring devices are becoming increasingly popular for their ability to continuously track body functions and help physicians diagnose potential health problems. However, most of the wearable monitoring solutions available today continue to heavily rely on cloud-based processing of user data. This heavy reliance on cloud-based solutions can result in high latency, increased power usage, and limited ability for clinicians to provide real-time support when there are unstable communications in the environment with the user. To overcome these issues, this work presents a wearable health monitoring device that can process data on the device without the need for cloud computing resources. The solution allows for real-time monitoring of patients and will transmit health data wirelessly to the clinician in a timely manner so that they can make timely clinical evaluations even if they aren't connected to the Internet. Experimental studies using data collected in clinical settings demonstrated that the device can predict clinical outcomes with a 94% accuracy and a 75 ms average inference time.

title: An Offline-First Intelligent Legal Chatbot for Indian Law Using Deep Learning and NLP
authors: Mallisetty Divya Sree, K. M. Rayudu, Nidamanuri Srinu, D. Bujji Babu, G. Sujatha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Navigating the complexities of the Indian legal system presents a significant challenge for the general public, largely due to convoluted legal terminology and limited access to reliable legal counsel. This paper introduces an intelligent, offline- first legal chatbot designed to demystify Indian law, specifically the Indian Penal Code (IPC) and the Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC). The proposed system leverages Natural Language Processing (NLP) for understanding user queries and a deep learning model Long Short- Term Memory (LSTM) for robust intent classification. Unlike conventional online legal assistants that rely on web scraping and keyword matching, our system operates entirely offline, retrieving precise and contextually relevant legal sections from a structured MySQL database. The chatbot supports both text and voice-based inputs, enhancing accessibility for a diverse user base, including those in low- connectivity regions. Experimental results demonstrate that the LSTM-based approach achieves high accuracy in classifying legal intents, outperforming traditional machine learning baselines. This work contributes a scalable and user-friendly solution that promotes legal literacy and provides preliminary legal assistance without requiring an internet connection, thereby democratizing access to legal information.

title: Smart Vehicle Safety Using Seatbelt Speed Limiter
authors: Kavithamani K, Valarmathi M, Antony John Paul A, Aadhith Krishna S, Rithiga R
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The level of carelessness on roads remains persistent, due to car owners who continue to habitually fail to use seatbelts properly and turn on manual headlights in low-light areas without consequence. In this project, a smart integrated vehicle safety system incorporating seatbelt-based speed limitation and fuzzy logic-based automatic headlight adjustment is proposed to enhance driving safety and energy efficiency. Moreover, the proposed seatbelt-not-worn-induced speed limitation system enforces seatbelt wearing dynamically by restricting vehicle speed unless worn, transforming a passive warning into an active safety function. A smart fuzzy logic control adjusts headlight intensity according to environmental light sensed by an LDR sensor, providing optimal illumination without dazzling oncoming vehicles. A DC-DC buck converter regulates voltage smoothly and manages power efficiently. Implemented using an ATmega328P microcontroller, Proteus simulation tested the system under various conditions. Results verify vehicle speed restriction to 35 km/h if occupants don't wear seatbelts, reducing glare due to sudden illumination changes. Experimental results show reliable real-time actuation, stable motor control, and consistent headlight modulation. The system eliminates human error, increases driver attention, and improves vehicle safety. The architecture provides an inexpensive, scalable, functional superstructure for modern automotive safety applications.

title: Pretrained Genomic Transformers for Accurate Mutation Position Prediction
authors: Anjana M P, Arun K S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Mutations play an essential part in the progression of disease, so accurate determination of both a mutation's location within a gene and its type is critical for identifying the underlying genetic mechanism of diseases. Additionally, identifying the mutation allows for precise diagnosis and targeted treatment through the use of precision medicine. The method proposed in this paper utilizes a transformer architecture to predict mutation type and position, using pre-trained genomic language models such as DNABERT-2 and the Nucleotide Transformer. Mutation predictions are formulated as token-level classification of each nucleotide within a sequence, labelling each nucleotide based on whether or not it contains a mutation, and if so, which type of mutation it contains. To train and evaluate the proposed model, we used a curated dataset comprising 31-base sequences that have been annotated with mutation position. To handle the imbalance between classes-especially the minority types such as Indels and duplications, which are much less frequent than singlenucleotide variations (SNVs)-several class-balancing strategies were used. These include applying focal loss and dice loss, using data-augmentation techniques, and sampling the minority classes to ensure they are better represented during training. The results show that the DNABERT-2 achieves an accuracy of 93%, while the Nucleotide Transformer attains 96% with strong precision, recall, and F1-scores, which proves better generalization. These findings emphasize the effectiveness of pretrained transformer architectures for fine-grained genomic sequence analysis and highlight their promise in improving computational mutation detection for disease research.

title: Leveraging Machine Learning for Automated Diagnosis of Soft Tissue Tumors
authors: Sanjai S, Sudharsan K, Thivya V, Rajan C, Insol Rajasekar S
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: This paper presents a machine learning model using CNN namely ResNet50 for the identification then diagnosis of Soft Tissue Tumours (STTs), specifically skin tumors. The diagnosis of STTs is complicated by their complexity and heterogeneous characteristics, causing potential misclassifications and further emphasizing the need for systems that can provide accurate and timely diagnosis with efficiency. The proposed methodology utilizes high-level data preprocessing and feature extraction to increase classification accuracy and decrease classification error. Specific attention is placed on tumor delineation, as it is critical for treatment planning. The focus of the proposed system is to establish a high level of accuracy in differentiating Melanoma from normal skin tissue, ultimately improving diagnostic accuracy, therapy, and patient outcomes.

title: EfficientShuffleNetV2 Framework for Automated Disease Detection in Niphad Grape Leaves
authors: Adiba Muttaqin, Purnendu Bhowmik Shuvro, Md. Junaid Rashid, Ashikuzzaman
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: For sustainable viticulture, the accurate and timely detection of grapevine diseases is essential, particularly in high-yield regions like Niphad, Nashik, India. Traditional identification methods are slow and vulnerable to human error, so they need to be replaced with modern technology. Although modern deep learning (DL) models can classify data very well, they require a lot of computing power to run. And it becomes difficult to run such heavy models on small limited-power devices used in precision agriculture. To overcome this efficiency-accuracy trade- off problem, we propose an Efficient ShuffleNetV2 Framework, which can automatically detect four types of grape leaves, like Bacterial Rot, Downy mildew, Powdery Mildew, and Healthy Leaves. We compared the proposed framework with four state-of-the-art architectures, such as InceptionV3, ResNet101, DenseNet121 and EfficientNetB0. The proposed ShuffleNetV2 model performed very well in all evaluation indices. It achieved the highest recall (98.78 %) and F1-score (98.50 %). In addition, its specificity and Cohen's kappa were very good <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(97.18 \%)$</tex>. These results show that ShuffleNetV2 performs very well, even though InceptionV3 does not perform well. It is both efficient and practical because it is lightweight and fast. Therefore, in real time, it is the most reliable model to use in field agricultural disease detection.

title: Early Identification of Fetal Heart Failure Through Feature Extraction using a Modified CSO Algorithm
authors: N. Nalini, D. Ferlin Deva Shahila, Ashwini. A, Mohyedin Hamza, Sreekanth Sura, G. Karthikeyan
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Optimization using fetal heart image is one of the difficult tasks for identification and predicting heart failure in the earlier stage. Fetal heart failure in earlier stage is due to the problem of high blood pressure, smoking habit and high stress environment in day today life. Heart failure from all groups need to be detected at an earlier stage of diagnosis through optimization algorithms of various parameter measurement in heart image and need to be diagnosed automatically. The diagnosis process automatically monitors the defect in the heart chamber through a modified cat optimization algorithm. The optimization algorithm segments the heart image and identifies the heart hole in the earlier stage for feature extraction and measurement. The proposed method of modified cat optimization algorithm segments the heart image with respect to mixed pixel in size, shape and intensity of heart image and delineated with accurate identification. To identify irregular edge and boundary of fetal heart image through convex region for dimension, velocity and fitness function to obtain best optimal solution. Furthermore, the result compares with existing optimization algorithms and compares with state of art in the solution space. The accuracy of heart hole prediction through a modified cat optimization algorithm detects 80% in the solution space.

title: Drought Prediction for Agricultural Planning
authors: Naji Ahmad Albatayneh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: The agricultural output is being reduced considerably due to the drought, consequently threatening global food securities. This research is based on very recent and advanced machine-learning models that predict drought to improve agricultural planning. The study uses old weather conditions, soil moisture levels, and satellite images to find the specific key patterns and variables related to drought claims. To predict the occurrence of droughts and thus act, the proposed framework integrates data preprocessing techniques, such as feature selection and machine learning algorithms such as Neural Networks, Gradient Boosting, and Random Forest, with the help of which the proposed method is approximately as good as or even better than using traditional methods in the drought prediction in regions with different climates. The case shows that besides these common climatic regions, the model's predictions can also be highly accurate in other areas and highly reliable and precise ones. The report emphasizes the role of early drought warning in farmers and policymakers' resource allocation, irrigation planning, and crop selection processes. This study supports the progress of intelligent decision-making systems, leading to more resource-efficient and sustainable agricultural systems in the changing climate.

title: ResNet18 with Triplet Attention Mechanism for Diabetic Retinopathy Detection
authors: Ravi Bhushan Dixit, Chandan Kumar Jha
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: Patients with diabetes may develop diabetic retinopathy (DR), which can cause visual loss and eventually blindness. This damage can be prevented if DR is identified initially. Current DR detection techniques based on fundus images are labour-intensive, complex, and prone to errors. In this paper, the ResNet18 architecture combined with the triplet attention mechanism is used to develop a deep learning based framework for DR detection and its classification. Its lightweight design suppresses the background noise and concentrates on disease-relevant retinal regions with improved classification performance. The proposed method employed pre-processing steps to enhance vessel visibility and reduce illumination variability. Furthermore, class weighting and augmentation strategies are also used to minimise the class imbalance problem. Based on experimental results, this framework, ResNet18 enhanced with Triplet attention, outperforms the other existing techniques and achieves 98.91% accuracy, 98.49% specificity, 99.40% sensitivity, 98.22% precision, 98.81% F1-score, and 99.74% AUC. The method provides an interpretable and computationally effective solution appropriate for mobile healthcare applications and large-scale screening.

title: Evaluating Coordinated Turn Model-Based EKF for Multi-Sensor Target Tracking in Curved Maneuvers
authors: Raviramanan V, Usha Rani S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: The Coordinated Turn Model (CTM) is a widely adopted approach for multi-sensor fusion in tracking applications involving targets executing curved maneuvers. This paper presents a detailformulation of the CTM in a state-space framework, assuming constant speed and constant yaw rate in two-dimensional motion. Discretetime state transition equations are derived and integrated into an Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) framework, highlighting the prediction and update procedures along with Jacobian linearization. To evaluate the effectiveness of the CTM-based EKF framework, a simulation study was conducted using data from four distinct sensors: GPS, LiDAR, RADAR, and IMU. The ground truth trajectory, modeled as a vehicle moving in a circular arc at constant speed and fixed yaw rate, provides a well-defined testbed for assessing filter accuracy. The CTM's performance is compared against conventional motion models, including Constant Velocity (CV), Constant Acceleration (CA), and WhiteNoise Jerk (WNJ). A comparative analysis summarizes the key computational complexity and tracking accuracy of all models. Index Terms-Coordinated Turn Model, Extended Kalman Filter, Multi-Sensor Fusion, Trajectory Estimation.

title: TAFSNet: A Type-Aware Low-Frequency Semantic Consistency Network for Robust Deepfake Detection
authors: Yuxin Shui, Jing Wu
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI in Education Technology and Applications (AIETA)
abstract: DeepFake detectors often degrade in open-world deployment, where forgeries are produced by unseen generators and test data suffer from domain shift. While frequency-space decoupling can improve transferability, fixed band partitioning introduces spectrum bias across heterogeneous manipulations, and low-quality cross-domain data may distort low-frequency semantics, encouraging spurious domain shortcuts. We propose TAFSNet with two components: (i) Type-Adaptive Frequency Attention, a lightweight type-conditioned routing that reweights frequency bands per input to capture type-specific spectral anomalies, and (ii) Low-Frequency Semantic Consistency regularization that aligns low-frequency content between synthesized faces and real backgrounds to suppress semantic drift. Experiments on standard DeepFake benchmarks under severe perturbations show consistent improvements over prior methods with minimal overhead, especially in cross-domain evaluation.

title: Dynamic Task Reallocation in UAV-Assisted LoRaWAN Networks for Smart Farming
authors: Samar Sindian, Imad Jawhar, Mohammad Ali Al Kadery, Hussein Hachem
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: Smart farming increasingly relies on wireless sensor networks for environmental monitoring; however, collecting data from distributed sensors remains challenging in large rural fields where fixed gateways are infeasible. This paper proposes CRAFT (Cooperative Relay-Aware Flight Tasking), a decentralized dynamic task reallocation framework for multi-UAV LoRaWAN-based data muling. CRAFT partitions the monitored area into relay-assisted zones and enables unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) to cooperatively and adaptively reassign sensing tasks during flight based on workload disparity and received signal strength indication (RSSI). A model-based performance evaluation, derived from analytical mobility and communication assumptions, indicates that CRAFT reduces mission completion time by approximately 23%, significantly improves energy balance across UAVs, and maintains high packet delivery reliability compared to static partitioning and greedy reassignment baselines. These results highlight the potential of lightweight cooperative control for scalable and energy-efficient UAV-assisted LoRaWAN networks in smart farming applications.

title: A Self-Powered Wearable Tracking System Based on Piezoelectric Energy Harvesting for Continuous Localization
authors: Khalid Alsaiari, Mohammed Alattas, Saleh Aljuehif, Ahmad Alzahrani
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: This project presents a self powered wearable tracking system driven by energy harvested from human gait. The system integrates piezoelectric transducers embedded in footwear with a power conditioning stage, energy storage, and a low power localization and transmission module. Mechanical energy generated during walking is converted into electrical energy and regulated to supply sensing and wireless communication functions without external charging or battery replacement. An adaptive power management strategy schedules sensing and data transmission based on harvested energy availability to maintain stable operation under variable walking patterns. Experimental evaluation using a prototype demonstrates sustained operation during normal walking conditions, with sufficient harvested energy to support periodic position acquisition and data transmission. Measured results confirm the feasibility of continuous autonomous tracking using gait induced energy harvesting, supporting applications in personal safety, health monitoring, and location aware wearable systems.

title: NLA-SQLGEN: An AI-Driven Natural Language Interface for Intelligent Database Query Generation
authors: Vellaturi Gopikrishna, Paramati Chandini, Pallapu Srikanth, Shaikh Aman, Oggu Ruchit, Jangam Akhilesh
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Natural Language Interfaces to Databases (NLIDB) enable users to interact with database systems using everyday language instead of structured query languages like SQL. However, conventional NLIDB solutions often struggle with linguistic ambiguity, schema variations, and multitable query interpretation. This research presents an AI-powered Natural Language Interface, termed NLA-SQLGEN, which translates user-generated natural language queries into optimized SQL statements. The proposed system incorporates a multi-stage methodology consisting of query preprocessing, intent detection, entity recognition, semantic parsing, SQL generation, and an adaptive feedback mechanism. A Transformer-based semantic model is employed to capture contextual meaning and align user terminology with database schema elements. The system was evaluated using the WikiSQL benchmark dataset, demonstrating high translation and execution accuracy of 92.3% and 89.7%, respectively. Experimental results show that NLA-SQLGEN outperforms existing NLIDB models such as Seq2SQL, SQLNet, and IRNet, particularly in handling schema ambiguity and conversational query patterns. The proposed interface significantly enhances user accessibility to databases and provides an efficient alternative to manual SQL query writing for both technical and non-technical users.

title: A Blockchain-Integrated Privacy Framework for Audio Copyright Protection Using ZKP and Attribute-Based Encryption
authors: J Umamageswaran, Nadigatla Jyothi Kumar, Paruchuri Jagannath Rao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Blockchain provides a basis that is decentralized, and without the possibility of tampering, as the infrastructure for the copyright administration of audios. It entails the clear distribution of royalties and the reduction of illegal copying as well. Through its unchangeable ledgers and contracts that automatically execute, it offers a stronger protection of the rights on the digital music platforms. Still, the current decentralized music-sharing applications cannot adequately deal with copyright infringement due to poor verification procedures. These platforms might achieve accurate fingerprinting but at the same time, they do not keep the validation or provide access control that is granular and private. Hence, the pirated or altered audio often remains undetected, and personal data gets exposed through verification. This study proposes a new framework incorporating advanced protection methods for audio copyrights. It uses highly resilient fingerprinting against filtering, compression, noise, pitchshifting, and time-scale transformation, private validation mechanisms for safe on-chain copyright verification, and encryption for access control management. The audio data is stored on a decentralized network, whereas smart contracts oversee the processes of registration, infringement detection, and royalty auditing. The proposed solution achieves high detection accuracy (92.64% match rate), low end-to-end latency (616.3 ms), and high robustness against advanced manipulations. It surpasses current decentralized music-sharing platforms in detection reliability, privacy preservation, and access control.

title: Context-Aware Interview Generation for Online Loan Screening Using LLaMA 3.2
authors: Ahmad Ihsan Farhani, Alhadi Bustamam, Rinaldi Anwar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: The high rate of non-performing online loans in Indonesia highlights the need for a system capable of assessing the prospective borrower's ability to repay the loan before the prospective borrower makes an online loan. One potential solution is to develop a system that analyzes a debtor's biodata and financial capability to generate a set of tailored questions based on their biodata. Given the large volume of online loan transactions in Indonesia, conducting manual interviews would be time-consuming and resource intensive. To address this, a Large Language Model (LLM) is proposed to create questions relevant to the borrowers' biodata. The authors have chosen the Llama 3.2-3b architecture for its modernity and superior overall performance. Based on the experiments, it shows that the trained model able to extract the prominent features of from the biodata and able to generate question that are based on the prospective borrower biodata this also supported by its quantitative measure using perplexity achieveing low perplexity score around 7-12.

title: Fog Computing Security: Leveraging Recurrent Neural Networks for Accurate Threat Detection
authors: A. Syed Musthafa, Lavanya Murugan, Vignesh Janarthanan, G. Jenifa, R.Nithya, Arulkumar R
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The adoption of fog computing at a rapid rate has led to significant improvements in the performance and scalability of the distributed systems, especially those that handle latency-sensitive tasks. Nevertheless, the good part also comes with a bad part in terms of the security aspects of the platforms caused by the decentralized character and limited resources capability of the fog nodes. The authors present here an intelligent threat detection framework that employs Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN) to identify and solve the security issues in the fog computing environment. The proposed plan is focused on the exploration of the network flows variability which actually represents both short-and long-range dependencies and thus, it is able to paint the full picture of the potential incursion. The RNN algorithm, by constantly decoding new information, not only increases the accuracy but also at the same time, it reduces the number of false alarms. The process involves a systematic data preprocessing stage, a controlled learning procedure, and a decision that is self-adapting in order to improve the system’s capability to deal with the incidents. Incorporating the existing model into the fog nodes enables it to perform the operations without being completely dependent on the cloud. Such a solution is instrumental in ensuring the security of the fog-based networks as it provides a scalable and lightweight tool which fits the rapidly diversifying edge computing scenarios..

title: A Novel Nonlocal Low-Time-Cost Framework for Hyperspectral Image Reconstruction of DD-CASSI
authors: Xiaorui Yin, Lijuan Su, Yan Yuan, Qiangqiang Yan
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: The coded aperture snapshot spectral imaging (CASSI) technique, inspired by compressive sensing (CS) theory, enables efficient acquisition of real-world 3D hyperspectral images. Several algorithms have been designed to recover HSIs from compressed measurements. Deep learning (DL)-based algorithms demonstrate outstanding performance. However, DL-based algorithms typically can only reconstruct HSIs with a predetermined number of spectral bands because of their fixed architecture. Optimization-based methods reconstruct HSIs by solving an optimization problem that incorporates various prior knowledge. However, their significant computational cost hinders practical deployment. In this paper, we introduce a low-time-cost framework for HSI reconstruction called the nonlocal low-tucker rank tensor (NLTRT), which solves the optimization problem using the alternate direction method of multipliers (ADMM), yielding the reconstructed results. Comprehensive experiments demonstrate that our NLTRT method outperforms state-of-the-art optimization-based methods with respect to both flexibility and runtime efficiency.

title: AI-Driven Fingerprint Recognition: A Technical Research Perspective
authors: M. Sivaraman, Kannadhasan B
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
