abstract: The greatest misfortune of biometric systems in general is fingerprint recognition is never fixed because the quality of a fingerprint image can vary easily with the existence of an influence on the type of used equipment, the ambiance of the surroundings and the behavior of a human being. The traditional systems do not absorb poorly the inputs of bad quality and the result is that they become inaccurate or wrong recognitions. In bid to overcome the issue we offer the introduction of more fundamental concept that is based on the machine learning concept and artificial intelligence that would check the quality of sensing of finger prints before the actual event of authentication takes place. We use a multi- level feature extraction scheme and we utilize all three canonical level of features of fingerprints: Level-1 (global ridges e.g. direction and making out), Level-2 (local minutiae e.g. bifurcations and terminations) and Level-3 (individual fine detail e.g. pores and ridges contours). In the hybrid design, CNNs are used to learn spatial features and SVMs and the random forests use conventional classification and the design has learned how to learn the sensor-invariant similarity using CNNs. This intelligent system moves the process of recognition of fingerprints out of the passive-mode and into the active and quality-sensitive authentication process. The given model permits increasing the rates of recognition, strength and anti- spoofing attacks or master prints, resulting in the biometric systems of the higher generation capable of adapting to the quality of the incoming information.

title: Unlearning for Clinical Deployment: Scalable Data Erasure in Medical Image Classification Models
authors: Urbana Jaman Orthee, Abu Saleh Muhammad Naeem, Tamanna Khatun, Meharun Ohona, Nafees Mansoor
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Machine learning has become integral to modern healthcare. However, models often inherit dataset biases or outdated information that compromise generalizability and clinical reliability. Machine unlearning (MU) offers a means to selectively remove the influence of unwanted data cohorts without necessitating full retraining. This research introduces a sparsity-enhanced unlearning framework for medical image classification, featuring two proposed methods: Fine-Tuning Confusion (FT Confusion) and Gradient Ascent Repair (GA Repair), which are extensions of the standard Fine-Tuning and Gradient Ascent approach. Each method is evaluated across both class-level and instance-level forgetting tasks. Experimental results demonstrate that FT Confusion achieves retraining-level performance in class-wise forgetting, whereas GA Repair most closely approximates retraining in random instance-level scenarios. Furthermore, model sparsity consistently narrowed the gap between approximate unlearning and complete retraining, thereby improving efficiency and stability. These findings indicate that no single strategy is universally optimal; instead, a complementary portfolio is needed, with FT Confusion most effective for class-level bias mitigation and GA Repair for heterogeneous patient-level removal. Our contribution lies in enhancing two widely used MU baselines (fine-tuning and gradient ascent) with optimization-driven mechanisms, providing clearer theoretical grounding and improved reliability over existing approximate unlearning methods. Overall, this work highlights the feasibility of scalable unlearning in clinical workflows, supporting the development of adaptive, bias-aware, and regulator-ready medical AI systems.

title: Automated Medulloblastoma Detection and Tumor-Side Localization in Pediatric MRI Using ViT Model
authors: Ramya D, S.K. Manigandan, Jeevitha S, Mukilan D, Deepa J, J. Velmurugan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Medulloblastoma is the most common brain tumor that is malignant in children, and the survival rates of such tumors are significantly low due to the inability to identify it as early as possible. Radiological examination is also highly subjective whereby the professional interpreting the radiology is highly volatile and this consumes a lot of time rendering it difficult to intervene in the radiology timely. This article is a reaction to this issue as it proposes a small-scale ViT-based deep-learning architecture to conduct automated early Medulloblastoma detection relative to pediatric brain MRI at the appropriate anatomical location of the tumor (left, midline, right). Patch-based embedding, multi-head self-attention, a classification head which learns to solve 64×64 grayscale cross-sectional MRI slices which have been preprocessed suggests two complementary classifiers; a tumor-side localization model (3-classes) and a Medulloblastoma detecting model (two-classes). In addition, it over lays projections on MRI slices to make them easy to read and hence applicable in clinical analysis. It was proved experimentally that ViT is better than the traditional CNNs at global structural patterns because of small datasets and offers a novel clinically-beneficial device of early brain-tumor detection and localization on a child, which is more effective and promotes faster and more confident choicemaking.

title: A Deep Learning Approach for Detecting Cercospora Leaf Spot and Anthracnose in Starfruit using EfficientNet-B0
authors: L. Selvam, A. Vegi Fernando, R. Santhana Krishnan, J. Relin Francis Raj, D. Vikkiramapandian, G. Ram Sankar
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Plant disease detection plays a crucial role in agricultural management, particularly for crops like Starfruit (Carambola). Current approaches face several challenges, including the lack of high-quality labeled data, the variability of environmental conditions, and the difficulty of achieving real-time detection accuracy. Furthermore, traditional methods such as manual inspection are often time-consuming, costly, and prone to human error, making inefficient for large-scale agricultural applications. These limitations hinder effective disease management and timely intervention, resulting in potential crop losses. This work proposes a deep learning-based methodology for the detection of two significant diseases affecting Starfruit: Cercospora Leaf Spot and Anthracnose. By leveraging publicly available datasets such as PlantVillage and Leafsnap, alongside the Starfruit-specific dataset, this approach addresses the challenges of limited data by incorporating data augmentation techniques to improve model generalization. The proposed architecture utilizes the EfficientNet-B0 model, which offers an optimal balance between accuracy and computational efficiency, making it well-suited for real-time disease detection. The model undergoes rigorous training using labeled images with a focus on precise classification of infected and healthy leaves, ensuring high recall for both diseases. Key features of this methodology include data augmentation for enhanced model robustness, the use of a highly efficient base model (EfficientNet-B0), and the comparative analysis across different datasets. The result is a highly accurate and efficient disease detection system that can be deployed in agricultural environments for early intervention, thereby preventing large-scale crop damage and improving yield management.

title: Diabetic Retinopathy Segmentation and Feature Extraction using Federated Learning-based Machine Learning Algorithm
authors: R.Ashok Kumar, N. Thillaiarasu
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Classical techniques of machine learning for segmentation and feature extraction in Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) often involve collecting and storing patient records in a central computing system. This raises not only privacy but also security issues. Hence, in this research, Federated Learning (FL) solves this problem by enabling several medical organizations of choice to train the models while the patient’s data is still stored within a single institution. In the beginning, the data is collected from the benchmark source that has been referred to as Asia Pacific Tele Ophthalmology Society 2019 Blindness Detection (APTOS 2019). Then pre-processing of these collected images is done by Contrast Limit Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE). These preprocessed images undergo the segmentation using the watershed segmentation technique. The last step entails feature extraction from those segmented images using the Linear Discriminant Analysis (LDA) technique. Moreover, this method reduces the necessity for data transfers which ensures that the privacy of the patients is maintained. Hence, the FL-based LDA not only propels research and development in medical imaging but also enables fair distribution of advanced diagnostic devices, thereby improving the DR management for the patients.

title: Bandgap Engineering of Two Dimensional Photonic Crystal for Sensing Applications
authors: N Anjani Dayananth, P Aruna Priya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Photonic crystals are periodic dielectric nanostructures that affect propagation of electromagnetic wave across the space. The strong optical confinement in the photonic crystal and its structural periodicity have led to the emergence of photonic bandgap. This paper investigates the photonic bandgap of two dimensional photonic crystal with hole arrays in the dielectric slab and obtain the band structure by varying radius, lattice constant, arrangement of holes, refractive index of dielectric slab and introducing lattice defects. The Finite element method is employed to find the band structure of two dimensional photonic crystal. The robust band structure provides a favorable theoretical basis for bio sensing applications.

title: A Unified Deep Learning Framework for Pose-Based Badminton Stroke Recognition and Skill Assessment
authors: K. Deepasree, P. Srividya, S. Giriprasad
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: This paper presents a pose-based approach for badminton stroke recognition using deep learning techniques. The suggested technique focuses on skeletal motion patterns that are taken from player movements rather than using raw video frames. 17 human keypoints are identified from badminton videos using YOLOv8 posture estimation, and these keypoints are then transformed into structured pose sequences. After that, temporal variations are modeled and strokes like forehand drive, backhand drive, clear, and net shots are categorized using a 1D Convolutional Neural Network (1D-CNN). The method lessens sensitivity to changes in the camera, illumination, and background noise. Results from experiments show that posebased learning works well for identifying sports actions. This research promotes smart sports analytics and helps develop more effective and healthful training methods.

title: BlockSafe: A Hybrid Architecture Combining AI-Driven Security and Blockchain Integrity
authors: Georges El Hajal, Gaby Abou Haidar, Roy Abi Zeid Daou
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: Due to the rapid increase in digital services, relying on passwords as a primary method of authenticating the identity is increasing. This shift has resulted in the continual increase in data breaches, credential thefts, and phishing attacks on users by the digital-service providers they use. Traditional password-management systems depend upon centralized architecture for their operation, which creates a single point of failure and exposes the system to exploitation by Distributed Denial-of-Service (DDoS) attacks. Current implementations of blockchain technology do offer a decentralized approach, with secure and immutable credentials; however, such implementations tend to be costly from both a hardware and an operational standpoint. BlockSafe is a lightweight, artificial intelligence-based password management system that combines machine-learning-driven security analytics with simple blockchain architecture. The application leverages the Gemini Large Language Model (LLM) to perform intelligent, real-time password strength classification and provide contextual security feedback. In addition, BlockSafe utilizes client-side asymmetric encryption through MetaMask and Ethereum-based smart contracts to ensure the immutability and verifiable integrity of credential data.

title: Time-Synchronized Wearable Sensor Fusion for Real-Time Multi-Muscle Fatigue and Gait Asymmetry Analysis
authors: Manvi Manjunath, Tarun Alur Gowri Shankar, Nikhil Shiraguppi, Shagun Gupta
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: The proposed wearable system is a system that combines surface Electromyography (sEMG) and Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU) to give real-time gait analysis. This system is constructed using ESP32 microcontroller and transmits synchronized data wirelessly to a Python server. Random forest and SVM machine learning algorithms are used to classify gait abnormalities providing a cost-effective and portable solution to clinicians and patients. This paper demonstrates that wearable technology has the potential to transform gait diagnostics.

title: Leveraging Human-Aligned and Probabilistic AI for White-Box Research and Knowledge Discovery: A Survey
authors: T. Sridevi, Jhansi Gonuguntla, Srishti Turki
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: There has always been a lot of time and energy invested in designing solid experiments in scientific research. Recently, though, AI has changed how these experiments are designed through automating aspects of the design process, such as thinking about how to complete the design, finding out cause-and-effect relationships, iterating based on feedback, the ability to help in planning the design phase, and learning from the user in a Human-in-the-loop. The paper discusses ways that using AI has changed the way experimental designs are created and provides an overview of how to use LLMs to increase the quality of experiments through constructing causal models, generating new ideas, and maintaining user involvement via Reinforcement Learning. The paper also discusses AI-based tools that provide what-if simulations using Bayesian Optimisation, explainable AI (XAI), Causal Inference, and Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback, which work together to support the development of intelligent agents capable of assisting with complex design activities. There has been substantial progress in developing this type of AI agent; however, Transparency and Interpretability continue to be significant challenges. To help resolve these issues, the hybrid personality framework IXORA (call name Zora) was put together. IXORA is a multimodal AI system that combines Domain-Specific Language Models with an integrated Knowledge Module to provide both deep domain knowledge and causal reasoning required to design high-quality experiments. IXORA will allow non-experts to access highquality experimental design tools.

title: Real-Time Drone Image Dehazing Using Adaptive Dark Channel Prior and Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization
authors: C. Siva Kumar, D. Sathyanarayanan, Thanikanti Jayakrishna, Varadappagri Chakradhar Reddy, V. Tarun Naga Sai Durga Suresh, Ponnekanti Sai Praneeth
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: In outdoor imaging scenarios, particularly in defense surveillance and UAV-based operations, imagery tends to be degraded by haze caused by atmospheric particles such as fog, dust, and smoke. The presence of haze reduces visibility makes contrast worse and distorts colour. This hinders our ability to recognize objects and analyze scenes. To address these issues, we introduce a Dark Channel Prior(DCP) based haze removal framework. The approach recovers clarity from a single hazy photo without the need for pre-trained models or large learning. In our solution, the transmission map and atmospheric light are estimated by DCP and guided filtering to refine the outcome and reconstruct an enhanced image. Additionally haze thickness is classified as thin, moderate, or dense. This classification helps adjust parameters to improve dehazing. To increase image contrast, a technique named Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE) has also been used. This technique improves image clarity to a greater extent, and hence it can be used in low visibility areas like surveillance, recognition, and navigation systems. Moreover, it requires less training and thus can be used in realtime applications like drones.

title: Benchmarking Sequential Against Parallel Reinforcement Learning for Multi-Agent Swarms in Dynamic Environments
authors: Samuel Abiodun Oyedotun, Joshua Ojo Nehinbe, John Temitope Ogbiti
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Reinforcement learning for multi-agent swarm simulations demands robust frameworks capable of rigorous comparison between sequential and parallel training paradigms. Nevertheless, yet existing approaches lack integrated hyperparameter optimization and statistical evaluation in vectorized environments. This study uses Python language to introduce a novel fully vectorized Stable Baselines3 framework that benchmarks sequential against parallel reinforcement learning under identical computational budgets. The approach uses Optuna for multi-objective hyperparameter tuning and enforces statistical rigor through Cohen's d, Cliff's delta, bootstrap confidence intervals and ANOVA. Empirical results from dynamically complex swarm environments reveal a fundamental performance trade-off. Sequential training (n_envs = 1) achieves superior asymptotic rewards (best −4.25 ± 29.06) and stability with efficiency scores near optimality (−0.006), while parallel execution (n_envs = 4) sacrifices 40-50 × reward performance and exhibits 3-5 × worse efficiency metrics (−0.76 to 2.97) despite 2-7 × faster training times. Further statistical analysis confirms that environment-level parallelism amplifies nonstationarity in highly coupled agent systems but hinders coordination and convergence. These findings demonstrate that concurrency materially impacts optimal hyperparameter selection with sequential methods requiring higher timesteps (20,000) and learning rates (0.0003) for peak performance whereas parallel approache is poor regardless of its configuration. This work establishes that sequential reinforcement learning is preferable for performance-critical swarm applications while parallel methods suit real-time scaling scenarios requiring stabilization mechanisms. This framework ultimately addresses critical gaps in configuration alignment, resource management and reproducible benchmarking by offering actionable insights for deploying RL in complex and vectorized robotic swarm environments.

title: A Unified Intelligence Framework for Early Liver Disease Prediction: Bridging Interpretability and Diagnostic Precision
authors: G. Sajiv, N. Meenakshisundaram
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Many liver diseases are a global health problem, with symptoms sometimes not appearing until the disease is very serious. intervention and better patient results depend on making early and correct predictions. This research presents a Unified Intelligence Framework for predicting liver disease by combining several machine learning classifiers into a single model. This framework splits the work between individual base learners, who spot different trends in the data and a meta-learner which combines what they have discovered for the best prediction. By looking at mutual information and doing correlation analysis, only clinically pertinent variables such as bilirubin, liver enzymes and albumin-globulin ratio were retained. The ensemble model achieved both a high accuracy of 89.70% and an AUC of 0.94, exceeding individual models and common traditional baselines. Detailed testing revealed that mistakes were reduced when looking for critical illnesses. This study proves that AI works best when its predictions are easy to relate to which helps build dependable AI-assisted diagnostic systems. It is possible that the framework can be applied usefully in clinical decision making in hepatology.

title: LLM Augmented Framework for Realtime Accident Detection and Analysis
authors: Aiswarya A, Falguni Shinde, P Sreejindeth, Subramaniyaswamy V
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: In this paper an Accident Detection System is proposed to analyze real-time traffic accident, focusing on rapid forensic alerting, through combined efforts of computer vision, machine learning, and natural language processing techniques utilizing Large Language Models (LLMs). Video captured through CCTV surveillance will be subjected to pre-processing techniques to standardize its dimension to 250 × 250 pixels in the RGB format, then classification by a convolutional neural network that classifies it as accident and non-accident scenarios. Accident detection occurs when the confidence level exceeds the defined threshold, resulting in automatic storage of relevant frames and immediate alerts via Telegram, a messaging platform. Here, two separate LLM implementations are evaluated: Gemini API, that demonstrated consistent performance, while LLaMA, established through Ollama showed operational inconsistencies when tested on standard computing infrastructure. Complete evaluation metrics include accuracy, precision, confusion matrix, ROC curve and precision-recall, allowing objective performance characterization.

title: Optimized Random Forest with Explainable AI for Accurate Boro Rice Yield Prediction and Real-Time Web App
authors: Venkateshwarlu Banavath, Arindam Kishor Biswas, Md Shafiul Islam, Sabrina Khan Mim, Md Asif Khan, Md Kamrul Siam
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: A precise prediction of rice yield is vital when it comes to planning of food security and decisions making in agricultural fields. This paper involves an elaborate machine learning model to forecast the Boro rice yield by using a data set consisting of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{4, 4 8 0}$</tex> records and 43 agricultural, environmental, and soil characteristics. We compared five regression models: Linear Regression, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, Support Vector Regression and K-Nearest Neighbors, optimizing hyperparameters using cross-validation grid search. The selection of feature was done using a mutual information analysis, and the most significant indicators are considered the inputs fertilizers (urea, muriate of potash, DAP), the characteristics of the soil (pH, texture, moisture) and the rainfall. The optimized Random Forests model performed better, with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R} \hat{\mathrm{2}}=\text{0. 9 9 9 9}$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{R M S E}=\text{0. 0 0 0}$</tex>, and the computational time of 0.01 seconds. Explainable AI methods such as SHAP and Morris sensitivity analysis offered an insight into the model decision process, where it was found that yield estimates are strongly affected by soil category, cultivated area, and fertilizer usage data. A practical web application was designed to ease its use in the field, where farmers and agricultural planners can have real-time estimation of their yields. The presented model shows a great enhancement of the available methods, as it yields high scores in accuracy, as well as availability of interpretation on sustainable agricultural management.

title: Designing a Dry-Lab Pipeline for Multiplex CRISPR µPAD Assays against Dengue and Chikungunya
authors: Mithila Arman, Lamia Akther Urmi, Maher Ali Rusho, Poritosh Dey, Intezab Alam Sheikh, Md. Khurshid Jahan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: This study demonstrate the design of a dry-lab multiplex CRISPR diagnostics pipeline for Dengue (DENV) and Chikungunya (CHIKV) in this manuscript. The pipeline integrates sequence input with CRISPR assay design through multiple critical steps: conserved-window discovery, primer design for RT-RPA, Cas12a guide discovery, and µPAD timing estimation. To provide an in silico platform for an easily re-producible pipeline to bridge the gap between genetic sequences and readily implementable multiplex diagnostics designed for µPADs. Here the propose method bypass the wet-lab testing in the early stages and generates the design assets such as ranked primers, guide sequences and µPAD timing for prototyping the device. This pipeline can facilitate rapid point-of-care diagnostics development for resource limited settings while bridging the translation gap between molecular diagnostics and practical device constraints. This method is very tunable, intuitive and delivers results ready for downstream experimental work directly.

title: Remote Teleoperation of an Anthropomorphic Robotic Hand with Cloud-Enabled Wearable Sensing and Lightweight MLP
authors: Faiaz Ahsan, Anamul Haque Akash, Najeeb Ahmed Bhuiyan, Nazia Hasan Dana
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Teleoperation of robotic hands has gained significant attention due to its potential in assistive technologies, remote interaction, and operation in hazardous environments. Recent advances in wearable sensing, embedded intelligence, and cloud-based communication have created new opportunities for enabling real-time, low-cost solutions in this domain. This work explores a framework that integrates a flex-sensor–based wearable glove with a lightweight multilayer perceptron (MLP) model to classify finger bending into discrete postures. The predicted commands are transmitted via a cloud-based database to a tendon-driven robotic hand, enabling remote actuation across independent networks. Experimental evaluation demonstrated high classification accuracy and optimal end-to-end latency, validating the feasibility of combining affordable hardware, embedded learning, and scalable cloud infrastructure for practical remote teleoperation of anthropomorphic robotic hands.

title: Design and Analysis of Adaptive Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) for Wireless Communication
authors: Vijayashanthi R S, Arunaachalam G S, Hariharan N, Lingeswaran A, Pawan Sidharth R G
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: This paper presents a new adaptive QKD system with a Genetic Algorithm optimizer. The issue of security in modern wireless network is of alarming significance, especially to freespace optical connections in which the dynamics in the environment are hazardous with regard to confidentiality. Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) is now available over air channels and can be used to secure a key exchange but it is highly impaired by atmospheric turbulence, beam misalignment, background radiation, path loss, and scintillation and pointing errors. These weaknesses elevate Quantum Bit Error Rate (QBER) and slow down secret key rate, making it impractical to deploy. In this paper, we present a new adaptive QKD system with a Genetic Algorithm (GAD) optimizer adjusting decoy intensities, the probability of choice of a basis in selecting a detector, detector threshold, and privacy amplification rates dynamically. The active control of channels under different atmospheric conditions by continuous monitoring of channels with pilot frames and the QBER feedback allows intelligent parameter reconfiguration. The performance of the simulation using various turbulence regimes shows that the proposed adaptive one boosts the average secret key rate by The simulation results show a 25-40% improvement in secret key rate, reduced QBER, and increased secure transmission range as well as the range and the resilience of transmission, which confirm the efficiency and practical importance of the proposed adaptation technique.

title: Tabu Search-Based User Scheduling and Analog Beam Selection for CSI-Light Opportunistic Hybrid Beamforming in mmWave Massive MIMO
authors: Nam Van Dinh, Ngoc-Thanh Nguyen, Thuan Van Le
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: We investigate a CSI-light downlink hybrid beam-forming framework for mmWave massive MIMO, where the base station avoids full channel estimation and relies solely on beam-domain SNR feedback obtained from analog codebook probing. Based on the reported SNR matrix, the scheduler jointly selects a subset of users and analog beams to enable opportunistic multiuser transmission under practical feedback constraints. To efficiently address the resulting combinatorial user-beam selection problem, we develop a Tabu search-based algorithm that explores structured neighborhood moves using a fairness-aware utility combining sum-rate and worst-user performance. After selection, digital precoding (ZF or MMSE) is applied over the reduced-dimensional effective channel to suppress residual interference. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed scheme consistently outperforms representative CSI-light baselines in both sum-rate and fairness across different SNR regimes and user densities. These results confirm that Tabu search provides an effective performance-complexity trade-off for opportunistic hybrid beamforming under limited feedback.

title: Visual Person Recognition Using Cross-Attention Fusion
authors: S. Sowmyayani, J. Kavitha
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Visual person recognition is a fundamental problem in computer vision with applications in surveillance, access control and human-computer interaction. Traditional methods often rely on single visual cues such as face, body appearance or gait, which limits robustness under occlusions, pose variations and illumination changes. To address these challenges, this work proposes a cross-attention fusion framework for visual person recognition that effectively integrates multiple visual modalities. The proposed approach extracts complementary features from different visual sources, such as facial regions, body appearance and contextual cues, using transformer-based encoders. A cross-attention mechanism is then employed to model inter-dependencies between these feature representations, allowing the network to selectively emphasize discriminative information across modalities. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed cross-attention fusion framework achieves Rank-1/mAP accuracies of 93.4%/80.3% on Market-1501, 87.6%/72.5% on DukeMTMC-reID and 78.9%/92.7% on MSMT17, outperforming recent state-of-the-art methods. In future, multi-spectral modalities such as infrared or depth data may further improve performance in low-light environments.

title: Recognition of Human Facial Expression using Novel Feature Descriptor
authors: Mousumi Bala
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Recognition of human facial expression has achieved significant significance in human–computer interaction, security systems, and behavioral analysis. This study introduces a great recognition model using the CK+ database. To effectively capture face patterns, three functional descriptions were used: Local binary pattern (LBP), Gradient local ternary pattern (GLTP), and a novel Purification-Gradient Local Ternary Pattern (PGLTP). Classification was performed through the Support Vector Machine (SVM). The experimental evaluation showed that the proposed PGLTP description achieved better accuracy and computational efficiency compared to conventional methods. These findings demonstrate that the strategic integration of refined handcrafted features with modern classifiers remains a highly effective approach for robust facial expression recognition, with direct applications in human-computer interaction and security systems.

title: A Machine Learning and DEM-Based Approach for Predicting Sea Level Rise and Assessing Coastal Risk in Bangladesh Using AI Insights
authors: Arjan Ghosh, Farhana Tazmim Pinki, Rajesh Baidya, Farjana Alam, Shovon Mandal
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Bangladesh is a very climate vulnerable country which has been identified to have great deal of threat imposed by sea level rise (SLR) to its coastal communities, ecosystems, and infrastructure. In this work we present an integrated machine learning and Digital Elevation Model (DEM) based framework which we put forth for the prediction of sea level rise and assessment of coastal flood risk in Bangladesh. We used historical tide gauge records from chosen coastal stations (which include Charchanga, Chittagong, Cox's Bazar, Khepupara, and Hiron Point) to develop and compare many forecasting models which are Linear Regression, ARIMA, Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) and a hybrid ensemble model. We then put together the predictive outputs which we obtained from high resolution DEM data to present flood risk maps which we did so under different sea level rise scenarios. Also we developed a Streamlit based web platform which we made to present projected inundation zones and which also gives AI supported information for decision support. We found out that the LSTM model did very well in terms of prediction of non linear temporal trends, also the hybrid ensemble model did very well in terms of stable and reliable performance across stations. DEM based simulations we did identified very vulnerable low level coastal areas which we present the value of putting together temporal forecasting with geospatial analysis. We validated our results using satellite imagery and historical data which we did to support the use of the put forth framework for coastal planning and climate change adaptation. We put forth a scalable and user friendly tool which policy makers, researchers and local stakeholders can use to support data based coastal risk management in Bangladesh.

title: An IoT-Based Optical Shunt Sensor for Hydrocephalus
authors: Zaman Mohammad Rafsun, S.M. Rafiur Rahman, Tasnim Anam Tusti, Kazi Zannatul Ferdwushee
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Hydrocephalus shunts are vital for diverting cerebrospinal fluid (CSF), but there can still be some problems regarding blockage and blood contamination. The presence of these problems cannot be observed until severe symptoms appear. Existing works in shunt monitoring are complex, costly, and limited to detecting only flow obstructions, ignoring complications such as blood contamination. To address this limitation, this study develops a 3D-printed prototype that adopts a simpler sensing approach compared to the complex methodologies used in existing shunt sensor designs. This represents a smart hydrocephalus shunt sensor that monitors real-time CSF flow. This prototype integrates an LS06-S photosensor along with an LED to distinguish three flow conditions: normal CSF flow, no flow, and blood presence. Arduino UNO is also present as the microcontroller, and it processes the data from the sensors’ output, while the HC-05 Bluetooth module enables wireless data transmission to the external platforms. The prototype is tested with CSF and blood-mimicking solutions, confirming its reliability for condition detection and effectiveness in sending wireless alerts. From this study, it can be observed that the prototype is successfully detecting all the complex conditions associated with hydrocephalus and can also provide real-time data to the external platforms for monitoring and alert purposes. This approach is simple yet effective for detectability beyond current methods and serves as a proof-of-concept for future miniaturized implantable systems.

title: Design of a Bluetooth - LoRa Hybrid Communication System for Remote Areas
authors: E Sai Prakash Goud, Sudhakar Koyye
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Reliable communication in hilly and remote regions remains a major challenge due to the absence or unreliability of cellular and internet-based infrastructure. Short-range wireless technologies such as Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) offer convenient smartphone connectivity, but are limited by their communication range, while long-range solutions based on LoRaWAN depend on internet-connected gateways, restricting their use in infrastructure-free environments. To address these limitations, this paper presents the design and implementation of an infrastructure-free Bluetooth - LoRa hybrid communication system. In the proposed architecture, BLE is used as a local interface between a mobile device and an ESP32-based embedded node, while point-to-point LoRa communication enables long-range data exchange between geographically separated nodes without depending on gateways or internet connectivity. The system supports decentralised and bidirectional communication. Experimental evaluation demonstrates reliable end-to-end data transmission with stable signal quality, validating the feasibility of the proposed approach for emergency communication, disaster response, and connectivity in hilly and remote areas.

title: Helmet and Number Plate Detection using CNN in Deep Learning
authors: V P Leena, P Prabu, P Ragupriyan, F Rahul Christober, S Sathish Kumar, S Sridesinguraja
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: In recent years, the rise in road traffic violations, especially among two-wheeler riders, has raised serious safety concerns. One of the most common issues is the non-compliance with helmet regulations and the use of unregistered or unidentified vehicles. This paper presents a deep learning-based approach that utilizes Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) for automatic detection of helmet usage and recognition of vehicle number plates. The proposed system is designed to process real-time images or video frames from traffic surveillance cameras. It consists of two major components: helmet detection using a CNN classifier, and number plate localization followed by Optical Character Recognition (OCR) for alphanumeric extraction. The system is trained and evaluated using a diverse dataset with varying lighting and environmental conditions. Experimental results show high accuracy in detecting helmets and identifying license plates, proving the system's effectiveness in supporting automated traffic monitoring and law enforcement.

title: Smart Traffic Signal Optimization Using AI
authors: Amro Dabash, Omar AlSheikSalem
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: The optimization of efficient traffic signals is a key element in traffic flow improvement, congestion reduction, and urban mobility enhancement. Conventional traffic light systems have a difficult time efficiently responding to the constantly changing traffic patterns in modern cities. In this study, we present a fresh concept of traffic signal optimization with the help of artificial intelligence (AI) techniques like deep learning and reinforcement learning, amongst others. The project's purpose is to create an artificial intelligent signaling system that could provide a real-time response to the dynamic traffic data and guarantee that intersections will work as a smooth traffic flow with no delays. By using information from connected cars, cameras, and sensors, the AI-based system automatically learns via the surveillance of the traffic patterns. The performance of this method was evaluated by simulations and the considerable developments of the traffic system, energy saving as well as a decrease in the CO2 release caused by the traffic were demonstrated too. The paper presents the design of the AI-based solution, how it is affiliated with the current infrastructure, and the possible advantages for smart cities.

title: Performance Comparison of Hybrid Intrusion Detection Approaches in IoT Systems
authors: Jagdeep Singh, Harjit Singh
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Two very simple yet opposite challenges that have a big impact on the Internet of Things (IoT) ecosystem's stability and operational performance are energy efficiency and security. Even while data privacy, integrity, and authentication are important, typical security techniques might add extra processing power and battery life requirements, which is not feasible on resource-constrained IoT devices. In an IoT scenario, traditional encryption and key management solutions are inadequate due to limited processing power, memory, and battery capacity. One of the most potent approaches to address the security concerns of the Internet of Things (IoT) in recent years has been hybrid learning approaches, which can make decisions that are adaptable, data-driven, and intelligent. Hybrid learning algorithms can detect anomalies and intrusions while maintaining trust with minimal human intervention. This review paper assesses the relevance and contribution of several hybrid techniques, including Decision Trees, Support Vector Machines (SVM), Random Forests (RF), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), and Artificial Neural Networks (ANN), in improving the security of the Internet of Things (IoT). The paper also discusses how hybrid models are becoming increasingly important. These models, such as CNNs and RNNs, can extract more features and detect attacks in real time in large IoT deployments. We pay particular attention to hybrid learning methodologies, which integrate ML/DL models with optimization algorithms to find the optimal balance between detection accuracy and energy consumption. The paper also addresses other important challenges with using ML/DL in heterogeneous IoT contexts with limited resources, including data imbalance, computational complexity, and rapidly adaptive attack patterns. Finally, it identifies gaps in the research and proposes new research directions, such as lightweight language systems, federated learning, and explainable AI-based intrusion detection systems to make the next generation of IoT networks more secure, scalable, and sustainable.

title: Diffusion-Enhanced Attention U-Net for Next-Generation Cancer Treatment Planning
authors: Happila T, Rajendran A, Boomika E, Jayasri S, Pradhiksha S
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Medical image segmentation is very important for the treatment planning and cancer diagnosis. It is used to identify tumor boundaries, which directly affects clinical decisions. Even though U-Net based deep learning models are used widely, they face limitations in preserving fine boundary details, suppressing noise, and producing segmentation results which is suitable for treatment-oriented applications. The proposed work suggests a Diffusion-Enhanced Attention U-Net (DEAU-Net) framework which is designed for next-generation cancer treatment and mainly focused to overcome these issues. Although a diffusion-based refinement technique improves structural coherence and minimizes segmentation artifacts, the model fails attention mechanisms to focus on tumor-relevant regions and surrounding tissues. The key features of the proposed work include: (i) a diffusion-enhanced refinement strategy that enhances boundary consistency without iterative diffusion overhead, (ii) an attention-guided feature learning framework highlight tumor-relevant regions, and (iii) a treatment-oriented segmentation design validated using patient-wise cross-validation. These contributions improve robustness and clinical reliability over conventional U-Net based approaches.

title: Operational Efficiency Clustering Analysis of Wind Farms in Peru Using Multidimensional Performance Metrics
authors: Elmer Arellanos-Tafur
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: This research presents a comprehensive analysis of wind energy generation efficiency clustering in Peru using multidimensional performance metrics. The research analyzes operational data from eight major wind energy companies spanning from May 2023 to April 2025, encompassing a total generation of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6,876.3 \text{GWh}$</tex>. The methodology employs clustering algorithms based on four key performance indicators: total generation capacity, capacity factor, operational variability, and efficiency index. Results reveal three distinct operational clusters: high-efficiency operators (Parque Eólico Tres Hermanas and Parque Eólico Marcona) with capacity factors exceeding 55%; medium-efficiency operators (Enel Generación Peru, Energía Eólica, and Energía Renovable del Sur) with capacity factors between 25% and 32%; and high-volume operators (Engie, GR Taruca, and GR Paino) with lower efficiency but higher absolute generation. The clustering analysis demonstrates significant operational disparities, with variability coefficients ranging from 43.3% to 109.8%, while seasonal patterns show peak generation during autumn months (March-May) with average monthly outputs of 270 GWh. The research findings provide valuable insights for optimizing wind farm operations, strategic planning, and policy development in Peru's renewable energy sector, contributing to the understanding of regional wind energy performance patterns and establishing benchmarks for future wind farm developments in similar geographical and climatic conditions.

title: AI-Optimized Sensor Network and Signal Processing Model for Advanced Manufacturing and Green Energy Applications
authors: Sarvaree Bano, Priyanka Gupta
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The Study discusses the application of AI optimization of sensor networks and signal processing models to achieve efficiency and sustainability in advanced manufacturing and green energy systems. This research investigates how artificial intelligence-powered tools improve efficiency in monitoring equipment by enabling rapid predictions about its condition and enhancing overall functionality. In the production stage, the document presents the application of artificial intelligence methods, such as supervised, unsupervised, and reinforcement learning, in predictive maintenance plans in order to minimise equipment breakdowns by ensuring that the imminent failure of machines is detected in time. In the context of sustainable technology, artificial intelligence can be used to improve the operation of renewable energy stations such as photovoltaic arrays and hydroelectric power plants by providing real-time adjustments to the existing activities based on instant measurements of the environment, thereby increasing the overall performance of the facilities and reducing the wastage of resources. The approach entails selecting suitable sensors and data collection techniques, and designing advanced signal-processing models for noise elimination, feature removal, and anomaly detection. The performance of the AI algorithms used can be tested, with the AI algorithms proving better at predicting uptime and energy generation than traditional algorithms. The results indicate that adopting AI leads to high operational efficiency, cost reduction, and sustainability. The paper concludes with implications for the two industries, recommendations for future research, and the adoption of AI-driven technologies. The paper can bring a lot of lessons on why AI is important to simplify sensor networks to promote smarter and greener industrial practices.

title: Evaluating Context-Aware Scoring and Supervised Classification Methods for Toxicity Detection in Social Media Comments
authors: Nayan Adhikari, Sayan Duari, Debdut Parali, Soumyadeep Maity, Siddhartha Chatterjee
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: The heavy usage of social media platforms has led to a massive increase in toxic, abusive, and offensive comments. Detecting such content is particularly difficult in multilingual and code-mixed settings such as Hinglish, where comments often contain informal language, slang, emojis, and expressive punctuation. Traditional supervised machine learning models, including logistic regression, support vector machines, and Naive Bayes classifiers, are widely used for toxicity detection; however, these approaches depend primarily on statistical patterns and may struggle with ambiguity, leading to higher false-positive rates. This paper presents a comparative study between traditional supervised machine learning models and a context-aware rulebased approach called the Score-based Toxicity Classification Algorithm (STC-Algo). STC-Algo assigns severity weights to abusive terms and incorporates contextual modifiers to compute an interpretable toxicity score using predefined thresholds. Our experiment shows that STC-Algo gives 82.9 % accuracy in predicting the toxicity classes.

title: Bundle-Based Adaptive Dynamic PSO for Spectral Variability-Aware Endmember Extraction in Hyperspectral Imagery
authors: Linpei Yang, Lizhe Wang, Ruyi Feng
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: The inherent spectral variability in hyperspectral imagery significantly compromises the accuracy of traditional endmember extraction and spectral unmixing. To address this critical challenge, this paper proposes a novel endmember bundle extraction method based on Adaptive Dynamic Particle Swarm Optimization (ADPSO). The proposed ADPSO framework effectively handles spectral variability by integrating an adaptive mutation strategy for dynamic optimization. Experimental evaluations on the Urban dataset demonstrate the exceptional performance of our method. The results confirm that the proposed ADPSO achieves favorable outcomes in both SAD and RMSE metrics, providing an effective and robust solution for endmember bundle extraction, which offers valuable insights for addressing spectral variability issues.

title: Interpretable Sentinel-Converted Hyperspectral Data Using Deep Image Prior and Spatiospectral Super-Resolution Deep Unfolding
authors: Chia-Hsiang Lin, Jui-Ting Chen, Shih-Min Hsu
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: Sentinel-2 satellite offers global spatial coverage with high revisiting frequency, while its spectral information is not as sufficient as hyperspectral images. Sentinel-2 images have only 12 spectral bands with varying spatial resolutions of 10, 20, and 60 meters. This work aims at spectrally super-resolving Sentinel-2 into hyperspectral images. Existing spectral super-resolution methods could only achieve 31-band CAVE-level hyperspectral data generation over the RGB spectral range. This is far from being applicable for accurate object identification, and does not meet current standard of remote sensing specifications that require the critical near-infrared (NIR) spectral information. Thus, we aim at converting the multi-resolution Sentinel-2 data into NASA's high-standard AVIRIS-level hyperspectral images. This ambitious technique can be elegantly done by a simple deep image prior formulation, solved by a trickily deployed deep unfolding network. Our interpretable spatiospectral deep unfolding network (iSDUN) is not only user-friendly (just requiring users to directly input the multi-resolution Sentinel-2 data without needing to unify the spatial resolutions across the 12 bands), but also returns high-quality 172-band hyperspectral images over RGB/NIR ranges with uniformly high spatial resolution of 10 meters. The efficacy of iSDUN is comprehensively verified across diverse land types.

title: Improving Software Testing with RAG-Based LLMs and Supporting Indian Regional Languages
authors: Carol Joanna Bright M, Kripaa P, Karishma S, Freida Judith I, Usama Abdur Rahman
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Automated generation of software test cases are one of the crucial tasks in modern software engineering, while the traditional method of manually writing test cases is time-consuming, prone to making errors and often fails to achieve contextual or linguistic accuracy. This paper proposes a Retrieval-Augmented Generation based method, which integrates dynamic knowledge retrieval with LLMs to generate highly faithful and contextually grounded test cases. Unlike general fine-tuned LLMs, which require extensive retraining, the proposed RAG pipeline uses lanceDB to store domain related knowledge and SentenceTransformers to semantically retrieve knowledge from the database, thus offering more reliability with much lower computational cost. Besides this, the suggested pipeline allows multilingual test case generation done by the LLM model across English and other Indian regional languages like Hindi, Tamil, Telugu, Malayalam and Kannada making software test more inclusive for language sensitive software ecosystems. The generated test cases are observed to be semantically aligned with the retrieved context and real-world user stories thus improving factual consistency and software reliability. Experimental evaluation shows consistent faithfulness scores, showing the effectiveness of the RAG pipeline and LLMs generation. Results confirm the embeddings quality, retrieval accuracy and reasoning capability within the model. Also, the architecture can be optionally extended for user feedback-based continual learning by integrating it with QA platforms such as Jira via Model Context Protocol (MCP) which gives the possibility to modify the model as per the project requirements. Overall, this study shows that RAG based models could provide scalable, multilingual and intelligent automation in future software quality assurance.

title: Modeling Lightweight XOR-APUF with Improved Resilience to Machine Learning Attacks
authors: Koushik B., Raahul L.S., Sreegayathri K.K., Mohankumar N.
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: In this modern world, increasing hardware attacks and insecure key storage vulnerabilities have made traditional security approaches unreliable. Physical Unclonable Functions (PUFs) provide a robust and hardware-rooted mechanism for achieving strong security and reliability. Physical Unclonable Function exploits inherent manufacturing variation to provide a unique device fingerprint, offering a lightweight solution for secure authentication and key generation for resource-constrained systems. To evaluate robustness, seven machine learning models were trained on XOR APUF CRPs. Our design prediction rate ranges between <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(50 \%-57 \%)$</tex> indicates that the PUF is highly secure against the modeled attack, remaining near random guessing. In this work, we have proposed an enhanced XOR APUF design that is resistant against machine learning-based attacks while maintaining high uniqueness, uniformity, and reliability of the design. The Proposed APUF has achieved 45.62 % uniqueness, 94.00 % reliability, and 45.00 % uniformity, whereas the XOR APUF attains 49.71 % uniqueness, 98.04 % reliability, and 50.02 % uniformity, demonstrating stability and high random distribution. The proposed XOR APUF demonstrates high randomness and scalability, making it suitable for large-scale IoT deployment.

title: Diagnosing Java Memory Leaks and Performance Bottlenecks: A Machine Learning-Driven Approach to Caching Optimization
authors: Srikanth Reddy Gudi
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Java Virtual Machine (JVM) applications are critical to enterprise solutions, and for good reason: they are resilient, portable, and scalable. However, performance bottlenecks still exist and one of the most important reasons is due to memory leaks and inefficient caching. Traditional profiling tools like VisualVM, JConsole etc are reactive, meaning many times the intervention is manual and adjustments to dynamic runtime requirement are not very flexible. This work presents novel, machine learning based approach to detect JVM memory leaks and optimize the cache intelligently in real-time. It uses telemetry data such as heap usage, frequency of garbage collection, object allocation rates, and performance measures for caches. On a synthetic dataset of a JVM with simulated known anomalies, we evaluated four models—Autoencoder, Isolation Forest, One-Class SVM, and Random Forest. ROC-AUC score value of 0.9820 and 0.9868 were achieved by the Autoencoder among the Random Forest model, which shown excellent classification performance. The Autoencoder demonstrated useful in unsupervised scenarios, effectively pinpointing subtle behavioral anomalies in the use of memory. The framework provides a fine-grained anomaly identification and serves as a starting point of reinforcement learning based automatic cache leakage elimination. Our results highlight the disruptive potential of machine learning in diagnosing JVM-level problems, reducing downtime, and improving application performance. The approach is scalable, flexible and and suitable to integrate into DevOps observability for intelligent Java applications oversight. The code is available on the GitHub repository https://github.com/Srikanthrgudi/Diagnosing-Java-Memory-Leaks-and-Performance-

title: A Compact Frequency Reconfigurable Hybrid Monopole-Patch Antenna for Wideband Applications
authors: Lujain F Al-Hunity, Raneem A. Al-Mashni, Yanal S. Faouri
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: This paper introduces a compact frequency- reconfigurable wideband antenna for ultrawideband (UWB) adaptive and spectrum-aware applications. The antenna inherently operates as a wideband (WB) radiator, where frequency reconfiguration is realized by shifting the wideband operating region across the UWB spectrum, enabling full UWB coverage. The proposed antenna was fabricated on a low-cost FR-4 substrate with compact dimensions of 20 mm × 60 mm × 1.6 mm. Frequency tuning is implemented through two PIN diodes, which alter the surface current paths and the effective electrical length of the radiator. Stable impedance matching and reliable radiation performance were preserved across the entire reconfiguration range. Owing to its compact size, simple structure, and continuous wideband frequency reconfigurability, the proposed antenna can be considered a suitable candidate for cognitive radio systems and reconfigurable wireless communication platforms that require efficient and flexible spectrum utilization.

title: Real-Time Touchless Human Computer Interaction Using Hand Gesture–Based Virtual Keyboard and Mouse
authors: P. Priyanka, Jaddu Hemalatha, Saikamal Suro, Akkalapotu Surya Pavithra, Tedlapu Sanjuna Sharmista
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Recent developments in computer vision and machine learning has significantly interact with computers in so many natural ways. This study describes the devel- opment of low-cost virtual keyboard and mouse and it works through hand gestures by using OpenCV, MediaPipe and PyAuto Gui for real-time hand landmark tracking. By eliminating the need for traditional input devices, it provides completely touch-free interaction, which is mainly used for people having mobility impairments and it is also beneficial for the environments where hygiene is important. The main feature of the system is its support for multilingual typing in English, Hindi, Telugu,Tamil, and Kannada, helping accessiblility in different regions. Experimental evaluation shows that the system achieves a gesture recognition accuracy of 87 % with an average response time of about 0.4 seconds. The system runs by using a standard webcam and making it practical for everyday use. This work supports to develop an user- friendly human- computer interaction technologies.

title: Explainable Machine Learning for Liver Cancer Prediction Using SMOTE and SHAP Analysis
authors: Roshni Rajendran, Jeena Joseph, DivyaLakshmi S
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: Liver cancer is among the deadliest cancers in the world as it is mostly diagnosed at late stages and the high-risk patients are not detected at an early age. To enhance clinical decision support, it is thus of importance to develop reliable prediction systems. In this paper, a prediction system of liver cancer has been introduced that incorporates three complementary elements namely Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) to correct the issue of class imbalance, Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) to make predictions and SHapley Additive explanations (SHAP) to explain the results. The data set included 5,000 records of patients that contained demographic, lifestyle, clinical, and biomarker variables. Balancing of the minority group of cancer cases was done by SMOTE to ensure that there was fair representation in the model training. XGBoost was selected as it is efficient and has the capacity to incorporate nonlinear interactions of features, whereas SHAP generated both patient-level and global prediction explanations. High predictive performance was observed in terms of accuracy of 98, F1-score of 0.95 in cancer cases, and general ROC-AUC of 0.997 was observed through experimental evaluation. Interpretability analysis also showed that age, history of cirrhosis, and alphafetoprotein were also some of the most effective predictors, which are consistent with the clinical risk factors. The predictive accuracy and transparency provide an insight into the possibility of the presented system to aid in the early recognition of liver cancer and promote the trust in the AI driven healthcare solutions.
