title: Global Sensitivity Analysis of Whole-Body Physiologically Based Pharmacokinetic Model Parameters in [Lu]Lu-PSMA-I&#x0026;T Radioligand Therapy: Key Determinants of Individual Kidney and Tumor Absorbed Dose Uncertainty and Implications for Digital Twin Development_supp2-3690503.pdf
authors: Deni Hardiansyah
publish: N/A
abstract: Understanding how uncertainties in physiological parameters propagate into the uncertainty of estimated absorbed doses (eADs) is desirable for optimizing individualized radiopharmaceutical therapy (RPT). In this study, we applied global sensitivity analysis (GSA) to a published whole-body physiologically-based pharmacokinetic (PBPK) model, developed using biokinetic data from 13 patients treated with [<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">177</sup>Lu]Lu-PSMA-I&T, to identify influential parameters on the individual eAD uncertainty. Model inputs included patient-specific parameters such as receptor densities (R₀), blood flow densities (f), and release rates (λ) across relevant organs, with their uncertainties and correlations incorporated. A GSA method extended for dependent parameters (GSADPK) was employed to compute main effects (S<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</sub>) and total effects (S<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Ti</sub>), with kidney and tumor eADs as the outputs of interest. Overall, kidney eADs showed lower uncertainty than tumors. For kidneys, receptor density emerged as the dominant contributor (S<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</sub>, median [range] = 0.40 [0.10–0.58]), followed by blood flow density (S<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</sub> = 0.27 [0.12–0.48]). In tumors, receptor density was also found as the primary determinant (S<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</sub> = 0.42 [0.02–0.80]), with tumor release rate as a secondary contributor (S<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</sub> = 0.04 [0.00–0.67]). These findings highlight that accurate estimation of receptor density and blood flow density could substantially reduce eAD uncertainty. By identifying the physiological parameters most critical for dose prediction, this work provides a foundation for digital twin–based frameworks.

title: CSCMTrack: Cross-Scale Correlation Modeling for Event-based UAV Tracking
authors: Yuchen Zhao, Xianda Xu, Yisa Zhang, Yang Feng, Shilong Jing, Hengyi Lv
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: With the rapid emergence of the low-altitude economy, the deployment scale of unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) systems has also experienced substantial growth. This evolution toward airspace intelligence profoundly empowering traditional industrial paradigms, while introducing significant challenges to aerial security. Specifically, UAV object tracking still faces three major issues: 1) large variations in target scale, severe viewpoint changes, and background clutter hinder the clear capture of dynamic characteristics; 2) mainstream event representation methods lack spatiotemporal expressiveness, failing to reconstruct finer texture details; 3) prevailing object tracking frameworks suffer from redundant architectures and struggle to establish long-range dependencies between semantic information. In this paper, we construct the first event-based single object tracking dataset for UAVs (UAVEOT Dataset), providing a benchmark platform for tracking algorithms in challenging scenarios. Furthermore, we provide a novel event representation called Chaotic Sequence Encoder (CSE), which jointly employs temporal sequences, temporal entropy, and temporal weighting to convert asynchronous event streams into dense event tensors. Additionally, we propose a backbone named Hybrid State Space Attention (HSSA) network, incorporating three designed modules (e.g., Cross scale Fusion, Local Self Attention, and Global State Space), introducing state space duality into the tracking framework firstly, enabling efficient global spatiotemporal sequence modeling. Finally, extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed tracker CSCMTrack achieves significant performance on the UAVEOT and VisEvent datasets, outperforming all other state-of-the-art tracking frameworks.

title: Superconducting Persistent Current Switch for Controlling the Helium Free Magnetic System of 1.5 T MRI
authors: V.S. Vysotsky, V.V. Zubko, S.S. Fetisov, A.A. Nosov, S.Yu. Zanegin, D.S. Kaverin, L.V. Potanina, K.S. Marinin, A.V. Glushaev, O.A. Kovalchuk
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: We developed a superconducting persistent current switch adapted to a 1.5 T MRI magnetic system. The superconducting switch is designed for a helium-free cooling system, relying solely on thermal conductivity for cooling, which is a notable shift from previous designs that utilized helium cooling. The switch must accommodate two operational modes: standard current charge/discharge during ∼4 hours and the emergency mode with current discharge not more than 1.5 hours with the strict demands to the heat release from the PCS.. The switch features a copper frame and a stainless-steel bushing, which contribute to its structural integrity and thermal management. The design and analysis of the switch were conducted using a numerical model based on the finite element method, which allows detailed digital simulation of thermal behavior under various conditions. The results indicate a calculated temperature distribution within the switch during both operation modes. Calculations were verified by tests and with the modified stainless-steel support the persistent current switch developed demonstrated heat release-cooling conditions demanded and coincided with new calculations. The development of this helium-free superconducting persistent mode switches demonstrates significant progress in superconducting technologies for MRI systems. Details of simulations and test results of the persistent current switches developed are presented.

title: Hybrid Architecture Gets Fluid: A New Paradigm for Direction-of-arrival Estimation in 6G Networks
authors: Ye Tian, Jiaji Ren, Tuo Wu, Wei Liu, Maged Elkashlan, Matthew C. Valenti, Naofal Al-Dhahir, Hing Cheung So
publish: IEEE Transactions on Signal Processing
abstract: High-precision direction-of-arrival (DOA) estimation, as a key sensing capability for 6G-enabled applications such as autonomous driving and extended reality, is increasingly dependent on the effective exploitation of spatial degrees of freedom (DOFs). This paper integrates two frontier DOFs-oriented paradigms and proposes a fluid antenna-enabled hybrid analog-digital (FA-HAD) architecture, which features an extremely lightweight front-end configuration mechanism and efficient spatial DOFs exploitation. Within this architecture, a collaborative spatial-phase sampling strategy is first developed to enable real-time 2-D DOA estimation under compressive observations, and a single-source CRLB analysis is provided to quantify the achievable performance limit, offering quantitative guidance for accuracy-overhead trade-offs. Furthermore, an efficient virtual-array spatial covariance matrix reconstruction method is proposed to recover a physically meaningful covariance representation, thereby providing a covariance-domain interface that is directly reusable by a broad class of existing covariancebased array processing and array design techniques, which strengthens the scalability and transferability of the proposed architecture. Building upon the reconstructed SCM, a Jacobi-Anger expansion based dimension-reduced MUSIC estimator is further derived for arbitrary planar arrays with a favorable computational cost. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed FA-HAD framework attains DOA accuracy close to fully digital systems while substantially reducing RF hardware complexity and training overhead.

title: Resilient Prescribed-Time Event-Triggered Formation Control for Multiple Spacecraft: A Hierarchical Approach
authors: Han Gao, Yuhan Bi, Yingjie Pan, Bing Cui, Yuan Zhang, Yuanqing Xia
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: This article investigates the resilient formation control problem for multiple spacecraft considering unknown communication link faults and system dynamics under the directed communication graph topology. Motivated by this challenge, a hierarchical prescribed-time spacecraft formation control scheme is formulated. With the aim of acquiring the reference command for each follower, an adaptive prescribed-time event-triggered observer is developed at the communication layer. Compared with the existing results, the proposed observer guarantees reliable information exchange among formation members under communication link faults, while effectively reducing the communication frequency. In the formation layer, the fuzzy logic system (FLS) based distributed prescribed time controller is developed utilizing the reconstructed reference command, in which the FLSs are employed to handle both the estimation errors and the unmodeled dynamics. By virtue of graph theory and Lyapunov stability theory, the practical prescribed time stability is rigorously demonstrated. The performance of the developed framework is ultimately verified through numerical simulations.

title: An AI-Driven Low-Complexity SVD Approximation Method for Massive MIMO Communications
authors: Zihan He, Junling Li, Xiaoyu Chen, Xueke Dong, Chen Huang, Cheng-Xiang Wang
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: Singular value decomposition (SVD) is fundamental to channel decoupling and transmission optimization in massive multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) systems. To reduce the computational complexity associated with conventional SVD, we propose an artificial intelligence (AI)-driven SVD method. The proposed method consists of three modules. A feature extraction module is first employed to learn channel features using different deep learning algorithms. Orthogonality refinement and implicit singular value calculation modules are then used for constraint enforcement and approximation, respectively. This modular design preserves mathematical fidelity compared with other baselines. The proposed convolutional neural network (CNN)-based algorithm together with the remaining modules offers the best trade-off between accuracy and efficiency.

title: Cross-City Traffic Prediction via Self-Supervised Graph Learning
authors: Yingqian Zhang, Chao Li, Yi Fang, Shibo He, Jiming Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Traffic prediction plays a critical role in intelligent transportation systems. Recently, data-driven methods for traffic prediction have become increasingly popular and have outperformed traditional approaches in terms of effectiveness. However, the data scarcity problem that arises from inadequate sensing and data storage infrastructure in underdeveloped urban areas and dilapidated districts impedes the deployment of existing deep learning-based models, which typically require substantial training data. To address these issues, we propose a novel self-supervised graph learning-based cross-city learning framework (SSGL-CTP) for traffic prediction across cities. The framework first pretrains a transferable spatial feature extractor on a data-abundant source city and then adapts it to the target city through parameter transfer, without requiring direct cross-city data sharing. Specifically, the framework combines a dynamic trajectory-transfer graph and an origin-destination graph to model short-range and long-range spatial-temporal dependencies, and learns transferable representations through masked node recovery before target-city fine-tuning. The effectiveness of SSGL-CTP is validated through extensive experiments utilizing real-world taxi data from Chengdu and Wenzhou, China. These experiments demonstrate its superiority over contemporary baseline methods, with improvements of up to 9.2%.

title: Optimizing Information Freshness in Covert Digital Twin-enabled Smart Grids: A Full-Duplex Jamming Approach
authors: Yuanai Xie, Xingxing Ran, Haiyan Zhao, Zan Zhou, Bo Zhang, Zhixin Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Smart Grid
abstract: The proliferation of Digital Twin (DT) in community-level smart grids makes data exchange between physical entity (i.e., Data Collection Points, DCPs) and the virtual Utility Node (UN) more frequent, and thus vulnerable to interception by potential Eavesdroppers (Eves), which imposes stringent requirements on both data freshness and transmission security. To address this, we propose a covert communication framework utilizing a full-duplex UN that emits artificial noise to obscure uplink transmissions from a potential fixed Eve. To ensure timely information updates and cyber-physical synchronization within the DT architecture, an average Peak Age-of-Information (PAoI) minimization problem is formulated under constraints on covertness, link reliability, total energy, and maximum power. Given the channel randomness, the closed-form minimum detection error rate is derived based on binary hypothesis testing. Then, we utilize the channel’s statistical distribution to convert the probabilistic reliability constraint into a deterministic form. Since the problem is proven to be a convex optimization problem, a distributed iterative algorithm is developed based on the Lagrange dual decomposition and the Karush–Kuhn–Tucker (KKT) conditions. Furthermore, we establish a rigorous theoretical proof of the solution’s uniqueness and optimality by employing the standard interference function framework, the Descartes’ rule of signs, and the intermediate value theorem. Finally, extensive numerical simulations demonstrate the proposed algorithm reduces the average PAoI by 34% and 49.2% compared to baseline strategies while lowering energy consumption by 14.8%, validating its effectiveness in securing DT-enabled community-level smart grids.

title: Modelling of Misalignment, Surface Deviations, and Thermal Deformation in Optics of Submillimetre Wave Instrument on Juice
authors: Mikko Kotiranta, Daniel Sved, Paul Hartogh, Axel Murk
publish: IEEE Transactions on Terahertz Science and Technology
abstract: The Submillimetre Wave Instrument (SWI) is a passive dual-beam heterodyne radiometer on the Jupiter Icy Moons Explorer (Juice) spacecraft that operates in the frequency bands 530 – 630 GHz and 1066 – 1280 GHz. This paper discusses the misalignment, surface deviations, and thermal deformation related to its optics and how they are included into the optical model of the instrument. The misalignment and surface deviations of the SWI protoflight model that are caused by the manufacturing and assembly tolerances were measured with a coordinate measurement machine. Thermoelastic deformation of the optics that is subjected to typical operational temperatures ranging from -70°C to -130°C is determined with finite element method simulations. The optical performance of the instrument is then simulated for several telescope pointing angles using the optical model containing the above-mentioned imperfections. The effect of each imperfection is investigated also separately.

title: Design and Analysis of an Energy-Selective Surface with Ultra-Wide Absorption Band for High-Power Protection
authors: Jia-Min Wu, Lin Wang, Wei-Chao Yang
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper presents an energy-selective surface (ESS) with high-power microwave protection and electromagnetic stealth capabilities. The proposed ESS is composed of an absorptive layer and a selective layer. By embedding resistive elements, the absorptive layer achieves bimodal resonant characteristics and ultra-wideband microwave absorption performance. The selective layer integrated with PIN diodes achieves adaptive electromagnetic protection performance. Under low-power signal incidence, the ESS operates in transmission mode and achieves effective microwave absorption within 7.8–23.18 GHz. For high-power microwave irradiation, the shielding effectiveness (SE) of the designed structure is greater than 10 dB across 0.1–24.1 GHz. Meanwhile, the absorptivity remains above 90% in the 7.5–22.9 GHz band, covering X-band to K-band microwave frequencies. Experimental measurements are consistent with simulated results under both low- and high-power conditions. In transmission mode, the ESS achieves an insertion loss (IL) of 0.98 dB at 2.05 GHz. Furthermore, the SE exceeds 30 dB under high-power incidence within the operating frequency band. These results demonstrate that the designed ESS possesses excellent potential for electromagnetic stealth and high-power electromagnetic protection applications.

title: Task-Generalizable Deep Joint Source-Channel Coding via Hierarchical Masked Autoencoders
authors: Simin Dai, Xuechen Chen, Xiaoheng Deng, Siyu Lin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Deep learning-based joint source-channel coding (DeepJSCC) has demonstrated significant potential for task-oriented semantic communication. However, existing DeepJSCC frameworks exhibit limited generalization across multi-tasks. This limitation stems from bandwidth compression, which results a trade-off between preserving details and capturing semantics. To overcome this challenge, we propose a novel Task-Generalizable DeepJSCC framework, called TGDJSCC. In the pre-training phase, we devise a hierarchical masked autoencoder (HMAE) to learn hierarchical representations within bandwidth-constrained latent spaces. Specifically, we introduce a novel content-aware tree for establishing hierarchy, along with a dual self-supervised mask image modeling scheme to learn transferable hierarchical representations. During fine-tuning, a task-adaptive feature selection module dynamically routes hierarchical features according to specific task requirements. This enables efficient adaptation to new tasks with only a few fine-tuning epochs for both pixel-level and semantic-level tasks, eliminating the need for full retraining. Comprehensive experiments demonstrate that TGDJSCC significantly surpasses existing DeepJSCC methods in task generalization, while maintaining communication efficiency under stringent bandwidth constraints.

title: PureFL: Trust-Weighted Federated Learning with Noise-Resilient Homomorphic Encryption for Blockchain-Based IoV Networks
authors: Hope Leticia Nakayiza, Love Allen Chijioke Ahakonye, Dong-Seong Kim, Jae Min Lee
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Federated learning (FL) in the Internet of Vehicles (IoV) networks is challenged by privacy vulnerabilities, model poisoning attacks, and the limitations of centralized aggregation, which threaten security and scalability. While homomorphic encryption (HE) provides an additional layer of privacy, existing schemes often suffer from cumulative noise growth, resulting in degraded model accuracy and increased inference latency, critical factors for real-time vehicular safety applications. This paper presents PureFL, a novel FL framework that combines adaptive Cheon-Kim-Kim-Song (CKKS) homomorphic encryption with a custom permissioned blockchain (PureChain), utilizing a proof of authority and association (PoA<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) consensus mechanism. PureFL incorporates dynamic precision scaling to manage noise accumulation, ensuring computational efficiency without sacrificing privacy or accuracy. The framework also implements a trust-based aggregation mechanism based on cosine-similarity trust scoring, which dynamically weights client contributions according to historical reliability and anomaly detection, thereby enhancing resilience against model poisoning. Experimentation results on the CICIOV2024 and 5G-NIDD datasets demonstrate that PureFL achieves superior anomaly detection accuracy of 99.9%, reduces CKKS HE overhead via precision scaling, and that the proposed PoA<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> consensus outperforms other consensus mechanisms in terms of throughput and latency, making it suitable for real-time IoV operations.

title: Problem-Specific Knowledge and Learning-Based Meta-Heuristics for On-Demand Food Delivery Scheduling_supp1-3688234.docx
authors: Kaizhou Gao
publish: N/A
abstract: With the increase of orders and riders, how to allocate orders to riders appropriately and complete delivery timely are huge challenges in on-demand food delivery (OFD). This work focuses on the multi-objective on-demand food delivery scheduling problems (OFDSP) with minimizing the total delay time of all orders and the total traveling distance of all riders simultaneously. First, both order-based mathematical model and a location-based mathematical model are established for the concerned problems, respectively Then, three basic algorithms (differential evolution (DE), particle swarm optimization (PSO), and artificial bee colony (ABC)) are improved to solve OFDSP. Combining order and rider information, a double string encoding method is designed to represent candidate solutions. A multi-constraint satisfying initialization strategy is proposed to avoid infeasible solutions. Seven problem-specific knowledge-based local search operators (KLSOs) are developed to speed up the convergence of the algorithms. Third, two reinforcement learning (RL) algorithms with two Q-table design strategies are constructed, yielding a total of four RL-based strategies for local search operator selection during iterations. Finally, 30 instances from real scenarios are solved to verify the performance of the proposed algorithms. The two developed mathematical models are verified and compared. The effectiveness of the improvement strategies is validated in the respective algorithm groups. Furthermore, the algorithms with the best performance in the respective groups are compared to the two advanced OFD algorithms. The results and comparisons show that the ABC_LS_Q1 is most competitive among all compared algorithms.

title: From Zero to Detail: A Progressive Spectral Decoupling Paradigm for UHD Image Restoration with New Benchmark
authors: Chen Zhao, Yunzhe Xu, Zhizhou Chen, Enxuan Gu, Kai Zhang, Xiaoming Liu, Jian Yang, Ying Tai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Ultra-high-definition (UHD) image restoration poses unique challenges due to the high spatial resolution, diverse content, and fine-grained structures present in UHD images. To address these issues, we introduce a progressive spectral decomposition for the restoration process, decomposing it into three stages: zero-frequency enhancement, low-frequency restoration, and high-frequency refinement. Based on this formulation, we propose a novel framework, ERR, which integrates three cooperative sub-networks: the zero-frequency enhancer (ZFE), the low-frequency restorer (LFR), and the high-frequency refiner (HFR). The ZFE incorporates global priors to learn holistic mappings, the LFR reconstructs the main content by focusing on coarse-scale information, and the HFR adopts our proposed frequency-windowed Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (FW-KAN) to recover fine textures and intricate details for high-fidelity restoration. To further advance research in UHD image restoration, we also construct a large-scale, high-quality benchmark dataset, LSUHDIR, comprising 82,126 UHD images with diverse scenes and rich content. Our proposed methods demonstrate superior performance across a range of UHD image restoration tasks, and extensive ablation studies confirm the contribution and necessity of each module. Project page: https://github.com/NJU-PCALab/ERR.

title: FDSOI Based Cryogenic Circuit Performance Enhancement Using Back Biasing and Threshold Voltage Engineering
authors: Tapas Dutta, Fikru Adamu-Lema, Djamel Bensouiah, German Cherstvov, Plamen Asenov, Asen Asenov
publish: IEEE Journal of the Electron Devices Society
abstract: In this work, we use predictive cryogenic spice based compact models derived using a process design kit re-centering approach for 22 nm FDSOI technology to analyze the impact of back-gate biasing on circuit performance. We focus on analysis of power-delay trade-offs while varying the supply voltage at room and cryogenic temperature (4K). We show that back-biasing is necessary to mitigate the effects of the higher threshold voltages observed at cryogenic temperature. We further show that simple “threshold voltage engineering” has the potential to provide much better performance, compared to room temperature.

title: Economical Ultra-Large-Capacity MMC with Non-Uniform Switching Devices and Its Optimized Design Methodology
authors: Yunfei Wang, Xiaobin Zhao, Qiang Song, Qingming Xin, Zhengxuan Li, Junjie Feng, Wenhua Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: With the increasing capacity of modular multilevel converters (MMCs) for integrating ultra-large-scale renewable energy bases, their high cost and need for excessively high-current rating switches become increasingly prominent. Given the unidirectional power flow in large-scale renewable power transmission applications, this paper proposes non-uniform switching device submodules (NUS-SMs) and an NUS-SM-based hybrid MMC (NUS-HYB-MMC). By utilizing the unidirectional dc bias in arm currents, this MMC design enables half of switching devices to employ significantly lower current ratings while eliminating the need for less mature high-current-rated devices in the remaining switches. This study further demonstrates that the NUS-HYB-MMC design can be optimized through two key approaches: extending linear modulation range by utilizing negative voltage states (NVSs) in additional submodules, and implementing circulating current injection to flexibly distribute current stress. Additionally, the energy storage requirement and control strategy of the NUS-HYB-MMC are also analyzed. Furthermore, to minimize overall costs, a comprehensive design methodology is developed for the NUS-HYB-MMC that jointly optimizes negative voltage state utilization and circulating current injection factors. Through the 10 GW case studies, the cost advantages of the NUS-HYB-MMC are analyzed and compared. Finally, simulations and experiments verify the effectiveness of both the proposed NUS-HYB-MMC and its optimized design methods.

title: Telekinetic UI: Using Unconscious Facial Micro-motions forIntent-based Cursor Control
authors: Genki Akimoto, Shuto Takashita, Hiroto Saito, Masahiko Inami
publish: Proceedings of the Augmented Humans International Conference 2026
abstract: None

title: A Fabric-Aware Intelligent Washing Machine Using Multi-Input Fuzzy Logic Control
authors: Gautami G. Shingan, Priyanka N. Jadhav, Amol M. Jagtap, Prashant P Nagre, Tejas N Ghule
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: This paper presents an intelligent washing machine model based on an optimized fuzzy logic controller (FLC) for improving washing efficiency and adaptability. The proposed system integrates seven input parameters, including dirt type, dirt degree, fabric type, cloth color, load quantity, detergent type, and stain sensitivity, to represent real-world laundry conditions. Using a Mamdani-type fuzzy inference system with triangular membership functions and centroid defuzzification, the controller dynamically determines washing duration, water temperature, detergent amount, and spin speed. Compared with conventional and baseline fuzzy controllers, the proposed model provides smoother control behavior, improved fabric protection, and enhanced resource utilization. Simulation-based evaluation and test case analysis demonstrate an overall accuracy improvement of 78.24 % along with reduced energy and detergent consumption. The results confirm that the proposed fuzzy logic-based approach offers an effective and practical solution for intelligent and sustainable household appliance automation.

title: A Novel Walsh Encoding Scheme for Efficient ATPG in Embedded Processors
authors: A.Abinaya A. Abinaya, Radha N, S.Rajapriya, Babitha Lincy R, P.Malini, Jency Rubia J
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Reliable in-field testing has grown more and more important due to a growing number of chip failures after deployment. This study presents an innovative method for creating software-based self-test (SBST) routines for processor cores utilizing the Walsh encoding technique. The approach leverages execution traces obtained while running training programs to streamline the mapping process and extract functional constraints for internal component ports, thereby enabling gate-level structural test generation and automatic test instruction generation (ATIG), including for hidden control logic (HCL) with applied constraints. Experimental findings show that the proposed ATIG approach attains high structural fault coverage while producing compact test programs suitable for contemporary processors.

title: Beyond Administrative Compliance: Implementing a Service Quality Management System (SQMS) in the e-Rapor Platform for Madrasah
authors: Fathor Rozi, Farida Isroani, Halimatus Sadiyah, Saifuddin, Widhy Wahyani, Eny Nur Aisyah, Ahmad Zubaidi, Abu Hasan Agus R
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Education is a crucial sector in national development, but monitoring of education quality in Indonesia remains fragmented and lacks integrated quality standards. This study aims to develop and implement a Service Quality Management System (SQMS) within the e-Rapor platform to improve school performance monitoring in Madrasahs. The method used is a qualitative case study approach in Madrasahs that have adopted e-Rapor. Data were collected through interviews, observations, and documentation. The results show that integrating SQMS into e-Rapor has a positive impact on school management, teaching performance, and stakeholder satisfaction. Transparency, data-driven evaluation, and increased parental involvement strengthen the quality of education provided. This study contributes to the development of a service-quality-based school performance monitoring model integrated with e-Rapor, an innovative step toward improving the effectiveness of management and teaching in Indonesia. Further implementation requires intensive training and support to maximize the system's use.

title: Designing a Secure Architecture for Web-Based 3D Shape Modelling
authors: Ali Abdallah, Yasmine Shaaban, Farah Yassine
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: The rapid advancement of web technologies has significantly transformed collaborative 3D modelling, enabling multiple users to create, modify, and share models in real time through online platforms. With the emerging Internet infrastructure, this shift has improved accessibility; however, sharing 3D models online exposes both system components and digital assets to unauthorized access, code tampering, and digital piracy; thus, security has become a critical concern. To address these challenges, a secure and flexible web-based 3D shape modelling architecture is introduced, integrating multiple layers of protection to ensure confidentiality, integrity, and controlled accessibility of these assets. This work reflects a client–server design and combines advanced rendering techniques with essential security mechanisms including code obfuscation to protect source code, user authentication and authorization to regulate access, and encrypted 3D models to mitigate data theft. Experimental results demonstrate that this proposed architecture improves security in online 3D modelling environments while maintaining high interactivity and performance, establishing a strong foundation for secure, scalable, and efficient collaborative 3D modelling applications.

title: Vehicle Detection and Tracking Using Thermal Cameras in Adverse Visibility Conditions
authors: Damodaran A R, Dhinesh Kumar R, Venkatesan S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Standard cameras really struggle when conditions get tough-think fog, rain, or darkness. That's why we built an AI-driven thermal system. It uses thermal cameras so it doesn't matter if it's misty, pouring, or pitch black, it still tracks cars like a hawk. For detection, we picked YOLOv5 since it's fast and reliable, and for tracking, Deep SORT keeps tabs on every vehicle as it moves through the frames. We wanted the model to hold up no matter what, so we trained it on a mix of open-source thermal datasets and over 20,000 images we collected ourselves. That blend makes it pretty robust, so it's not easily thrown off. And we didn't just stick to standard methods either-we used data augmentation, model ensembles, and made it even faster by optimizing with TensorRT. When we tested it, the system pulled off a mean average precision of 86.6%, and test-time augmentation really boosted that score. It's not picky about hardware either; it works in real time even on smaller devices like the Jetson Nano. Right now, we're working with discriminative deep learning, but we're itching to try generative AI next something like GAN-based domain adaptation. That should help the system better bridge the gap between thermal and RGB images, making detection even more spot-on.

title: A Robust Ensemble Framework for Brain Stroke Detection with Explainable Artificial Intelligence
authors: Nashin Tabassum, Md. Salah Uddin Yusuf
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Brain stroke is a severe neurological disorder caused by disrupted blood flow to the brain, leading to rapid cell death and major healthcare burdens. Early detection is vital to minimize risks and long-term damage, yet traditional diagnostic methods are often slow and complex. This study proposes an advanced stacking ensemble model for improved stroke detection using optimized feature sets and explainable AI (XAI) techniques. Data preprocessing included handling missing values, encoding, scaling, and balancing with SMOTE. Backward Feature Elimination (BFE) was applied to retain the most relevant features, reducing overfitting and improving performance. The stacking ensemble approach combines multiple ML models for more robust predictions, while SHAP, LIME, and ELI5 ensure model interpretability. The proposed model achieved 97.99% accuracy using optimized features set across multiple datasets, surpassing existing methods by offering both superior predictive power and clear interpretability through the integration of feature selection, ensemble learning, and XAI.

title: Energy Consumption Forecasting in Smart Agriculture
authors: Mohammad Bani-Doumi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: In the process of modernization of agriculture, in smart agriculture, optimizing energy consumption has become a crucial prerequisite for the sustainable operations of agriculture and reduction of the costs. In this study, we aim to enhance the robotics and energy efficiency, by developing a smart agricultural energy forecasting model based on real-time data and advanced machine learning techniques. Our approach integrates the analysis of energy requirements for various farming activities such as irrigation, greenhouse climate control, and automated machinery with the creation of predictive insights to enable efficient energy allocation. The energy forecasting model that we propose combines the historical data about energy consumption with environmental factors such as the weather and the crop growth phases to predict a more accurate answer. Such an approach can support the implementation of a federated learning framework that protects the data privacy of multiple farms while also improving the model on the whole. The outcomes of the application of the model indicate that the model could provide an energy service forecasting with very high accuracy, which in turn enabled farmers to take proactive measures to their energy consumption and subsequently resulted in the optimization of resource management together with the assistance of transition to usage of greener farming practices. This research can be identified as a valuable addition to the flourishing field of smart agriculture since it produces a scalable, privacy-preserving energy management solution.

title: AI Based Web Platform for Cyber-Fraud Prevention in Job Portals Using Machine Learning
authors: Boya Malleswari, Y. Indira Priyadarshini
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: The rapid expansion of online recruitment platforms has also led to a surge in false job postings. Cyber fraud pertaining to false job postings causes economic loss, pilferage of data, and emotional trauma for job seekers. In this regard, this paper proposes an AI-driven web platform that detects cyber-fraud in online job portals by conducting real-time advertisement screening. Quadratic Discriminant Analysis serves as the prime classifier for this model as it can capture non-linear boundaries and different covariance patterns across job-related features. A dataset consisting of fraudulent and non-fraudulent job postings was pre-processed using text normalization, feature extraction, and feature selection, and the resultant selected features were then used to train the classifier. Experimental results prove the efficiency of the QDA classifier in terms of precision and recall, showcasing high performance even under noisy and imbalanced data conditions. The developed model has been hosted on a web interface, which allows users to instantly check job advertisements and fetch a trust-score output. To summarize, this work offers a practical and scalable solution for safeguarding job seekers against recruitment-based cyber scams and enhancing security in digital employment ecosystems.

title: Automated Heart Disease Detection from ECG Images using a Hybrid Machine Learning Framework for Accurate Clinical Decision Support
authors: Paramati Chandini, Chityala Mahith, Yedhunuri Deepak Reddy, Badavath Rajitha, Asuri Nandhan, Vellaturi Gopikrishna
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Cardiovascular disease accounts for a significant portion of worldwide deaths and makes the development of rapid and precise diagnostic tools imperative. The traditional methods of interpreting Electrocardiograms (ECGs) are inefficient, slow, and prone to mistakes. This issue is magnified when dealing with poor quality recordings. This study introduces a completely automated multi-step heart disease detection system capable of analyzing digitized ECG images using multi-faceted computer vision and machine learning techniques. The digitized ECG images undergo multistep preprocessing consisting of noise removal, edges of interest being highlighted, grey scale adjustments, and region of interest cropping. A combination of machine learning and statistical feature extraction techniques is used to fully characterize the digitized ECGs and their corresponding images. The two feature sets are combined, weighed, and classified through an ensemble of machine learning models. The proposed system was tested with datasets composed of normal and abnormal (arrhythmia and myocardial infarction) ECGs, and was shown to outperform the state of the art with respect to accuracy, precision, and recall on all abnormal ECG datasets. The system democratizes evidence-based medicine and is a valuable clinical decision-support tool for front-line healthcare workers in the absence of specific cardiology training. Additionally, the architecture has the potential ability, along with the proper optimization, to extend to higher-level deep learning models, including Vision Transformers, as well as the ability to be integrated into smartphone-based ECG screening systems for real-time applications or into federated learning environments for safe large-scale utilization.

title: Sustainable Smart Vertical Axis Wind Turbine System for Highways with IoT-based Monitoring and Energy Management
authors: Vijay M, Nagella Nagavenkat, Kommineni Kavya Sree, Maruprolu Jyothsna, Munugu Tejasree, Kumaran Gobika
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: This paper presents the design and development of a smart Vertical Axis Wind Turbine (VAWT) system that can be installed next to highway road dividers. In order to generate electricity, the system uses the wind created by moving cars in both directions. In addition to producing electricity, the turbines have protective covers and touch sensors that can identify tampering. A buzzer is activated and real-time alerts are transmitted to a web-based monitoring interface upon detection of tampering. Roadside amenities like street lights and specialized electric vehicle charging stations are powered by the energy produced, which is stored in batteries. Enhancing roadside infrastructure and promoting electric mobility can be done safely and sustainably with this integrated solution.

title: Exploring Fine-Tuned Vision-Language Model for Multi-Class Skin Disease Classification
authors: Atia Shahnaz Ipa, Mohiuddin Ahmad
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Accurate automated classification of dermatological diseases is crucial for early diagnosis and treatment. However, the use of fine-tuned vision-language models for skin disease classification involving a large number of classes remains underexplored. This study presents a fine-tuned vision-language model, Qwen2-VL-2B, for skin disease classification using a curated subset of a publicly available dataset with 15 image classes. The fine-tuned model achieves a 14.1% higher accuracy compared to its non-fine-tuned counterpart and outperforms commonly used architectures such as ResNet-50, DenseNet-121, and Swin Transformer. The methodology incorporates effective preprocessing, lightweight quantization, and optimized training strategies to enable resource efficient fine-tuning. Evaluation results, including class-wise metrics and confusion matrix analyses, demonstrate the model’s superior ability to recognize common conditions while highlighting challenges with rare classes due to dataset imbalance. Although limited by a relatively small dataset, this work underscores the promise of fine-tuned vision-language models for dermatological diagnosis. Future directions include expanding the dataset and integrating a visual question answering system to enhance diagnostic support and interactivity.

title: Simulation of Highly Efficient CdTe/CdS based Solar Cell with Al-ZnO as Buffer Layer using SCAPS-1D
authors: M. Chindamani, M. Dharani, S. Esther
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Al-dopped ZnO/CdS/CdTe solar cells with varying thicknesses and stoichiometric divisions were meticulously modeled using the SCAPS-1D simulation program. The analysis primarily focused on the I-V characteristics, utilizing the SCAPS-1D modeling. Optimal results were achieved Al-ZnO (Aluminum-doped Zinc Oxide)serves as a transparent, conductive window layer that allows light in while providing good electrical conductivity. CdS (Cadmium Sulfide) acts as the n-type buffer layer, forming a stable heterojunction with the p-type CdTe (Cadmium Telluride) absorber with corresponding variation in open circuit voltage, current density, and fill factor values. This configuration yielded an impressive efficiency from 16.64 % to 19.36 %. These findings underscore the significance of precise control over the CdTe layer's thickness and the involving fraction in optimizing the result of AlZnO/CdS/CdTe solar cells

title: Blockchain-Enabled Explainable Intrusion Detection System for Securing Stock Market Transactions
authors: Malathy S, R. Anandhi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Unfair trading practices including spoofing and layering are risks to financial markets that should be dealt within a time interval due to their detrimental effects on transparency and investor confidence. A while back, the SECURE framework was introduced which is an AI-driven intrusion detection architecture for stock market data, that combines machine learning with explainable AI (XAI) to detect malicious and manipulative behaviour while providing justifications suitable for regulators. It is worth noting that the SECURE framework retains alerts in traditional databases, which are more susceptible to manipulation and reduce trust in regulatory audit requests. This work explores the working of SECURE framework and developed a Blockchain-Enabled Explainable Intrusion Detection System (BE-IDS) to use a permissioned-layer audit-type blockchain to robustly store intrusion alerts. The performance of integrated framework SECURE-CUM-RF-CUM-IDS-CUM-XAI is compared against BE-IDS frameworks on Level-2 order book data with over <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1. 2}$</tex> million events and included <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1. 2}$</tex> million simulated exploitations including spontaneous occurrences of spoofing and layering activity. The results demonstrate that the detection accuracy remained unchanged <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(F 1 \approx 93 \%)$</tex>, since the detection and XAI modules are performing at-par with BE-IDS performance being affected only by the additional braid of blockchain latency of about 12 % and additional storage of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${\sim 2 0 \%}$</tex>. The findings of this article demonstrate that BE-IDS presents a practical step in allowing financial cyber-security systems to offer transparency and maintain trust among the users.

title: DeepEyeNet-XAI: A Hybrid Deep Learning Framework with Grad-CAM Explainability for Multiclass Retinal Disease Classification
authors: Sadia Afroz, Najmus Saadat, Mamunur Rashid Alex, Md Masud Rana, Umma Habiba Mitu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Retinal diseases, including diabetic retinopathy, glaucoma, and cataracts, are leading causes of vision loss worldwide, making timely and reliable diagnosis a clinical priority. Recent advances in deep learning have improved automated image-based diagnosis, yet many existing models face two key barriers: limited adaptability to diverse datasets and a lack of interpretability that restricts clinical acceptance. In this paper, DeepEyeNet-XAI: a hybrid deep learning architecture, which is built on top of ResNet50 and EfficientNet-B3, is introduced, which takes the features together to generate more powerful multiclass retinal disease classes. A total of 4217 retinal fundus images have been curated and separated into training, validation and test sets in proportion of 70:15:15 and pre-processing tasks including resizing, normalization and augmentation were conducted. This model was optimized with the help of AdamW optimizer and was tested based on the traditional performance indicators. DeepEyeNet-XAI was the best with a total accuracy of 98.70% in contrast to the individual ResNet50 98.20% and EfficientNet-B3 97.50%. To enhance the transparency, Grad-CAM visualization was used and it was found that the network tended to pay attention to clinically significant retinal structures like the optic disc and vascular areas. These results show that DeepEyeNet-XAI strikes a balance between interpretability and high predictive accuracy, indicating its potential as a useful tool for ophthalmic diagnosis decision support.

title: Hybrid Quantum SVM for Communication Signal Classification
authors: B. VSS Praneeth, Ch. Rupa, Ch. N. Manikanta, D. Pavan Kumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Automatic Modulation classification (AMC) is an important task in wireless communication systems, as it helps identify the type of signal being transmitted and ensures reliable data transfer. Traditional machine learning methods, such as Support Vector Machines (SVM) and Random Forests, have been used for this purpose, but often face challenges in handling non-linear and complex signal patterns. The proposed work presents a Hybrid Quantum Support Vector Machine (QSVM) model for communication signal classification to address these limitations. The proposed approach aims to investigate the effectiveness of Hybrid QSVM framework that includes the role of quantum feature mapping and quantum kernel estimation and their impact on classification under varying channel conditions. The proposed method integrates classical machine learning with feature mapping and quantum kernel estimation, allowing data to be represented in a higher-dimensional quantum space for better separability. The system is evaluated using the RadioML 2016.10a dataset containing various modulation schemes under different channel conditions. Experimental results show that the Hybrid QSVM achieves better accuracy and generalization compared to the classical models. This study highlights the potential of combining quantum computing concepts with classical machine learning to improve signal recognition in modern communication networks.

title: Acoustic Side-Channel Attacks: An Experimental Study on Keystroke Detection using SVM in Deep Learning Techniques
authors: Jeevitha Kandasamy, S Sorna Lakshmi, S. Sabena, Kalaivani C, Ponrekha M, Thenmozhi S
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Acoustic side-channel attacks provide a considerable security risk by leveraging sound emissions from electrical equipment to extract secret data. This study offers a comprehensive overview of acoustic side channels, encompassing considerable scholarly research, security ramifications, and countermeasures, while delineating avenues for further inquiry. The primary emphasis is on auditory cryptanalysis, wherein adversaries extract cryptographic keys by scrutinizing sound waves (20 Hz to 20 kHz) produced during cryptographic processes, especially from elements like voltage regulators and motherboards. These components produce vibrations by electromagnetic and electrical forces, releasing sound waves that, while invisible to humans, can be detected with specialized equipment. Furthermore, we illustrate that PC keyboards are vulnerable to attacks utilizing support vector machine (SVM) in deep learning to detect keystrokes through acoustic characteristics. This study highlights the pressing necessity for hardware reconfigurations and sophisticated countermeasures to protect devices from rising sonic threats.

title: Real-Time Multi-Class Brain Haemorrhage Detection with Visual Explanations using Grad-CAM
authors: Sai Vijaya Ch, Khyathi Pradha Rayavarapu, Keerthana Komirishetty, Rohith Reddy, Sanath Bharadwaj Pendam
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Intracranial hemorrhage (ICH) is an emergent and potentially life-threatening diagnosis caused by intracranial or surrounding structure bleeding. Detection needs to be timely and correct to enhance outcomes in patients but is frequently impeded by radiologist shortages, limited time, and human error in high-pressure or low-resource environments. In order to overcome this challenge, we introduce a real-time, interpretable deep model based on the ResNet-50 architecture for multi-label classification of the five most common forms of brain hemorrhages: subdural, subarachnoid, intraventricular, intraparenchymal, and epidural. The model is trained on a balanced portion of the RSNA Intracranial Hemorrhage Detection dataset and makes use of sigmoid activation with binary cross-entropy loss to manage multiple labels per CT scan. To further the transparency and trustworthiness, we integrate Grad-CAM (Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping), which produces heatmaps demarcating regions of interest within the CT images that impacted the model's predictions. Such visual interpretability assists the clinicians in comprehending and validating the AI's outputs. Moreover, a light-weight Streamlit-based web interface enables users to upload scans and get on-the-fly predictions along with the respective Grad-CAM outputs. The system offers a useful, easy-to-use tool for supporting emergency triage and facilitating the use of explainable AI in radiological workflows.

title: A Comprehensive Review of IoT based Real time Tracking and Monitoring Applications
authors: S. Santhoshkumar, C. Sanjeev Kannan, J. Arumai Ruban, T.Suresh, R.Bhagavathi Lakshmi
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: This paper examines the use of Internet of Things (IoT) technologies for real-time shadowing and monitoring across crucial sectors such as healthcare, education, transportation, public safety, and diurnal exertion support. It begins with a disquisition of IoT perpetration in healthcare, focusing on sensor types, communication methods, and RFID wristbands used to cover patient movements. The study also reviews systems that track individuals’ daily routines and locations using GPS and mobile apps to help disorientation and support routine moments. In the field of education, RFID-grounded attendance systems are anatomized for their capability to enhance accuracy, effectiveness, and real-time access to student data for schools and parents. Investigate vehicle tracking results that integrate GPS, GSM networks, and IoT devices to ensure dependable location tracking, even in areas with limited internet access. Also, it discusses AI-powered public safety systems similar to stray dog discovery using YOLOv5 and CCTV, which support timely cautions and geographic analysis for external operation. The review identifies participated technological frameworks, assesses system performance, and outlines the benefits and challenges of real- time IoT monitoring across different real-world scripts.

title: Hardware Design of Lightweight QTL Cipher for High Throughput and Low Area Applications
authors: Pulkit Singh, Veer Chandra, Archana Pandey, Abhishek Kumar, Kriti Kundu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: This paper describes the hardware implementation of the QTL cipher, a 64-bit lightweight encryption scheme designed for low-area and quick processing requirements. It uses a 16-round SPN (Substitution-Permutation Network) model with cyclic shifts for diffusion, XOR for key mixing, and compact <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4 \times 4$</tex> S-boxes for non-linear transformation. A constant-driven key schedule generates 16 round keys from a single 64-bit key with minimal logic demands. The cipher is written in Verilog HDL and evaluated using a variety of simulated scenarios. The results demonstrate QTL's economical use of hardware and constant encryption performance, making it suited for embedded, IoT, and lowpower systems.

title: Adaptive IoT-based Smart Agriculture System: Overcoming Implementation and Scaling Challenges
authors: Vaishnavi.T, Nalini N, Dontu Rahul, Mohyedin Hamza, B.Manideep, G. Karthikeyan
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: This study presents the Self-Learning Agronomic AI Framework (SLAAF), an AI-powered IoT-based smart agriculture system that leverages reinforcement learning and adaptive decision-making for optimized crop health monitoring and resource management. By integrating real-time sensor data, robotics, satellite imagery, and predictive analytics, SLAAF enhances precision farming, improving efficiency, sustainability, and scalability. The proposed framework addresses challenges such as connectivity issues, high implementation costs, and interoperability with traditional farming equipment. Comparative analysis demonstrates SLAAF’s superior performance in yield optimization, resource utilization, and response time, making it a transformative solution for modern agriculture. The results highlight the framework’s ability to self-adapt to varying environmental conditions, ensuring 94.6% accuracy in disease prediction and 22% reduction in resource wastage. Additionally, SLAAF's reinforcement learning-driven optimization strategy improves response time by 37% while enhancing scalability across diverse farm sizes, making it a novel advancement in smart agriculture.

title: LoFo: A Smart RFID and IoT-based Solution for Lost and Found Management in Public Transport
authors: Sanjivani Adsul, Manas Shende, Vedant Shelke, Vaishnavi Sarode, Sania Khan, Isha Sable
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The need to deal with recurrent instances of people losing personal items and property on public transit systems is a dilemma facing users and authorities alike. In this paper we put forth an intelligent Lost and Found (LoFo) System which employs radio-frequency identification (RFID) technology and the Internet of Things (IoT) to facilitate recovering lost or misplaced items. This solution allows users to associate RFID tags to items and to personal details (primary and secondary contact information). When recovered, the owner details appear on an OLED screen after scanning the RFID tag, and are logged into a cloud service database where it can be viewed in real-time. Feedback from a buzzer is also supplied to enrich the interaction and confirmation. Our solution employs inexpensive hardware and cloud communication to produce a scalable, reliable, and user-friendly solution of lost property management for public transport. Experimental results show higher recovery rates, less administrative management, and greater overall satisfaction.

title: Cyber Threat Detection in Cloud Storage Environment Using Artificial Intelligence
authors: Aishvi Tyagi, Ronit Matthew George, Jayapandian N
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Cloud computing is defined as using computing services like networking, storage, servers and database over the internet instead of keeping everything on our own computer hard drive freeing the space on the computer for better performance output. Cloud computing has already become an important aspect of modern businesses and other day to day life services which provides many important features like flexibility, scalability and cost effectiveness as it follows pay as use feature. Nowadays cloud computing is used to support numerous businesses, procedure and workflow operations. Now that the use of cloud and services has become so common, there is always a fear of cybersecurity threat on the service. The traditional Intrusion Detection System also known as IDS struggle to keep up with the complex and the dynamic changes of the cloud environments which results in errors and bugs in threat detection and its response which is very harmful for the cloud environment. Therefore, the need for modern day security measures is very much required. This research article is mainly focus on cyber-attack during cloud storage; proposed method is used for artificial intelligence to detect data attacks. This can achieve with in-depth analysis and monitoring of the network traffics and its patterns. This helps in analysing and identifying any unusual behaviour or patterns in the network traffic which tells us about any potential attacks on cloud servers or resources.

title: Development and Optimization of a Deep Learning Model Based on Inception v3 for Automated Detection of Retinopathy Using Optical Coherence Tomography Images
authors: Devdoot Parial, Punam Das, Jahidul Islam Shikdar, Tofayet Sultan, Ramisa Anjum Oishi, Md. Asraf Ali
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: This study addresses the critical challenge of accurately detecting retinal diseases, a leading cause of irreversible blindness worldwide. The need for automated and scalable solutions is especially urgent in low-resource clinical settings where trained ophthalmologists are scarce. While several machine learning and early convolutional neural network (CNN) based methods have been proposed, they often struggle with image variability and classification accuracy. To address these limitations, we propose a deep learning based diagnostic system built on the Inception v3 architecture, optimized via transfer learning. The model was trained on a large publicly available OCT image dataset containing 84,000 expert-labeled scans across four categories: choroidal neovascularization (CNV), diabetic macular edema (DME), drusen, and normal retina. A robust data augmentation pipeline including affine transformations, normalization, and color jittering was employed to improve generalizability under real-world imaging conditions. The optimized model achieved strong performance metrics, with an overall classification accuracy of 99.7% and outperforming baseline models such as VGG-16, ResNet-50, and Xception. These results highlight the potential of our Inception v3–based system for efficient and accurate retinal screening, particularly in telemedicine applications and remote healthcare environments.

title: Designing an Efficient Algorithm for Early Diagnosis of Acute Appendicitis in Pediatric Patients
authors: N Nalini, A. Rosi, T Vaishnavi, Mohyedin Hamza, Sreekanth Sura, G. Karthikeyan
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Acute appendicitis is a critical surgical emergency that requires accurate and timely diagnosis to reduce complica-tions. In this study, we develop and compare machine learning models using the Support Vector Machine (SVM) and Decision Tree (DT) algorithms for the diagnosis of acute appendicitis. A dataset containing clinical symptoms, laboratory findings, and imaging features was collected from Kaggle, and each algorithm was trained and tested on a sample dataset of 20 cases. Experimental results demonstrate that the SVM model outperforms the Decision Tree model, achieving an accuracy of 90 percentage, sensitivity of 87 percentage, and specificity of 92 percentage, whereas the Decision Tree model exhibited comparatively lower performance. The high specificity of the SVM model minimizes unnecessary surgical interventions, while its strong sensitivity ensures fewer missed diagnoses. These findings highlight the effectiveness of SVM in accurately predicting acute appendicitis and suggest its potential as a valuable clinical decision-support tool. Future work will focus on expanding the dataset and integrating additional predictive features to further enhance model performance.

title: Visual Recognition of Plant Leaf Disease and Classification using Deep Learning
authors: Khaleelullah Shaik, Chiluveru Navyasri, D Shiva Kumar, K Asritha Reddy, M Sai Sumanth, G Chanakya
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Early detection of plant leaf diseases is important for maintaining crop health and sustainable farming. Manual inspection methods can be time-consuming, unreliable, and hard to scale. This paper presents a combined deep learning system that uses Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) for classifying diseases and the U-Net architecture for pinpointing infected areas. Trained on the PlantVillage dataset, the system achieves high diagnostic accuracy and provides clear visual outputs that highlight affected regions. This streamlined process allows for quick intervention by enabling both disease identification and visualizing severity. The method is designed to be scalable and lightweight, making it suitable for real-world farming. This work advances precision agriculture by providing an automated, understandable, and practical way to monitor plant diseases.

title: Air Pollution Prediction Using Machine Learning
authors: Raed A. Said, Mohamed Bin Zayed, Mohammad Shkoukani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Since air pollution has become a big environmental problem, its adverse effects on health, climate, and ecosystems should be addressed seriously. To adequately plan and manage cities, senior officials need to be able to assess the quality of the air accurately. In this study, I will present the usefulness of using machine learning methods in the prediction of urban air pollution levels in various locations. In this context, various algorithms (ml) like regression models, decision trees, and neural networks will be treated to have a sense of the effectiveness of the air quality forecasting. More specifically, investigators will take historical data and environmental factors, including temperature and humidity, wind speed, and gaseous pollutants, as predictive risk factors. The whole process of studying the problem of air pollution forecasting, including data quality, feature selection, and model interpretability, was as well examined. Teaching machines, specifically through ensemble methods and deep learning models, helped to obtain more accurate data forecasts and real-time monitoring of air quality with quite promising results. The study emphasizes the ability of machine learning to enable sustainable urban development, health risk assessment, and the formulation of policies to mitigate air pollution.

title: AI-Powered Energy Intelligence: Redefining Power Consumption for a Sustainable Tomorrow
authors: Ajit Kumar Singh, Anuradha Kushwaha, Dinesh Sharma, Priyanka Sikarwar
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Smart management of energy is key to achieving our sustainability objectives and reducing expenses. This study is aiming at developing a machine-learning model that will be able to estimate electricity consumption based on the analysis of various meteorological and environmental variables. By employing statistical techniques and projecting over the long run, the research provides concrete measures for better energy utilization and pinpointing significant developments in electricity consumption. The combination of AI and machine learning allows us to not only make right predictions but also to discover hidden trends in the data that are characterized by a large size. This contributes to the more efficient allocation of resources and the diminished generation of energy waste. The objective is to devise a prediction that model that accurately forecasts energy consumption, which, in turn will facilitate the management of resources and the optimization of power usage.

title: Comparative Analysis of Recent Big Data Analytics Algorithms for Privacy Preseration
authors: Mohana Chelvan P, Rajavarman V N
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Big data is the key term for almost every organizational data analytics because mountains of data are accumulated from customers' online transactions which are of variety including unstructured, semi structured along with structured data. Along with the upsurge in volume, variety, and velocity, the dimensionality of big data also increased because of advancements in digital devices along with communication and internet technologies. Hence feature selection is necessary to eliminate irrelevant and noisy data as a vital dimensionality reduction technique and an indispensable preprocessing step. The solidity of attribute selection is the robustness of the selection of a comparable subset of traits in succeeding iterations for small perturbations of data. The solidity of attribute selection is a critical area of research as scientists can't rely on their research findings if each iteration of attribute selection selects a different subset of attributes as solidity of attribute selection is interrelated to the accuracy of data analytics consequences. Recently, scientists resolved that the solidity of feature selection is not algorithm dependent as previous assumptions, but generally hang on the dataset's physical statistical characteristics. Privacy preserving modifications are vital to protect the customers' privacy before the dataset is sent for data analytics. However, privacy conserving modifications disturb the solidity of attribute selection and data analytics results as they depend on the dataset's physical statistical characteristics. This research work gives a comparative analysis of recent privacy conserving big data analytics algorithms regarding the solidity of feature selection and concert.

title: A Cross-Platform Malware Classification Using Domain-Aware Representation Learning
authors: Asmitha K. A., Vinod P., Rafidha Rehiman K.A., Serena Nicolazzo, Antonino Nocera
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Malware analysis across heterogeneous platforms such as Android and Windows presents significant challenges due to fundamental differences in executable structure, obfuscation strategies, and malware family characteristics. Existing malware classification approaches are largely based on end-to-end deep learning models trained on homogeneous datasets, limiting their scalability, interpretability, and robustness in cross-platform settings. In this work, we propose a domain-aware neural-classical hybrid Mixture-of-Experts framework for malware classification using image representations of binaries. The proposed framework decouples representation learning, domain identification, and family classification into three modular stages. A lightweight convolutional neural network learns shared malware embeddings, a neural domain classifier performs routing between platforms, and domain-specialized classical machine learning models act as classification experts. Extensive experiments on Android and Windows malware datasets demonstrate that perfect domain separation can be achieved at the embedding level, enabling effective domain-filtered inference. Results show that Random Forest experts significantly outperform Support Vector Machines and k-Nearest Neighbors under domain specialization, achieving up to 91 % F1-score for Android malware families and 94 % F1score for Windows malware families. The modular design further enables interpretability through routing confidence analysis and embedding visualization.

title: CNN-Driven Performance Modeling of a Wideband Terahertz Microstrip Antenna for 6G Wireless Systems
authors: Subbarayudu B, V. Nagaraja, Etikoti Prathyusha, Sibyala Santhosh Kumar, Shaik Mahaboob Basha
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: This paper proposes a thorough performance analysis of a high-frequency antenna design intended for Terahertz and millimeter-wave frequencies. The antenna's gain, return loss, voltage standing wave ratio, and efficiency have been analyzed over the frequency range of 0 to 110 GHz. The gain pattern clearly reveals a gradual rise to over 18 dBi in the range of 90 GHz, establishing its directional pattern. In conformity, the return loss values remain substantially below −10 dB over an expansive frequency range, thus establishing the good matching levels of the impedance. The VSWR values were found to be around 1.5 over the majority of the frequency range, establishing the efficient transfer of power. The efficiency values clearly establish peaks for the antenna over the range of 43 GHz, 60 GHz, which are over 2.0. The polar plots of the radiation patterns at significant frequencies such as 43.5 GHz, 58.9 GHz, 79.4 GHz, 89.7 GHz, 94.8 GHz, and 100 GHz illustrate nearomnidirectional radiation patterns with certain asymmetries. The design of the proposed antenna possesses enormous potential for application in the next generation of wireless technology such as 6G and beyond, due to the emphasis given to ultra-wideband performance and high gain parameters. The presented study validates the universality of the designed antenna for various GHz operational frequencies with promising applications for high-speed low-latency communication technology.

title: Fraud Detection in Financial Transactions Using AI
authors: Yuvaraj Ganesan, Amro Dabash, Khaleel Ibrahim Al-Daoud, Thayer Mhd Nadim Marouf, Munther T. Momany, Obidah Mustafa Alqudah, Amjed Alfityani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Fraud detection in financial transactions is a chief concern for banks and finance companies since fraudsters are continuously upgrading their skills, making the job of detecting them very difficult for banks. Also, in this study, the use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) is examined in the context of real-time fraud detection with a focus on machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) models. The proposed architecture uses such sophisticated algorithms as ensemble methods and neural networks to study transaction patterns and to spot anomalies and to report scams with great accuracy. By using big data files, the paper is able to tackle problems related to data imbalance, feature engineering, and model interpretability. The current research utilizes the hybrid approach which combines both supervised and unsupervised learning techniques to improve detection of the data and to have the highest hit rate with the least number of false positives. It goes on to assess the scale and deployment of these systems in real-world situations in which case techniques go through changes of patterns. The results of the study illustrate the success of the proposed system through better accuracy in detection and higher operational efficiency which leads to a more secure financial ecosystem. Furthermore, the research indicates the ability of AI to eliminate the problem of fraud and to ensure the safety of financial transactions.

title: AMC Back Dual Band Monopole Antenna for Wireless Health Monitoring System
authors: Aditi Singh, Pushkar Verma, Pratik Mondal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: A dual-band monopole antenna with an artificial magnetic conductor (AMC) for health monitoring in a wireless body area network (WBAN) is presented in this research. Reliable telemetry on the body and short-range communication off the body are made possible by the antenna's operation in the 2.45 GHz and 5.8 GHz ISM bands. Dual-resonant behavior is produced in a limited space using a slot-loaded ring radiator. When the body is close, the AMC surface reflects in-phase, which improves impedance stability, enhances forward gain, and decreases backward radiation. A FR-4 substrate is used to create the antenna. The reflection coefficient is less than −10 dB for both bands, according to the simulated data. This is due to the fact that AMC integration enhances gain and radiation performance.

title: Semantic Retrieval and Fine-Tuned MPNet Embeddings for Intelligent Life Insurance Policy Recommendation Systems
authors: Charan Aravind A, V Bala Narendra Kumar, M Sairam, K Ajay Kartheek Dora, P N V Syamala Rao M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: The LIC (Life Insurance Corporation) has many different types of policies with increasing complexity, and additionally, with the increase in demand for personalised digital services, there is an expectation of intelligent recommendation systems that can interpret user intent and provide relevant product suggestions that extend beyond the use of keyword matching. We developed a semantic retrieval-based LIC Policy Recommendation System using transformer embedding models to find the best matching LIC Policy for each user's needs. We compiled all LIC Policies into a single canonical text corpus and created a domain-specific dataset by combining this corpus with user query data. We evaluated many forms of embedding approaches (TF-IDF, transformer, etc.) and focused primarily on Sentence-BERT (MPNet) and an LIC domain term fine-tuned version. LIC Policies were indexed using FAISS and HNSW to allow for scale via efficient similarity searches across thousands of policies. The experimental results showed that the fine-tuned system consistently outperformed the base system in all major information retrieval metrics (Precision@1, Precision@2, Recall@5 and Mean Reciprocal Rank [MRR]). More relevant policies found in higher-ranking retrieves indicate a strong semantic understanding and alignment with user intent for this system. This research lays the groundwork for developing a production-level LIC recommendation system, demonstrating the benefits of utilizing both domain-adaptive embeddings and hybrid retrieval methods. Continuing enhancements could include providing richer fine-tuning data, BM25 Lexical Signals reranking, and offering real-time recommendations based upon user profiles.

title: A Dataset for IoT-Enabled Smart Irrigation Using UAVs for Multispectral Sensing in Agriculture
authors: Ahmed Rahmani, Mohanad Alayedi
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Precision Agriculture (PA) can help reduce water consumption in dry regions, yet many PA systems still rely on cloud-based processing. This dependence introduces latency and requires stable internet connectivity, which is often unavailable in rural environments. Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) offer high spatial resolution compared to satellite platforms; however, most UAV-based approaches depend on computationally intensive deep learning (DL) models that are unsuitable for real-time execution on edge devices. In this paper, we propose an edgecomputable and autonomous framework for smart irrigation.
