A pilot multispectral dataset imported from IEEE Dataport, which was collected by a UAV and paired with in-situ soil moisture measurements, is analyzed to identify a minimal modelling approach that is both accurate and practical for deployment. Experimental results indicate that the Near-Infrared (NIR, 840 nm) band exhibits the strongest relationship with soil moisture, with a Pearson correlation of r = −0.64 between mean NIR reflectance and soil moisture content (SMC) at 0 – 20 cm depth. A single-band NIR calibration achieves R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>=0.41, with an RMSE of 0.0123 and an MAE of 0.0105. Four regression models-Linear Regression, Random Forest, Support Vector Regression, and K-Nearest Neighbors-are evaluated using Leave-One-Out Cross-Validation (LOOCV). Random Forest achieves the lowest prediction error (RMSE = 0.0125, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>=0.39), while a lightweight multivariate linear regression remains competitive (RMSE = 0.0131, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>=0.33). Because the linear model requires only a small set of coefficients, it can be deployed on low-power edge hardware with minimal memory requirements (below 15 KB). The proposed sensing-toactuation architecture reduces reliance on cloud connectivity, enabling fast, autonomous irrigation decisions and supporting scalable water management.

title: CROPSIGHT: Smart Crop Delivery with AI-Based Price and Quality Intelligence
authors: L. Mary Gladence, Nandha Gopal M, J. Merlin Mary Jenitha, Richard Xavier A. K.
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Indian agriculture encounters ongoing issues like inadequate pricing, restricted market access, and ineffective logistics, resulting in diminished farmer earnings and post-harvest losses. The main goal of this study is to create an AI-powered, comprehensive digital marketplace that guarantees dependable crop verification, accurate price evaluation, and clear agricultural transactions. Cropsight combines computer vision for image verification and machine learning for price prediction to link farmers, buyers, and transporters on one platform. A two-step image verification process utilizing EfficientNetV2 and Vision Transformer (ViT) guarantees the authenticity of images and assesses crop quality. Fair price ranges are estimated through a CatBoost regression model that is trained on current, web-scraped mandi price information. Experimental results show that CatBoost attains a lower RMSE than baseline models, indicating enhanced accuracy in price prediction. Authenticated listings, smart logistics management, multilingual assistance, offline availability, and fraud protection together improve platform dependability. The suggested system decreases reliance on intermediaries, enhances price transparency, and fosters a sustainable and inclusive agricultural market.

title: Performance Evaluation of Classification Models for E-Commerce Customer Churn Prediction
authors: Dhruv Saxena, Armita Patro, Diya Agarwal, Jay Prakash Singh, Sonal Jain, Mahendra Kumar Gourisaria
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: Customer churn is a critical concern for modern online businesses like e-commerce sites, where retaining customers is more economical than acquiring a new one. Predictive models can be employed beforehand so that can help to prevent customer churn by retaining significant customers in order to address the concern. This paper presents a comparative assessment of several classification models based on real data available from a customer engagement platform related to their demographics, usage patterns, as well as other factors. The classification models used for this review are AdaBoost, XGBoost, K- Nearest Neighbour(KNN), Extra Trees, Random Forest, LightGBM, Logistic Regression, Decision Tree and Gradient Boosting. These models are applied to evaluate parameters like F1-Score, Accuracy, Precision, Recall and many others. Data Training indicate that ensemble tree-based models, particularly LightGBM achieve the highest performance among all other traditional models. The findings demonstrate that boosted ensembles show complex non-linear relations of churn behaviour more effectively and are applicable for practical development in actual customer retention systems.

title: Kullback–Leibler Divergence Based Fuzzy C-Means Segmentation Algorithm for Magnetic Resonance Imaging Data
authors: Deepa Joseph, S.N Kumar, Midhun P Mathew
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: An enhanced 3D brain tissue segmentation framework is developed based on Kullback-Leibler divergence. This divergence parameter is introduced into the classical FCM model. This model utilising IBSR dataset, operates directly on volumetric MRI data. The parameters such as normalized intensity, bias-corrected intensity, gradient magnitude, local mean, and local variance are used for feature representation. KLD-based regularization is added to the optimization function to improve robustness against intensity overlap and noise.To improve the accuracy post processing involved is MRF smoothening. The proposed framework is validated on IBSR 3D dataset and obtained high segmentation accuracy with good Dice and Jaccard scores

title: GeoSMAViT: Geographical-Context Spectral-Invariant Multi-Scale Adaptive Vision-Language Transformer
authors: Aqsa Abu Bakar, Amirreza Khoshbakht, Erchan Aptoula
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: Cross-domain hyperspectral image classification faces challenges from deficiencies in geographical context modeling, spectral instabilities, and suboptimal multimodal fusion. We present GeoSMAViT, a geography-aware vision-language transformer with: (1) climate-aware spectral processing using region-specific band weighting and atmospheric correction, (2) multi-scale regional pattern recognition for different geographic land-use types, (3) single-stage cross-modal fusion with multi-head attention between vision and geographic text features, (4) progressive domain adaptation using source features for invariant representation, and comprehensive anti-overfitting measures including dynamic regularization and adaptive text dropout. On Houston 2013-to-2018 and PaviaU-to-PaviaC, GeoSMAViT achieved 98.37% and 96.29% OA, respectively, surpassing state-of-the-art baselines. Ablations confirmed that geographic awareness provided the largest contribution (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+38.33 {\%}$</tex>) to cross-domain generalization.

title: Free-Standing Symmetric Complementary Terahertz Metasurface for Non-Invasive Glucose Sensing
authors: Saiqa Imtiaz, Ali Alhaj Abbas, Ibraheem Al Naib
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: A free-standing symmetric complementary terahertz metasurface is proposed for non-invasive glucose sensing. The structure is designed on a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$25 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex> aluminum layer and achieves a high sensitivity of 222 GHz/RIU with strong field confinement. Capacitive and inductive coupling enhance lightmatter interaction. A <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$20 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex> glucose analyte layer is placed on both sides of the metasurface using experimentally reported dielectric constants corresponding to glucose concentrations from 54 mg/dL to 342 mg/dL. A clear redshift in resonance frequency is observed with increasing glucose concentration. The results confirm that the proposed design could provide high sensitivity, structural simplicity, and low loss, making it a promising platform for compact terahertz biosensing and glucose monitoring applications.

title: A Secure Meta-Heuristic Framework for MANET based IoT Privacy Protection Using AMU-HHO Algorithm
authors: P. Satyanarayana, Nerusu Siva Vennela, Chilla Preethi, Sakhamuri Suryanarayana, Kanaka Raju Penmetsa, V. Gokula Krishnan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: The integration of Mobile Ad hoc Networks (MANETs) with the Internet of Things (IoT) has allowed the flexible, infrastructure-less communication in critical operations and data driven applications. Nevertheless, MANET-based IoT networks possess dynamic topology, open wireless medium and resource constraints that facilitate sensitive data to extreme privacy and security risks. Traditional cryptographic and routing implementations tend to have high computational costs, low flexibility and are not very resistant to smart attacks. To solve these issues, this paper presents a Secure Metaheuristic Framework of MANET-based IoT Privacy Protection with the help of Adaptive Multi-Utility Harris Hawks Optimization (AMU-HHO) algorithm, which is the adaptive optimization and security-conscious decision-making framework that provides protection in data transmission. The suggested AMU-HHO framework is dynamically balanced between exploration and exploitation with control of adaptive parameters and multi-utility formulation of fitness. Analysis is essential in assessing the arbitrariness and flexibility of the produced optimization keys under dynamic MANET-IoT, in order to make it immune to adversarial inference. Fitness analysis studies the efficiency of the suggested multiobjective utility function through exploring the security of optimal solutions selected while ensuring algorithmic stability regarding node mobility and network uncertainty. Moreover, convergence analysis is done to determine the behavior of optimization of AMU-HHO and it proves to be faster in convergence speed and better accuracy in the solution than traditional meta-heuristic methods. The analysis of the results shows that the proposed framework has a secure and reliable optimization that has good convergence properties.

title: A Semi-Supervised and Evasion-Aware Framework for Reducing Alert Fatigue in Security Operations Centers (SoC)
authors: Kalimera Nandu Goud, Kurunandan Jain, Prabhakar Krishnan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Security Operations Centers often depend on automated detection tools that produce many alerts, most of which reflect normal, non-malicious activity. This situation can cause alert fatigue, increase analysts' workload, and raise the risk that genuine attacks go unnoticed. Machine-learning alert triage systems may help sort and prioritize alerts, yet their performance often suffers when there are too few labeled examples, when patterns change over time, and when attackers adapt their behavior to avoid detection. This paper proposes a semisupervised, risk-aware approach to SOC alert triage that accounts for practical operational constraints. This method combines a hybrid source-target labeling scheme with rankbased tri-training and probabilistic calibration to learn from large amounts of unlabeled alert data while keeping attack recall as the main objective. Risk-aware decision thresholds are set so the system can close alerts conservatively while avoiding an increase in false negatives. We used temporally split network flow data from the CI-CIDS2017 dataset to simulate SOC alert streams. The experiments show that supervised models lose a large amount of recall when the data shift over time. In contrast, the proposed semi-supervised framework reduces this recall drop and supports automatic closure of many benign alerts. A proof-of-concept integration with the Wazuh SIEM platform was conducted to examine practical issues and constraints related to deployment. The results suggest that semi-supervised, risk-sensitive learning can reduce alert fatigue while preserving detection performance in dynamic and adversarial SOC settings.

title: An Improved Tiny Vision Transformer-based Approach for Explainable Apple Leaf Disease Classification
authors: Syma Haque Roshni, Sirazam Munira, Sunjida Sikder Sumaya, Md. Saymon Hosen Polash, Sami Al Zabid
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Apples are one of the most important and widely cultivated fruits around the world, and they hold significant economic value in many regions. However, apple production and fruit quality are greatly affected by leaf diseases such as Rust and Scab. Therefore, early and accurate detection of apple leaf diseases is essential for sustainable orchard management and effective disease prevention. Traditional inspection methods are slow, labor-intensive, and prone to human error, which highlights the need for automated and reliable solutions. This paper proposes an optimized Tiny-ViT-5M-224 model for three-class apple leaf disease classification (Healthy, Rust, Scab) using a Kaggle dataset of 1,730 images. The proposed model is based on the combination of transfer learning and domain specific preprocessing, structured data augmentation, and regularization techniques, which allows it to achieve strong generalization at a low computational cost. Tiny-ViT achieves a test accuracy of 99.23 % and means 5-fold cross-validation accuracy of 98.38 % which is way better than other evaluated lightweight transformer baselines. The model also has strong statistical reliability as its Matthews correlation coefficient is 0.9884, Cohen kappa is 0.9901, and the 95 % interval is small <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$([0.9811,0.9981])$</tex>. Notably, Grad-CAM visualizations indicate that biologically relevant lesion areas of the prediction are directed by artifacts of the background instead of biologically important areas, thus increasing transparency and credibility. Collectively, these results show that Tiny-ViT is a reliable, explainable and practical object detector of precision agriculture, demonstrating how tiny transformer designs can also be used to achieve high performance in detecting plant diseases in resource-limited conditions.

title: Hybrid 3D-CNN and Vision Transformer Framework for Multi-Class Retinal Disease Classification
authors: Soleman Hossain, S. T. A. Mahmud Tonmoy, Sihab Mahmud, M Shamimul Haque Mondal Shimul, Riasat Khan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: The retina is an intricate and multilayered tissue that uses light to create images using the brain’s visual cortex. Retinal disease happens when the retinal tissue is disturbed, which results in different types of vision problems that lead to complete or partial blindness. In 2020, 196 million people were affected by AMD globally, and it is expected to increase and reach the edge of 288 million by 2040. Identifying the disease is needed to reduce the number of people going blind. Methods like fluorescein angiography, retinal examination, and optical coherence tomography (OCT) are traditionally used to identify retinal diseases, but they are not effective or quick. In this work, we have presented an automated multi-class retinal disease classification system using OCT images by implementing various deep learning techniques. A public dataset named Retinal OCT Image Classification C8 and various preprocessing techniques, i.e., resizing images, normalization, and augmentation, have been used. Pretrained models and vision transformer frameworks have been used to train the dataset with and without the augmentation technique. Using augmentation, variances of 2D image data were created and stacked to form a 3D image to capture both spatial and depth-related features. The dual-branch network processes pseudo-3D slices with the 3D-CNN and resized images with the ViT, concatenating their features for highly accurate classification. This 3D representation was then used to train the Hybrid 3D-CNN + ViT model, which achieved an accuracy of 0.9168 and an F1-score of 0.9167. The EigenCAM explainable AI approach is implemented to illustrate how the model works.

title: Characterization and Detection of Diseases in Sugarcane Leaves using Deep Learning: An End-to-End Automated System
authors: Vishesh Goyal, Abiman Kurunth, Trisha Gupta, Yash Swarup, Neha Sharma
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Diseases that affect the leaves of sugarcane plants have a significant impact on the yield of sugarcane crops. This paper proposes an end-to-end automated system for multi-class classification of sugarcane leaf diseases and the estimation of the severity level of the diseases. This paper proposes a system using deep learning techniques. EfficientNetV2B2 is utilized for the classification of sugarcane leaf diseases into seven classes. ResNet-18 is utilized for the estimation of the severity level of two main sugarcane leaf diseases: Banded Chlorosis and Mosaic. The model is trained using the Mendeley Sugarcane Leaf Disease Dataset. The experiment is conducted to show that the system is capable of achieving classification accuracy of more than 90%. The severity level is estimated with an accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5 \%}$</tex>. The system is also implemented using a web application developed using the Streamlit framework. The proposed system provides more accurate and reliable results as compared to the existing ML techniques in this domain.

title: Intelligent AI-Driven Framework for Precision-based Healthcare Diagnosis and Decision Support
authors: Poreddy Venkata Shiva Charan Reddy, Chintalapalli Vasista Somanath, Shaik Irfan Basha, Shaik Mahammad Eassa, K Vinod Kumar Reddy
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The increasing number of diabetes mellitus cases, along with delayed medical diagnosis, creates a critical situation that demands modern diagnostic support systems capable of high precision and understandable outputs for patients. Traditional health service models that apply machine learning techniques make predictive models, but they fail to achieve transparency and clinical implementation capability, which reduces their effectiveness for real-world use. The research targets the creation of an intelligent AI system that helps healthcare professionals make precise medical decisions through diagnosis support for diabetes prediction from structured medical data. The framework structure contains a comprehensive set of procedures that start with preprocessing the data before moving on to training the model and implementing interpretability features. Eight clinical features were documented in the representative PIMA Indians Diabetes Dataset, which contains 768 records. The evaluation process revealed that XGBoost provided the best results; thus, it was chosen among the evaluated supervised learning models. The developed model was enhanced through SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations), which provided detailed interpretability of individual patient data alongside feature analysis. Results from experiments showed that XGBoost produced 81.3% classification accuracy and 0.87 AUC-ROC value, which surpassed the other baselines, including logistic regression, random forest, and SVM. The predictive features detected by SHAP analysis included glucose along with BMI and age, which confirmed clinical assumptions about diagnosis. The system achieves both exceptional predictive performance and understandable decision assistance capabilities while providing a framework suitable for deployment in clinical diagnostic solutions and personalized patient treatments.

title: QuickBite: Empowering Local Eateries Through a Personalized and Efficient Food Delivery Platform
authors: Varun Jain, Vivek Tawade, Vaibhav Gohil, Arya Nadkar, Shilpa Kalantri
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The food delivery industry is currently dominated by large platforms like UberEats and Zomato, which pose significant challenges for small, local restaurants. These businesses struggle with high commission fees, limited visibility, and complex onboarding procedures. QuickBite is a specialized platform designed to address these concerns by providing a vendor-friendly interface, cost-effective operational structures, and analytical tools to help small food establishments thrive. The platform integrates features such as seamless menu management, secure payment processing, real-time order tracking, and customer engagement mechanisms. This paper elaborates on the need for an inclusive food delivery ecosystem and the technical aspects of QuickBite’s development. It also explores the challenges faced during implementation and the potential impact on the local restaurant industry. Keywords—Food Delivery, Local Restaurants, AI Recommendation, Vendor Dashboard, QuickBite.

title: Granular Text-to-Image Synthesis Using Attentionbased Generative Adversarial Networks
authors: Saravanan M. S, T. Ramyasree
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: This research paper explores the text-to-image generation by using Generative Adversarial Networks (GAN). The proposed model takes given granular text to convert into images with different levels of abstraction. The words used in the text from various sources such as social media, web and other mediums are used to understand, analyze and synthesis to process the exact convert from text to image. The images are developed from sentence level also from overall text consolidation by combining number of texts into sentence in broader context. The combination of word level and sentence level composing mechanism, this research study proposing a new model called “Attention Based Generative Adversarial Networks” (ABGAN) can generate high-resolution images with higher accuracy. This proposed model ABGAN gives better conversion than any existing models such as traditional Markov Random Fields (MRF) and Bayesian Approaches. The results and findings show the significant improvement on conversion accuracy, that is exact model from text to image.

title: Exploring Sleep Disorder Classification: An Evaluation of Traditional and Ensemble ML Models with XAI Interpretability
authors: Rakibul Hasan Efty, Ashfaqul Islam, Muhaimen Khan, Abdul Kadar Muhammad Masum, Khandaker Mohammad Mohi Uddin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: The classification of sleep disorders is important to improving human health in general. Sleep disorders and apnea may have a serious effect on human health. Manual classification of sleep stages by experts is a tiresome task, and subject to errors. In order to enhance this process, it is necessary to generate accurate machine learning algorithms to classify the sleep disorders to help in laboratory analysis, monitoring, and diagnosis of the disorders. The proposed study is a large predictive model on preliminary data and individual medical records. Machine learning algorithms like Logistic Regression (LR), LightGBM (LGBM), Gradient Boosting (GB), Random Forest (RF), AdaBoost (AB) and Decision Tree (DT) were used to execute the classification. Utilizing XGBoost (XGB) as the feature selection technique improved overall performance and enhanced feature extraction. The highest accuracy is obtained by using stacking ensemble models of RF, XGB, and LR with the accuracy being 97.33% with Precision, Recall, F1-score of 95.69%. Further, methods of Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI), such as SHAP and LIME, were applied to measure the importance of features and also as a way of making decisions transparent, therefore enhancing model interpretability and clinical applicability.

title: BRCA-GNN: Graph Neural Network for Breast Cancer Gene Module Discovery
authors: M Anbarasan, K. Kokilavani, S Shankar, C. Chandru Vignesh, Stewart Kirubakaran S
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Breast cancer takes the highest cancer death toll worldwide, with the BRCA1 and BRCA2 gene mutations being some of the most well-studied and critical genetic risk factors. This work proposes a framework called BRCA-GNN for graph neural networks that can identify relevant modules containing the disease genes thorough learning from interaction. It was integrated with high-confidence gene interaction information from STRING and BioGRID to create a biologically relevant graph of approximately 8,000 nodes and 12,000 edges. The graph contains 150 breast tissue samples, consisting of 90 tumor samples and 60 normal samples. Each gene was represented as a node with features corresponding to its level of expression. The edges were known interactions that existed among the genes. This was achieved by a Graph Attention Network (GAT) that captured contextual and topological relationships between genes. It outperformed the standard machine learning baseline, reaching accuracy 87.5, AUC 0.91, precision 0.89, and recall 0.85. Attention mechanisms enabled better predicting and understanding interactions between BRCA1 and BRCA2 and significantly enhanced predictive capabilities. The suggested framework offers an approach for finding functional gene modules that contribute to tumor growth which offers an alternative to traditional classifiers with a structural focus. Also, it assists in later applications such as biomarker discovery and personalized medicine due to its interpretability and modular construction. BRCA-GNN illustrates the applicability of GNNs for genomic data analysis when driven by biologically relevant interaction networks.

title: Improved Probability Calibration Techniques for Accurate Heart Disease Risk Assessment
authors: A.Akilandeswari
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Accurate probability calibration is a critical yet often overlooked aspect of clinical decision support systems. This paper presents a comprehensive evaluation of calibration techniques for heart disease prediction models, focusing specifically on the application of isotonic regression for improving probability estimates. While logistic regression models are widely used in medical diagnostics, their raw probability outputs frequently exhibit miscalibration, potentially leading to suboptimal clinical decisions. Our novel approach demonstrates that post-hoc calibration can improve reliability of risk predictions while maintaining or enhancing discriminative performance. Experiments on a standard heart disease dataset show that calibrated models provide more trustworthy probability estimates, with performance improvements validated through both Brier score analysis and reliability diagrams. This work addresses an important gap in clinical predictive modeling by focusing on probability calibration---a critical factor for responsible deployment of AI in healthcare settings.

title: Alzheimer's Disease Classification Using Graphical User Interface
authors: Chandralekha R, S Kayalvizhi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: The technique of deep learning has been very popular for solving problems in many different fields for a long time, including analyzing medical images. In the last few years, clinical image assessment has changed the way medical services work by letting doctors find early symptoms of Alzheimer disease also helping patients get better quickly. The images are a big part of figuring out if someone has Alzheimer disease. It is neurological condition of brain that slowly impairs memory and mental abilities. Brain imaging tools including MRI & CT scans were used to examine brain abnormalities associated with initial sign of Alzheimer's-related dementia. The main purpose of the proposed systems is to design an efficient image processing framework model is used to improve Alzheimer's disease diagnosis and classification integrated with a GUI. The proposed researches that are being looked at here are matrix-based and use Modified ResNet-50 classifier model combined along with domain-specific preprocessing and optimization techniques.

title: Real-Time EV Charging Station Energy Management Strategy Enabled by IoT
authors: Arun Kumar V, Deepika A, Sharmila K, Madhan P
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The fast integration of electric vehicles (EVs) requires a smart charging infrastructure that can efficiently, reliably, and in real-time handle energy. The traditional EV charging stations usually do not have adaptive monitoring and control, which leads to the inefficient use of energy and the overload of the grids. In this paper, the author introduces an IoT-enabled real-time energy management system of EV charging stations based on an ESP32-based controller with sensing, communication, and control modules. The system constantly checks the voltages, currents, temperature, battery state of charge (SOC), and the presence of charging ports. The information is sent to a cloud platform in real-time to analyse, visualise, and control the data and analyse the energy. The solution enhances the efficiency of energy, makes the charge safer, and enables the scalability of the installation of the solution in institutional and publicly charged settings.

title: Bridging Knowledge and Innovation in River Restoration: A Scoping Review of Open Innovation and Knowledge Management
authors: Romero-Gómez David, Carlos Alberto Flores-Sánchez, Richard Evans, Muhammad Faraz Mubarak, Ahumada-Tello Eduardo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Freshwater ecosystems are increasingly threatened by urbanization, pollution, and climate change. River restoration efforts require Knowledge Management (KM) and Open Innovation (OI) to bridge research and practice. KM facilitates structured knowledge-sharing for evidence-based decision-making, while OI promotes multi-stakeholder collaboration for adaptive, sustainable solutions. A scoping review of 235 studies identifies key contributors, thematic clusters, and challenges in implementation. Findings highlight the synergy between KM and OI in overcoming barriers and enhancing ecological resilience. Future research should explore policy frameworks, digital knowledge platforms, and economic incentives to scale restoration efforts and ensure long-term sustainability.

title: End-to-End Optical Propagation Modeling for Water-to-Air Channels under Sea Surface and UAV Effects
authors: Mohamed Nennouche, Mohammad Ali Khalighi, Alexis Alfredo Dowhuszko, Djamal Merad
publish: IEEE Photonics Journal
abstract: Underwater observatories have recently emerged as an efficient solution for marine biodiversity monitoring. The primary objective of this work is to enable efficient and cost-effective data muling from underwater sensors by investigating the use of optical wireless communications to transmit data from the underwater sensors to an aerial node close to the water surface, such as an unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV). More specifically, we utilize a direct water-to-air (W2A) optical communication link between the sensor node equipped with an LED emitter and the UAV equipped with an ultra-sensitive receiver, i.e., a silicon photo-multiplier. As a main contribution, we develop a comprehensive Monte Carlo-based ray-tracing algorithm to characterize this complex channel. This framework rigorously incorporates the impact of air bubbles modeled through the Mie scattering theory, a realistic sea surface representation derived from the JONSWAP spectrum, and an analytical derivation of the channel loss resulting from UAV instability under wind-induced perturbations. Furthermore, we conduct a comprehensive analysis of the W2A channel, examining the influence of key parameters such as wind speed, transmitter configurations, and receiver characteristics. The end-to-end performance evaluation demonstrates the practical feasibility of the proposed approach, achieving a bit-error rate of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10^{-3}$</tex-math></inline-formula> at a data rate of 1 Mbps for a transmitter depth of  47 m and wind speeds up to 13 m/s.

title: Fluid Antenna System-Enabled UAV Communications in the Finite Blocklength Regime
authors: Xusheng Zhu, Kai-Kit Wong, Hanjiang Hong, Han Xiao, Hao Xu, Tuo Wu, Chan-Byoung Chae
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: This paper develops a comprehensive framework for the performance analysis of fluid antenna system (FAS)-enabled unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) relaying networks operating in the finite blocklength regime. This work establishes a rigorous methodology for characterizing system reliability under diverse propagation environments. Closed-form expressions for the block error rate (BLER) are derived by employing a tractable eigenvalue-based approximation of the spatially correlated UAV-to-user link, whose underlying independent diversity components are modeled as Nakagami-m fading. This approach addresses both line-of-sight (LoS) dominant rural and probabilistic non-line-of-sight (NLoS) urban scenarios. Furthermore, a high signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) asymptotic analysis is developed, revealing the fundamental diversity order of the UAV-to-user link. Based on this, we further address the practical issue of energy efficiency. A realistic energy efficiency maximization problem is formulated, which explicitly accounts for the time and energy overhead in the FAS port selection process. An efficient hierarchical algorithm is then proposed to jointly optimize the key system parameters. Extensive numerical results validate the analysis and illustrate that while FASs can yield substantial power gains, the operational overhead introduces a non-trivial trade-off, leading to an optimal number of ports and fundamentally different UAV deployment strategies in rural versus urban environments. In summary, this work provides both foundational analysis and practical design guidelines for FAS-enabled UAV communications.

title: Scalable Decomposition Gradients for Multimodal Learning
authors: Mengxuan Wang, Hongjie Jiang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Optimizing multimodal models using unimodal objectives has proven effective in mitigating modality bias and enhancing generalization. This multitask-style training paradigm has motivated a range of gradient-based optimization methods. However, existing approaches predominantly focus on adjusting gradient magnitudes during aggregation, while overlooking the directional inconsistency between the final update direction and the gradients derived from unimodal and multimodal objectives, which may result in suboptimal optimization. To address this issue, we propose Scalable Decomposition Gradients (SDG), a novel framework for conflict-free optimization in multimodal learning. SDG explicitly decouples the optimization of gradient magnitude and direction. Specifically, we employ modality-aware orthogonal projections to remove conflicting components across gradients, ensuring that the final update direction aligns with both unimodal and multimodal learning objectives. We further utilize the projection residuals to adaptively scale each gradient, balancing their relative contributions and mitigating magnitude disparities. Theoretical analysis demonstrates that such conflict-free updates with consistent step sizes promote balanced convergence across losses from different modalities. Extensive experiments on several widely used multimodal benchmarks validate the effectiveness of SDG, showing notable performance improvements without introducing additional model complexity.

title: A Novel Five-Level Switched-Capacitor Inverter Topology Featuring Uniform Device Voltage Stress
authors: Atif Iqbal, Mohammad Zaid, Jagabar Sathik, Jaroslaw Guzinski
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper introduces a new five-level switched-capacitor multilevel inverter (SCMLI) topology that operates effectively over a broad input-voltage range, making it ideal for renewable energy applications such as photovoltaic (PV) systems. Unlike conventional SCMLI structures that rely on multiple capacitors and complex balancing networks, the proposed topology achieves both voltage boosting and inherent capacitor self-balancing using only a single switched capacitor. The reduced number of switches and passive components lowers conduction and switching losses, thereby improving overall efficiency. A comprehensive comparison with existing topologies highlights the performance advantages and structural merits of the proposed design. To further validate its feasibility across different operating conditions, extensive simulation and experimental studies are carried out. The results confirm its ability to deliver high voltage gain, maintain low Total Harmonic Distortion (THD), and ensure robust performance under varying load conditions.

title: Agentic AI-Based 5G and Beyond Radio Planning Framework
authors: Mehmet Galip Tezcan, Ahmet Yazar, Sümeye Nur Karahan, Mustafa Serdar Osmanca, Hüseyin Oktay Altun
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The evolution from Fifth Generation (5G) to Sixth Generation (6G) wireless networks introduces diverse service requirements and increases radio-planning complexity. Traditional planning approaches based on static propagation models and manual optimization struggle to adapt to dense deployments, heterogeneous services, and changing traffic patterns. This paper proposes an Agentic AI-based radio planning framework for 5G and beyond networks. The proposed architecture employs a multi-agent system in which specialized agents collaborate to perform intent interpretation, data aggregation, analysis, simulation, optimization, and verification tasks within a unified planning workflow. Language Models are used as reasoning engines that orchestrate domain-specific planning tools and heterogeneous data sources. The framework integrates operator intents, network measurements, geographic information, and demand-related data through interface, agent, tool, and data layers. The planning problem is formalized as a multi-objective optimization task balancing coverage, demand satisfaction, and energy efficiency. Quantitative evaluations, including preliminary simulations, control-level analysis, operator-inspired deployment scenarios, baseline comparisons, and reasoning-accuracy assessment, demonstrate the practical relevance of the proposed framework. In addition, the framework is extended to support emerging domains such as Internet of Vehicles (IoV) environments and proactive sidelink collision management in Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) systems. The proposed approach demonstrates how agentic architectures can support scalable, explainable, and progressively autonomous radio planning for next-generation wireless networks.

title: Development of the New IEEE Recommended Practice for Prognostics and Health Management
authors: Louis J. Gullo, Rex Sallade
publish: IEEE Reliability Magazine
abstract: This article discusses the development of a new IEEE standard, IEEE P1856.1, titled “Recommended Practice for Development and Implementation of Prognostic and Health Management (PHM) Systems in Accordance with IEEE-1856.” This article discusses the collaboration between the IEEE Reliability Society Standards Committee (RS-SC) and the Society of Automotive Engineers (SAE) in developing PHM-related standards. This article is a follow-up to the article published in the Reliability Magazine, 4th edition, titled “History of the First IEEE Prognostics and Health Management Standard.”

title: PSFD-Net: Progressive Supervision-Based Frequency Domain Decoupling Network for Hyperspectral and Multispectral Image Fusion
authors: Yan Mou, Genji Yuan, Jintao Song, Jinjiang Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Hyperspectralmultispectral image fusion (HMIF) aims to reconstruct high-fidelity, high spatial-resolution hyperspectral images (HR-HSI) by combining the complementary features of both modalities. However, the low spatial resolution of low-resolution hyperspectral images (LR-HSI) and the sparse spectral sampling of high-resolution multispectral images (HR-MSI) limit the representation of complementary features. Spatial-domain methods still suffer from spatial and spectral distortions, which stem from the coupling between spatial and spectral features in the spatial domain, making it difficult to decouple and calibrate complementary features and ultimately affecting fusion accuracy. To address this issue, we tackle the feature coupling problem from a frequency-domain perspective. Specifically, we propose a Progressive Supervision-Based Frequency Domain Decoupling Network (PSFD-Net). The core idea of PSFD-Net is to decouple, calibrate, and effectively fuse high- and low-frequency components in the frequency domain, while imposing training constraints to facilitate high-fidelity fusion reconstruction. We first introduce an Adaptive Frequency Decoupling (AFDM) Module to decompose features into high- and low-frequency components, and then design a Physically-Aware Feature Calibration (PAFC) Module to calibrate and enhance frequency components under physically-aware constraints. Finally, a Full-frequency Guided SpatialSpectral Fusion (FGSS) Module is employed to effectively integrate frequency information under full-frequency guidance. In addition, we design a progressive supervision strategy based on an annealing mechanism to achieve high-fidelity reconstruction from global spectral information to local structural details. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed PSFD-Net outperforms current state-of-the-art methods.

title: Hierarchical Semantic Segmentation for Autonomous Driving in Unstructured Traffic
authors: K. Namitha, Athi Narayanan
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Semantic segmentation is critical for accurate perception in autonomous driving, particularly in unstructured traffic conditions like India. The Indian Driving Dataset (IDD), unlike organized road datasets, has uncertain borders, erratic traffic behavior, and extensive occlusions. The present article introduces three useful improvements and benchmarks the state-of-the-art CNN and transformer-based models on IDD to improve semantic segmentation for Indian traffic. First, a hierarchical cascaded segmentation framework is proposed, which improves Level-2 mIoU by 3.1% by utilizing IDD’s multi-level label taxonomy to direct fine-grained segmentation. Second, a Scene Complexity Index (SCI) is introduced to quantify image complexity based on object density, class variety, occlusion, and illumination, indicating transformers’ greater robustness in complex settings. Finally, SegFormer-Lite, a transformer optimized for deployment, uses knowledge distillation and 8-bit quantization to double inference performance and reduce parameters by 57% while maintaining accuracy. Experiments reveal that SegFormer-B2 delivers the best baseline performance, while the proposed approaches improve fine-grained accuracy and real-time deployment.

title: A Sentiment-Informed Algorithmic Cryptocurrency Trading Strategy Validated Across Diverse Market Conditions
authors: Rentaro Noguchi, Ashrita Mahadevu, Aariv Aggarwal, Huifang Qin
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Cryptocurrency markets are highly volatile, making short-term price prediction challenging. Because cryptocurrency prices are largely driven by supply and demand, prior work has explored social media sentiment to predict price changes, typically finding weak correlations. Reported successes usually come from richer frameworks such as those combining sentiment with financial indicators, making it unclear whether gains arise from sentiment signals alone. Prior studies are often limited to short windows under one year; although they demonstrate success in specific market conditions, such studies often fail to account for year-to-year changes in market behavior. We developed a rule-based sentiment-informed trading algorithm using signals extracted from Reddit comments on six cryptocurrencies from 2018 to 2025. Our algorithm, Cryptocurrency Algorithm using Relative Volume Sentiment (CARVS), achieved a return of 72,240.4% and a Sortino ratio of 3.035 over eight years, compared with 1,235.9% and 1.216 for a buy-and-hold baseline. CARVS also outperformed a raw sentiment polarity baseline, which replaced the Relative Volume Sentiment signal with unprocessed sentiment polarity scores to isolate the contribution of our signal construction and noise reduction methods. This baseline produced a return of -15.2% and a Sortino ratio of 0.748. All reported results are net of transaction costs. These results were validated over an out-of-sample period of 2024–2025, in which CARVS generated statistically significant risk-adjusted excess returns over both baselines. Evaluated over the longest available historical period for the selected cryptocurrencies, these results suggest that properly handled social media sentiment can serve as an independent, effective trading signal in cryptocurrency markets.

title: Broad Learning Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory for Robotic Peg-in-Hole Assembly Based on Force and Position Sequence Modeling
authors: Sheng Liu, Yangjun Liu, Sheng Xu, Senlin Fang, Zhi-Xin Yang, Chenguang Yang
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: This article proposes a learning-based method, named Broad Learning Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BLS-biLSTM) method, for robotic bidirectional peg-in-hole assembly tasks. First, the bidirectional assembly tasks are formulated, and the bidirectional assembly data collection method is proposed, from which the manipulation skills will be learned. Second, a new learning approach named BLS-biLSTM is developed. It benefits from the robust feature extraction capabilities of broad learning systems with the deep sequence modeling abilities of bidirectional LSTM, facilitating comprehensive feature extraction and enhancement of the expert-demonstrated assembly trajectories with force data. As a consequence, the proposed method outperforms the methods based on the Transformer and recurrent neural networks in the offline simulation of robotic USB assembly. In addition, the superiority of the proposed BLS-biLSTM over other methods is further verified by real-robot USB insertion and pulling experiments. Moreover, the proposed BLS-biLSTM demonstrates leading generalization ability in previously unseen Type-C, high-definition multimedia interface (HDMI), and two-prong charger assembly tasks. Furthermore, the performance of the BLS-biLSTM with different architectural hyperparameters is studied, as well as the ablations of connection orders.

authors: Ali Khalid, Jaiaid Mobin, Sumanth Rao Appala, Avinash Maurya, Stephany Berrio Perez, M. Mustafa Rafique, Fawad Ahmad

title: Semantic Index Encoder for Transformer-Based Hyperspectral Image Classification
authors: Romario Gualdrón-Hurtado, Javier Torres-Quintero, Laura Galvis, Henry Arguello
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: We present a hyperspectral image (HSI) classifier that fuses a Vision Transformer (ViT) with a semantic index encoder. The encoder computes physically grounded remotesensing indices (e.g., NDVI, NDWI, NDRE, NDBI) using wavelength windows. This yields a compact numeric semantic embedding that is linearly fused with ViT logits. The architecture is end-to-end trainable, numerically stable, requires no textual tokenization, and yields optional humanreadable pixel narratives at inference. Experiments on six HS public datasets demonstrate consistent gains over a ViT baseline, highlighting the benefit of integrating physics-inspired indices into transformer-based HSI classification.

title: AI-Driven Inventory Optimization in Supply Chains
authors: Musab Kasim Alqudah, Haitham M. Alzoubi, Ahmad Mohammad Alzghoul, Miral Samarah
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: The successful flow of goods and services through the supply chain is inextricably linked to efficient inventory management. This is particularly the case in a globalized world with dynamic market demands. Conventional approaches to inventory optimization are often incapable of keeping pace with fast changes in the supply chain variables which is one reason inefficiencies like stockouts, overstocking, and increased costs are prevalent. The paper discusses the role of AI in inventory optimization and assesses its capability to enhance supply chain management. It is through a combination of machine learning, predictive analytics, and real-time data that AI-driven systems can better predict demand, adjust inventory levels, and improve decision-making. The work looks at current AI models in inventory management, evaluating their performance and the challenges faced including data quality, systems' integration and bias. To illustrate the theories in a real-world context, a case study was conducted on AI-driven inventory optimization, giving an insight into its functioning at a retail supply center. Improvements in efficiency, cost-cutting, and customer satisfaction were noted. According to the findings, the use of AI in the development of resilient and agile supply chains is highlighted, and future research directions and practical applications for businesses wishing to remain competitive in a fast-changing marketplace are discussed.

title: A New Multi-Class Framework for Electrocardiogram Arrhythmia Classification Using Artificial Intelligence
authors: Mohammad Zuhair Abo Hadba, Amira J. Zaylaa, Mohammad Ayache
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases remain a leading global cause of mortality, highlighting the need for accurate and scalable electrocardiogram (ECG) analysis systems that can support clinicians without increasing interpretation burden. This paper presents an inter-patient ECG arrhythmia classification framework based on a hybrid neuro-symbolic architecture. The system is evaluated under the clinically realistic AAMI DS1/DS2 inter-patient protocol using the MIT-BIH Arrhythmia Database, while additional beats from the MIT-BIH Supraventricular Arrhythmia Database (SVDB) and the St. Petersburg INCART database are incorporated during training to reduce class imbalance and improve robustness. The proposed framework combines beat-centered segmentation and z-score normalization, a lightweight 1D CNN–BiGRU backbone, temporal RR-interval context features, augmentation-based oversampling for minority classes, and a neuro-symbolic arbitration module that integrates neural outputs with physiologically motivated rules. Experimental results show that temporal context substantially improves supraventricular beat detection, while the neuro-symbolic stage improves overall decision consistency and interpretability. Statistical validation using bootstrap confidence intervals, McNemar’s test, and Cochran’s Q test confirms that the observed gains are significant. The framework is lightweight, interpretable, and suitable for real-time ECG monitoring and human-in-the-loop clinical decision support.

title: Satellite-based Image Transmission with Hazard Detection and Real-Time Environmental Monitoring via LoRa and ESP32-CAM
authors: Daniel George Ashok, Parthiv Raj, Pragathi B, Suganya J
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The Satellite System uses real-time image processing and environmental monitoring to detect hazards such as fires or chemical spills. The ESP32-CAM module captures images, while on-board sensors measure temperature, pressure, and orientation of the satellite. This data is transmitted via LoRa (Long Range) to a ground station, where they are processed and used to create hazard maps and safe paths. The system aims to improve detection and mitigation, with future enhancements to optimize navigation and risk assessment. Experimental results demonstrate successful image transmission with average latencies of 62.3s for QVGA (18.0 KB), 148.8s for VGA (35.0 KB) and 171.5s for SVGA (32.5 KB) over a 100-meter range, validating core functionality under constrained field conditions.

title: Enhancing Transformer-Based Hidden Suicidal Intention Detection with Sequential Modeling and Psycholinguistic Feature Fusion
authors: Midhun Prahash S. R, Rhea Alphonsa Jose, Sam. V. George
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Mental health crisis intervention immediately follows the use of digital text analysis to detect concealed suicidal intentions through Natural Language Processing (NLP) operations. Safety-critical scenarios experience prediction mistakes because current transformer models struggle to understand complex language features that include negation, context changes, and sarcasm. The study conducts a complete assessment of seven fine-tuning strategies while testing various model structures that tackle these issues. The study evaluated the performance of baseline BERT and DistilBERT models and hybrid systems that integrate Bidirectional LSTMs (BiLSTMs) with psycholinguistic features from the Linguistic Inquiry and Word Count (LIWC) framework. The research investigates layer-freezing as a method to enhance computational performance while sustaining system effectiveness. The research used a combined dataset of actual and simulated data for assessment purposes. Dataset evaluation included accuracy and F1-Score and precision and recall as well as training duration and model size. The hybrid DistilBERT system that includes BiLSTM and LIWC features demonstrates better accuracy, efficiency, and robustness than transformer-based baselines for hidden suicidal language detection. Practical guidance from the research helps select proper system designs for mental health monitoring operations.

title: ESP32-Based Wearable Safety Monitoring System for Coal Mine Workers
authors: S. Vimalnath, Kavimani K, Mohanaprasath K. R, Manoj M. T, Logeshwaran J
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Coal mining exposes workers to severe safety hazards caused by toxic gases, extreme temperature variations, oxygen deficiency, and intense physical workload. Although industrial safety practices have improved, many existing monitoring systems operate in isolation by tracking either environmental parameters or basic health indicators, thereby limiting comprehensive risk assessment and timely emergency response. This paper presents an ESP32-based wearable safety monitoring system designed to continuously monitor both environmental and physiological conditions of coal mine workers. The proposed system integrates temperature and toxic gas sensors with heart rate and blood oxygen saturation (SpO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) sensors into a compact, low-power wearable device. Sensor data are transmitted to a cloud platform through an IoT framework, where threshold-based analytics identify unsafe conditions and generate real-time alerts for supervisors and rescue personnel. An integrated emergency SOS mechanism enables workers to request immediate assistance during critical situations. In addition, continuous data logging supports historical analysis for identifying recurring risk patterns and enhancing preventive safety strategies. Experimental evaluation demonstrates reliable sensing, timely alert generation, and energy-efficient operation, leading to improved situational awareness, reduced emergency response time, and proactive decision-making in underground mining environments. The proposed system offers a unified and scalable solution to enhance worker safety and operational reliability in coal mines.

title: TeaLeafVision: An Explainable and Robust Deep Learning Framework for Tea Leaf Disease Classification
authors: Rafi Ahamed, Sidratul Moon Nafsin, Md. Abir Rahman, Tasnia Tarannum Roza, Munaia Jannat Easha, Abu Raihan
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: As the world's second most-consumed beverage after water, tea is not just a cultural staple but a global economic force of profound scale and influence. More than a mere drink, it represents a quiet negotiation between nature, culture, and the human desire for a moment of reflection. So, the precise identification and detection of tea leaf disease is crucial. With this goal, we have evaluated several Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) models, among them three shows noticeable performance including DenseNet201, MobileNetV2, InceptionV3 on the teaLeafBD dataset. teaLeafBD dataset contains seven classes, six disease classes and one healthy class, collected under various field conditions reflecting real-world challenges. Among the CNN models, DenseNet201 has achieved the highest test accuracy of 99%. In order to enhance the model reliability and interpretability, we have implemented Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM), occlusion sensitivity analysis and adversarial training techniques to increase the noise resistance of the model. Finally, we have developed a prototype in order to leverage the model's capabilities on real life agriculture. This paper illustrates the deep learning model's capabilities to classify the disease in real-life tea leaf disease detection and management.

title: Hybrid Sustainable and Popularity-Aware Music Recommendation System Using Machine Learning
authors: Barninee Samanta, Khushi Singh, Debolina Ghosh, Sonal Jain, Aadi Poddar, Mahendra Kumar Gourisaria
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: Music Recommendation Systems recommends songs like a given input song based on metadata such as genre, artist name and track name. In this study, a framework using machine learning is developed to identify –popular– music tracks based on the idea of balancing popularity for a broad market with sustainable features in the audio. The data used for this study includes <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 1 4, 0 0 0}$</tex> different recordings and was drawn from a wide variety of genres available on Spotify. To determine the sustainability of a given recording, we developed a composite feature that brought together features of danceability, energy, valence, tempo, and loudness. Minmax scaling has applied tempo and loudness to normalize the data. Z-Scosre Scalling have also been applied on all 13 features. Here we tested ten different machine learning models including logistic regression, decision trees, Naive Bayes, XGBoost, LightGBM, and CatBoost etc. XGBoost gives the maximum accuracy of 97.3%

title: Efficient Vector Database Management in Retrieval-Augmented Generation Systems: A Selective Embedding Update Approach
authors: Navyansh Kothari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Question-answering over large document corpora RAG systems have become viable and potent solutions to such applications. Nevertheless, classic RAG implementations are computationally inefficient in case of document updates and modification, where one has to entirely re-embed whole document collections. In this paper, we will introduce the RAG chatbot system, which is an efficient system, that uses an intelligent document versioning and differential processing method of selective embed- ding updates. We use AWS OpenSearch to store vectors, Amazon Bedrock to provide embeddings and inference of language models, and a pipeline of smart PDF treatment which augments the difference between new documents and their modifications. Experiments in comparative analysis show that the processing time, the cost of computation and scalability of the system is much more improved. The experimental outcomes demonstrate the reduction of up to 85 percent of the overhead on embedding generation when document is updated, and the accuracy of retrieval is still maintained. The proposed system has response time of queries of less than a second and real time document update without failure to access its services.

title: Automatic Generation of Responses for Higher Education Question-Answering Robots Based on an Improved Seq2seq-Attention Model
authors: Jiahui Hu
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI in Education Technology and Applications (AIETA)
abstract: Generating responses based on simple rule matching only involves mechanical content splicing, resulting in low-quality responses generated by higher education questionanswering robots. To address this issue, this paper studies an automatic response generation method for higher education question-answering robots based on an improved Seq2SeqAttention model. First, web crawling technology is used to collect higher education question-answering data from multiple platforms. After cleaning to remove noise and redundancy and word segmentation, data augmentation techniques such as synonym replacement and sentence restructuring are used to expand the data and enhance the data in the higher education question-answering domain. Then, a BiLSTM is used as the encoder to encode the input sequence bidirectionally, and the decoder introduces a self-attention mechanism to focus on the information of the generated sequence. A multi-head attention mechanism is used to capture the features of the input sequence from multiple subspaces. Finally, the improved Seq2Seq-Attention model is applied to capture the semantic features of higher education question-answering. Finally, the enhanced dataset is divided and the improved model is trained. After input processing, encoding, attention calculation, and decoding, the model inference module loads the trained model to generate output. The output is then processed by the response generation module and returned to the user, realizing automatic response generation for the higher education question-answering robot. Experiments show that after applying the research method, the METEOR value exceeds 0.95 in multiple scenarios. Compared with the comparison method, this method maintains a stable high BLEU score across various text length ranges, and the fluctuation of the average sentence length is closer to that of standard text. At the same time, the ROUGE score remains stable above 0.95 and significantly outperforms the competition, effectively improving the quality of response generation for the higher education question-answering robot.

title: Process of Ecommerce Shipments using Two-Dimensional Encryption
authors: I. Manimozhi, S. Uma, M. Deepika, A. Akila, A.Naresh Kumar, Y. Mohammed Zakir Hussain
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The rapid expansion of the Internet has fueled the growth of e-commerce, leading to advancements in the logistics sector. However, traditional logistics systems often struggle with safeguarding personal information, making privacy breaches a significant concern. This study explores the various channels through which privacy information may be exposed during e-commerce logistics and introduces a two-dimensional code-based encryption system designed to enhance data security. The system was tested on a laboratory server, demonstrating that mobile terminals with distinct access permissions could retrieve only the necessary logistics details within their authorized scope. Additionally, unauthorized terminals attempting to scan unrelated two-dimensional codes received meaningless error outputs, ensuring data protection. The findings confirm that two-dimensional code encryption technology effectively secures personal information throughout the logistics process.

title: Early Detection of Autism Spectrum Disorder in Children Using Deep Learning-Based Facial Image Analysis
authors: Nithisha J
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is a neurodevelopmental disorder that lacks social interaction, communication, and behavioral patterns. Early diagnosis has a great impact on the outcomes of intervention; the traditional modes of diagnosing patients are time consuming, subjective and in most cases results in late diagnosis. This paper introduces that non-invasive and automated deep learning to detect early ASD when children are involved and whether the analysis is conducted on a facial image. The system removes discriminative facial patterns in social and emotional behavior like eye contact, facial expressions, and social smiling with facial landmark detection and convolutional neural networks (CNNs). Pretrained buildings promote generalization and adjustability to fresh data. Benchmarking of experimental performance on the face image dataset shows that the proposed method is more accurate than the traditional machine learning models, and the computational efficiency is sufficient to implement in the real world. The findings suggest that deep learning based facial image screening can be used as a cost effective and scalable tool of early ASD detection. The experimental results show better performance in comparison with the traditional machine learning models, which illustrates the viability of cost effective and scalable early screening.

title: EEG Signal-Based Spectral and Complexity Features Analysis for Emotion Recognition Utilizing CNN Architecture
authors: Rabita Hasan, Sheikh Md. Rabiul Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: This study investigates the efficacy of EEG signal-based spectral and complexity features for emotion detection through a one-dimensional Convolutional Neural Network (1D CNN) architecture. Two complementary feature sets were extracted from EEG recordings: Power Spectral Density (PSD), reflecting frequency-domain energy distribution and Sample Entropy, representing temporal signal complexity. The model classified emotional states along the valence and arousal dimensions. Experimental analysis demonstrated that PSD features achieved higher accuracy 76.85% for valence and 74.67% for arousal compared with 68.78% and 66.82% obtained using Sample Entropy. To verify the statistical significance of these differences, an independent-samples t-test was applied on accuracies from 10-fold cross-validation. The results showed significant improvements in PSD performance for both emotional dimensions: t (13.71) = 6.95, p = 7.61×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-6</sup> for valence and t (15.69) = 4.75, p = 2.30×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-4</sup> for arousal. These findings confirm that PSD-based spectral features capture more stable and discriminative neural representations than entropy-based measures, establishing a statistically validated and computationally efficient 1D-CNN framework for EEG-driven emotion recognition and affect-adaptive human–computer interaction.

title: Resilient IoT Disaster Communication using Adaptive AODV Routing in LoRa Mesh Networks
authors: Usha S, Karthik M, Sanmugapriya G, Vignesh E G, VenuPrasaath V R, Aksharaa B
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The natural disasters such as floods, earthquakes, and cyclones have the potential of devastating the normal communication systems; hence information flow is very crucial in rescue and relief operations. The project involves a LoRabased mesh network, which will be used to create a natural disaster communication system with a long-range and lowpower communication that will not rely on infrastructures to send data. The suggested system would be made of LoRaequipped nodes installed throughout the impacted area and create a self-organizing multi-hop mesh network. The nodes can detect and transmit important information including environmental information, location information and emergency alerts and pass information of the neighbouring nodes to ensure network connectivity, even when a node or a link fails to operate. A gateway node collects the transmitted data and sends the data to a local server where it is processed, stored, and visualized. It includes a graphical user interface (GUI) through which you can monitor the health of the network, the state of nodes, signal strength, and disasters and receive alerts in real-time. The system has been designed keeping in mind robustness, scalability, and power consumption; hence, the system is ideal in places where rapid deployment is necessary, or remote locations where disasters are likely to plague. The offered LoRa-based mesh network offers an option to have a decentralized data structure that guarantees situational data backup and emergency response in case of natural disasters. The performance of the proposed system is evaluated through the experimental setup consists of three nodes.

title: Yield Prediction in Organic Farming
authors: Amneh Shaban
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Organic farming has become a really popular and durable alternative to traditional high input agriculture as it is good for the environment as well as health. On the other hand, however, it is still a really hard job to forecast the crop yield in organic farming because of the complicated interplay of extenuating circumstances such as soil quality, etc., and pest dynamics and crop management practices. Through the use of machine learning (ML) techniques this paper will dive into the yield prediction in organic farming based on various data sources such as soil composition, and weather forecasts, and past yield records. The study will devise advanced ML models that are used in Web services such as random forests, support vector machines, and neural networks for the purpose of creating an intense method for perfect yield predictions. The models presented will be trained on and confirmed by organic farm datasets such as organic soil amendments, pest control methods, and crop rotation patterns. Hence, the results show that the ML-based method is way better than the traditional statistical way as it gives higher accuracy and is more flexible to the recent changes in organic farming. The research is a paradigm of the combination of artificial intelligence with organic farming practices that would give a good harvest to farmers as well as help in global food security and sustainability in an environmentally friendly way.

title: C-Trust System for Anti-Counterfeiting in Supply Chain Management Using Block Chain Technology
authors: Kopparthi Bhaskara Naga Pradeep, R. Yugha, Illa Mohan Siva Rakesh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Counterfeiting of products is a serious issue in the world market, as it affects brand value, consumer confidence, and public safety. Holograms, barcodes, or packaging markers are considered to be quite traditional authentication mechanisms that are easy to reproduce and cannot be utilized in the fight against contemporary counterfeiting modes. This study offers a Crypto Based Product Authentication System, which is a combination of blockchain, cryptographic hashing, role-based access, and verification based on QR code to establish end-toend product authenticity. To ensure the retail sale verification, the system applies the Hardhat-based Ethereum test network for decentralized and immutable recording of product lifecycle data, AES-256 encryption as the way of securing QR code identifiers, and the use of the SHA-256 hash to maintain the privacy of customers. Details on the movement of the products can be updated on the blockchain by authorized users, such as manufacturers, distributors, retailers, and staff members, which ensures an immutable supply-chain trail. Customers can authenticate products through encrypted QR scan and identity validation based on OTP. Customers have an independent reporting system that enables them to report suspected counterfeit products so that verifiers can hear their complaints and reward them.

title: Revolutionizing Grid-Tied PV Inverters: Overcoming Common Mode Challenges with Shoot- Through Solutions
authors: G.T Sundar Rajan, C. Bhuvaneswari, A. Ramesh Babu, M. Pushpavalli, W. Abithamemala, J. Barnabas Paul Glady
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The extensive implementation of grid-integrated photovoltaic (PV) power systems is hindered by issues with ordinary transformerless inverters, such as common-mode leakage currents and shoot-through failures. The amalgamation of these technological issues undermines system operating efficiency, leading to reduced dependability and posing significant electrical safety risks and electromagnetic dangers. The suggested method employs an innovative transformerless inverter architecture to address current challenges. A dualbuck half-bridge inverter linked to a buck-boost converter enhances performance capabilities while ensuring safety systems. The system design eliminates shoot-through failure that impacts conventional inverters during concurrent switch conduction in the same leg, hence assuring improved operational stability. An enhancement in topology enables the system to eradicate hazardous common-mode leakage currents by establishing a direct conduit from the PV array ground to the grid, so addressing intrinsic issues associated with transformerless systems. The inverter system, via its buck-boost converter, enhances its capacity to accommodate various input voltage ranges, making it suitable for numerous photovoltaic array configurations. The efficiency of dual-buck half-bridge inverters increases due to their capacity to prevent current from passing through the body diodes of switching devices, hence reducing power conversion losses and improving overall efficiency. The dual-buck half-bridge inverter has heightened applicability in residential and commercial renewable energy systems due to its superior operating performance and greater dependability.

title: Design and Development of a Low-Cost 3D-Printed Hand Exoskeleton for Rehabilitation
authors: Tasnim Anam Tusti, Sraboni Hossain, G. M. Mahmudur Rahman, Md Abu Shahid Chowdhury
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Stroke survivors often experience difficulty in voluntary hand movements due to hand impairments. Most commercially available hand exoskeletons are typically expensive, making them unaffordable for patients in low-income countries, such as Bangladesh. To address this problem, this work presents a cost-effective, 3D-printed, fully wearable, portable, and easily customizable hand exoskeleton using locally available resources and simple technology. The model is designed in SOLIDWORKS. The device is employed with a rotary-encoder-based control system instead of biosensors to ensure operational reliability and consistent performance with minimal hardware complexity, particularly in rural areas. The hand exoskeleton has dual functionality: an assistive mode to support daily activities and a therapeutic mode for structured motor rehabilitation therapy sessions. Evaluation of the model through manual control and automated therapy sessions with a post-stroke patient demonstrated significant improvement in the finger range of motion (ROM) and gripping ability, ensuring its effectiveness for neurorehabilitation.

title: A Multi-Stage Framework for Sand-Dust Image Enhancement Using Color Compensation and Visibility Restoration
authors: Muhammad Khawaja Kashif Masood, Pablo Otero
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: The process of handling sand dust in images acquired within extreme weather conditions continues to be a major challenge in image improvement. This paper seeks to develop a creative, multi-step process to remove sand dust and improve images with color tuning, dehazing, sharpening, contrast editing, and noise reduction preprocessing transformations. As of the beginning, a process of color compensation is implemented to evenly align color channels and reduce color shifts caused by dust in the images. Following that, a dehazing operation is carried out by using CLAHE which helps to restore visual clarity because of haze and dust distortion. Then, after sharpening, an unsharp mask is applied to the image to clean up the picture before contrast and brightness are enhanced for a better visual realism. In a nutshell, we apply median filter to preserve key image properties by eliminating noise. By comparing both traditional and GAN-based methods, our proposed method outperforms them in terms of both qualitative and quantitative analysis. The proposed method’s performance is assessed on a dataset of sand-dust images and compared with all previous methods, showing a significant improvement. It provides a comprehensive solution for sand dust removal and image enhancement, with diverse applications in autonomous vehicles, vision systems, and outdoor surveillance.

title: Performance Analysis of Multi-Source Intelligent Reflective Surface-Assisted Bi-Directional Full-Duplex Communication System
authors: R Rajesh, K T Panneerselvam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: This study investigates the performance of a multisource intelligent reflecting surface (IRS)-assisted bi-directional full-duplex (FD) communication system operating under Rayleigh fading channel environment.
