The proposed system model comprises multiple source nodes and a sink node, employing an optimized node selection strategy to facilitate bidirectional communication with the sink node through the IRS. The selection of source node is based on maximizing the end-to-end signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR). Closed-form analytical expressions are derived for the outage probability and its asymptotic behavior, providing a rigorous characterization of system reliability. Numerical results are presented to evaluate the impact of key system parameters on outage performance. The accuracy of the analytical derivations is corroborated through Monte Carlo simulations. The numerical results highlight the effectiveness of the proposed selection scheme and provide significant design considerations for IRS-assisted bi-directional FD systems.

title: Arabic Speech Synthesis: A Comprehensive Analysis of Current Resources and Application Advancements
authors: Hamza M. Alabdallah, Suleiman Y. Yerima, Khaled Shaalan
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: This paper presents a systematic review of the state of Arabic text-to-speech (TTS) covering primary works in the literature from 2016 to 2025. It analyses resources, modelling strategies, domain-specific applications, and evaluation practices. We find two competing data regimes: quality-first mono-speaker, fully diacritised corpora (ASC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 3.4 ~\mathrm{h}$</tex>; ClArTTS 12h; MSAC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$15-16 ~\mathrm{h})$</tex> that enable high MOS under controlled conditions, and quantity-first multi-speaker resources (QASR <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 2000 ~\mathrm{h}$</tex>) that allow zero-shot, dialect-aware synthesis but require heavy cleaning and pseudo-labelling. Architecturally, the field transitions from HMM/parametric pipelines with explicit diacritisation and DNN G2P to end-to-end Tacotron/Tacotron2 with WaveNet/WaveGlow, and most recently to zero-shot XTTS-style multi-dialect cloning. Comparative results show that careful fine-tuning of pretrained models can match or exceed from-scratch Arabic Tacotron on speech quality; however, challenges persist: (1) robust vowelisation/G2P under diglossia; (2) limited multi-speaker, affectrich corpora; (3) fragmented evaluation (MOS without shared protocols; sparse objective measures such as WER on synthesised speech); and (4) fairness/ethics (gender/dialect imbalance; identity cloning). We outline a roadmap for front-end-aware end-to-end training, dialect-balanced corpora with consent, and multi-aspect evaluation (naturalness, intelligibility, similarity, task success) to advance reliable and inclusive Arabic TTS.

title: Spiking Neural Networks in Neuromorphic Computing: A New Paradigm for Machine Learning
authors: Prasanta Panda, Debaryaan Sahoo, Mukundan A P, Debarjun Sahoo
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: The growth of neuromorphic computing has enabled the development of efficient and biologically inspired computational models, with Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) emerging as a promising approach. Unlike conventional neural models, SNNs represent neurons and synaptic interactions in a manner closer to biological processes, using discrete spikes to transmit information. This paper presents a detailed methodology for implementing Spiking Neural Networks on neuromorphic hardware, describing synaptic connectivity, spike generation mechanisms, and the evaluation of network architectures for energy-efficient operation. Neuromorphic chips are uniquely designed to support the event-driven, lowpower operations of SNNs, making them suitable for applications requiring real-time and energy-efficient processing. Our methodology includes configuring neuron models, establishing spike-based synaptic interactions, generating spike trains from input stimuli, and incorporating Spike-Timing-Dependent Plasticity (STDP) to enable learning. The implemented SNN is simulated using the Brian2 framework and evaluated on the MNIST dataset, achieving 91.66% accuracy. Using recent Loihi 2 benchmarks from literature, we extrapolate the potential energy and latency benefits when deploying such SNNs on neuromorphic chips, underscoring their applicability in robotics, sensory processing, and edge computing.

title: A Multi-View Deep Learning Framework for Malware Detection Using Opcode Analysis
authors: Juvvala Bala Ambedkar, Morukurthi Sreenivasu, A.Iswarya Gold, Vinod Kumar Uppalapu, Somaraju Kalidindi, T. V. Sai Krishna
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Malware continues to pose a serious threat to modern computing systems, requiring effective and reliable detection mechanisms. Traditional malware detection approaches often rely on a single feature representation, which may fail to capture the diverse characteristics of malicious software. To address this limitation, this paper proposes a multi-view deep learning framework for malware detection using opcode-based static analysis. The proposed approach exploits two complementary representations of malware samples: opcode sequences modeled using recurrent neural networks to capture execution behavior, and opcode statistical features learned through fully connected neural networks to represent global structural characteristics. Multiple machine learning and deep learning models are first evaluated using individual views to establish baseline performance. A multi-view deep learning model is then introduced by jointly learning and fusing representations from both views. Experimental results on a publicly available malware opcode dataset demonstrate that the proposed multiview framework consistently outperforms single-view models, achieving improved classification performance. The findings confirm that integrating complementary opcode representations enhances the robustness and effectiveness of malware detection systems.

title: Distributed ESP32-based Smart Grid Honeypot System for Intrusion Detection
authors: Anirudh Robens, Shashwath P., Manitha P.V.
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Smart grids are critical infrastructures that are increasingly vulnerable to cyber threats because they connect with communication and IoT networks. This paper presents a lightweight, offline honeypot system built entirely with ESP32 microcontrollers to simulate a decentralized intrusion detection framework for smart grids. The system includes one central ESP32 that serves as a Wi-Fi access point. It provides a fake administrative login interface and logs any unauthorized access attempts persistently. When it detects any login activity, the main ESP32 device sends real-time alert messages to three additional ESP32 nodes using UDP. These nodes mimic smart grid components and respond by flashing onboard LEDs, giving immediate physical alerts. This is the first fully embedded, offline, peer-to-peer honeypot with distributed visual alerts and forensic logging designed specifically for smart grid cybersecurity research and education. The system is self-contained, requires no internet connection, and offers a low-cost, scalable platform that is valuable for studying cybersecurity vulnerabilities in smart grids.

title: AI-Enabled Smart Agriculture System for Intelligent Irrigation Management
authors: Snehal B. Jadhav, Shashank Kumar Singh, Saurabh B Bansod, Jayaraj U Kidav
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Efficient irrigation management is a critical challenge in modern agriculture due to increasing water scarcity and the need for sustainable farming practices. This paper presents an AI-enabled smart agriculture system designed for intelligent irrigation management using real-time sensor data and adaptive decision-making techniques. The proposed system integrates soil moisture, temperature, humidity, and environmental parameters with crop-specific inputs such as crop type and growth stage to optimize irrigation scheduling. An adaptive threshold-based approach is employed, where irrigation decisions are dynamically learned from historical sensor data rather than relying on fixed moisture limits. The system architecture supports both cloud-assisted analytics and partial edge-based decision-making using an ESP32 microcontroller, improving scalability and reducing dependency on centralized computation for small-scale deployments. Experimental evaluation and comparative analysis demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves significant water savings while maintaining favorable crop growth conditions when compared with conventional irrigation methods. The results indicate improved irrigation efficiency, reduced water wastage, and potential enhancement in crop yield stability. The proposed system offers a cost-effective, scalable, and intelligent solution suitable for precision agriculture applications, particularly in resource-constrained farming environments.

title: Machine Learning for Rainfall Prediction in Smart Cities
authors: Yousef Elsheikh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Predicting rainfall is an integral part of urban planning, h2>water resource management, the prevention of flood. In a world increasingly focused on cities powered by technology (smart cities), reliance on real-time data and predictive analytics to optimize operations and improve resilience. This paper analyzes the feasibility of using machine learning models to predict rainfall patterns, by leveraging a variety of data sources, including weather data, atmospheric parameters, and sensor information. To this end, various machine learning algorithms, like regression, decision trees, and neural networks, have been employed and their performance compared to the traditional one in rainfall forecasting. The construction, training, and testing of our models used publicly available datasets, such as those from meteorological sources and IoT sensors in urban environments; we have pointed out the importance of feature selection, preprocessing, and model tuning. The strong performance of advanced machine learning methods over traditional statistical ones could provide policymakers with many actionable insights for urban planning. This study demonstrates that machine learning can enhance weather prediction systems in smart cities, leading to sustainable urban management and disaster mitigation issues proactively resolved.

title: Multi-Hop Routing Optimization in Wireless Networks Using Matlab
authors: G. Veeravenkata Vignesh Babu, S. Rohith Reddy, N. C. Sasikiran, Debashish Pal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) depend on sensor nodes with limited power to enable multi-hop data transmission in fields like environmental monitoring and precision agriculture. It is still hard to design routing mechanisms that guarantee a long network lifetime and an acceptable end-to-end delay at the same time because of conflicting quality-of-service (QoS) requirements. This paper introduces a QoS-constrained multihop routing framework that explicitly equilibrates residual node energy and transmission delay through a lightweight weighted cost function. This work's originality comes from the systematic formulation and quantitative evaluation of different QoS operating modes-delay-critical, lifetime-critical, and QoS-balanced. This makes it possible to do a clear and repeatable analysis of the trade-off under realistic energy limits. The suggested method is tested with MATLAB-based simulations that use a firstorder radio energy model and standard network lifetime metrics. Simulation results show that the QoS-balanced mode increases network lifetime by 44.62% compared to Dijkstra-based routing and cuts average end-to-end delay by 53.23%. A comparison with the Min-Hop and Energy-Greedy routing schemes shows that the proposed framework strikes a good balance between delay stability and energy efficiency. This makes it a good choice for QoS-sensitive multi-hop wireless sensor network applications.

title: An End to End Hybrid CNN and LSTM Framework for ECG Classification with Demographic and Statistical Insights on QT and QRS Dynamics
authors: Maitreya Moola, Abhirup Basu, Mayank Kishor, Latha P, Suman Saha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Automated interpretation of electrocardiogram (ECG) readings remains challenging because ECG shape and timing can vary across patients and demographic groups. This study presents an end to end ECG evaluation framework that combines a hybrid convolutional neural network and long short term memory model with statistical analysis of key ECG parameters across demographic groups. The framework is evaluated on the PTB-XL dataset, which includes 22,497 ECG beats from individuals aged 2 to 89 years. To assess demographic effects, QT interval, QRS duration, and heart rate are analyzed across sex and age groups. The statistical results show significant differences in QT interval and QRS duration associated with age and sex, although the effect sizes remain small. This makes contextual interpretation important. Grad-CAM and SHAP interpretation are used to examine the proposed CNN and LSTM system, which achieves strong classification performance while offering better transparency. Overall, the results show that demographic analysis can improve the generalizability, interpretability, and fairness of automated ECG classification systems.

title: Interpretable Machine Learning for Early Diabetes Prediction with Statistical Validation and Explainable AI
authors: Fathhur Rahaman Sams, Sanjana Kazi Supti, Md. Junaeid Ali, Faisal Ahmed, Mahir Faysal Haque Dipto, Raiyan Gani, Monisha Bani Nibedita Shuci, Mohammad Rifat Ahmmad Rashid, Shamim H Ripon
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Early detection of diabetes is critical to reducing complications and improving patient outcomes. Diabetes mellitus, a chronic metabolic disorder, disrupts normal glucose regulation and manifests through symptoms such as excessive urination, thirst, weakness, and impaired healing. Conventional diagnostic approaches often fail to identify early-stage indicators due to complex interactions within medical data. This study investigates interpretable machine learning methods for early diabetes prediction using a dataset of 520 patient records with 17 demographic and symptomatic attributes collected from the Sylhet Diabetes Hospital in Bangladesh. Multiple classifiers were evaluated, including Support Vector Machine, Random Forest, AdaBoost, Ridge Classifier, Naïve Bayes, and Gradient Boosting. Among these, the Random Forest classifier achieved the highest performance with an accuracy of 96.79% and an AUC-ROC score above 0.98, demonstrating strong predictive capability. To ensure reliability, formal statistical hypothesis testing was performed to validate significant demographic and symptomatic factors associated with early diabetes. Furthermore, Local Interpretable Model-Agnostic Explanations (LIME) were applied to provide transparent insights into feature contributions. The results confirm that interpretable machine learning, particularly tree-based ensemble models supported by statistical validation and explainable AI, can enhance early diabetes detection and contribute to trustworthy, data-driven clinical decision-making.

title: A Hybrid Feature Learning Framework Based on Deep Feature Integration for Better Skin Cancer Diagnosis using Transfer Learning
authors: S. Kamalesh, S. Chidambaram
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Globally, skin cancer has become increasingly common and remains one of the most serious health concerns.” cancers globally and is becoming more widespread every year. Misdiagnosis, or simply a delay in diagnosis, can greatly impact patient prognosis, most often leading to advanced disease. In this study, we present DRMv2Net, a deep learning framework for multi-class diagnosis of skin cancers that relies on hybrid transfer learning. The hybrid model creates a feature fusion pipeline that includes a variety of spatial, structural, and textural information, fusing three highly effective CNN architectures chosen from DenseNet201, ResNet101, and MobileNetV2. The proposed system adopts several highly effective preprocessing strategies, such as extensive data augmentation to balance classes, adaptive thresholding to remove hair artifacts, and image inpainting for occlusions to improve results. The experiments on ISIC 2357 and PAD-UFES 20 benchmark datasets achieved accuracies of 96.11 % and 96.17%, respectively, which exceeded CNN baselines. The results indicate that classification robustness and reliability improved considerably through hybrid feature integration when combined with preprocessing and balanced training. Given the potential for real-world applications, particularly in resource-constrained clinical and mobile health settings, this framework offers a significant tool for diagnosing skin cancers.

title: Avalife – An IoT-Based Wearable System for Real-Time Avalanche Victim Monitoring and Localization
authors: Mohamed Mubeen H, Dhanya Dharshini K, Mary Joshiny J, Aaron Kevin Cameron Theoderaj
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Avalanche rescue requires rapid location and vital sign monitoring to improve survival rates in remote, harsh environments. This paper presents AVALIFE, a wearable IoT system based on dual ESP32 modules that continuously monitor heart rate, temperature, motion, sound, and GPS location of avalanche victims. Data is transmitted offline using ESP-NOW from the victim node to a rescuer node and optionally visualized via Blynk over Wi-Fi. Experimental results show reliable short range communication and accurate sensor integration. AVALIFE enhances existing rescue technologies by incorporating physiological awareness and dual communication modes, enabling faster and better informed rescue decisions in connectivity challenged avalanche zones.

title: Design and Simulation of a Biodegradable Circular Microstrip Patch Antenna Using Chitosan Film for Millimeter-Wave Applications
authors: Koilada Bharath Surya, S Revathi, A. Christina Josephine Malathi, Vallam Yaswanth, Pothireddy Manoj Kumar Reddy, Raghavee Senthil
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: A circular microstrip patch antenna fabricated on a chitosan film substrate is presented for millimeter-wave applications. The antenna is designed and analyzed using ANSYS HFSS over the frequency range of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$60-130 \text{GHz}$</tex>. The simulated reflection coefficient <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S_{11}$</tex> indicates multiband behavior with a dominant resonance near 95 GHz, achieving a return loss of approximately -30 dB. Additional resonances are observed near 80 GHz and 120 GHz. The Voltage Standing Wave Ratio (VSWR) remains below 2 across the operating bands, indicating good impedance matching. The antenna achieves a maximum realized gain of approximately 2.7 dBi. These results demonstrate that the proposed biodegradable antenna is suitable for short-range and low-power millimeter-wave communication systems.

title: Trustworthy Explainable AI for Cross-Dataset Brain Tumor Diagnosis from MRI
authors: K. Manjusha, K. Nagaraju, Pelluri Hari Priya, Munagala Sumalatha, Konkala Tejaswini, Paruchuri Sreeshma
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Diagnosing brain tumors through magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is of great importance, correctly and reliably because of the domain shift, uncertainty in the models, and even because of insufficient interpretation of automated systems. In this paper, an integrated credible and explicable framework of artificial intelligence aimed at incorporating tumor-sensitive representation learning, exposition-steadfast feature selection, uncertainty-senient classification, and selective prediction coupled with probability modification, to aid sound clinical decision representation, is presented. On a cross-dataset evaluation protocol, which trains the models using the Figshare Brain Tumor Dataset and test them using the BraTS MRI Dataset, the proposed framework shows an accuracy of 92.37% and area under the ROC curve (AUC) of 0.948, with substantially better calibration reliability, with an expected calibration error of 0.031, in comparison to hybrid, convolutional, and transformer baselines. Quantitative explainability tests show more faithfulness (0.74) and stability (0.81) scores compared to other competing methods. Reliability maps and visualization illustrations also show consistent estimates of confidence and attention of the models focused on the lesions. These findings support the fact that the framework is always improving the predictive performance, calibration reliability, and the explanation quality across architectures. The future research will broaden the assessment to bigger multi-centric studies on clinical cohorts, study semi-supervised pretraining techniques, in addition to causal interpretability using human-in-the-loop mechanisms to enhance further the bridge between automated reasoning and expert clinical delineations.

title: Systematic Analysis of Cybersecurity Attacks and Evaluation of Mitigation Strategies
authors: Ishan Chowdhury, Somnath Sinha, Binayak Dutta
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: There are established frameworks in place to address the problem, such as OWASP, MITRE ATT&CK, but web applications and critical infrastructure keep suffering from a rapidly growing and increasingly complex threat. This paper shows a systematic empirical analysis on 14,133 records in total of 63 security categories, and it shows that the most frequent attack vectors are Hardware Interface Exploitation (161 cases), Wireless Attacks Advanced (95 cases), PLC Attacks (26 cases), Use-After-Free (22 cases), and Stack Overflow (20 cases). Statistical validation through chi-square testing (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\chi^{2}=756580,\ p<0.001$</tex>) indicates non-random attack-category relationships, in which uniform one-size-fits-all defenses are rejected. To fill in gaps in understanding, we propose two novel frameworks: the Category-Adaptive Defense Framework (CADF) that produces frequency-weighted, category-specific mitigation playbooks based upon empirical distributions, and Multi-Phase Intelligence (MPI), a closed-loop adaptive defense architecture with phases of prediction, adaptation, and response. Backtesting of the entire dataset shows efficiency improvements of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 0 - 7 3 \%}$</tex> over static controls for smaller and medium-sized businesses (SMEs) where CADF achieves a playbook coverage of 62 % vs 28 % in static OWASP controls and MPI reduces dwell time by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 3 \%}$</tex> (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 7}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 3}$</tex> days, mean dwell time).

title: Image Based Deep Learning Model for Fake News Detection on Social Media
authors: Kevin. J, Krishna Kaushik. N, Darshan. S, Ceronmani Sharmila V
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: The fast spread of rumors on social media is a severe danger to people trusting it, its stability in society, and the quality of information, and a great number of deceptive posts use pictures, not words as their main source of information. In order to overcome this difficulty, this paper proposes an explainable image-based deep learning structure of rumor detection that solely works with visual images. The suggested model is trained on discriminant image representations to learn visual manipulations, semantic contradictions, and anomalies in contexts, which are usually linked to image-based misinformation. To alleviate the black-box aspect of deep neural networks, visual explainability methods are included to indicate how significant areas of an image can influence the model predictions to allow making transparent and interpretable decisions. Experimental assessments using standard social media rumor datasets show that the specified approach can score higher in terms of accuracy, precision, and robustness than the traditional unimodal baselines, and the visual explanations produced by the algorithm increase the level of trust and usability by the end users, moderators, and policymakers. This paper highlights the significance of the practical application of explainable computer vision methods in ensuring effective and clear rumor detection in the real-life social media setting where images are of a leading role.

title: XCA-TransUNet: Cross-Scale Attention Guided Deeply Supervised Transformer Network for Retinal Vessel Segmentation on the FIVES Dataset
authors: Saimum Islam Jeem, Tarsheed Zaman Radif, Saiba Aabira, Md Hossam-E-Haider
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Accurate segmentation of blood vessels in the retina is essential for the diagnosis and monitoring of eye and systemic disease in the initial stages. However, the task remains difficult due to low contrast, noise, and intricate nature of thin blood vessels. To address these challenges, this paper introduces XCA-TransUNet, a hybrid encoder–decoder model that integrates convolutional downsampling with cross-scale transformer modeling, leveraging windowed self-attention in earlier layers and global self-attention in subsequent stages. A compound loss employing Dice, binary cross-entropy, and boundary loss with deep multi-scale supervision is employed to encourage both global consistency and boundary sharpness. This research contrasts the proposed approach with the FIVES dataset based on a deterministic training and validation protocol. Our model achieves a Dice score of 0.9064, IoU of 0.8361, accuracy of 0.9888, precision of 0.9249, recall of 0.8923, and specificity of 0.9952 on the held-out test set. Boundary-sensitive metrics also validate strength, with HD95 = 3.4572 and ASSD = 0.6423. These results suggest that XCA-TransUNet has outstanding vessel segmentation results on fundus images with excellent boundary preservation and strong generalization, and thus has potential for integration into computer-aided retinal screening systems.

title: ATLCS: An Educational Linear Control System Tool
authors: Mohamad Arnaout, Khaled Chahine, Hassan N. Noura
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: Lecturers are increasingly utilizing sophisticated application tools to provide students with intuitive, high-fidelity simulation environments that offer greater structural adaptability. This paper presents the Application Tool Interface for Linear Control Systems (ATLCS), a comprehensive computational framework developed using MATLAB GUIDE and Simulink. The tool streamlines modeling of electrical and mechanical systems by integrating transfer functions, state-space equations, and root-locus analysis. By employing a user-centered graphical interface, the ATLCS bridges the gap between theoretical abstraction and real-world application, enabling rapid assessment of parameter variations on system performance. Empirical validation through a comparative study of two student cohorts (N=60) demonstrated that integrating this software significantly enhances learning outcomes and student mastery of core pedagogical concepts. Statistical survey results highlight the tool’s efficacy in improving research efficiency and reinforcing foundational control theory within modern engineering curricula.

title: Cultural and Sarcastic Context Aware Sentimental Analysis with Large Language Model and Rule Based Heuristics: An Empirical Study
authors: Amrita Parashar, Umair Ullah, Jay Yadav, Hardik Rawat, Yuvraj Singh, Bhaskar Chari
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: In today's world of reasoning models, the sentiment behind the corpus is a significant piece of information in the field of natural language processing, for day-to-day tasks like social networks with a high degree of linguistic variability. The structural parts of communication like irony, idioms, sarcasm, and domain-specific cultural contexts, are frequently wrongly interpreted by traditional methods using machine learning, rules based heuristic and large language models. This made sentiment analysis largely ineffective in terms of automated systems used for critical decision-making through public opinion monitoring and market research. We suggest a symbolic distilled architecture that incorporates the best of both worlds, the reasoning power of large language models and the transparency and resource optimization of the rule-based heuristic architecture. We incorporate a large language model that was fine-tuned on our lexicon dataset which is responsible for the interpretation of the sentiment score generated by the core rule-based heuristic model, the heuristic models referred frequently as “rule-based heuristic model” is SentimentR and the large language model is LLaMA-2 hereafter. Our symbolic distilled architecture outperformed the large language model as well as the rule-based heuristic model by a significant margin, as shown in the later sections. This approach can be seen as an effort to combine the reasoning and transparency of the large language model and rule based heuristic model respectively.

title: BlockBotChain+: Edge-Intelligent Blockchain Framework for Trusted Robotic Supply Chains
authors: Sowmiya S, Swedhaa S, Sri Kannika U K, Sasirekha S P, Mekala N
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The quick deployment of collaborative autonomous robots in Industry 4.0 supply chains brings out important questions on trust, responsibility and blame assignment in decentralised systems. To address these limitations, this work proposes a next-generation blockchain-based trust and audit architecture named BlockBotChain+, which integrates strong edge intelligence with permissioned distributed ledgers for multi-robot cooperation. Contrary to classical trust models, we leverage a GNN-LSTM hybrid trust model on the-edge to infer spatio-temporal relations among robotic agents, through the telemetric streams (e.g., task latency, energy consumption, trajectory deviation and interaction consistency). A synthetically generated realistic warehouse dataset using ROS-Gazebo simulations was employed for training and testing that comprised both benign and adversarial task behaviors across multiple robots. Edge-based trust scores and anomaly classifications are synchronized with a Hyperledger Fabric blockchain that is facilitated by smart contracts ensuring task execution validation, and adaptive trust elevation. BlockBotChain+ achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 8. 7 \%}$</tex> trust classification accuracy, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 9. 1 \%}$</tex> anomaly detection precision and sub- 100 ms blockchain recording latency in the presence of injected faults, outperforming baseline rule-based and centralised solutions by a large margin. The scheme ensures scalable, credible and transparent robotic cooperation with verifiable trust guarantee and is applicable to the next-generation autonomous supply chain systems.

title: Data Transaction Platform Architecture and Performance Optimization Based on Multi-Party Secure Computation
authors: Xiaolu Zhang, Wuqiang Shen, Zheneng Liang, Lei Cui, Yechao Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: There are several challenges in the current data transaction field, including computing performance bottleneck and high communication cost. A layered platform architecture is proposed: the bottom layer is the key management and authentication layer based on threshold distributed key generation and trusted execution environment cluster; the intermediate adaptive communication layer is based on hybrid transmission protocol, real-time compression and gradient sparse technology; the top computing layer is based on dynamic task scheduling engine and intelligent load balancing capability. On this basis, this paper further introduces the collaborative optimization strategies such as batch transaction pipeline, communication round merger and hierarchical secret sharing calculation. Under the scale of hundreds of nodes, the scheme can control the end-to-end latency to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 1 9. 2}$</tex> minutes; the system throughput reaches 73.2 TPS; the bandwidth consumption is optimized to 78.1 Gbps, which improves the success rate of computational integrity verification.

title: Advancing Data-Driven Decision Support Systems Through Explainable Artificial Intelligence
authors: Suresh Chaganti
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The use of Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) in Data-Driven Decision Support Systems (DSS) is a paramount development in several fields of work, such as healthcare, finance, or engineering. XAI aims at making AI models transparent, interpretable and understandable to human users so that any decision-making process that is fuelled by AI can be trusted and explained. The paper explores the use of XAI in improving the reliability, transparency and accountability of DSS. Based on the examination of the current methods and models, we come up with a holistic methodology to incorporate XAI methods to DSS, with a focus on the trade-off between model accuracy and interpretability. The effectiveness of the proposed approach is demonstrated with the help of a case study of a predictive healthcare system. The results indicate the urgency of explainability in AI-based decision support, and the framework suggested can increase the acceptance of the use of AI and compliance with the regulations.

title: E3DNM: An Efficient 3D Node Deployment Model for Underwater Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: Jayant Kumar Rout, Priyal Sharma, Kruthika Tummala, Rahul Priyadarshi, Anish Kumar Vishwakarma, Rakesh Ranjan
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Underwater Wireless Sensor Networks (UWSNs) enable a wide range of ocean monitoring applications, including environmental observation, offshore infrastructure inspection, and marine resource exploration. However, reliable deployment of sensor nodes in underwater environments remains challenging due to the three-dimensional monitoring space, acoustic communication constraints, water-current-driven node mobility, and depth-dependent sensing performance. Conventional deployment strategies adapted from terrestrial wireless sensor networks are inadequate for volumetric underwater coverage and often neglect acoustic propagation characteristics and buoyancy-based node actuation. This paper proposes E3DNM, an Efficient 3D Node Deployment Model designed to improve coverage reliability, acoustic connectivity, and energy efficiency in UWSNs. The proposed framework integrates three coordinated mechanisms: a depth-stratified initialization strategy using hexagonal close packing for efficient volumetric coverage, a 3D acoustic virtualforce refinement algorithm incorporating depth-adaptive sensing radii and acoustic propagation constraints, and a buoyancybased mobility compensation mechanism that mitigates currentinduced drift and preserves deployment stability. Extensive simulations in a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$500 {m} \times 500 {m} \times 200 {m}$</tex> underwater monitoring volume with 120 sensor nodes demonstrate that E3DNM achieves 94.8% volumetric coverage, improves network lifetime by 28.9 %, and reduces communication energy consumption by 41.3 % compared with conventional 3D random deployment while maintaining acoustic connectivity in 97.6 % of trials. These results indicate that E3DNM provides a scalable and energy-efficient deployment framework for reliable large-scale underwater sensing applications.

title: Enhancing Security in Event-Driven Architectures: Threat Detection and Mitigation Strategies
authors: Saket Chaudhari, Archana Dnyandev Jagdale, Subh Prabhat, Madhavi Najana, Sanjeet Kumar Choudhary, Piyush Ranjan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Event-driven architectures (EDAs) provide high-level scalability and responsiveness in the modern wave of distributed systems. Dynamic nature in event propagation and event asynchronous communication, however, exposes the systems to many tough security challenges such as unauthorized event injection, data integrity breaches, or event-based denial-of-service (EDoS) attacks. This paper presents the Event-Driven Oncology Model for Security (EDOM) as a security model for aiding detection and mitigation of security threats in EDA environments. EDOM introduces real-time event monitoring, deep learning-based anomaly detection, and an adaptive response mechanism to protect critical event-driven systems. The system has a context-aware event filtering mechanism to guard against malicious event propagation and a blockchain-backed event validation to guarantee data integrity and non-repudiation. Finally, EDOM presents a hybrid cryptographic model to secure event channels against unauthorized access risks. Experimental evaluations show that EDOM achieves 97.1% threat detection accuracy, effectiveness against injecting events of 87.6%, and event integrity verification improvements of 92.3%. Total latency of the security response of EDOM is reduced by 23.5%, enabling real-time protection without compromising on system performance. Compared with the state-of-the-art security models, this model is specifically suitable for health care applications in which event integrity and real-time security are paramount since EDOM is most resilient and adaptive for event-driven environments.

title: Optimizing Real-Time Visual Emotion Perception Using Influential Landmark-Based Spatial Feature Extraction for Edge Devices
authors: Muhammad Faisal Zia, Sara Ali, Syed Hamza Gilani, Khawaja Fahad Iqbal, Anum Jaffar, Yasar Ayaz, Zaib Jahan
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Perception of emotion is an intuitive replication of a person's internal state without verbal communication. While visual emotion recognition has been broadly studied, existing deep neural network (DNN) and machine learning models often lack suitability for low-specification edge devices like robots due to high processing costs and memory requirements. This constrains their application in Human-Robot Interaction (HRI) and Human-Computer Interaction (HCI). To address this, we present a computationally inexpensive, robust Facial Emotion Recognition (FER) system for perceiving six basic emotions (disgust, surprise, fear, anger, happy, sad) on embedded devices. Our approach extracts geometric features from detected facial landmarks, considering spatial positions among influential landmarks, to train an SVM classifier. We evaluated the system using the Karolinska Directed Emotional Faces (KDEF) and Extended Cohn-Kanade (CK+) databases, including a fusion dataset to generalize for ethnic and racial variations. The results demonstrate optimization for real-time embedded applications, yielding accuracies of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{9 6. 8 \%}$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{8 6. 7 \%}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{8 6. 4 \%}$</tex> for CK+, KDEF, and the fused dataset, respectively. Future work aims to enable robotic agents to perceive emotion and interact naturally without additional hardware.

title: Performance Analysis of OOK/DPSK Based UWOC System and Distance Prediction Using Machine Learning
authors: Tripti Singh, Jyotsna D H, Aryan Bothra, Sanjay Kumar Sahu, Debanjan Sarkar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Underwater Wireless Optical Communication (UWOC) has emerged with a promising solution for highspeed data transmission with low latency in an underwater environment. The performance of UWOC employing On-Off-Keying (OOK) and Differential-Phase-Shift-Keying (DPSK) modulation schemes has been investigated in terms of BER and power penalty. Also, supervised Machine-learning (ML) is used to anticipate the transmission distance. The analysis has been done by considering different bit rates and water types such as Pure Water, Clear Water and Coastal Water. The received optical power corresponding to optimized BER levels in the order of 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-9</sup> was analyzed to quantify the relative power penalties for different underwater environments. Subsequently, the system parameters are optimized to determine the maximum achievable transmission distances for each scenario. The simulated results are used to construct a dataset consisting of 1022 samples, including bit rate, water type, and the corresponding optimized transmission distances for both modulation schemes. A Random Forest regression model is then employed to predict the achievable transmission distances for OOK and DPSK schemes based on the input parameters.

title: Behind the Veil: Exploring the Hidden Strengths and Weaknesses of Tors Security Framework
authors: Anushka Jain, Arnav Rathi, Lav Upadhyay, Jay Prakash Singh, Sachin Gupta
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: The Tor network is an important tool for protecting privacy online. It works by using overlay network software that sends messages through multiple relays that help to hide a user's location and identity. This case study focuses on Tor's overall effectiveness, its security features, and its architecture, especially at this time when widespread surveillance is seen as a serious threat to personal privacy. Firstly, we look at Tor's architecture that shows how it's a privacy-enhancing method that encrypts users' data at every layer. As we know, visiting a webpage can be risky, but Tor is designed to protect privacy, anonymity, and security while accessing the internet, and it overcomes the risks of webpage access through multi-layer encryption, routing through multiple nodes, and hiding the IP address completely, and even prevents DNS leaks. Tor Browser includes HTTPS Everywhere, which forces secure connections whenever possible, but traffic leaving the exit node is unencrypted, which can result in theft of passwords sent over HTTP, or even exit node operators can spy on your traffic if the site is not using HTTPS. From there, we closely study Tor's ability to give anonymity while also pointing out the limits and weaknesses that can sometimes reduce its main promises. Tor is designed for anonymity first, not for speed. As Tor routes the traffic through 3 relays, it provides better anonymity, but there can be slower page load times, especially for multimedia and dynamic content. Also, these relays are run by volunteers, not by high-performance cloud servers, which results in lower throughput. Similarly, multi-layer encryption increases CPU overhead and higher computational cost that slows down the browser performance. This shows that every performance drawback comes from a decision to increase privacy. So this case study highlights Tor's important role in safe communication along with the ongoing need to balance strong protection with better ease of use.

title: Cross-Layer Security Architectures for Internet of Medical Things: a Systematic Literature Review on Lightweight Cryptography, Federated Learning, Blockchain, and Zero Trust Integration
authors: Agander Parashar, M. Mohan
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Through sophisticated diagnostics, remote patient management, and real-time monitoring, the rapid development of the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) has greatly improved healthcare delivery. Also, there are significant trust, privacy, and security concerns due to the sensitive nature of patient health information and the limited resources of implantable and wearable medical devices. While recent research has proposed answers in ultra-lightweight cryptography, federated learningbased intrusion detection systems (FL-IDS), blockchain-enabled cryptography, and Zero Trust Architecture (ZTA), these techniques are mostly developed in isolation. An exhaustive literature survey of multi-layer IoMT security frameworks published between 2021 and 2026 is presented in this research. This study chose and evaluated 40 main papers using a systematic review technique used across all databases indexed by Scopus. Finally, the authors discuss the current state of the field and where researchers should focus their efforts to develop next-generation healthcare ecosystems with cross-layer security frameworks that are both resilient and scalable.

title: Authentication-based Efficient Packet Prevention using Secure Hashing-based Data Encryption Standard Algorithm in Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: K.Prakash, S. Sathya
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: A wireless sensor network (WSN) is an open network of widely spaced wireless links where every node serves as a measurement node, forwarding packets to their intended destination and acting as a router. In contrast to a conventional WSN, sensor data is ultimately sent to a fixed receiver from the installation location. Fixed pathways, mobile receiving nodes, and relay nodes all have very slow speeds in mobile settings; therefore, nodes must keep track of the most recent location of the mobile receiver sending the data. Concentrating on transmission speed and communication verification is a major problem for minimizing WSNs. Path verification lacks an appropriate solution for detecting communication assaults caused by transport link failure, even though these attacks have been explored. To resolve this problem, propose an efficient on-demand Secure Hashing Data Encryption Standard Algorithm based on Dynamic Link Aggregation in a wireless sensor network (SHDES-DLA). Through centralized cluster node authentication (CNA), this secure communication offers unified coverage, enabling you to monitor paths in cluster node edge areas. Transmit verification across nodes can be included to confirm that a packet is connected to the public network. Link for packet transmission. Every packet has a Source Authentication Request (SAR) protecting its terminations. In order to protect data, the Protect Hash-Based Data Encryption Standard (HDES) algorithm generates a unique key that is used to both encrypt and decrypt secure interactions with the destination node. The results of the simulation verify that the suggested techniques, SHDES-DLA and CNA algorithms, perform well when it comes to safety restrictions. The suggested solution uses energy-harvesting resources to generate a more secure transmission in the network connection mode.

title: Establishment of an Evaluation System Based on the AHP Model in the Big Data Context
authors: Xue Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: In the era of digitalization, the development of artificial intelligence and big data related technologies is rapid, and has gradually been widely used in various industries. This article, By virtue of an analysis of the present situation regarding the establishment of the ideological and political evaluation system of higher mathematics courses, proposes the “six-directional evaluation mechanism”. this basis, the APH model is used to construct an ideological and political teaching effect evaluation system for higher mathematics courses, which incorporates 6 primary indicators and 24 secondary indicators. The method of hierarchical analysis and expert scoring is used to determine the same level weight and the comprehensive global weights of indicators across all tiers, and the evaluation system of the effect of the ideological and political teaching of higher mathematics courses based on the OBE education concept is constructed. Drawing on big data technical support, 460 questionnaires collected by using questionnaire survey method, and the empirical analysis of this evaluation system was conducted by comparative analysis method. This research provides more clear and detailed guidance on approaching to boosting the outcomes of ideological and political education in higher mathematics.

title: Voice as Policy: A Language-Driven HMI with Visual Target Coordinates
authors: Soufiane Ameur, Mohamed Tabaa, Mohamed Hamlich, Kaouter Karboub
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Multidisciplinary Conference on Engineering Technology (IMCET)
abstract: Programming collaborative robots remains tedious for non-experts, especially when tasks depend on the current scene. This paper presents Voice as Policy, a language-based HMI that transforms spoken goals into safe actions for robots anchored in the visual coordinates of targets. Speech is transcribed and normalized into a compact intent schema (action, target, parameters) a lightweight OpenCV pipeline segments the target and converts the centroids of pixels into metrics (x, y) via an eight-point planar homography with z fixed to the plane of the work surface. Verifiable controller links the intention to the unearthed object checks accessibility and human zone constraints and executes movements via the cobot SDK while the HMI exposes transcripts, normalized intentions generated action code and execution logs. Implemented on a tabletop cell the system consistently located the screwdriver handle within a calibrated ROI and executed predictable seamless voice-action cycles under typical lighting and clutter. The contribution is a practical recipe minimal detection explicit security gates and an explicable control surface that makes policy-like voice interaction, both deployable and trustworthy in shared workspaces.

title: Drug Gene Interaction Mapping in Autism Spectrum Disorder Using BioGRID for Repurposing Opportunities
authors: Samad Ali, K.M. Navid-Al-Islam, Abdullah Al Bassam, Abdus Sobhan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is an intricate neurodevelopmental condition with fractionally available pharmacological therapies, placing drug repurposing as a significant prospective path in search of novel therapeutics. Here, we utilized curated chemical-gene interaction data from the BioGRID ASD project (Release 4.4.249) in a bid to systematically investigate drug-gene associations implicated in ASD. A bipartite drug-gene interaction network was built, and drugs and ASD-associated genes were ranked based on degree centrality. Jaccard similarity between pairs was used in order to cluster drugs based on shared gene targets, thus unveiling mechanistic groupings of compounds. Enrichment analyses of pathways highlighted several key processes of synaptic signaling and immune regulation, neuronal development, etc., as critical modules targeted by drugs in the top ranks. Beside that, the candidate drugs were also cross-validated with ClinicalTrials.gov to identify known compounds or novel repurposing opportunities. Integrating network topology, clustering, and pathway enrichment establishes an interpretable framework to prioritize drug candidates in ASD research. Unlike previous transcriptome-driven approaches, ours is the only one using curated BioGRID chemical-gene interactions and thus provides a reproducible and mechanistically transparent strategy for drug repurposing in ASD.

title: PaMoSplat: Part-Aware Motion-Guided Gaussian Splatting for Dynamic Scene Reconstruction
authors: Yinan Deng, Jianyu Dou, Jiahui Wang, Jingyu Zhao, Yi Yang, Yufeng Yue
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Dynamic scene reconstruction represents a fundamental yet demanding challenge in computer vision and robotics. While recent progress in 3DGS-based methods has advanced dynamic scene modeling, obtaining high-fidelity rendering and accurate tracking in scenarios with substantial, intricate motions remains significantly challenging. To address these challenges, we propose PaMoSplat, a novel dynamic Gaussian splatting framework incorporating part awareness and motion priors. Our approach is grounded in two key observations: 1) Parts serve as primitives for scene deformation, and 2) Motion cues from optical flow can effectively guide part motion. Specifically, PaMoSplat initializes by lifting multi-view segmentation masks into 3D space via graph clustering, establishing coherent Gaussian parts. For subsequent timestamps, we leverage a differential evolutionary algorithm to estimate the rigid motion of these parts using multi-view optical flow cues, providing a robust warm-start for further optimization. Additionally, PaMoSplat introduces an adaptive iteration count mechanism, internal learnable rigidity, and flow-supervised rendering loss to accelerate and optimize the training process. Comprehensive evaluations across diverse scenes, including real-world environments, demonstrate that PaMoSplat delivers superior rendering quality, improved tracking precision, and faster convergence compared to existing methods. Furthermore, it enables multiple part-level downstream applications, such as 4D scene editing.

title: Adaptive 3D Convolution for Remote Sensing Image Fusion
authors: Siran Peng, Xiangyu Zhu, Shang-Qi Deng, Liang-Jian Deng, Zhen Lei
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: Remote sensing image fusion aims to create a high-resolution multi/hyper-spectral image from a high-resolution image with limited spectral information and a low-resolution image with abundant spectral data. Recently, deep learning (DL) techniques have shown significant effectiveness in this area. Most DL-based methods approach image fusion as a 2D problem by encoding spectral information into feature map channels. However, our research suggests that this strategy introduces notable spectral distortions. In contrast, some methods consider spectral data as an additional dimension, utilizing standard 3D convolutions to preserve spectral information. Nevertheless, in a standard 3D convolutional layer, the same set of kernels is applied across all input regions, which we have found to be sub-optimal for image fusion. Furthermore, standard 3D convolutions necessitate substantial computational resources. To address these challenges, we propose a novel convolutional paradigm called Adaptive 3D Convolution (Ada3D) for remote sensing image fusion. Ada3D applies a unique set of 3D kernels to each input voxel, enabling the capture of fine-grained details. These adaptive kernels are generated through a two-step process: (i) spatial and spectral kernels are derived from their respective image sources; (ii) these two types of kernels are then combined to form content-aware 3D kernels that effectively integrate spatial and spectral information. Additionally, adaptive biases are introduced to enhance the convolutional outcome at the voxel level. Furthermore, we incorporate the group convolution technique to reduce computational complexity. As a result, Ada3D offers full adaptivity in an efficient manner. Evaluation results across five datasets demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance, underscoring the superiority of Ada3D. The code is available at https://github.com/PSRben/Ada3D.

title: Neural-Enhanced Sliding-Mode Control for Quadrotor Helicopters Operating in Dynamic and Uncertain Environments
authors: Mati Ullah, Hongbo Gao, Alam Nasir, Xinmiao Wang, Runda Niu, Muhammad Humayun, Chengbo Wang, Lin Zhou, Jianan Wang, Jinpeng Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: Robust altitude tracking and attitude stabilization of a quadrotor helicopter (QH) operating in dynamic and uncertain environments remain challenging due to unknown external disturbances, time-varying parametric uncertainties, and practical actuator and sensor constraints. To address these challenges, this article proposes a neural-enhanced sliding-mode control (NE-SMC) framework that integrates a lightweight multilayer perceptron neural network (MLP-NN) with an SMC backbone within a unified adaptive control architecture. The neural module employs a sliding-mode-inspired weight-update law to enable real-time compensation for disturbances and uncertainties without requiring explicit disturbance models or prior knowledge of system parameters, thereby strengthening system-level robustness and adaptability. A rigorous Lyapunov-based analysis guarantees closed-loop stability and finite-time convergence of the tracking errors. Extensive high-fidelity simulations, including deterministic scenarios and a 100-run Monte Carlo study, are conducted under severe wind disturbances and turbulent conditions, up to 25% mass and inertia variations, actuator saturation, and sensor constraints. Comparative results demonstrate that the proposed NE-SMC achieves substantially improved tracking accuracy and robustness compared with standard SMC and conventional hybrid SMC (H-SMC) schemes. Specifically, the root-mean-square error (RMSE) is reduced by 83.3%–88.9% relative to standard SMC and by 75%–87.5% relative to conventional H-SMC. These results confirm that the proposed framework provides a robust, constraint-aware, and computationally efficient control solution suitable for real-time QH applications, aligning with the requirements of intelligent systems in complex, uncertain environments.

title: A Distributed Primal-Dual Method for Constrained Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning With General Parameterization
authors: Ali Kahe, Hamed Kebriaei
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control
abstract: This paper proposes a novel distributed approach for solving a cooperative Constrained Multi-agent Reinforcement Learning (CMARL) problem, where agents seek to minimize a global objective function subject to shared constraints. Unlike existing methods that rely on centralized training or coordination, our approach enables fully decentralized online learning, with each agent maintaining local estimates of both primal and dual variables. Specifically, we develop a distributed primal-dual algorithm based on actor-critic methods, leveraging local information to estimate Lagrangian multipliers. We establish consensus among the Lagrangian multipliers across agents and prove the convergence of our algorithm to an equilibrium point, analyzing the sub-optimality of this equilibrium compared to the exact solution of the unparameterized problem. Furthermore, we introduce a constrained cooperative Cournot game with stochastic dynamics as a test environment to evaluate the algorithm's performance in complex, real-world scenarios.

title: TransVort: A Temporally-Coherent Physics-Guided Neural Network for Super-Resolving and Denoising 4D Flow MRI of Cerebrospinal Fluid_supp8-3690957.xlsx
authors: Vitaly Rayz
publish: N/A
abstract: Objective: To enhance the diagnostic utility of 4D flow MRI in assessing cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) dynamics by super-resolving and denoising measured velocities using temporally coherent, physics-guided neural networks (PGNN). Methods: Synthetic 4D flow MRI was generated from 40 computational fluid dynamics (CFD) simulations across 10 ventricular geometries. These simulations were used to generate paired synthetic 4D flow MRI and high-resolution velocity fields used for supervised training. Here, we compare a previously developed temporally independent network (div-mDCSRN-Flow) using divergence-based regularization with two novel temporal PGNNs (tempo-mDCSRN-Flow using divergence-regularization and TransVort additionally constrained by the vorticity transport equation). Results: In application to synthetic 4D flow MRI of a double gyre flow showed the temporal PGNNs improve vorticity estimation. Similarly, both temporal methods improved estimation of vorticity and time-averaged wall shear stress (TAWSS) of synthetic 4D flow MRI in the 3rd and 4th ventricle. While using temporal PGNNs improves velocity and vorticity quantification across temporal resolutions, TransVort demonstrated additional improvement at fine temporal resolutions. Application of TransVort to in vivo 4D flow MRI of CSF flow captured vortex formation and dissipation in the 4th ventricle over the cardiac cycle. Conclusion: Leveraging the temporal information of 4D flow MRI improves reconstruction of high-resolution velocity fields. This leads to better estimation of gradient-based flow metrics such as vorticity and TAWSS, which are associated with neurodegenerative and neurovascular diseases. Significance: Augmenting the accuracy of 4D flow MRI increases its potential for adoption as a clinical tool for diagnosing and monitoring disorders of neurofluid dynamics.

title: R2C-MGR2L: Enhancing Multi-granularity Relational Representation in Dual-temporal Optical Remote Sensing Images through LLM-based Scene Graph Generation_supp1-3691258.docx
authors: Lilu Zhu
publish: N/A
abstract: Recent advances in Earth observations have enabled the acquisition of high-precision optical remote sensing imagery (ORSI) with sub-meter spatial resolution and hourly revisit periods. While early neural network-based methods have demonstrated notable limitations in visual perception, more recent multimodal fusion approaches still struggle to effectively recognize and reason over geospatial object relations. To fully promote the collaborative analysis of very-high-resolution (VHR) dual-temporal ORSIs, this paper presents a novel framework named R2C-MGR2L that is designed to enhance multi-granularity relational representation in dual-temporal optical remote sensing images through LLM-based scene graph generation (SGG). This framework presents a two-stage learning paradigm—knowledge alignment and cross-modal fusion—that incorporates structured knowledge representations and a hierarchical attention mechanism to improve the refinement and reasoning of fine-grained relational semantics. First, we investigate the types of geospatial object relations and assess the feasibility of enhancing relational semantic representation through multimodal knowledge extraction and embedding. We subsequently employ a pre-trained code large language model (Code-LLM) to transform multigranularity relation triplets from extracted optical remote sensing (ORS) scene graphs into semantically equivalent programs, thereby alleviating the scarcity of domain-specific ORSI knowledge. Moreover, a hybrid-structured asymmetric contrastive learning network is developed to further refine the semantic representations derived from complex scenes. By hierarchically fusing dual-temporal ORSIs and corresponding scene graphs, an integrated representation of visual targets and structural knowledge across multiple depths is achieved. We evaluate our proposed framework on three relation-aware ORS scene understanding tasks. The experimental results suggest that the R2C-MGR2L outperforms the state-of-the-art generic methods, resulting in consistent improvements in accuracy, completeness and timeliness for relational semantic recognition.

title: Design and RTL Implementation of XOR based BRAM Memory
authors: Adarsh B, Druva Kumar S, Santhosh Kumar R
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Multi-ported memory is frequently used in modern FPGA devices. However, relying heavily on BRAM to implement multi-ported memory in FPGA designs can limit its availability for other critical design components. This becomes especially challenging in applications that require large amounts of internal storage. Increasing the number of read and write ports typically demands additional memory resources, which can lead to inefficiencies if not managed properly. To address this, it is essential to develop an effective BRAM utilization strategy that supports multiple ports while optimizing resource usage. Such an approach can significantly enhance memory bandwidth and scalability compared to traditional methods. By synthesizing and implementing this strategy on various FPGA platforms, its benefits in terms of performance and efficient memory access can be clearly demonstrated.

title: The Uptake of AI in Healthcare: Human-AI Synergy as a Pillar of Excellence
authors: Moza Alkhayyal, Elham AlNuaimi, Kawthar Al Raeesi, Tayma Dweikat, Mounir El Khatib, Nabil Yousuf Fakhri
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: AI, or Artificial Intelligence, is already in use in multiple sectors and is enhancing them in a very constructive manner. In the healthcare industry, AI helps in automating administrative procedures, predicting patient outcomes, customizing treatment plans, and increasing the accuracy of clinical diagnoses. AI operates in conjunction with imaging technology by analyzing thousands of pieces of medical data through its algorithms to assist in the early diagnosis of cancer and other diseases. However, human skills are equally important. While AI is proficient in data analysis, a human healthcare provider brings compassion, decisions, communication, and ethics. This study has been conducted based on the AI-integrated health care systems to determine whether this technology complements human skill or replaces it. The results indicate a remarkable improvement in the diagnosis and treatment plan for patients and optimization of human resources due to AI technology. The collaboration of human skill and AI greatly enhances the standard of care that patients receive and improves overall efficiency in healthcare delivery.

title: Performance Evaluation of UWOC System Employing BPSK Modulation and Coherent Detection
authors: Aryan Bothra, Praveen Kumar S., Pranav A., Sanjay Kumar Sahu, Debanjan Sarkar, Prithiviraj Rajalingam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: This paper presents a theoretical performance analysis of a horizontal simplex Underwater Wireless Optical Communication (UWOC) system employing Binary Phase Shift Keying (BPSK) modulation with coherent detection. The Channel Impulse Response (CIR) for different water types is estimated using the Monte Carlo simulation technique and subsequently modeled using a double gamma function to derive a closed form analytical expression. The developed model is utilized to evaluate the Bit Error Rate (BER) performance under different Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) levels, Bit Rates, and Attenuation lengths (AL). Simulation results illustrate the dependence of BER performance on water quality and transmission distance, highlighting critical trade-offs between system parameters. The proposed analytical framework serves as a valuable tool for the design and optimization of high-performance UWOC links.

title: Examining the Influence of Social Media Marketing and Customer Experience on Brand Equity mediated with Customer Engagement in the Indonesian E-commerce Sector
authors: Ghayda Yola, Enggal Sriwardiningsih
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: The fast expansion of Indonesia's e-commerce sector has increased brand competitiveness, making brand equity a critical determinant for long-term success. This study examines the influence of social media marketing and customer experience on brand equity in the Indonesian e-commerce sector, using consumer engagement as a mediating variable. Data were obtained from 320 active e-commerce consumers and evaluated using Structural Equation Modeling (SEM) in a quantitative research approach. Findings reveal that social media marketing boosts customer engagement but does not directly impact brand equity, while customer experience strengthens both engagement and brand equity. Customer engagement fully mediates the effect of social media marketing and partially mediates the effect of customer experience on brand equity. The study adds to the existing literature on digital marketing strategy and makes practical recommendations for Indonesian e-commerce enterprises.

title: Forest Inventory Classification Using Transfer Learning from Prisma to Hypso-1 Hyperspectral Satellite Data
authors: Sif Anna Darling Lyken, Cameron Penne, Milica Orlandić
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: Forest monitoring is critical for biodiversity and forest management. However, accurate classification using hyperspectral imagery (HSI) remains challenging due to the scarcity of labeled training data, especially for emerging satellite missions like HYPSO-1 and -2. To address this limitation, this study explores the use of transfer learning for HSI classification. Convolutional neural networks (CNNs) are pre-trained on a larger and more diverse PRISMA dataset and adapted to HYPSO-1 using spectral alignment techniques. Several CNN architectures are evaluated, including VGG-16 enhanced with attention mechanisms such as squeeze-andexcitation (SE) and convolutional block attention modules (CBAM). Results demonstrate that transfer learning enables knowledge transfer between sensors. Among the models, VGG-16 with SE achieves the highest overall accuracy (94.98 %) and average accuracy (94.28 %), emphasizing the importance of channel-wise attention. These findings support the development of sensor-agnostic approaches for classification and demonstrate the potential of transfer learning to overcome labeled data scarcity in Earth observation missions.

title: SemanticStylenet: A Modular Framework for Generating Text-to-Image With Prompt Refinement, Semantic Re-Ranking, and Neural Style Transfer
authors: Abhilash T, Priyadharshini S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Creating visually coherent and semantically accurate images from natural language is the goal of text-to-image (T2I) generation, which has emerged as a key challenge in multimodal AI. Despite recent improvements in image quality brought about by LLM-driven and diffusion-based models, many systems lack fine-grained semantic control and modularity. This paper presents a modular T2I framework that combines neural style transfer, prompt refinement, diffusion-based synthesis, and semantic evaluation. One important addition is a semantic reranking module that reorders candidate images based on CLIP alignment scores, allowing for controlled trade-offs between style and semantic fidelity. Experiments on MS-COCO and LAION 400 M subsets show up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 8 \%}$</tex> CLIPScore gains, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 - 1 2 \%}$</tex> FID reductions, and improved LPIPS and MOS over Stable Diffusion, while retaining practical runtime efficiency and discussing limitations and future directions.

title: Data-Driven Machine Learning Framework for Early and Non-Invasive Kidney Stone Detection
authors: Kottaimalai Ramaraj, Pallikonda Rajasekaran Murugan, Arunprasath Thiyagarajan
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Kidney stone disease, a prevalent urological condition that affects millions of individuals worldwide, is becoming more widespread as a result of factors related to surroundings, lifestyle, and diet. Early identification is crucial to preventing issues including obstruction, kidney injury, and recurrent stone accumulation. Despite their accuracy, existing diagnostic methods—such as imaging modalities like CT and ultrasound—are laborious, expensive, and radiation-exposed to individuals. Therefore, non-invasive, rapid, and reasonably priced detection methods based on urine specimen analysis are growing in popularity. This review of the literature compiles recent research on the quick identification of kidney stones using machine learning (ML) algorithms and urine-based biomarkers. Numerous research have suggested that by integrating deep learning (DL) and predictive models with common urine indicators including pH, specific gravity, oxalate, citrate, calcium, and uric acid, it may be possible to forecast the development of stones with significant accuracy. Additionally, advancements in urine biosensor technology and handheld analyzers have made it feasible to identify metabolic biomarkers in real time with great specificity and sensitivity. The efficiency of classification techniques has significantly risen because to feature selection strategies and optimization algorithms. This study emphasizes the primary methodologies, benchmark datasets (such 24-hour urine data), sensor-based detection techniques, and clinical importance. Combining biochemical urine analysis with artificial intelligence holds great promise for timely response and personalized risk identification. The paper ends by highlighting current issues and outlining possible research directions in order to develop urine-based kidney stone detection systems that are reliable, efficient, and scalable and suitable for therapeutic and community-level use.

title: A Voice-Assisted Smart Device for the Visually Impaired People: Leveraging AI Vision, Object Detection, and GPS-Based Navigation
authors: Shithi Datta, G. M. Mahmudur Rahman, Prionty Majumder, Shaira Afra Redhila, K M Fazle Rabbi Nipun
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Visually challenged people face various difficulties in their daily life as a consequence of weakened or absent visual capacity and lack the capacity to perform basic tasks without assistance. There are different approaches to help visually impaired people but primarily but most of them focus on detecting and reading texts aloud. There are also certain frameworks that assist a visually impaired individual in safely and effectively navigating their surroundings, going beyond simple text extraction. So, the systems for proper assistance of visually impaired people need further optimization. Therefore, this study introduces a comprehensive assistive system that empowers blind users with multiple functionalities by leveraging advanced AI model for diverse queries, enhancing autonomy, environmental awareness, and location awareness. The key features of this device include text detection, object detection, conversational AI interaction, scene description and location services which are implemented via Raspberry Pi, making the system accessible and portable for daily use. It detects object through YOLOv8n and utilizes Gemini 1.5 Flash for conversational AI, scene description and text extraction. Neo M8N GPS module is used for geolocation services and real-time location data can be sent to caregiver via Telegram messages. The developed system suggests an architecture that ensures a voice controlled, real time assistance in accessible form, which is tailored for everyday use of visually impaired individuals.

title: An Advanced AI Framework for Pharmaceutical Drug Identification Based on Attention-Driven Vision Transformers and Evidential Neural Networks
authors: Brindha M, Dhivya R, Kavya M, Madhumitha. T, Menaka S
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The growing need of automated monitoring of pharmaceuticals and quality control, precise detection and identification of drugs have become an issue of patient safety and health care effectiveness. Current techniques based on CNN, ANN, or GNN may have disadvantages in the form of background noise, similarity between the appearance of different drugs, and a lack of interpretability. In order to fill these gaps, this study suggests a new AI-based pharmaceutical detection and identification workflow. First, the Bilateral Filter is used to do preprocessing of the image in order to eliminate noise and maintain the edges and impressions of the tablets. Subsequently, Recursive Feature Elimination (RFE) picks up the most discriminative features among the images that have been processed. To be highly effective in detection, a Swin Transformer with Deformable Attention (STDA) focuses on and identifies tablets and blister packs, even when there is a cluttered or irregular background. Lastly, Deep Evidential Neural Network (D-Evidential NN) is a type of classifier of the drugs, but it makes predictions with a measure of uncertainty, enhancing reliability and interpretability.
