The presented workflow is very accurate, precise, and reliable in pharmaceutical detection, classification, and monitoring.

title: Real-Time Pothole Detection Using Dual Accelerometer Sensors with Temporal Convolutional Networks and Attention Mechanisms
authors: Vijayaragavan Sivasamy, B. Varun Karthik, Aditya Ullas, Sriram R, TYJ Naga Malleswari, S Ushasukanya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Road-surface deterioration causes substantial vehicle damage, traffic disruption, and maintenance inefficiency. This paper presents a real-time pothole detection framework that combines dual MPU6050 inertial sensors with a Temporal Convolutional Network and attention mechanisms (TCNAM) deployed on an ESP32. In contrast to thresholding and single-sensor pipelines, the proposed model explicitly captures front-rear temporal coupling through dual-stream dilated causal convolutions and multi-head self-attention. On an identical heldout benchmark, the full model achieves 96.7 % accuracy and a 4.1 % false-positive rate, improving by 4.4 percentage points over dual-sensor SVM and by 8.5 points over single-sensor SVM. A compressed edge variant (distillation + structured pruning + quantization-aware training) retains 95.8 % accuracy with 342 KB memory and 47 ms inference latency. Independent-area validation and cross-vehicle testing confirm robust generalization. The proposed system costs Rs.6,225 per unit, enabling practical deployment at a fraction of camera- or LiDAR-centric alternatives.

title: QuickAI: An AI-Powered Content Creation System Using PERN Stack
authors: P.L. Rajarajeswari, Arya Lakshmi M, Hobika A, Tharikesani S, Vibhooshna D M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: There is an expanding demand of quality digital content on online platforms, which has necessitated automated and scalable content generation systems. The current processes operate on a group of disjointed applications to generate texts, create images, or edit the media, which is inefficient and is expensive in terms of operation costs. QuickAI is a fully deployed multi-modal generative AI system, the content of which is presented in the present paper and involves utilizing transformer-based large language models (via Gemini and OpenAI APIs) to generate articles and titles with the use of diffusion-based text-to-image synthesis to generate visual assets. The system also uses deep learning-based background removal and object inpainting tools to improve the refinement of media. QuickAI is based on a PERN-stack configuration using the PostgreSQL and Prisma ORM data orchestration packages and uses asynchronous task execution to save latency and throughput. The results of experimental evaluation involving 50 content creators show that the efficiency of the workflow was increased by 28 % as well as both design and publishing preparation time were reduced significantly. The findings confirm the usefulness of integrated generative artificial intelligence pipelines in improving the productivity and reduction of the tool fragmentation in digital content ecosystems.

title: Predictive Modelling of Farms and Crop Growth through Satellite and Remote Sensing Data Processing using Ensemble Learning Algorithms
authors: G Ramkumar, Parabathini Bulah Pushpa Rani, Hayel Khafajeh, D. Bujji Babu, T Mrudula, Uma Reddy
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The use of advanced sensing technologies, machine learning, and intelligent task scheduling is crucial for precision agriculture, aiming to enhance decision-making, predict crop yields, and support sustainability. This study introduces the Predictive Modelling of Farms and Crop Growth through Satellite and Remote Sensing Data Processing using the Ensemble Learning Algorithms (PFCSRE) model, which integrates machine learning to analyze leaf nitrogen content, predict chlorophyll levels, and utilize satellite imagery for improved farming outcomes. The model employs Quantum-Driven Mongoose Optimization (QDMO) for tuning, Random Forest (RF) regression, and a virtual machine (VM)-based scheduling method, ensuring real-time, scalable, and reliable performance in smart agriculture. The study covers three large carrot fields in Saudi Arabia over two growing seasons, tracking over 90 sample plots with SPAD meters and GPS-tagged data collection on soil and crops. The Growing Degree Days (GDD) model was used to track growth stages, and the Leaf Chlorophyll Content (LCC) was calculated by combining satellite vegetation indices with SPAD data. The LCC layers helped create productivity zones for predicting crop yields. The PFCSRE model, with training and validation accuracy exceeding 98%, and confirmed classification reliability through ROC curve and AUC analysis, outperforms other baseline models. The regression analysis indicated that mid-growth phases significantly influenced carrot yields, while late-stage chlorophyll content negatively affected output. The PFCSRE model, adaptable to various crops, aids sustainable farming by optimizing resource use. The intelligent scheduling model improved efficiency in cloud-based computation, offering better predictive accuracy and data integration compared to traditional models.

title: Multi-Scale Lightweight Transformer for Hyperspectral Image Classification
authors: Liyu Tan, Danfeng Liu, Liguo Wang
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: In recent years, convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and their variants have made significant progress in hyperspectral image classification (HSIC). However, these methods often face some limitations, such as limited receptive field, insufficient feature extraction ability, and high computational cost. To address these challenges, a Multi-scale Lightweight Transformer for Hyperspectral Image Classification is proposed for hyperspectral image classification. First, a joint spatial-spectral embedding is used. At the same time, a group convolution module is designed, which allows each tagging position to learn local spectral representations from multiple adjacent bands. To reduce computational complexity and enhance feature modeling capabilities, a hierarchical framework is adopted. In addition, A multi-head feature tagger is also introduced. The semantic feature tags extracted at different stages are fused as the input of the Transformer.

title: Self-Supervised Deep Image Prior with Blind-Spot Networks for Multiplicative Noise Removal
authors: Mahipal Jetta, Appana Saketh Krishna Rao, Kushal, R V S Srikasyap Sagar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Multiplicative noise, common in coherent imaging modalities such as SAR and medical ultrasound, poses significant challenges for conventional denoising methods because of its signal-dependent nature. While the first supervised deep learning approaches have shown promising results, they require paired clean-noisy datasets which are often unavailable or difficult and cumbersome to acquire in practice. This paper proposes a new self-supervised framework for multiplicative noise removal, combining the implicit regularization of Deep Image Prior (DIP) with blind-spot network architectures and complementary subsampling. The proposed approach adopts a two-branch encoderdecoder structure that independently reconstructs both signal content and noise characteristics by a multiplicative decomposition model. Three key contributions are made: 1) Blind-spot convolutions proposed in Noise2Void that prevent the network from learning identity mappings by masking out center pixels in receptive fields, 2) Neighbor2Neighbor loss calculated on complementary checkerboard subsamples that enforces consistency without requiring access to clean references, and 3) multi-scale texture preservation layers aiming to preserve fine structural details while suppressing noise. In contrast to other state-of-the-art methods, our framework operates without any form of supervision, necessitating merely a single noisy observation at inference time. Comprehensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed method achieves competitive performance compared with supervised approaches while avoiding their reliance on clean data during training. Due to this, it is particularly well-suited for scenarios where no ground truth can be obtained. The method balances between noise suppression and texture preservation over a wide range of noise levels and different image characteristics.

title: Dual-Input Transfer Learning for Efficient and Robust Masked Face Recognition Using an Enhanced ResNet-50 Architecture
authors: Santo Cherian, Binu Thomas
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Trends in Information Technology (ICITIIT)
abstract: Wearing of face masks on a large scale during the COVID-19 pandemic and even after the event has been a significant contributor to the deterioration of traditional face recognition systems, relying on upper-face features that are not obscured, is not working well. It is a resource-efficient deep learning model that employs strategic transfer learning, dualinput feature fusion, and advanced data augmentation to be one of the best in the masked face recognition drivers. The paper proposed a ResNet-50 model which is enhanced and transformed to accept 6-channel inputs by concatenation of the pairing of masked and unmasked face; hence enabling the model to acquire the occlusion-robust identity invariant features. The model can be able to converge very quickly, as well as reduce overfitting, by selective freezing more early layers and finetuning deeper layers using ImageNet pre-trained weights. The powerful augmentation pipeline that employs geometric distortions, color perturbation, and random erasing also helps in generalizing to new types of masks, lighting, and pose variations. The proposed method gets 98.59% average accuracy, with class-wise F1-scores higher than 98.6% and only 17 errors on 1,226 test samples when it gets evaluated on an adapted LFW dataset that contains 23,285 balanced masked-unmasked pairs. The model is of high accurateness but also stays lightweight in terms of computation with its need for only 24.5 M parameters, GPU memory of less than 0.5 GB, and around 3 hours for training when 11 epochs are applied. The proposed approach, in comparison with the attention-based, transformer-based, and multi-modal fusion methods, not only provides better accuracy but also does this at a significantly lower computational cost, thus making it a very good candidate for real-world deployment on resource-limited platforms.

title: Advanced Low Power EDAC with a Clock Gating Design and Enhanced DFT-Compatible Design
authors: Denisha.E, Maruvarasi.P.R, Litheesh.A
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: This work proposes a novel design-for-testability (DFT) compatible in-situ error detection and correction (EDAC) architecture that significantly enhances timing error resilience while achieving low power and test overhead through a clock-gating-enabled, scan-friendly design. The proposed system introduces a highly efficient scannable EDAC cell (SEDC), which operates as a latch-based timing error detection element in normal mode and seamlessly transitions to a flip-flop configuration in scan mode for testing purposes. Unlike traditional EDAC implementations that suffer from high area, power, and timing overheads, as well as test complexity due to their dependence on critical path sensitization, the SEDC leverages transistor reusability and simplified detection logic to minimize these penalties. The architecture includes a globally controllable error propagation mechanism that enables error signals to be generated and observed during shift-based test operations, thereby eliminating the need for intricate launch-on-capture patterns. This innovation facilitates both static and at-speed testing with significantly reduced pattern count and test cycles, improving test efficiency and lowering overall design complexity. Furthermore, the integration of clock gating ensures reduced dynamic power consumption by disabling inactive logic stages, making the system particularly suitable for energy-constrained applications.

title: IoT-based System for Monitoring and Controlling Industrial Pollution
authors: P Prabu, S Sakthi Vinayagam, Shebin Shajahan, A Sreejith, V Srikhil, M Vipinraj
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Today Pollution of the environment emerges as key challenges faced by urban areas and industrial sectors. The rapid expansion of industries, infrastructure and transport vehicles rising industrial activity, infrastructure development, and increased vehicle use have generated environmental problems that directly affect humans and, therefore, require a special monitoring system. To address environmental pollution problems, here we tend to adopt the IoT -based contamination system that oversees and regulates processes. Where it identifies harmful gases circulating in the air. The suggested Embedded IoT solutions examines the amount of CO -emitted gas in the course of manufacturing process, temperature, and pH through Sensing equipment and arduino technology and LCD. There will be a different data control and control mechanism for each process. The devices will be installed through a mobile application that can show real -time updates of the level of environmental pollution of manufacturing sectors or isolated locations. The concept enabled by IoT provides access information from remote places. Most of the concepts underlying the execution project is intended to establish their genuine time completely automated control management systems using Internet of Things.

title: Automated Brain Tumor Segmentation in MRI using Bidirectional CNN and YOLO with Non-Maximum Suppression
authors: S Sandhya, S Sakthi Vinayagam, X Leena Miriam, Sai Lokesh, Madhan Mohan Reddy, Chappidi Vinay Kanth Reddy
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Manual segmentation of brain tumors is time-consuming and tedious to manually separate brain tumors in MRI images. High accuracy is needed for diagnosis, treatment planning, and estimation of the effectiveness of therapy. Even though deep learning models like CNNs have a tendency to require large amounts of labeled data, which are still scarce in the field of medical imaging, traditional segmentation methods rely on hand-designed features. The model uses a bidirectional CNN structure that balances the learning of parameters in order to address the overfitting and instability problems. YOLO for any object that it might have. Non-Maximum Suppression (NMS), a critical part, removes the overlapping predictions to make detection more reliable. Generally, the NMS deletes duplicate and overlapping bounding boxes such that only the best-fitting bounding box describes an item.

title: Optimizing Intra-Container Communication with Memory Protection Keys: A Novel Approach to Secure and Efficient Microservice Interaction
authors: Fnu Yashu, Shubham Malhotra Rochester, Muhammad Saqib
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Microservices within cloud-native applications are usually deployed in containers for greater scalability and flexibility. However, the inter-process communication (IPC) among co-located microservices tends to have significant overhead, particularly when traditional networking protocols are used in containers. This paper presents a novel approach, MPKLink, Still, communication orchestration among microservices that operate in the same container can be very costly, especially if conventional networking protocols are being used. MPKLink, presented in this paper, utilizes Intel Memory Protection Keys (MPK) to allow for better intra-container communications and to secure safety. By applying access control according to MPK in shared memory, we reduce redundant networking latencies, which help achieve better resource utilization and faster performance. By doing so, we show how through rigorous testing, MPKLink is superior to REST and gRPC in microservices with clear advantages. Furthermore, we describe the procedure for making MPKLink compatible with mainstream container orchestration systems, to ensure it functions properly in the environments of deployment. The results described in this paper mark a paradigm shift for developers trying to enhance communication in microservices environments without compromising the security and stability of containerized systems.

title: Superimposed Sunscape Pilot Pattern for Channel Estimation in OTFS-Based ISAC
authors: Lianet Méndez-Monsanto, Andrés Reyes-Castro, Kun Chen-Hu, María Julia Fernández-Getino García, Ana García Armada
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: Orthogonal time-frequency space (OTFS) is a promising waveform for future Sixth Generation (6G) networks and beyond, offering robustness to high-mobility scenarios and enabling seamless integration of sensing and communication services by operating in the delay-Doppler (DD) domain. Reliable channel estimation is essential for both functions; however, conventional embedded-pilot (EP) schemes sustain high pilot overhead and an increased peak-to-average power ratio (PAPR). Superimposed training (ST) methods have been proposed as an alternative to reduce these limitations, since pilots and data symbols share the same DD resources. Nevertheless, existing ST-based techniques typically depend on computationally expensive algorithms or exhibit degraded communication and sensing performance. To overcome these restrictions, this work introduces a novel low-power superimposed pilot design, called sunscape pattern, and a corresponding ST-based channel estimation algorithm specifically tailored to this structure. The core design principle of the sunscape pattern is to distribute pilot energy following a structured layout in the DD domain that resembles a seascape with a sun over the sea. The sun enables accurate delay and Doppler detection (both integer and fractional), while the sea inherently supports interference averaging between pilot and data components, thereby mitigating self-interference and noise without requiring computationally expensive iterative interference cancellation. Simulation results validate the proposed scheme. They show that it achieves accurate channel estimation and reliable DD-domain detection with low computational complexity and manageable PAPR, outperforming existing approaches in both communication and sensing performance.

title: Beyond Moore's Law: a Simulation Driven Study of VLSI Scaling, AI Assisted Design Automation, and Emerging Technologies
authors: A Subbulakshmi, Praveen Kumar K, Sandhiya M, Jeeva Gladwin F S, Navin Athreya, Subramanian M
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The Law of Moore has been the for decades. base that semiconductor advancement has occurred. by the predictable doubling of transistor density by Moore, about every two years. Yet, as we close in on atomic scale, scaling limits are becoming more evident with CMOS technology, namely, problems with shortchannel effects, leakage currents, and the economic fact of commercial viability. This paper is a review of the alternatives available in VLSI development beyond Moore's Law with a focus on new device architectures, AI assisted automation, and heterogeneous integration. It includes a comparison of the FinFET and Gate All Around FET (GAAFET) structure at the 7 nm node, backed up by TCAD based simulation data, and provides an examination of electrical properties, scalability, and electrostatic control of GAAFET, from within traditional FET complementary devices, including using 2D materials to improve performance. Second, the paper explores the adoption of artificial intelligence in EDA workflows to support automation using Binary Speculation Diagrams, reinforcement learning, and Large Language Models (LLMs). Third, it observes chiplet based heterogeneous integration and innovative packaging techniques including 2.5D, 3D stacking and high bandwidth. connects to enable scaleable AI and HPC architectures. workloads. These innovations outline a multidimensional roadmap to further VLSI innovation in the post Moore period.

title: Learnease AI: A Multimodal Deep Learning and Computer Vision Framework for Real-Time Emotion and Stress Pattern Recognition in Academic Environments
authors: Deepak K Y, Aswin B, Mary Gladence L, Merlin Mary Jenitha
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The students tend to be under high emotional stress and stress level, which can have a negative impact on the learning process and mental health in general. The paper suggests the application of a smart tool called Learnease AI, which is aimed to identify emotions of students and measure levels of stress based on the biometric analysis (facial recognition, etc.), which is one of the strategies, and behavioral parameters. The computation of facial images and emotional expressions is achieved using OpenCV, and the methods of assessing the stress perceived with help of facial expressions, survey answers, and behavioral patterns are applied using the machine learning model. It is a web application based on Django that will enable the students and educators to document the data, visualize emotional states and obtain personalized stress-related feedback in a convenient dashboard. The research aims to properly identify the emotion of students, measure the levels of stress and give actionable recommendations that can be used to promote mental health. The results show that the system is capable of detecting emotional state and trends of stress with a high degree of accuracy, which suggests that the system has the potential to be a useful tool that educational establishments use to track the health conditions and academic success of students.

title: IoT-based Smart Plant Watering System with Moisture Sensor
authors: P. Priya, B.Baranitharan, Karthick S, S. Nalini Jayanthi, Rajasekaran.A, Deepak Arumugam
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The agricultural sector, which is one of the oldest sectors, has been transformed by the high rate at which the Internet of Things (IOT) technology has developed and currently, it is possible to develop smart irrigation systems that optimize water consumption and enhance crop production. To automatically and optimize watering schedules, this project proposes the use of smart plant watering system based on the Internet of Things whose implementation utilizes sensors that measure moisture of the soil and the specific amount of water that the crop requires Using a water pump connected to a relay module, the system uses an ESP8266/ESP32 microcontroller to continuously monitor soil moisture levels in real-time and manage the irrigation process. Farmers can choose the crop type with the use of an intuitive mobile or web interface, which guarantees that the system will supply the exact amount of water needed for optimal growth. The system enables remote monitoring and irrigation control by integrating IOT platforms like Blynk, giving farmers with practical advice on how to maintain crop health and increase water usage efficiency. The suggested system is suitable for both large agricultural fields and small-scale gardens because it is economical, scalable, and environmentally friendly.

title: AI-Powered Real-Time Scheduling for Time-critical Parcel Distribution
authors: M. Jaeyalakshmi, Jagadeesh Baskar, Gopikrishnan R
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: Rapid urbanization and rise in e-commerce platforms impacts the last mile delivery market which is severely lacking in efficiency. The old logistics systems are based on the fixed routing that cannot respond to the constant change in urban traffic and time-sensitive fulfillment needs. The present paper describes an AI-based real-time scheduling system that was created to reduce the latency of the delivery process and operational expenses of the system in changing conditions. The proposed solution addresses the problem of vehicle routing by integrating K-Means Clustering for spatial zoning with a hybrid metaheuristic engine that consists of ACO and genetic algorithms. The microservice architecture uses MERN stack which is used for user interaction and python implements optimized logic for routing. It is a system that incorporates real-time traffic flow using Google Maps API that perform constant re-optimization of route which handles congestion or ad hoc pickup requests. The results suggest that this hybrid system performs much greater than the baseline models that remain stationary and enhance the average travel time and on-time delivery rates of high-priority deliveries.

title: A Real-Time Multi-Modal Assistive System for the Visually Impaired Using Computer Vision
authors: Gnanesh Charlin A, Ashraf Sheriff A, Guruprasath A, Madhavan M, Harini V, Keerthana R
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: People with visual impairment often depend on others to identify objects, read printed information, and understand their surroundings. Many existing assistive technologies support only one function at a time and require the use of multiple devices, which limits practicality. To address this problem, this paper proposes a real-time wearable assistive system that combines visual sensing and audio feedback in a single platform. The system is developed using a Raspberry Pi and a camera to capture and analyze visual data locally. Object detection techniques are applied to recognize common indoor and outdoor objects, while optical character recognition is used to extract text from signboards and printed materials. The processed information is converted into speech, allowing the user to receive hands-free audio guidance without relying on internet connectivity.Real-world testing demonstrates that the system provides dependable performance, achieving object recognition accuracy in the range of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$88-90 {\%}$</tex> with an average response time of 1.2-1.5 seconds. Due to its low cost, portability, and modular design, the proposed system is suitable for everyday use and can be extended in the future to include additional assistive features. The system improves environmental awareness and supports independent movement for visually impaired users.

title: Exploring the Effect of Leveraging Advances Technologies to Enhance Financial Advisory Services: (Case Study Based on Jordanian Auditing Firms)
authors: Abd Al-Salam Al-Hamad, Mohammad Alqsass, Riyad Darwazeh, Ibrahim Ezmigna, Jassim Algasawneh, Ghada Al-Rawashdeh, Mahmoud Alghizzawi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: The study examines the influence of AI & ML over the financial advisory services, emphasizing its capacity to ensure enhance innovation and customer satisfaction in the financial sectors of Jordan. We used suitable statistical techniques to study our data sample (25 auditing firms in Jordan), as well as descriptive statistics. Simple regression analysis was used in testing our hypotheses. We consider AI and ML usage as independent variable and financial advisory services as dependent variable in this study. However, it can be concluded regarding the results that financial advisory services have benefited from the integration of AI and ML. Its use of can lead Jordanian auditing firms to operational efficiency; computerized quality controls in the software environment; brings the benefits of reduced human errors; and at the same time rationalizes how they allocate resources. Also, with the help of AI and ML technologies, businesses can formulate personalized budgetary solutions for their customers based on each investor's individual lifestyle. These include enhancing the sophistication of forward-looking forecasting and scenario studies and, in doing so, augment the capabilities of Jordanian audit firms in projecting risk and assessing financial risk.

title: CyberShield: Cyberbullying Text Detection in Social Media
authors: Sohani Hazra, Pratyusa Mukherjee, Samriddhi Kundu, Supratik Karmakar, Sankhadip Roy, Subhadeep Bhadra
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Computational Intelligence and Networks (CINE)
abstract: Cyberbullying has become one of the critical problems in. the present generation that demands the necessity of the detection systems. to enjoy secure online interactions. This paper presents a deep learning based strategy to detect cyber-bullying in the social arena. Naturally, media written through Natural Language Processing(NLP). We suggest a Multi-layer Perceptron(MLP) approach using data. augment and subsequently compares its performance with the standard models such as Long Short-Term Memory(LSTM) and Bidirectional LSTM. We have a methodology that includes text-preprocessing. (tokenization, lemmatization, removal of stopwords), feature engineering (ngrams, TF-IDF) and model optimization. The results show that model MLP proposed with data augmentation has accuracy of 93.48% and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score of 91.69% that surpasses conventional machine learning models. This highlights the significance of situational awareness and preprocessing of efficient cyberbullying detection that offers a basis to realtime content regulation frameworks.

title: Decentralized Malware Propagation: A Proof-of-Concept Study on Self-Spreading Botnets
authors: Subramanya Bharathvamsi Koneti
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: With the increasing sophistication of cyber threats, self-propagating malware remains a critical challenge in network security. This study explores a novel botnet-worm hybrid designed for stealth and resilience. Leveraging Rust for its efficiency and security, we developed a controlled proof-of-concept botnet that autonomously spreads using a self-replicating mechanism while maintaining encrypted communication. The implementation utilizes a historical exploit to simulate real-world vulnerabilities and evaluates the feasibility of decentralized malware propagation. Our findings highlight the potential risks of combining self-replicating worms with botnet-like coordination in a decentralized fashion. The core contribution of this work lies in demonstrating a proof-of-concept botnet-worm hybrid that operates without centralized control, showcasing a new paradigm for stealthy and resilient malware propagation and highlighting implications for future cybersecurity defense mechanisms.

title: Predictive Monitoring of Harmful Algal Blooms Using Synthetic Data Augmentation and Machine Learning
authors: KaviyaPreetha R, Subramani Kavitha, Jabasheela L, Soorya S, Sathish Kumar P J, Banumathi S
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Harmful Algal Blooms (HABs) present notable hazards to public safety, economic stability, and aquatic ecosystem health. Thus, it is important to predict them accurately and in a timely manner to support effective management of public health and the environment. That said, predictive modeling will ultimately be limited by the quality of the observational data available. This study considers the potential to augment observational data to utilize machine learning-based forecasting for HAB occurrence. A sample set of 190,340 observations were obtained from the Harmful Algal Bloom (HAB) Monitoring Database, utilizing Chlorophyll-a concentration as a target variable and various environmental measures as key predictive features. From three model configurations, which were built using both LightGBM and Gradient Boosting Regressor frameworks, a baseline model trained using original data only, an augmented model utilizing synthetic data from the baseline model, and a tuned model without augmentation, the baseline model had an RMSE of 0.47 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(R^{2}=0.62\right)$</tex> and exhibited a significant improvement under the augmented model with an RMSE of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.18\left(\mathrm{R}^{2}=0.91\right)$</tex> using balanced synthetic data. The findings of this study demonstrate the effectiveness of the potential of data augmentation scaling, and cost-effectiveness of augmentation as a viable method for improving decision science and in situ monitoring applications in ecosystems and public health management.

title: Information Asymmetry and Financial Inclusion: A Fuzzy Inference Mechanism for the Role of Digital Technologies
authors: Anam Fazal, Madad Ali, Leena Anum, Yasir Javaid
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: The concept of information asymmetry in financial markets underlines uneven spread and heterogenous flow of information among participants. This condition leads to discrimination in financial sector when people with privileged information takes more benefits from investments and other options. Literature highlights information asymmetry as a significant barrier to financial inclusion. There is a need to understand the factors that cause information asymmetries in financial markets. Technological revolution including Artificial Intelligence (AI), Machine Learning (ML), big data, cloud computing and blockchain are systematically changing the behaviors of legacy financial institutions. In the light of this, the purpose of this study is twofold: 1) to identify the factors of information asymmetry and, 2) to propose a solution for the problem of information asymmetry. The research is adopting qualitative approach to fulfilling its objectives. A thorough literature review is performed to identify the factors of information asymmetry. Furthermore, fuzzy inference mechanisms are applied in MATLAB to gain evidence on the role of ML in reducing the level of information asymmetry in the financial sector. This study provides unique insights for solving the problem of information asymmetry with the help of ML technology. A new perspective is opened with the findings of this study for implementation of AI in financial infrastructure of countries.

title: Digitally Controlled Home Automation
authors: Mano Jemila M R, Vinisha M, Vishnu N, Vishnuprabhu S, Balamurugan S
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Smart home automation of the Internet of Things (IoT) is one of the uses that allow control remotely and successful monitoring of household appliances. This paper presents the design and development of a dual-mode, low-cost home automation system using ESP8266 NodeMCU that is a microcontroller. The proposed system (Wi-Fi connectivity) operates on the Blynk IoT platform (internet mode) and switches to Bluetooth interface (offline mode) in case there is a network failure. Safe switching of appliances is made possible by GIO operated relay modules. AC loads such as water pumps, fans and lights. The system allows switching modes without human intervention. The results of the experiment indicate reliable relay switching under both modes, low control latency, and long-term stability of operation. To be realistic in applications in the sphere of smart home automation, the proposed solution is an inexpensive, dependable, and extensible approach.

title: Corrections to “Integral-Type Terminal Sliding Mode MPPT Controller for a Standalone PV System With a DC–DC Boost Converter Under Real Time Varying Weather Conditions”
authors: Jawhar Aiboudi, Habiba Rizki, Redouane Chaibi, Nawfal Jebbor, El-Mahjoub Boufounas
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Presents corrections to the paper, (Integral-Type Terminal Sliding Mode MPPT Controller for a Standalone PV System With a DC–DC Boost Converter Under Real Time Varying Weather Conditions).

title: Soil Chemistry Estimation for Agriculture Planning and Land Management from Hyperspectral Imaging
authors: William Basener, Alexander Miller
publish: 2025 15th Workshop on Hyperspectral Imaging and Signal Processing: Evolution in Remote Sensing (WHISPERS)
abstract: Soil chemical composition and properties are critical drivers of plant growth, ecosystem function, and geological processes. We develop and evaluate multiple algorithms for predicting soil chemistry-including pH and concentrations of clay, carbon, nitrogen, calcium, magnesium, potassium, and sodium-from VNIR-SWIR spectra. The best-performing models achieve <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> scores of 0.73(PH), 0.84 (clay), 0.67 (c), 0.66(Mg), 0.64(Na), 0.61 (calcium), 0.44(N), and 0.29(K). Our top performing model is a neural network incorporating PCA preprocessing. We utilize the Globally Distributed Soil Spectral Library, compiled by ICRAF and ISRIC, comprising 4,438 soil spectra from 58 countries across five continents. Spectral-based soil property estimation is an active research area due to its potential for rapid, cost-effective large-scale monitoring and optimization for agriculture, particularly in regions lacking laboratory soil analysis infrastructure. A major challenge in estimating soil chemistry from spectra is that the most spectral libraries are locally sourced with limited variation. Models trained on locally-sourced libraries are not effective when use beyond the area where the libraries were collected. Moreover, accuracy scores for models trained and tested on locally-sourced libraries will be high relative to to when global libraries are used, providing a potentially skewed perspective of general applicability. Moreover, there is not sufficient understanding of how a particular highperforming model incorporates particular spectral features in its predictions. The effectiveness of PCA in our tests indicate that much of the information is contained in the first 50 principle components. The results of our Bayesian optimization of our neural network explains the nonlinear nature of the information within the data. This nonlinear nature is further confirmed by the observation that the nonlinear methods like neural networks outperform the linear methods like PLS that are often used to estimate chemistry from spectra.

title: Skin Lesion Diagnosis from Dermoscopy using Deep Neural Networks: Custom CNN vs. Transfer Learning Approach
authors: Sallauddin Mohmmad, Eshwar Nomula, Nagasri Pingili, Anjani Enumula, Hansika Vangari, Gulab Singh Chauhan
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Skin cancer is among the most common and potentially fatal cancers worldwide, where early diagnosis significantly enhances the chances of successful treatment. This research explores a deep learning framework for classifying skin lesions from dermoscopic images. The study utilized the HAM10000(2019) and MSLDv2.0 datasets, which contained images representing 14 categories of skin conditions. Two distinct models were developed and evaluated: a custom-designed Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and a pre-trained Fine-Tuned InceptionV3 model adapted through transfer learning. Comparative analysis revealed that the fine-tuned InceptionV3 achieved superior performance, attaining an accuracy of 72.73%, surpassing the 68.07% accuracy of the custom CNN.

title: Factors Affecting Artificial Intelligence (AI)-Driven Transformation of e-Government: Evidence from South Korea
authors: Chang Hee Yun, Ai Ping Teoh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: Empirical studies on artificial intelligence (AI)-based internal government networks remain limited. This study addresses that gap by examining factors influencing the AI-driven transformation of e-Government services in South Korea, focusing on the government’s intranet administrative platform. Grounded in the Unified Theory of Acceptance and Use of Technology (UTAUT) and the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM), the study adds an AI-specific construct to enhance explanatory power. Performance Expectancy (PE) and Perceived Intelligence (PI) were modeled as predictors, Attitude as a mediator, and AI-driven e-Government Transformation Acceptance (AITA) as the outcome variable. Survey data from 259 government employees were analyzed using SPSS and PLS-SEM. PE had a strong direct effect on AITA (β = 0.632, p < 0.001), while PI was significant only through Attitude mediation (β = 0.109, p = 0.002). Six of seven hypotheses were supported, highlighting the role of Attitude (β = 0.262, p = 0.001). This study bridges the theory-practice gap by providing empirical guidance for AI adoption in government.

title: Faster R-CNN and Siamese Network-Based Inspection Framework for PCB Defect Identification
authors: M S Saranya, Vijaya Prabhu R, Maheshwaran T, Nandhini I, Leo Prasanth L
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: In electronic manufacturing, assembly of Printed Circuit Board (PCB) is decisive process to make the undefective products. The main challenge in assembling PCB includes incomplete circuit, Components missing, misalignment. An automatic PCB defect detection system has been proposed which reduces manual inspection. A dual deep learning approach includes Faster R-CNN for detecting objects and a Siamese Neural Network for result analysis has been achieved high mean Average Precision (mAP) scores of 89.52. Experimental results demonstrate higher accuracy of 96.34 in detecting anomalies as well as reducing the inspection time.

title: Predicting Cyber-Attacks in Distributed Smart Healthcare Networks using a Federated Cognitive Deep Learning Framework
authors: Vandana Dharmapuri, Sushama Rani Dutta
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Increasing dependence on interconnected Internet of Things (IoT) devices and cyber-physical infrastructures are exposing smart healthcare systems to complex cyber-attacks, and traditional centralized intrusion detectors have scalability issues, privacy concerns, and high latency and thus are not suitable in a distributed healthcare setting. To address these issues, a Federated Cognition Deep Learning Framework (FCDLF) is recommended in this paper to identify a cyber-attack in a secure, privacy-preserving, and low-latency manner using a gated recurrent unit (GRU) model, trained with a federated learning paradigm. The proposed framework is thoroughly tested on a simulated healthcare IoT dataset, as well as the standard CICIDs2017 dataset and its effectiveness is determined in the terms of accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, detection latency, communication overhead, and computational cost. The findings of the experiment prove that the federated GRU model with an average score of 98.7 attains higher detection rates, communication overhead is lessened by 35 percent, and computation expenses are lowered by 28 percent while at the same time maintaining data privacy than traditional centralized and distributed baseline models. These findings prove that the proposed framework can be successfully used to provide secure, scalable, and effective collaborative threat intelligence to resource-constrained medical IoT devices, and it is therefore quite applicable to real-time smart healthcare cybersecurity application.

title: An Ensemble Machine Learning Approach for ASD Detection: Handling missing channels with IDW Interpolation and Unveiling Key EEG Features through Explainable AI
authors: Al Mukshit Plabon, Sazid Salahuddin, Abdul Mukit, Omar Faruk Jehady, Fatima Tuz Zuba, Md. Faisal Mina
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Prompt and efficient diagnosis of Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) through Electroencephalography (EEG) signals is crucial for timely identification. This paper presents a machine learning (ML) approach to overcome the limitations regarding clinical expertise and resources, ensuring accurate ASD diagnosis. The framework starts from Inverse Distance Weighting (IDW) interpolation for recovering the missing channels and then filters the EEG signals to get rid of noise. A novel feature population is utilized using time domain descriptive, frequency domain power measures, and nonlinear complexity indices for improved analysis. To deal with noisy and incomplete data, a robust interpolation and validation of endpoints are developed to extract robust features. For classification, machine learning algorithms are applied to differentiate cases from those of non-ASD. Explainable AI methods aided in discovering the most important feature for accurate detection. The most important feature, Waveform length at Centro-Parietal region (lead 19), accounts for 1.48% of total ensemble importance. The proposed model validates a 95.9% cross-validated accuracy using a voting ensemble classifier, surpassing the performance of the existing methods. The results suggest that, in contrast to direct EEG signals, advanced preprocessing and structured EEG feature extraction, as well as careful missing data treatment using the IDW interpolation, along with explainable AI for feature importance analysis, play a beneficial role by providing transparency into the decision-making process of ML models and good interpretation, enhancing the confidence of ASD detection from EEG signals.

title: Fault Tolerant Routing Strategy for Delay Sensitive Industrial Internet of Things Communication Infrastructures
authors: M. Menaka, S. Sathya, M. Sakthivanitha, Vishwa Priya V, M. Mohamed Sirajudeen, J. Anciline Jenifer
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Fault-tolerant routing is crucial for reliable and delaysensitive communication for Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) infrastructures where the harsh environments, interference and dynamic network conditions typically degrade Quality of Service (QoS). The main issue that is tackled in this work is the high vulnerability of industrial sensor networks to link failures and congestion, leading to unacceptable delays in mission-critical applications such as predictive maintenance, robotic control, and real-time monitoring. The goal is to create a strategy for faulttolerant routing, in order to reduce the end-to-end latency and ensure high reliability of packet delivery under different network conditions. The proposed method combines a hybrid approach of combining dynamic link quality estimation, multipath redundancy, and delay-aware adaptive route switching using lightweight distributed decision logic that is appropriate for resource constrained nodes. Simulation results are conducted on a 200-node industrial topology, which show a 27% reduction of end-to-end delay, a 19% improvement of packet delivery ratio and a 22% reduction of routing overhead against state-of-the-art protocols such as RPL-OF0, LOADng, and CQRP. These results validate the use of this strategy to enhance the network resilience and timeliness, which supports its use in real-time industrial control environments. These enhancements show that the suggested routing plan can be implemented directly in the IIoT environments that require reliable low latency communications to safeguard safety-critical industry applications like automated production lines, proactive maintenance systems, and real-time robots.

title: MRDP-UNet: A Multi-Resolution Deep Learning Framework with Directional Gabor Feature Enhancement and Adaptive Parallel Attention for Retinal Vessel Segmentation
authors: Tirumalasetti Supraja, Kankanala Srinivas
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Accurate segmentation of retinal blood vessels from fundus images is a critical prerequisite for computer aided diagnosis of ophthalmic diseases such as diabetic retinopathy and glaucoma. Despite recent advances in deep learning, reliable extraction of thin vessels remains challenging due to low contrast, intensity inhomogeneity, and severe class imbalance between vessel and background pixels. This paper proposes a multi-resolution directional parallel UNet (MRDP-UNet), segmentation framework that integrates explicit directional Gabor based feature enhancement with a multi scale encoder-decoder architecture. Directional Gabor responses are employed to strengthen vessel oriented structures and preserve thin capillary continuity. In addition, an adaptive parallel attention mechanism is introduced to refine spatial and channel wise features during decoding. To address foreground-background imbalance, a balanced focal Tversky loss function is adopted, and a hysteresis thresholding based post-processing strategy is applied to enhance vessel connectivity. Experimental evaluation on the DRIVE dataset demonstrates an accuracy of 95.09% and sensitivity of 83.00%, while on the STARE dataset the proposed method achieves an accuracy of 97.52% and sensitivity of 83.27%, outperforming several state-of-the-art methods, particularly in thin vessel detection.

title: Automated Passion Fruit Woodiness Disease Detection: A Deep Learning Approach with EfficientNet-B3
authors: S. Naveen, G. Vinuja, R. Santhana Krishnan, S. Sumathi, M.SahanaPreethi, S. Kavitha
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: Passion Fruit Woodiness Disease (PWD) is a significant threat to passion fruit cultivation, causing yield loss and economic damage. Early detection is critical for effective management and prevention, yet the task is challenging due to the diverse manifestations of the disease, environmental factors, and the need for high accuracy in distinguishing between healthy and infected plants. Current detection methods often rely on manual inspection, which is labor-intensive, time-consuming, and prone to human error. Furthermore, the existing automated systems lack robustness across different environmental conditions and disease stages, resulting in suboptimal detection performance. This paper presents a deep learning-based approach for the automatic detection of PWD using the EfficientNet-B3 model, trained on the PassionFruit-Atlas and PassionFruit-ImageNet datasets. These datasets contain images representing various stages of disease progression under different environmental conditions. The proposed methodology addresses the limitations of current systems by providing a scalable and efficient solution for real-time disease detection. By utilizing EfficientNet-B3, which is known for its scalability and computational efficiency, the model achieves high accuracy in distinguishing between healthy and infected passion fruit plants. The model's architecture is designed to extract hierarchical features from the input images, ensuring the accurate detection of subtle disease symptoms. The evaluation results demonstrate the model's robustness, with high precision, recall, and F1-score, making it suitable for practical deployment in precision agriculture.

title: Hybrid Vision–Language and YOLO Framework for Detecting Rare Road Objects in Autonomous Vehicles
authors: K. Saiveena, P. Praveen
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Object detection is a foundational component of autonomous vehicle (AV) perception systems. Although modern detectors achieve high accuracy on predefined classes such as vehicles and pedestrians, they struggle to recognize rare, long-tail, and out-of-distribution road objects including debris, animals, fallen cargo, and unusual obstacles. This limitation stems from the closed-set assumption of traditional detection frameworks. In this work, we propose a vision-language prompted object detection framework that integrates conventional detectors with openvocabulary vision-language models (VLMs) to recognize rare road objects using natural language prompts. Leveraging recent advances in VLM-based detectors such as OWL-ViT and Grounding DINO, the proposed system enables semantic detection beyond fixed class labels. We further introduce a rare road object benchmark constructed from existing AV datasets to evaluate long-tail detection performance. Experimental analysis demonstrates improved recall for rare objects and enhanced robustness to out-of-distribution scenarios while maintaining realtime feasibility. This work highlights the importance of languageguided perception for safer autonomous driving in complex realworld environments.

title: AI-Integrated Solar Panel Illumination Network Using MATLAB Traffic Estimation and GSM Alerting
authors: Merlin Inbamalar T, Darshana K, Gowsalya M, Iswarya Lakshmi S
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: The proposed work will pose an Artificial Intelligence-based Smart Street Light System, which could be used to improve energy-saving, city safety, and environmental sustainability. It combines Arduino Uno as the board with an ESP8266 Wi-Fi system, which will allow controls based on intelligence and real-time monitoring of street lights. PIR sensors respond to pedestrian and motor motions to provide light when it is needed whereas LDR sensors respond to the dynamic changes in light conditions by increasing or decreasing the brightness. Traffic sensors can be Infrared (IR) traffic sensors that measure traffic density to regulate the brightness of lights based on that density and currentvoltage sensors to measure power usage to manage energy usage better. The lighting system will be powered by renewable solar energy with the addition of a solar panel, charge controller and rechargeable battery to provide autonomous power. LED lights that are dimmable using relay modules give accurate lighting hence less power is wasted. Moreover, a video-based traffic density detector with AI-enabled features in the MATLAB increases the adaptive lighting control and facilitates intelligent traffic control. The AI-assisted system will help in cutting costs, sustainability, intelligent fault detection, and make decisions based on the data, which will come as future-ready smart urban infrastructure.

title: Smart Prescription Decoder: An Intelligent Framework for Medicine Receipt Analysis and Alternate Medicine Suggestion using OCR and NLP
authors: Ashlin Deepa R N, Rahul Sangubotla, Arjun Shettigar, Errabelli Annapoorna, Neela Karthik
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The convergence of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) in health systems is revolutionizing conventional workflows and the delivery of services. In this paper, the design of an AI system that can identify handwritten prescriptions for medicine based on Optical Character Recognition (OCR) and deep learning algorithms is discussed. The prime objective is to read medicine names and related information from free-hand prescriptions automatically and transform unstructured text into structured, machine-readable format. The system draws on a hybrid deep learning framework that combines Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) for image feature extraction and Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) for sequence text recognition. In addition to data retrieval, the system offers intelligent suggestions for replacement drugs based on a comprehensive pharmaceutical database to facilitate informed decision-making and cost-effective prescription management. The suggested solution significantly reduces manual effort, minimizes errors, and improves overall efficiency in dispensing drugs. This research aims to bring about improved healthcare delivery by offering an accurate, scalable, and intelligent framework for the analysis of prescriptions and suggestion of medicines.

title: Addressing Trust and Efficiency in Pediatric Anemia Diagnosis Using Interpretable Decision Trees
authors: Shudipta Banik, Abrar Fahim, Mohammad Shahin Uddin, Birupaksha Biswas, Hasna Hena Joti, Shat-El-Shahriar Khan, Md. Shakhauat Hossan Sumon
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: Pediatric anemia is a common health problem in the entire world that may result in poor cognitive development, exhaustion, and irreversible developmental deficiencies if not diagnosed and treated. Traditional laboratory tests are resource-intensive and less available in low-resource settings, but they are accurate. Recent machine learning techniques have demonstrated potential in detecting anemia, but they tend to use small datasets, are not interpretable, and do not consider computational efficiency, which restricts their use in clinical settings. In order to overcome these limitations, we suggest an optimized Decision Tree that is interpretable to classify pediatric anemia. A two-step feature selection approach which combines both the statistical tests and a Genetic Algorithm, determines Hemoglobin (Hb) and Packed Cell Volume (PCV) to be the most informative features. The hyperparameters are optimized using GridSearch, whereas Local Interpretable Model-Agnostic Explanations (LIME) and Morris sensitivity analysis improve the transparency of the model. The computational efficiency of the model is strictly tested in terms of deployment in real-time. Results of an experimental study on a pediatric dataset indicate that the proposed Decision Tree has an overall testing accuracy of 99.60%, a Matthews Correlation Coefficient of 0.9914, am acro F1-score of 0.9957, and a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5 \%}$</tex> confidence interval of [<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 8 8 2 - 1. 0 0}$</tex>]. Robustness in terms of mean accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.9922 \pm 0.0038$</tex> is confirmed with five-fold cross-validation. It takes 0.0132 s to train and 0.0051s to infer, which is appropriate in resource-constrained clinical settings. These findings suggest <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$t$</tex> hat the suggested method results in a valid, understandable, and effective decision aid tool to identify early cases of pediatric anemia.

title: Advancing Tabular Stroke Modelling Through a Novel Hybrid Architecture and Feature-Selection Synergy
authors: Yousuf Islam, Md. Jalal Uddin Chowdhury, Sumon Chandra Das, Tajwar Elahi Choudhury, Suhrath Tahra, Rukaiya Akther Sharmin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Biomedical Engineering, Computer and Information Technology for Health (BECITHCON)
abstract: Brain strokes are a prominent cause of death and disability globally, making early and accurate forecasting essential for prompt medical intervention. This study introduces a thorough machine learning method for classifying brain strokes through the use of ensemble learning techniques and targeted feature selection. We analyze various classification algorithms, including LightGBM, Random Forest, Support Vector Machines, and Neural Networks, on a dataset comprising 4,981 patient records with 11 clinical features. To tackle the inherent class imbalance (with 95.02% being non-stroke cases), we apply the Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) along with systematic outlier removal using the Interquartile Range method. Our approach integrates three different feature selection methods: the complete feature set, correlation-based selection, and Random Forest-based importance ranking. Advanced ensemble techniques, such as soft voting and stacking classifiers, are utilized to improve prediction accuracy. Experimental findings reveal that the stacking ensemble achieves exceptional performance with a ROC-AUC of 99.66% ± 0.29%, an accuracy of 97.20% ± 1.57%, and an F1-score of 97.15% ± 1.70%. Individual classifiers indicate that LightGBM consistently performs well across various feature sets, with ROC-AUC scores surpassing 91%. The suggested ensemble method outperforms individual classifiers significantly, offering a solid framework for clinical decision-making in stroke prediction and illustrating the effectiveness of integrating multiple learning algorithms for medical diagnostic applications.

title: Construction of a Multi-Dimensional Blended Teaching Model for Mental Health Education Based on Knowledge Graph and OBE Concept
authors: Mingfang Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI in Education Technology and Applications (AIETA)
abstract: The importance of mental health education in higher education is increasingly evident, but traditional teaching methods struggle to meet students' diverse psychological needs and learning styles. To address this, this paper builds a multidimensional blended teaching model for mental health education based on existing knowledge graph education models and the Outcome-Based Education (OBE) concept. The model integrates knowledge graph technology to structurally represent mental health-related knowledge, student profiles, and competency goals, forming a semantic network for personalized learning paths. At the same time, it introduces the OBE concept to systematically design teaching objectives, assessment methods, and feedback mechanisms, ensuring a close alignment between the teaching process and learning outcomes. The model consists of three core layers: the knowledge representation layer maps mental health topics, competency items, and teaching resources through a multi-relational knowledge graph; the teaching design layer embeds OBE-oriented learning objectives into content recommendations and interactive teaching scenarios, and learner modeling to achieve real-time personalized teaching. Experimental results show that the proposed framework improves link prediction accuracy on the educational knowledge graph, enhances personalized path relevance and OBE goal-content alignment, and supports real-time adaptive recommendation under learner-state updates.

title: Benchmarking U-Net for Low-Light Image Enhancement: A Comparative Perspective
authors: Ramesh Jitty, Kankanala Srinivas, Sathish Mothe, Saladi Saritha, Rama Krishna Bandaru, Yepuganti Karuna
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advances in Electrical, Electronics, Ubiquitous Communication, and Computational Intelligence (RAEEUCCI)
abstract: Improvement of images captured in insufficient lighting, known as Low Light Image Enhancement (LLIE), is an essential goal in computer vision. This problem becomes more complex when the improvement should be done using only single image, without access to depth cues or sequential pictures. In this work, we conducted a detailed comparative study on a baseline U-Net Architecture adapted for Low Light Improvement. Several the settings are explored, including adjustments to the depth of the network, activation functions, Loss criteria and optimization algorithms, to understand its influence on improvement performance. The popular LOL data set is used for experiments and the structural similarity index Measure (SSIM) and the Peak Signal to Noise Ratio (PSNR) are used to assessment. The results indicate that specific options of architectural parameters and training significantly affect the quality of production, with a configuration reaching a PSNR of 20.92 dB and an SSIM of 0.723.

title: Adversarial Robustness Evaluation of Attention-Enhanced LSTM-ResNet Hybrid Architecture for Mobile Malware Detection
authors: Iffath Yasmeen, Mohammed Ali Shaik
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: Mobile malware detection is difficult despite great accuracy in benign conditions. This study presents a hybrid architecture that detects fraudulent Android apps while resisting adversarial perturbations using Long Short-Term Memory networks, Residual networks, and multi-head attention methods. AE-LSTM-ResNet uses sequential temporal dependencies from LSTM units and hierarchical feature representations from residual connections with attention mechanisms to target crucial malware indications. On the Drebin dataset of 11,120 samples from 179 malware families, the model achieves 97.38% clean accuracy and 94.53% adversarial accuracy, a 4.12% improvement over previous methods. Systematic assessment against FGSM, PGD, and AutoAttack is included. Certified robustness bounds provide realistic security assurances, and extensive tests show improved robustness-accuracy tradeoff. Through thorough adversarial robustness evaluation, our study bridges theoretical detection accuracy and actual deployment security.

title: Flight Dynamics: A Unified Model for Flight Pricing and Delay Forecasting using Flask
authors: K. Shiva Rama Krishna, G. Rama Krishna, K. Sanjana, A. Harshika, O. Sridhar, P. Sharada
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The project's goal is to create an application that offers thorough insights for planning air travel by forecasting flight costs and delays. To generate precise forecasts, the system uses machine learning algorithms to examine past flight data, meteorological conditions, and other pertinent variables. The suggested system adds more capabilities for delay prediction, since the current system mainly concentrates on predicting flight costs using historical data. Personalized suggestions, real-time updates, environmental impact assessments, integrated booking, and interaction with travel insurance are some of the main features. To provide accurate and current forecasts, the system makes use of real-time data from weather services, airlines, and air traffic control. A user- friendly interface allows users to get flight information and make well-informed selections. Additionally, a login page is included in the program to improve security and user experience.

title: Enhanced SSVEP Response Detection using Deep EEGNet with Temporal-Spatial Feature Integration
authors: Sunil A, M Selvi
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: The current landscape of Steady-State Visually Evoked Potential (SSVEP) based Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCIs) faces significant challenges, including high computational complexity, limited generalization across subjects, and sensitivity to noise and artifacts in EEG signals. Traditional machine learning approaches often require extensive handcrafted feature extraction and large datasets, limiting practical deployment in real-time applications. Furthermore, many existing models struggle to maintain accuracy with minimal preprocessing, affecting the usability in dynamic environments. The proposed methodology leverages EEGNet, a compact and efficient deep learning architecture, to overcome these limitations. By employing an end-to-end convolutional neural network tailored for EEG signal processing, the model reduces dependence on complex feature engineering and adapts effectively to diverse EEG patterns. The workflow incorporates systematic data collection from multiple SSVEP stimuli frequencies, followed by minimal preprocessing steps including filtering and normalization to retain essential signal characteristics while mitigating noise. Key features of this approach include a lightweight model architecture optimized for low computational cost, robust training strategies using balanced datasets, and comprehensive evaluation metrics such as accuracy, precision, and recall. This framework ensures improved classification performance and better generalization to new subjects and recording conditions. The streamlined pipeline facilitates deployment in real-time BCI systems, enhancing usability without sacrificing reliability.

title: deFOREST: Fusing Optical and Radar satellite data for Enhanced Sensing of Tree-cover loss_supp1-3689741.pdf
authors: Julio Enrique Castrillon-Candas
publish: N/A
abstract: In this paper we develop a deforestation detection pipeline that incorporates optical and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) data. A crucial component of the pipeline is the construction of anomaly maps of the optical data, which is done using the residual space of a discrete Karhunen-Loéve (KL) expansion. Anomalies are quantified using a concentration bound on the distribution of the residual components for the nominal state of the forest. This bound does not require prior knowledge on the distribution of the data. This is in contrast to statistical parametric methods that assume knowledge of the data distribution, an impractical assumption that is especially infeasible for high dimensional data such as ours. Once the optical anomaly maps are computed they are combined with SAR data, and the state of the forest is classified by using a Hidden Markov Model (HMM). We test our approach with Sentinel-1 (SAR) and Sentinel-2 (Optical) data on a 92 km × 92 km region in the Amazon forest. The results show that both the hybrid optical-radar and optical only methods achieve high accuracy that is superior to the recent state-of-the-art hybrid method. Moreover, the hybrid method is significantly more robust in the case of sparse optical data that are common in highly cloudy regions.

title: Intelligent Detection of High Impedance Faults in Microgrid Distribution Lines Using Optimized Machine Learning Models
authors: Imen Ben Hamida, Pallav Kumar Bera, Taha Al-Saadi, Samita Rani Pani, Majdi Mansouri
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper presents a data-driven protection scheme for high impedance fault (HIF) classification in microgrids. Conventional overcurrent protection often lacks the sensitivity to detect HIFs because of their low current magnitude and nonlinear characteristics. To address these limitations, we investigate an approach based on multiple machine learning (ML) classifier models for distribution line fault diagnosis. The proposed technique trains ML classifiers directly on raw three-phase differential current signals, allowing the models to autonomously learn patterns without manual feature extraction. The classifiers are optimized through a grid-search procedure and evaluated using a PSCAD/EMTDC model of a 5-bus AC microgrid test system with distributed generations (DGs) operating in both grid-connected and islanded modes. The generated dataset includes healthy conditions, internal faults (with/without HIF), and external faults with current transformer (CT) saturation. Nine ML models are assessed using accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, receiver operating characteristic (ROC) analysis, computational cost, and noise sensitivity tests. The results indicate that the feedforward neural network (FFNN), random forest (RF), and stochastic gradient descent (SGD) classifiers exhibit robust and consistent classification performance on the test set. Significantly, even when using raw input data, the proposed methodology effectively discriminates subtle HIF signatures from other transient events and CT saturation conditions. These findings highlight the potential of optimized ML models, particularly FFNN and RF classifiers, to provide accurate and robust support for real-time microgrid protection.

title: Effectiveness of STAR-RIS-Assisted JP-CoMP: Minimum-Rate and Sum-Rate Perspectives
authors: Jaein Lee, Si-Hyeon Lee
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: In this letter, we comprehensively analyze the effectiveness of joint-processing coordinated multipoint (JP-CoMP) assisted by simultaneously transmitting and reflecting reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (STAR-RISs) in a multi-user multiple-input single-output (MU-MISO) scenario. To investigate the relative effectiveness of different CoMP strategies and RIS types under distinct objectives, we consider minimum-rate and sum-rate maximization, and jointly optimize the beamforming vectors and RIS configurations to achieve the best performance under each objective. Simulation results show that, under the minimum-rate objective, the JP scheme significantly outperforms coordinated beamforming (CB), while the performance gain becomes marginal under the sum-rate objective. Moreover, STAR-RISs yield substantial improvements over RISs for both objectives, whereas the lack of beamforming optimization can even cause STAR-RIS-assisted JP-CoMP to underperform the beamforming-optimized RIS-assisted CB-CoMP.

title: Evidential Reliable Fusion for Partial Multi-view Incomplete Multi-label Classification_supp1-3689103.pdf
authors: Jiaying Zhou
publish: N/A
abstract: In recent years, the missing data problem in multi view multi-label classification (MvMlC) has attracted extensive attention from researchers, with numerous solutions for partial multi-view incomplete multi-label classification (PMvIMlC) emerging. Nevertheless, two critical challenges persist. One is suboptimal coarse-grained multi-view fusion: traditional dynamic fusion at the view level is unable to accommodate the practical fusion demands of samples with diverse qualities. The other is neglecting latent information within missing labels: during the training phase, existing works only focus on the limited supervised information of unmissing labels while ignoring the underlying information at missing positions. To address these issues, we propose Evidential Reliable Fusion for Partial Multi view Incomplete Multi-label Classification, termed ERF. ERF comprises two core modules: 1) Uncertainty-guided fusion module via evidence theory and 2) adaptive negative label pseudo labeling. The former quantifies sample-level uncertainty of each view based on evidence theory, which is then used to guide multi-view fusion, enabling a fine-grained, instance-level multi view fusion scheme. For the latter, leveraging the model's perception ability for neighboring samples in the label space, we design a strategy to select reliable negative pseudo-labels. This module enhances supervisory information to aid model training by recovering reliable negative pseudo-labels. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our ERF delivers significantly superior classification performance over existing methods.

title: A Groupwise Add–Multiply–Shift–Accumulate Datapath for Efficient DNN Accelerators
authors: Zhiwang Huo, Wenzhe Zhao, Yuanchang Gong, Tian Xia, Zheng Wang, Pengju Ren
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: Multiply–accumulate(MAC) units account for a large fraction of the power and area in modern deep neural network (DNN) accelerators. Although low-bitwidth quantization reduces hardware overhead, the high cost of multipliers remains a fundamental bottleneck in modern accelerator datapaths. This article proposes add–multiply–shift–accumulate (AMC), a groupwise arithmetic datapath that reduces multiplier count by sharing base multiplications across groups of neighboring weights and generating residual products using lightweight shift operation. To support efficient deployment, we design a compact residual encoding and buffer organization that allows AMC arrays to be constructed with minimal decoding and control overheads. While AMC can be directly applied to existing quantized models, we further introduce a lightweight residual-aware fine-tuning (RAF) procedure to increase AMC compatibility. We implement AMC-based accelerators in SystemVerilog and synthesize them in TSMC 28-nm CMOS technology across operating frequencies from 500MHz to 1GHz. At the compute unit level, AMC reduces arithmetic area by 39.5%–62.8% and dynamic power by 32.2%–60.3% compared with optimized baseline multipliers. When integrated into CNN and Vision Transformer accelerators, AMC achieves <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1.34\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula>–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$18.90\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> higher area efficiency and up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10.16\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> higher energy efficiency than prior designs while preserving baseline inference accuracy.

title: Defects Diagnosis for Transformer Winding using 1-D FRA DATA Augmentation and AGAF-ConVNeXtV2 Framework
authors: Dongyang Wang, Weiheng Liu, Anzhi Fu, Xiaobo Zhang, Long Zhang, Kaijie Xu, Guochao Qian, Lei Guo, Lijun Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Winding defect diagnosis is a key research focus in transformer detection. Deep learning has been widely applied in this field, but its performance is limited by insufficient defect samples and class imbalance. To tackle these problems, this paper proposes a MultiImageTime data generation model based on one-dimensional(1-D) Frequency Response Analysis(FRA) data, which enhances the generative model's ability to learn frequency response characteristics, thereby achieving high-discrimination data augmentation. Meanwhile, an adaptive Gramian Angular Field (AGAF) method is introduced to image the 1-D FRA data, adaptively focusing on critical defect frequency bands to reduce defect information loss. On this basis, a MultiImageTime-AGAF-ConvNeXtV2 framework for winding defect diagnosis is constructed, which fully exploits ConvNeXtV2’s efficient image feature extraction capability to achieve high-precision defect identification. Experimental results show that the framework performs excellently even with small samples, reaching a classification accuracy of 95.12%.
