title: A Hybrid Pure Pursuit–PID and TD3 Control Framework for Differential-Drive Mobile Robots
authors: Ufuk Demircioglu, Halit Bakir, Badar Almarri, A. H. Abdul Hafez
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: In this study, a hybrid control structure that combines classical control methods with reinforcement learning is proposed for target tracking of a differential-drive mobile robot. In the proposed framework, Pure Pursuit–based reference generation and an inner-loop Proportional–Integral–Derivative (PID) controller are integrated with the Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (TD3) algorithm. While the classical Pure Pursuit–PID structure ensures baseline stability and safety, the TD3-based learning component enhances performance by generating corrective torque contributions when classical control becomes insufficient. First, experiments are conducted under nominal system conditions to demonstrate that the proposed approach preserves the fundamental behavior of the classical controller and achieves comparable trajectory-tracking performance. Subsequently, the robustness of the control schemes is evaluated under parametric uncertainties applied to the chassis and wheel masses. Experimental results show that the classical PID-based control structure fails to achieve stable convergence under these uncertainties, whereas the proposed reinforcement learning–enhanced controller consistently reaches the target in a stable and reliable manner across all tested scenarios, achieving a 100% success rate. Quantitative analysis further indicates that the proposed method significantly improves convergence behavior and tracking stability by reducing trajectory deviations and eliminating oscillatory behavior observed in the classical controller. Furthermore, the influence of key learning components—such as minibatch size, replay buffer capacity, optimization algorithm, and network architecture—on control performance is systematically analyzed through a comprehensive ablation study. The results demonstrate that the proposed residual learning approach provides superior stability and robustness, effectively overcoming the practical limitations of classical control methods. Accordingly, this study presents a reliable and generalizable mobile robot control architecture capable of coping with model uncertainties and dynamic variations that are inevitable in real-world applications.

title: Teaching Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning to Non-Majors: A Scoping Review
authors: O. Koklu, M. Şahal, M. Dede
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Equipping university students with Artificial Intelligence (AI)/Machine Learning (ML) skills is essential for future career integration. Considering the crucial role of AI/ML and the identified shortage of instructional guidelines and resources for students, our objective was to evaluate AI/ML instruction for non-majors. In this study, we provide a scoping review of AI/ML instruction at the tertiary level, identifying 24 journal articles, nine conference papers, and three book chapters published from 2010 to 2025, gathered from seven databases. By analyzing the specific focus areas of AI/ML learning and teaching, we detected major trends in practice and areas to be improved in AI/ML educational research: (a) studies predominantly focus on AI/ML literacy and models that are accessible for non-majors, (b) a diverse set of technological tools and platforms are used in AI/ML instruction, (c) student-centered pedagogical approaches are claimed to be adopted in the courses, but most of the assessment relies solely on student surveys instead of examining learning outcomes, and (d) studies often lack a rigorous research design. Future research should prioritize providing more robust evidence of instructional effectiveness in AI/ML. This study identifies existing gaps and future research directions. Its findings provide insightful feedback to university instructors, instructional designers, and researchers on AI/ML research trends, aiming to enhance the overall quality of this emerging and important field.

title: Robust Data-Driven Model Predictive Control With Soft Constrained Strategy
authors: Xiao-Long Wu, Wen-Hai Han, Xin Li, Hong-Gui Han, Jun-Fei Qiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: A robust data-driven model predictive control (RDD-MPC) with a soft constrained strategy is proposed in this article. This method is designed to address model predictive control (MPC) constraint violations caused by external disturbances and outliers arising from measurement errors. First, a robust data-driven prediction model is constructed using kernel functions, and its parameters are updated via the soft-margin-based iterative quadratic programming (SMIQP) algorithm. This algorithm introduces a model slack variable (MSV) to tolerate the negative effects of outliers. This algorithm improves the robustness of the prediction model against outliers and ensures that the model can capture the complex dynamic characteristics of the system. Second, an enhanced soft constrained method (SCM) is designed to handle constraint violations. Unlike SCMs with a single slack variable, which may lead to excessive or insufficient constraint relaxation, this method employs two controller slack variables (CSVs) for coordinated constraint relaxation, thereby ensuring optimization feasibility. Combined with an adaptive weight penalty term, the proposed SCM maintains acceptable control performance in the presence of disturbances. Third, the input-to-state stability (ISS) of the proposed RDD-MPC method is rigorously proven. Finally, experimental results on classic nonlinear systems demonstrate that the proposed method can effectively improve both control performance and robustness. In addition, this method is evaluated on the Benchmark Simulation Model No. 1 (BSM1) for wastewater treatment processes (WWTPs). The experimental results further validate the effectiveness of the RDD-MPC method for systems engineering applications.

title: Holo-Current Spectrum – A novel non-invasive detection method of Misalignment and Rub-Impact Faults in Three Phase Induction Motor
authors: Umair Khalique, Ahsan Ashfaq, Xu Guanghua, Arslan. A. Rizvi, Zhen Yang, Renghao Liang, Yao Weidao
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: In industrial applications, incipient misalignment and rub-impact faults in three phase induction motors is critical, as they induce abnormal vibrations resulting in accelerated wear and tears, elevated energy consumption and may even result in a catastrophic failure. This paper presents the practical implementation of a novel non-invasive technique known as Holo-current spectrum method, that detects induction motor faults by using frequency spectrum of stator current. This method eliminates the use of complex external sensor systems. The core novelty of this work lies in the key-phasor averaging technique that establishes a baseline 0° reference thus enabling precise extraction of shaft position and phase from α–β phase vectors of stator current. This process results in a 2D elliptical orbital representation for quantitative eccentricity analysis. The validation of method was carried out on a hybrid experimental setup by synchronously acquiring current and vibration data. The results identify that the misalignment fault is prominent in 1<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">st</sup>, 2<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">nd</sup>, and 3<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">rd</sup> order elliptical components while rub-impact faults are more prominent in higher order elliptical components i.e. 4<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup> and 5<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup> order. Moreover, a correlation exceeding 0.92 was observed between orbital eccentricities and the longitudinal major axis inclination from both current and vibration signals at low angular speeds. This correlation confirms that stator current analysis alone can diagnose these faults with a precision comparable to vibration analysis. The Holo-current spectrum technique provides a direct, non-invasive assessment of fault severity level, is robust against rotor asymmetries, and serves as a significant progression in early fault identification through self-monitoring intelligence in induction motors.

title: DASFA-Net for Accurate Ship Detection in Optical Remote Sensing Images
authors: Hanfu Li, Tianwei Zhang, Shikai Jiang, Xiyang Zhi, Dawei Wang, Lianru Gao, Dong Yang, Jindong Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Ship detection in optical remote sensing imagery is pivotal for various civilian and military applications. However, due to the presence of cluttered backgrounds and the significant variations in ship scales, accurately detecting ships in complex maritime environments remains challenging. To address these challenges, we propose the Dual Attention and Scale-aware Feature Alignment Network (DASFA-Net), to effectively detect multi-scale ship targets under clutter interference. The DASFA-Net comprises two primary components: Deformable Spatial Attention Module with Channel Integration (DSAM-CI) and Bidirectional Flow Alignment Network (BiFAN). The DSAM-CI enhances discrimination between ships and distractors by jointly modeling correlations across the channel and spatial domains and applying deformable spatial attention. Concurrently, BiFAN employs a bidirectional feature flow alignment strategy to resolve spatial misalignment in feature fusion, significantly improving the quality of multi-scale feature fusion. Lastly, we design the Dynamic Alpha Complete Intersection over Union (DA-CIoU) based on prior knowledge of target characteristics to explicitly refine our detector for maritime ships. Extensive experiments on three challenging ship detection benchmarks, including HRSC2016, SCCOS, and FGSRCS have demonstrated the superiority of our DASFA.

title: Constraint-Aware Multi-Agent Decision Transformer for AoI-Optimal Multi-UAV MEC
authors: Haitao Zhao, Yihang Jia, Wenchao Xia, Yadong Wang, Weiyuan Sun, Yiyang Ni
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Multi-unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) assisted mobile edge computing enables aerial platforms to collaboratively provide computing services for delay-sensitive applications. In such systems, information freshness must be preserved while multiple UAVs simultaneously perform trajectory control, task offloading, and resource scheduling under practical energy, computation, and quality-of-service constraints. The Age of Information (AoI) metric inherently couples these decisions over long time horizons, making the design of effective coordination policies difficult for conventional optimization techniques and reinforcement learning methods. In this work, we develop a constraint-aware multi-agent decision transformer framework, referred to as Prompt-CMADT, to address AoI optimization in multi-UAV MEC networks. By casting multi-UAV coordination as a sequential decision modeling problem, the proposed framework captures long-term temporal dependencies among heterogeneous agents while explicitly accounting for system-level constraints. Moreover, a constraint-aware prompt mechanism is designed to steer policy generation toward feasible solutions, and an opponent action prediction module is introduced to alleviate inter-UAV resource contention. Numerical results demonstrate that Prompt-CMADT consistently reduces the average AoI and overall energy consumption, while improving resource utilization, when compared with representative baseline schemes.

title: Physics-Guided and Structure-Aware Weakly Supervised Cloud Detection
authors: Shuangzhou Liu, Niangang Jiao, Jingxing Zhu, Feng Wang, Hongjian You
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Existing image-level weakly supervised cloud detection methods often exhibit incomplete spatial coverage, fragmented structures, and noisy pseudo-labels, limiting segmentation reliability. To address this, we introduce PhyStructNet, a physics-guided framework that jointly leverages physical priors and structural consistency to enhance pseudo-label quality under minimal supervision. The approach combines three synergistic components: (1) a dual-branch multi-scale consistency (DMC) mechanism that stabilizes class activation map (CAM) responses across global and local perspectives; (2) a physics-guided adaptive pseudo-label selection (PG-APS) module that generates multi-level cloud candidates from interpretable radiative properties and adaptively selects reliable supervision in a model-aware fashion; and (3) a superpixel-based shape fitting (SSF) loss that enforces region-level structural coherence, refining boundaries and suppressing fragmented activations. Experiments on GF1-WHU and AIR-CD datasets show that PhyStructNet produces more complete and reliable pseudo-labels than existing image-level methods. When used to train DeepLabV3+, these pseudo-labels enable weakly supervised models to approach fully supervised performance, demonstrating both effectiveness and generalization in high-resolution remote sensing cloud detection.

title: IntelliFog Architecture for Fog Computing Environment Using Machine Learning
authors: Ruchika Bindal, Mandeep Kaur, Righa Tandon
publish: 2026 IEEE 15th International Conference on Communication Systems and Network Technologies (CSNT)
abstract: og computing has become a very promising paradigm to assist Internet of Things (IoT) based applications that require latency sensitivity and high data volumes, especially in smart agriculture. Nonetheless, it is not easy to perform tasks efficiently and make intelligent decisions at the fog layer. In this paper, the author suggests IntelliFog, a three-level architecture of IoT devices that are combined with fog nodes and cloud infrastructure that is supplemented with machine learning to process data intelligently. An algorithm called SAML is presented as a hybrid machine learning based algorithm that provides a possibility to classify and prioritize agricultural data at the fog layer and execute tasks faster and more accurately. To check the proposed approach, the experimental analysis is based on the time of execution and prediction accuracy. The findings indicate that IntelliFog has a high level of accuracy of 99.60 per cent and the execution time is reduced significantly than the current methods. The results show that the IntelliFog architecture proposed is suitable to support smart agriculture applications because it enhances performance and responsiveness of fog computing environments.og computing has become a very promising paradigm to assist Internet of Things (IoT) based applications that require latency sensitivity and high data volumes, especially in smart agriculture. title: A Robust DNA Sequencing Analysis via Modified Machine Learning Approach
authors: Pranay Yadav, Sushil Kumar, Bhanu Pratap Singh, Tarannum Dar, Parmanand Prabhat, Rakhi
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Sustainable Computing and Smart Systems (ICSCSS)
abstract: In the current generation, DNA sequencing analysis is one of the challenging tasks for researchers. In the last decade there are different chronic disease solutions possible with the help of DNA sequencing. Machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) play a vital role in bioinformatics. This research work proposed the analysis of DNA sequencing with the help of different machine learning approaches. Sorting normal genes from invalid genes that are afflicted with illnesses is one of the main challenges. In genomic research, the activities of a novel protein are discovered by categorizing DNA sequences into pre-existing groups. The research aims to use machine learning techniques to tell apart normal genes from infected ones by using classification methods like KNN, MLP, GP, DT, and SVM. In this proposed research, compare this method on DNA sequencing. The proposed Gaussian classifier with RBF (Radial Basis Function) kernel is the backbone, due to its ability to solve the complex relationships. The proposed model shows better accuracy (89.9%) as compared to different previous research work. In a nutshell, the proposed research work shows an optimal solution for DNA sequencing with different result parameters.

title: Deep Learning for Drug Molecule Generation
authors: Mohammad Alnajjar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Business Intelligence for Technology Innovation (ICBITI)
abstract: The emergence of deep learning has transformed various sectors, including drug discovery and development, by facilitating efficient and innovative approaches to the creation of molecules. The objective of this paper is to examine the use of deep learning methods in the generation of new drug molecules, underlining their capabilities to hasten the discovery process, to broaden the molecular diversity, and to enhance the pharmacological properties optimization. The use of contemporary architectures such as recurrent neural networks (RNNs), variational autoencoders (VAEs), and generative adversarial networks (GANs) allows the training of deep learning models on vast chemical databases to understand the fundamental principles of molecular structures. As soon as these models are adequately trained, they can create new molecules that have the desired traits, thus helping to determine the most promising drugs. Moreover, we will look at how we could leverage reinforcement and transfer learning for the purpose of further improvement of the Technological Project in accordance with the specific treatment goals. The paper will also address predominant challenges, including model interpretability, scalability, and accuracy of property prediction, and highlight future research directions for the maximization of the potential of deep learning in drug molecule generation. The focus of this study is to demonstrate the revolutionary capability of deep learning in drug-related projects.

title: AI-Driven Vehicle Perception: Mimicking the Human Brain for Intelligent Driving Systems
authors: Sai Jagadish Bodapati, Saibabu Merakanapalli
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems (ICICCS)
abstract: An intelligent, context-aware perception system is vital for safe and reliable functioning of autonomous vehicles in real-world environments. This study presents a biologically inspired dual-module architecture that integrates a hybrid Convolutional Neural Network-Vision Transformer (CNN-ViT) for object detection with a Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) module for temporal action prediction. The system is trained and evaluated on the KITTI dataset, leveraging real-world traffic scenarios to ensure robust performance in dynamic environments. The CNN-ViT module captures both fine-grained spatial features and global contextual cues, while the GRU module models temporal dependencies to infer driving actions such as Proceed, Brake and Caution. The model fuses visual perception and sequential reasoning similar to the layered visual and cognitive mechanisms of human brain. This end-to-end pipeline enables perception-driven decision-making by synthesizing object class, depth and motion cues over time. The framework achieved <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$93.2 \% \text{mAP}, 86.1 \% \text{IoU}$</tex> and an RMSE of 0.49 m in spatial tasks. The GRU module attained 98.74% accuracy, 98.53 % precision, 98.75 % recall and an F 1 score of 98.64 %, confirming the reliability in action prediction. With the modular design and human brain-inspired structure, the proposed system offers a scalable and interpretable solution for real-world applications in Intelligent Driving Systems.
